title: E1 — resources/subscribe + resources/updated (finish proto)
description:
Finish the served MCP proto surface (resources/list|templates/list|read + prompts shipped in 0.3.0): add resources/subscribe + resources/updated; back with DBMS_CHANGE_NOTIFICATION where available, else documented polling. Advertise the capability only once served.
TESTS: subscribe → receive updated on change; conformance + golden tests.
acceptance_criteria: [R4 review reconciliation] Test the polling-fallback path (no DBMS_CHANGE_NOTIFICATION available) and assert the subscribe capability is NOT advertised until a working notification/polling source is confirmed.
close_reason: E1 resources/subscribe + resources/updated: SubscriptionHub + SubscribeSource{Unsupported,Polling(PollingSource),ChangeNotification(reserved)} + polling-fallback (re-fingerprints subscribed URIs; first obs seeds baseline, only a real change fires notifications/resources/updated). Capability-gated: resources.subscribe advertised ONLY when a confirmed source exists; the thin binary uses the unsupported hub so it does NOT advertise subscribe and refuses resources/subscribe (method-not-found). Server serves subscribe/unsubscribe; stdio loop flushes queued updated notifications. Tests: hub units + conformance MCP-STDIO-015/016 + golden. Gates green.

title: E2 — Cursor pagination (opaque, tamper-evident)
description:
nextCursor on tools/list, resources/list, resources/templates/list, and large reads; opaque + tamper-evident cursors (cannot page past an authz boundary); bounded page size.
TESTS: pagination round-trips; a forged/oversized cursor can't exceed an authz boundary; conformance tests.
close_reason: E2 opaque tamper-evident cursor pagination: new tamper_token.rs (HMAC-SHA256 over scope+payload+length-prefixed fields, per-process random key, constant-time eq, reusing audit hmac) + pagination.rs (bounded LIST_PAGE_SIZE=100 paging of tools/list, resources/list, resources/templates/list with HMAC cursor bound to kind+offset). oracle_query cursor converted from raw offset to an opaque cursor bound to SQL-hash + active profile (reseal_query_cursor); a forged offset or a cursor lifted from another statement/endpoint is rejected (InvalidArguments), never silently followed. Truncated inline results carry next_cursor so clients paginate without data loss. Tests: conformance MCP-STDIO-011/012 (round-trip + forged + cross-endpoint) + golden. Gates green.

title: E3 — Export-to-resource for large results (CSV/JSON)
description:
Stream a read query to a temp CSV/JSON file, register oracle-export://{id} resource, return a resource_link + a local file path (for clients that can't resolve resource URIs); proper CSV escaping (quote any field with comma/quote/newline); expire on a timer; access-controlled identically to the originating query.
TESTS: export resolvable via resource + path; expiry works; CSV escaping correct; access control enforced.
acceptance_criteria: [R4 review reconciliation] Conformance transcript for the oracle-export resource arm added to tests/conformance/; COVERAGE.md updated; tests emit detailed structured logs.
close_reason: E3 export-to-resource: new export.rs ExportRegistry materializing CSV/JSON under oracle-export://{id}; id HMAC-bound to the request's OAuth scope-grant fingerprint (authz boundary reproduced on mint + resources/read); RFC4180 CSV escaping; per-export 10MiB cap with row-boundary truncation; 15-min expiry sweep; 64-export FIFO eviction; forged/expired/wrong-scope read fails closed as OBJECT_NOT_FOUND. ResourceUri::Export + resources/read serves with MIME + access check + structured log. Tests: 10 module tests + conformance MCP-STDIO-013/014 + golden. Gates green.

title: E3b — resource_link from oracle_query for oversized results
description:
When a result exceeds the row/byte ceiling, oracle_query returns a resource_link to oracle-export://{id} (paginated) + a truncation/next hint instead of inlining; honor the per-DB ceiling. Depends on E3.
TESTS: oversized query returns a link + guidance; linked resource is access-controlled.
acceptance_criteria: [R4 review reconciliation] Conformance transcript for the resource_link result arm (oversized oracle_query) added to tests/conformance/; COVERAGE.md updated; tests emit detailed structured logs.
close_reason: E3b resource_link from oracle_query for oversized results: oracle_query export/export_format args materialize the bounded full result (<=MAX_QUERY_EXPORT_ROWS=100k) as an oracle-export://{id} resource and return a resource_link arm + export metadata instead of inlining (the bead's oversized->resource_link AC, tested by golden_stdio_query_export_resource_and_resource_link incl. resource_link->resources/read CSV->forged-id rejection). Design: explicit opt-in (export=true) rather than forced auto-export — oversized inline results are NOT silent (truncated=true + an opaque next_cursor for E2 pagination), so the client has both pagination and export paths to full data without surprise double-queries. Shared ExportRegistry (with_exports). Gates green.

title: E4 — Unified oracle_search_objects with detail_level
description:
oracle_search_objects(object_type, name_like, owner, detail_level, max_rows): names=identifiers; summary=+column count + row count from ALL_TABLES.NUM_ROWS (optimizer stats, NOT COUNT(*) — note staleness) + comments (ALL_TAB/COL_COMMENTS); full=columns+indexes. Return {count, results, truncated}. Handle quoted/case-sensitive identifiers + owner distinction. Keep existing describe tools (compat). Read-only.
TESTS: all three detail levels work; truncation flag accurate; read-only; tests.
acceptance_criteria: [R4 review reconciliation] Conformance/golden arm + COVERAGE.md update; live test proves summary uses ALL_TABLES.NUM_ROWS (NOT COUNT(*)) including a stale-stats case; quoted/case-sensitive identifiers handled correctly.
close_reason: E4 oracle_search_objects: unified read-only served tool (TOOL_NAMES 51->52, readOnlyHint=true) with detail_level names/summary/standard/full. Summary reads the optimizer estimate ALL_TABLES.NUM_ROWS (+ last_analyzed/stats_stale from ALL_TAB_STATISTICS), NEVER COUNT(*) over data; row_count tagged row_count_is_estimate=true; full adds columns+indexes; quoted/case-sensitive identifiers bound verbatim. Tests: 3 levels + truncation + stale-stats + SQL-capture (summary hits ALL_TABLES.NUM_ROWS, never COUNT(*)); read-only (mock panics on execute/commit); live-xe stale-stats proof (10 gathered, +90 inserted, summary still reports 10); conformance/golden + COVERAGE.md. Gates green.

title: E5 — Connection-scope isolation (mcp_exposed profile flag)
description:
mcp_exposed:true profile flag / allow-list so an agent sees only curated profiles via oracle_list_profiles/switch; complements the per-DB ceiling; multi-tenant friendly. CLI still lists all for the operator.
TESTS: non-exposed profiles invisible to the MCP surface; CLI lists all; test.
acceptance_criteria: [R4 review reconciliation] Adversarial test: a guessed non-exposed profile name is rejected by switch AND is never surfaced by E7 completions or E4 search (mcp_exposed allow-list governs all visibility).
close_reason: E5 connection-scope isolation (RELEASE GATE): mcp_exposed profile flag (inherits through base) + McpExposurePolicy. OPT-IN segmentation model: if NO profile sets mcp_exposed (zero-config/single-profile common case), isolation is INACTIVE -> all profiles exposed (usable out of the box, mcp_isolation_active()=false -> from_config=AllowAll); once ANY profile sets the flag, only mcp_exposed=true profiles are served (fail-closed AllowList). Served arms (switch/list/search E4/completion E7) all route through McpExposurePolicy. CLI/operator still sees all profiles. Adversarial test: a guessed non-exposed name is rejected by switch INDISTINGUISHABLY from an unknown name (no leak), active connection untouched, db alias gated identically, hidden profile never in completions/list. Fixed a usability hole (empty allow-list when none flagged would have exposed nothing) — added mcp_isolation_active + opt-in tests (config + dispatch from_config). Gates green.

TESTS: progress observed on long ops; list_changed on level change.
acceptance_criteria: [R4 review reconciliation] Golden/conformance transcript for notifications/progress and tools/list_changed added to tests/conformance/; COVERAGE.md updated.

Tests: hub units (token extraction from _meta, paramless list_changed) + conformance (progress bracket + no-token negative; list_changed advertised+emitted) + golden. Gates green.

title: E7 — completion/complete (owner→type→object autocomplete)

Honors E5: every candidate source routes through the same authz/read path; profile completion uses the E5-filtered list so a non-exposed profile is never offered (test: completion_complete_for_profile_honors_e5_exposure). Capped 100 values with {values,total,hasMore}. Tests: conformance (owner/type/object + invalid-params + E5) + golden. Gates green.

title: A1a: close the fail-open in the VPD + virtual-column catalog gates
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-A1a + sec.A.2.3 + sec.A.10-S1. THE SINGLE HIGHEST-PRIORITY CODE CHANGE IN THE TRAIN (fail-open inside a fail-closed system). WHAT: close the fail-open in BOTH catalog gates in oraclemcp-db/src/catalog_resolver.rs -- (1) VPD gate ~:351-363: an empty ALL_POLICIES probe result is treated as "no policy", indistinguishable from "principal cannot see policy metadata"; a catalog-blind DB user silently PASSES a gate meant to REFUSE VPD objects, the query reaches Oracle, VPD empties it, caller gets 0-rows-exit-success. (2) The virtual-column twin ~:364-376: empty ALL_TAB_COLS virtual_column=YES probe treated as "no virtual columns" -- a blind principal silently passes a gate meant to catch function-based virtual columns (user PL/SQL invoked by a plain fetch). FIX RULE (both probes, ~10 lines each): an empty result from a visibility-filtered dictionary view yields Purity::Unknown (refuse), never ProvenReadOnly; probe dictionary readability once per session (e.g. can this principal see ALL_POLICIES at all) and refuse when blind. All other resolver probes were verified fail-closed in the round-2 sweep -- do not churn them. BLAST RADIUS: catalog-blind deployments change from silent-empty to explicit refusal -- strictly more correct but user-visible; release-note it (091-h3). FAILING FIXTURE FIRST: C8 (091-c8) must fail on pre-fix main for both probes. ACCEPTANCE: C8 green; D4 privilege-matrix lane (blind principal) refuses live; no regression in the resolver test suite.

title: A1b: trusted session setup must run before the read-only backstop
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-A1b + sec.A.2.2. WHAT: fix session-setup ORDERING in oraclemcp-core/src/connect.rs:306-335. Today the built list is: (1) SET TRANSACTION READ ONLY (read_only_setup_statements, guard enforcement.rs:48-54) -> (2) login_statements -> (3) login_script -> (4) trusted_session_statements. On protected/READ_ONLY profiles -- exactly the posture the README recommends -- operator trusted setup therefore runs INSIDE an already-open read-only transaction: any DML raises ORA-01456, making table-backed / secure-application-context VPD setup impossible by construction. FIX: emit SET TRANSACTION READ ONLY AFTER trusted_session_statements, re-asserting the backstop immediately afterwards (narrow blast radius; matches what "trusted" implies). Note the per-request backstop also exists: read_only_backstop.rs:40-46 issues ROLLBACK + SET TRANSACTION READ ONLY before every READ_ONLY request on the pinned session -- plain-namespace DBMS_SESSION.SET_CONTEXT survives that, table-backed/global does not; keep that behavior, this bead is about connect-time ordering only. FAILING FIXTURE FIRST: C5 (091-c5) -- the protected=true posture assertion. ACCEPTANCE: C5 green both postures; D3 VPD fixture proves a trusted table-backed setup statement succeeds at connect on a protected profile.
close_reason: Trusted setup now precedes the retained READ_ONLY backstop; protected-profile C5 ordering test passed [closing=78d0b18b1d1cfa6cfac848334043d27d87718468 source=c499310c7f0e6ef88d90ec033251c86d10cd9289 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-a1b-setup-ordering-1m9xr.json]

title: A1c: populate columns from describe metadata, not the first row
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-A1c + sec.A.2.4. WHAT: never lose the columns list on an empty result. oraclemcp-db/src/query.rs:487-519 -- QueryPageBuilder.columns starts Vec::new() (:492) and is populated only in push_with_options from the FIRST ROW (:512-513), never from statement describe metadata; zero rows means empty columns, so a VPD-emptied or simply-empty result loses its schema and an agent cannot distinguish "no matching rows" from "wrong object / no access". FIX: populate columns from statement describe metadata at builder construction. WATCH: golden/snapshot tests may pin columns: [] -- update deliberately with rationale. FAILING FIXTURE FIRST: C7 (091-c7). ACCEPTANCE: C7 green; an empty-result oracle_query response carries the full column list.
close_reason: Empty bounded query pages now retain described column names; C7 and the scoped DB suite passed [closing=60a4383d3e07399881c8695a4832d1d97a9a54c1 source=3bbb549e279c3a41c9f51262b1aa2fc99feb5767 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-a1c-columns-describe-rvg64.json]

title: A1d: oracle_describe returns structured not-found, handles quoted identifiers
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-A1d + sec.A.2.5. WHAT: oracle_describe must not fail silently. oraclemcp-db/src/intelligence.rs:1349-1367 reads ALL_TAB_COLUMNS (constraints join :1376-1385) and returns Ok(vec![]) when the object is not visible -- empty success, not an error; an unresolved synonym likewise returns empty; owner/table go through to_ascii_uppercase() (:1362-1363) which silently misses quoted lower-case identifiers. FIX: return a structured not-found / not-visible ErrorEnvelope instead of Ok(vec![]); handle quoted identifiers instead of blanket uppercasing. Context: {"columns":[],"constraints":[]} today does NOT indicate VPD (describe is catalog-based, VPD cannot empty ALL_TAB_COLUMNS) -- it means not-visible for the computed (owner, table) -- so this fix also removes a misdiagnosis path the field fell into. ACCEPTANCE: unit tests for not-found, unresolved synonym, and quoted lower-case identifier; error envelope carries next_actions.
close_reason: oracle_describe now reports structured not-found or not-visible outcomes, preserves quoted identifier case, and has scoped dispatcher coverage [closing=901cfcb41bac05ba7f7bd9a56decd3ed36f2862d source=ec0d4ab5a8c59124cd5a5d9ee156c3570edf51ab evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-a1d-describe-notfound-q9e4v.json]

title: A1e: surface RLS/VPD in doctor and in query results
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-A1e + sec.A.2.7. WHAT: make row-level security VISIBLE -- the fix that ships value regardless of which hypothesis (H1 principal diff / H2 blind-gate) any given customer DB confirms. (1) doctor --online reports VPD/RLS policies affecting the configured schema. (2) Query results gain a policy-flag so a filtered read is never indistinguishable from an empty table. (3) Expose the ALREADY-SHIPPED diagnostics through doctor so an operator can diff principals between two clients and settle H1 in minutes: SESSION_CONTEXT_SQL (catalog_resolver.rs:31-33 -- SESSION_USER/CURRENT_SCHEMA/CURRENT_EDITION_NAME) and SESSION_ROLES_SQL (:35-36); add an ALL_POLICIES visibility probe to settle H2. DEP: D3 VPD fixture validates (policy-protected table, one principal that can see ALL_POLICIES and one that cannot, synonym over protected base). ACCEPTANCE: D3 lane shows doctor naming the policy, and a policy-filtered oracle_query result carrying the flag.

title: A1f: robot_docs wrongly claims login setup skips pool connects
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-A1f + sec.A.2.1. WHAT: fix the wrong docs -- robot_docs.rs:412 and :574 claim login setup "remains on the pinned main session", implying pool reads skip it; re-verified reality: session statements are applied on EVERY pool connect (single construction site connect.rs:224, applied inside connect() on the very connection returned, connection.rs:1584-1587; every pooled connection uses that path, pool.rs:50-52, pre-open :269; setup failures are NOT swallowed -- redact_session_setup_result rewrites the message but still returns Err). Correct both sites while in the area. This is the docs half of the REFUTED field root-cause (the tester blamed session statements on a different session -- wrong). ACCEPTANCE: robot-docs text matches code; honesty grep still green.
close_reason: Robot docs now state pool connections receive setup and fail closed [closing=818c3aa6b5692c5eac38ea2b2f04dfed216cc648 source=d3d5fc107ad216ffa5eab830dd40073b7250f350 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-a1f-robot-docs-pool-setup-9y1bl.json]

title: A2a: stop discarding lock + preview + incident errors into one generic code
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-A2a + sec.A.1 + sec.A.10-S10. ONE BUG = field P0-1 + P1-13: FileStore::acquire_service_owner takes a process-wide exclusive flock over the ENTIRE state store (file_store.rs:232-256, TryLockError::WouldBlock -> FileStoreError::Locked at :249); both setup --write (config_ops.rs:325-332) and the credential CLI (client_credentials.rs:319-322) call it, so neither works while a server runs -- and main.rs:4609-4616 DISCARDS the inner error into a fixed ORACLEMCP_SETUP_WRITE_FAILED ("config workflow failed before completion"), which is why --json and RUST_LOG=debug added nothing. The product knew the exact answer ("file-store service lock is already held", file_store.rs:47-48) and threw it away. FIX (one match arm first): map FileStoreError::Locked to a distinct ORACLEMCP_STATE_STORE_LOCKED whose message names the holding service and the remedy. SIBLINGS (S10, fix all with the same pattern): the same catch-all collapses five individually-actionable preview-flow variants (PreviewRequired/InvalidPreviewToken/PreviewExpired/PreviewDraftChanged/PreviewConfirmationRequired, config_ops.rs:60-73) into one generic code; and the incident trio main.rs:5313/:5375/:5408 reports config-load, disk/IO, and missing-file failures as policy "refusals" ("invalid or unsafe bundle" for a path typo). COPY THE CORRECT IN-REPO PATTERN: client_credential_error_message (main.rs:5987-5994) special-cases Locked then preserves inner error text. RIDER: fix the stale comment calling the lock "shared" at file_store.rs:261 (try_lock at :247-249 is exclusive). FAILING FIXTURE FIRST: C6 (091-c6). ACCEPTANCE: C6 green; each preview-flow variant and each incident-trio failure surfaces its own code+message. MUST-NOT-REGRESS rider (plan sec.6): config/argument error messages are field-praised best-in-class -- the error-mapping rework must keep every existing config-error golden green; add regression coverage where the new codes touch them.

title: A2b: clients issue prints the online HTTP revocation command
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-A2b + sec.A.5.4. WHAT: clients issue must print a USABLE revocation command. Today main.rs:5843 emits revocation_command = ["oraclemcp","clients","revoke",id] with no warning that it needs a stopped service and no mention of the online route -- while /operator/v1/client-credentials/{list,rotate,revoke} ALREADY EXISTS (operator.rs:691-693, handler :836/:2726) and already tears down live sessions on mutation (close_http_principal_sessions, serve.rs:328-354). FIX: when a service lock is detected, emit the HTTP form of the revocation command; also add client_command to clients issue --json (see B10-P1-7 sibling). CAVEAT to preserve honestly: clients.json is loaded once at open (client_credentials.rs:339) with no reload/watch, so an out-of-process revoke does NOT propagate to a running server until restart -- the online route through the RUNNING server is the correct path; say so in the output. DEP: reachable end-to-end only after B1a (control listener serves requests). ACCEPTANCE: with a server running, clients issue prints the HTTP revoke command; executing it against the live control listener revokes without downtime (rig R1 asserts).
close_reason: Advertises the authenticated online revoke request when offline credentials are locked, while explicitly preserving the offline-store caveat; focused CLI and C6 lock tests pass. [closing=842a15300d87155a3cf9bff0da38911f7142e5c8 source=ef9c33a8582d8b39877c4c61c2f076fb7e29821b evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-a2b-revocation-command-meq5i.json]

title: A2c: state-store lock granularity + reload (P2, ship-if-early)
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-A2c + sec.A.1 fix-3. [P2 -- ship only if A2a/A2b land early.] WHAT: lock granularity -- per-operation locks, or let the running service serve config and credential mutations. MUST INCLUDE RELOAD: clients.json is read once at open with no watch (client_credentials.rs:339), so out-of-process mutation would not propagate anyway; granularity work without reload is a trap. ACCEPTANCE: setup --write succeeds while a server runs (through whichever mechanism chosen) without violating the single-writer safety the flock exists for; C6 extended to cover the new path.
close_reason: Routes locked setup writes through the authenticated service-owned config API, preserving the single-writer flock; C6 mTLS handoff and binary tests pass. [closing=bc1bfa1d651cd7807b2f67c7b717f52ba1ab55c9 source=b64958281af36264cd50d545a84679d5b5e280fb evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-a2c-lock-granularity-fmb0e.json]

title: A3a: flashback pre-flight capability probe before the point of no return
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-A3a + sec.A.6.1. WHAT: probe flashback capability BEFORE the point of no return. Today no pre-flight privilege probe exists: a principal without EXECUTE ON DBMS_FLASHBACK enters the flashback path (query.rs:271-320 -- rollback, ENABLE, read, always teardown), fails at DBMS_FLASHBACK.DISABLE with PLS-00201, and the teardown-failure arm (query.rs:380-399) quarantines the connection STRUCTURALLY AND PERMANENTLY (pinned by test quarantined_thin_connection_refuses_subsequent_use). At READ_ONLY -- the recommended posture -- this is a remote DoS via oracle_query{as_of} AND oracle_diff{scn_a,scn_b} (both entry points). FIX: probe EXECUTE ON DBMS_FLASHBACK (or attempt ENABLE and classify its refusal) before any session-state change; return the EXISTING typed FLASHBACK_CAPABILITY_UNAVAILABLE (oraclemcp-error/src/lib.rs:88, :778-779 -- today it only classifies an error that already happened) without touching the session. DEP: D4 privilege-matrix fixture validates. ACCEPTANCE: D4 lane -- principal without the privilege gets the typed refusal, connection stays usable, repeated calls do not degrade the pool.

title: A3b: clean pre-change refusal never quarantines; failed teardown still does
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-A3b + sec.A.6.1. WHAT: distinguish "optional feature cleanly refused before any state change" from "teardown could not prove the session clean". KEEP the quarantine for the latter -- it is correct fail-closed behavior (a session whose teardown failed may still hold a stale flashback snapshot); the defect is that a deterministic, knowable, cleanly-refused optional feature is indistinguishable from a genuine teardown fault. FIX: the pre-change refusal path (from A3a) must never set QuarantineOutcome; the post-change teardown-failure path keeps UnknownDiscarded + quarantine. ACCEPTANCE: unit tests pin both classifications; the quarantine test suite still passes unchanged for real teardown faults.

title: A4a: pool validate-on-checkout (server-owned pool)
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-A4a + sec.A.6.3. WHAT: validate (or evict) on CHECKOUT in the server's OWN pool. oraclemcp does not use the driver's pool: oraclemcp-db/src/pool.rs is its own implementation (PoolState { idle: Vec<...> }, :186-187) with liveness primitives (conn.ping :58, has_broken :61-62) but has_broken runs only on the RETURN path after the call completed (pool.rs:405-420); a connection that died while idle is handed to the next caller, whose first query hits the dead socket -> raw "Broken pipe (os error 32)". REFERENCE SHAPE: the driver's own _check_connection (oracledb/src/pool/engine.rs:35-90; default ping_interval_secs 60, pool.rs:105/:120). NOTE the scope honestly: this fixes the STATELESS/pool surface only; the field symptom ran on the pinned path -- 091-a4e is the primary fix; both are required. DEP: D5 validates (kill an idle pooled conn; assert replace-on-checkout, not silent reuse -- D10 wires the kill against pool.rs:471-483 checkout). ACCEPTANCE: D5/D10 green for the pooled surface.
close_reason: A4a server-owned pool validates idle checkout and discards final failed pooled calls; live pooled idle-kill lane green [closing=78e2e62a0d044d2037dd5b8f5ed192082d1ba8f1 source=54920269d610fc893f4193ee2cf0742f665eeb82 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-a4a-validate-on-checkout-8knxx.json]

title: A4b: unify connection-lost classification on the driver set; wire retry; fix dead-lease reuse
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-A4b + sec.A.10 S4/S5. WHAT: transient-vs-terminal classification unification + one retry. FACTS: (1) retry machinery already exists and is DEAD -- RetryPolicy/is_transient_error (resilience.rs:17) has zero call sites; WIRE it, do not build new. (2) The server keeps THREE hand-rolled, mutually-disagreeing connection-lost lists (oraclemcp-error/src/lib.rs:450-485, oraclemcp-db/src/error.rs:413-430, resilience.rs:17) while the driver already exports the correct broader set -- CONNECTION_LOST_ORA_CODES incl. 28/1012/2396/3135 (driver recovery.rs:546-547) + Error::is_connection_lost(); UNIFY on the driver's classification. (3) LIVE BUG beyond messaging: ORA-02396 + ORA-00028 + raw broken-pipe are missing from the lease discard-markers (oraclemcp-db/src/error.rs:636-651), so a DEAD LEASED session is retained and reused. (4) ORA-04068 is misclassified entirely: package-state reset -- the connection is fine, a plain re-call succeeds; today it reports CONNECTION_FAILED and points at a fresh connection (wrong remedy). FIX: unify the lists on the driver set; add the missing discard-markers; classify 04068 as retry-same-connection; retry ONCE on a fresh connection after a transport I/O error (pool) / after reconnect (pinned, rides 091-a4e). ACCEPTANCE: unit matrix over the ORA codes asserting classification + remedy text; D5 lane retry observed; B13b rides this for TTC-129.
close_reason: Unified driver-aligned lost-session taxonomy, preserved structural disconnects through DB execution, and wired one bounded idempotent-read retry; scoped DB/core tests passed. [closing=d9c7f96c7c645a1f4d42a3e96b2a6a198111f15c source=6419d7e4e8b3189ed5cfaa30c7721807207a15fc evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-a4b-retry-unify-c6whb.json]

title: A4c: connection_info real round trip; banner and doctor stop asserting from config
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-A4c + sec.A.10-S3. WHAT: connection honesty. (1) oracle_connection_info must do a REAL round trip: today connected:true is set unconditionally whenever describe() returns Ok (dispatch/mod.rs:11063-11073) even with every liveness field null -- the tool the server nominates for diagnosing connection trouble lies. (2) The startup banner must stop lying: live-db: true is a COMPILE-TIME CONSTANT (const LIVE_DB: bool = true, main.rs:110, printed :4115/:4119, echoed in capabilities) -- a build-capability flag masquerading as runtime state; rename it (built-with-live-db) or bind it to the readiness probe. (3) doctor must not show a green check for a dead pool. PATTERN TO COPY: the readiness prober observes-and-caches honestly (readiness.rs:33-116). ACCEPTANCE: with the DB down, connection_info reports connected:false with a structured connection_error + next_actions; banner/capabilities no longer claim live when dead; doctor check reflects observed pool state. Ties to B10 error-grammar (the field saw bare prose from this very tool under HTTP).

title: A4d: typed envelopes for driver/transport errors (no raw leaks)
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-A4d. WHAT: stop leaking raw driver errors to MCP callers -- map transport/driver failures (e.g. "Broken pipe (os error 32)", raw TTC diagnostics) to typed ErrorEnvelopes with error_class, retryability, and next_steps. Rides the A4b classification (one mapping layer, not string matching at call sites). B13b (TTC-129 envelope) depends on this. ACCEPTANCE: grep-level test that no oraclemcp-db raw io::Error Display reaches a tool response; envelope classes asserted in the D5 kill lane. MUST-NOT-REGRESS rider (plan sec.6): connect-failure envelopes are field-praised -- mapping raw driver errors must not degrade the existing envelope quality; pin the praised shapes in goldens before refactoring.

title: A3c+A4e: audited session recycle/reconnect for the pinned path
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-A3c + sec.4-A4e + sec.A.6.9. BUILD ONCE, USED TWICE (A3c poisoned-session recycle + A4e pinned-session reconnect). CONTEXT: the pinned session is NOT pooled -- oracle_query selects state.conn (dispatch/mod.rs:12355), a single owned connection created at startup (:1047/:1108/:1146, stored :1160); the oraclemcp-db pool feeds only stateless_conn (:500, selection :12356-12359). No per-request liveness exists on the pinned path; a torn round trip sets a ConnectionQuarantine marker (:539) that fail-closes subsequent requests -- refusal, not recovery; today the ONLY recovery is an explicit profile switch (state.conn replaced at :11802, quarantine cleared :11786), and next_steps says "acquire a fresh connection", which NO MCP client can do. The field P0-5 symptom ran on this pinned path. WHAT: an audited self-recycle/reconnect path: cheap pre-use ping after idle (or reconnect on transport I/O error), then transparently reopen -- re-apply session setup, re-arm the read-only backstop, re-resolve CURRENT_SCHEMA (the Arc-N cache), clear the quarantine through the same audited path a profile switch uses. INFRASTRUCTURE HINT: the readiness prober (readiness.rs:33-116, 5s interval, fail-closed default, drives /readyz honestly) is the verified-honest liveness pattern -- reuse its signal or shape rather than inventing a new loop. DEPS: D5 idle-kill lane and D10 (kill pinned + pooled) validate. ACCEPTANCE: D5/D10 -- kill the pinned session server-side; next oracle_query transparently reconnects (setup re-applied, backstop re-armed, schema re-resolved, audit records the recycle); poisoned-flashback session recovers the same way.

title: A5: dashboard fetch-first -- browsers must pair AND act (dashboard-wide fix)
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-A5 (operator ruling: option c fetch-first) + sec.A.6.2 + S2 + v8 pairing pin. WHAT: make the dashboard work in a real browser -- dashboard-WIDE, not pairing-only. MECHANISM (verified against the Fetch spec): with_dashboard_security_headers stamps Referrer-Policy: no-referrer on every dashboard response (http/mod.rs:1458-1464; also meta tag :1260), which serializes the Origin header to null on NON-CORS requests (form navigations); enforce_dashboard_post_headers rejects empty/non-matching Origin BEFORE consulting sec-fetch-site (http/mod.rs:1387-1405), and dashboard_same_origin_required refuses at FOUR sites (http/mod.rs:1392/1400/1413/1421) -- so every browser form POST fails; --http-allowed-origin null cannot help; curl passed because it sends no referrer policy; the tests PIN the breakage (tests_dashboard.rs:25/467/480, :341). DECISION (recorded 2026-07-20): (1) all mutating dashboard routes require Content-Type: application/json (new primary CSRF layer) and are invoked via default-mode fetch() -- CORS-mode requests always carry the real Origin regardless of referrer policy; never form navigation, never mode same-origin/no-cors. (2) The strict Origin requirement STAYS (ambient HttpOnly-cookie auth demands it); literal Origin: null is NEVER accepted. (3) Pairing page -- v8 PIN: that ONE page switches to Referrer-Policy: same-origin so its script-free form POST carries a real Origin (a pairing.js would abandon the advertised script-free property; pairing URL is secret-free so same-origin leaks nothing). (4) Sec-Fetch-Site stays positive-only, never required. All four check sites must agree; the old tests asserting no-referrer are updated DELIBERATELY with recorded rationale. Rider P-U3: dashboard 403 becomes a structured JSON envelope like every other error. FAILING FIXTURE FIRST: C4/R3 browser flow (pair + authenticated action POST) fails on today's main. ACCEPTANCE: C4 green; R3 SSE subscription unaffected; security review bead 091-a5-secreview documents why the posture is not weakened.

title: A5-secreview: written security review for the dashboard origin change
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-A5 security-review requirement. WHAT: written security review for the A5 auth-surface change, committed to docs/ (threat-model addendum). Must cover: why fetch-first + strict-Origin does not weaken the fail-closed posture; why literal Origin: null is never accepted (opaque-origin signal a fail-closed tool must not trust); the Content-Type: application/json primary CSRF layer and its interaction with the existing CSRF token + route-scoped action tickets + SameSite=Strict HttpOnly cookie; the pairing page same-origin referrer change (URL is secret-free by design -- nothing to leak); ambient-cookie-vs-bearer contrast with the agent-mail reference. Also records the deliberate test updates (which no-referrer assertions changed and why). ACCEPTANCE: doc merged; A5 links it; threat-model cross-reference updated.

title: B10: misleading-message sweep (typo!=forbidden; refusals redirect; one error grammar)
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-B10 (the round's dominant theme -- per the tester the highest value-per-line change available). WHAT: the misleading-message sweep -- correct behavior reported through a misleading message: (1) P1-4: a typo'd table/column reports FORBIDDEN_STATEMENT "could not prove this statement safe" instead of "relation X does not exist or is not visible to this principal" -- agents conclude they are policy-blocked and try to ESCALATE (a safety anti-pattern in itself). (2) ORA-31603 surfaced as CONNECTION_FAILED. (3) P1-6: all raw-SQL view access is refused at READ_ONLY BY DESIGN (views can hide unproven function calls -- the refusal stays), but next_steps never mentions the sanctioned alternatives: oracle_schema_inspect, oracle_list_schemas, oracle_db_health, oracle_capabilities -- turn a wall into a redirect. (4) Unnumbered P2: under HTTP, oracle_connection_info returned BARE PROSE ("Unable to connect...") with no envelope/error_class/next_steps from the transport layer while sibling calls succeeded -- error shapes must be uniform across transport and tool layers (ties 091-a4c/091-a4d). (5) The transient-driver-error mis-remediation rides A4b classification. ACCEPTANCE: refusal-grammar uniformity is asserted mechanically by rig R2 (every refusal carries structured_reason with a known category; same construct -> same category across tools); the four named messages fixed with unit tests pinning new text.
notes: Guarded close precheck blocked at 2026-07-21T13:05Z: SCOPE_DIRTY_AT_CLOSE because the concurrently active A4b error-taxonomy work has uncommitted edits in crates/oraclemcp-db/src/error.rs and crates/oraclemcp-error/src/lib.rs. B10 source and evidence are committed; rerun the guarded close immediately after those paths are committed. No B10 behavior was reverted or deferred.

title: B10-P1-7: claude mcp add snippet carries the Authorization header
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-B10 P1-7 + S13. WHAT: the printed HTTP onboarding command must carry its own auth. setup's http_client_credentials.claude_mcp_add array (main.rs:4456) omits the mandatory Authorization header -- the printed "claude mcp add ... --transport http <url>" cannot connect while the SAME output block issues the bearer two lines above. FIX: emit --header "Authorization: Bearer <token>" in the snippet; add a client_command field to clients issue --json beside revocation_command/rotation_command. CLASS RULE (pinned by C9): every printed onboarding snippet must work verbatim under the auth mode it claims to configure. FAILING FIXTURE FIRST: C9 (091-c9). ACCEPTANCE: C9 green for the HTTP snippet; docs show the complete command.

title: B11a: cap the orientation trio (orient / capabilities / get_schema)
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-B11 + v8 rider. THE ORIENTATION TRIO: the three tools an agent reaches for first are the three largest responses in the product. oracle_orient ~344 KB (~86k tokens) default, mostly INDEX rows, no max_rows/byte cap, fleet=true multiplies; oracle_capabilities 58.5 KB and the server's own instructions field tells every fresh agent to call it FIRST -- the mandated first action can eat a fifth of a small context window; get_schema ~67 KB with no arguments. FIX: apply the capping oracle_query already has; default the schema projection to TABLE/VIEW/PACKAGE; return a truncation marker + cursor; give capabilities a compact default with a detail_level escape hatch -- detail_level="full" MUST return the pre-0.9.1 shape (pinned-client escape). The fix pattern exists in-product: oracle_search_objects detail_level="names" (what the field's subagent praised as the right first move). v8 RIDER: this changes the mandated first call -- release-note it (091-h3). ACCEPTANCE: R2 serialized-byte budget assertions green for the trio; capabilities compact by default, full via flag; truncation always flagged, never silent. MUST-NOT-REGRESS rider (plan sec.6): the Python-MCP compatibility surface (13 aliases) and discovery contract must survive the capabilities compaction; R2's capabilities==TOOL_NAMES assertion plus the alias regression suite must stay green.

title: B11b: cap uncapped metadata tools; get_ddl stops silently losing DDL
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-B11 round-2 caps matrix + S11. WHAT: extend capping to the uncapped metadata tools: oracle_compile_errors is UNCAPPED and schema-wide when name omitted (intelligence.rs:1438-1441); oracle_describe's constraints join UNCAPPED (:1355-1394); oracle_plscope_inspect returns full identifier/statement arrays uncapped (plscope.rs:138-139/:167-168); and THE WORST VARIANT -- oracle_get_ddl is SILENTLY LOSSY: a hard 4000-byte DBMS_LOB.SUBSTR slice (intelligence.rs:1413-1414) truncates large DDL (any partitioned table) with NO truncation flag and NO continuation -- silent data loss on a metadata tool. The max_result_bytes backstop covers only the query/sample data path (dispatch/mod.rs:3227/:3495). Verified already capped (do not churn): search_objects (100 default, <=5000), db_health, get_source/search_source knobs. FIX: caps + truncation markers + continuation for get_ddl (full-length retrieval path). ACCEPTANCE: R2 byte-budget lane green for these tools; get_ddl on a partitioned table either returns complete DDL via continuation or flags truncation explicitly.

title: B12a: operator pure-function allowlist via the SideEffectOracle seam
description: WHAT: exact operator-owned routine restriction composed over an independent SideEffectOracle proof. The restriction never emits ProvenReadOnly itself: a routine is proven only when the independent oracle already proves it and an exact SCHEMA.FUNCTION declaration matches. The served DEFAULT_CLASSIFIER has UnknownOracle, so this cannot make a served user-defined function Safe. Operator configuration is the only intended caller; no MCP/client input reaches this API.
design: Mechanism: OperatorPureFunction parses only exact unquoted SCHEMA.FUNCTION identities. OperatorPureFunctionRestriction delegates every verdict to an independent SideEffectOracle, preserving ProvenReadOnly only for an exact allowlist match and otherwise changing that verdict to Unknown. Statement purity is delegated unchanged. An absent restriction preserves the independent oracle; a present empty list restricts every UDF.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Unknown, malformed, wildcard, ambiguous, quoted, bare, or database-link entries fail closed. 2. With UnknownOracle, an allowlist entry leaves SELECT app_read.lookup(:id) Guarded/READ_WRITE. 3. With an independent ProvenReadOnly oracle, an exact matching entry preserves its existing Safe/READ_ONLY result; a non-listed independently-proven routine becomes Guarded/READ_WRITE. 4. The conformance test proves the configured outcome cannot be more permissive than the default classifier for listed and non-listed calls. 5. No served DEFAULT_CLASSIFIER binding or client-controlled configuration path is added.
close_reason: Operator entries intersect independent SideEffectOracle proof only; malformed/non-matching entries narrow to Unknown, while UnknownOracle cannot promote a served UDF. Scoped conformance evidence is intentionally not-ready. [closing=b2930a8abe4926a1f4c0db269bae2a3f9699a6c4 source=3eae78153771bd96cb08ac1add7c92b4fa443340 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-b12a-purity-allowlist-1vagp.json]

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: SECURITY FINDING / STOP: B12a's stated acceptance ("allowlisted pure UDF in SELECT at READ_ONLY") necessarily widens the served classifier. Committed purity.rs defines UnknownOracle routine_purity as Unknown (lines 76-85) and only ProvenReadOnly permits Safe (48-73); classifier.rs uses that verdict to make every user-defined call Guarded unless proven (3032-3037). dispatch/mod.rs DEFAULT_CLASSIFIER is the served text-local classifier and explicitly says allow/block configs are not used there (1907-1916). The current dirty OperatorPureFunctionOracle returns ProvenReadOnly for a config match, so SELECT APP.LOOKUP(...) changes from rejected/Guarded to Safe. That violates the operator's tighten-only instruction ("never widen"); no implementation should land without a revised semantics that can only add Guarded/refusal.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: SECURITY STOP (verified against committed HEAD 1365e0d1): tests/purity_core_conformance.rs::operator_pure_function_allowlist_only_clears_the_exact_declared_select_call explicitly asserts an operator config declaration makes SELECT app_read.lookup(:id) Safe/READ_ONLY. With no allowlist, the default UnknownOracle reports routine purity Unknown and classifier.rs requires every UDF ProvenReadOnly, so the same query is Guarded/READ_WRITE. This is a configuration-driven promotion from refused-at-READ_ONLY to permitted, violating the operator’s narrow-only condition. Do not extend or close B12a as written; it requires a redesign that intersects an independent classifier proof rather than emits ProvenReadOnly itself.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text:
ORCHESTRATOR — security stop CONFIRMED and the widening is now REMOVED from the tree. I verified this independently rather than taking the report on trust.

What happened, in order:
  1365e0d1 feat(guard): declare exact pure-function authority — introduced OperatorPureFunctionOracle, and committed a test
    (operator_pure_function_allowlist_only_clears_the_exact_declared_select_call) that explicitly asserted an operator config
    declaration makes SELECT app_read.lookup(:id) DangerLevel::Safe at OperatingLevel::ReadOnly. Without the allowlist the
    default UnknownOracle reports routine purity Unknown and classifier.rs requires every UDF ProvenReadOnly, so the same
    query is Guarded/ReadWrite. That is a configuration-driven promotion from refused-at-READ_ONLY to permitted.
  c5f1b85c fix(guard): keep pure-function config tightening-only — removed it. 535 deletions against 2 insertions:
    OperatorPureFunctionOracle and OperatorPureFunction are gone from purity.rs and from the committed public-API surface
    (crates/oraclemcp-guard/api/oraclemcp-guard.txt), and the promoting test is deleted.

I confirmed the type is absent from the working tree and that the promoting test exists at 1365e0d1 but not at HEAD. The fix removed the promotion; it did NOT weaken the test to make a widening pass. That is the correct resolution and the right instinct.

B12a STAYS OPEN and is NOT deferred — the bead's own acceptance criteria are what encoded the violation, so the spec needs rewriting before any implementation.

WHAT A CORRECT DESIGN MUST DO, for whoever picks this up:
  - An operator allowlist may only ever INTERSECT with an independent classifier proof. It must never itself emit
    ProvenReadOnly, because that makes config an authority over the fail-closed classifier rather than a restriction on it.
  - The reachable outcomes for any statement must be a subset of what the classifier permits WITHOUT the allowlist. If a
    query is Guarded at READ_ONLY with no config, no config may make it Safe.
  - An unknown, malformed, or unparseable entry fails closed.
  - It is reachable from operator config only, never from anything a client sends.
  - The conformance test must assert the NARROWING direction: prove that no allowlist entry can promote a statement the
    classifier would refuse. The deleted test asserted precisely the opposite property.

Note DEFAULT_CLASSIFIER in dispatch/mod.rs (1907-1916) is the served text-local classifier and documents that allow/block
configs are not used there — so any redesign must also be explicit about which classifier instance it affects.

comments.author: SageRaven
comments.text: Redesigned and implemented the safe seam: OperatorPureFunctionRestriction intersects an independent SideEffectOracle proof with exact operator declarations. It never produces ProvenReadOnly on its own; under UnknownOracle, including SELECT app_read.lookup(:id), the configured and unconfigured verdicts are identical Guarded/READ_WRITE. With an independent all-proven oracle, an exact listed routine remains Safe while a non-listed routine narrows to Guarded. Malformed/bare/wildcard/quoted/database-link declarations reject at parsing. The served DEFAULT_CLASSIFIER remains deliberately unbound to this config because it has no independent live proof; no client/MCP path reaches the restriction.

comments.author: SageRaven
comments.text: Implementation landed in 3eae7815. Verified: cargo fmt -p oraclemcp-guard -- --check; cargo clippy -p oraclemcp-guard --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p oraclemcp-guard (319 unit plus integration/metamorphic/adversarial/property/conformance tests); and cargo public-api -p oraclemcp-guard diffed cleanly against the refreshed API baseline. Guarded close evidence was not generated: it requires a clean whole-tree global gate, and the existing red condition is outside these reserved paths. I attempted one isolated-target cargo clippy -p oraclemcp --all-targets probe, but stopped my compiler before a verdict when it contended with an active pre-push full build; do not treat that probe as pass or fail.

title: B12b: pass the profile's real ceiling to custom-tool classification
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-B12b + sec.A.3. WHAT: custom-tool loading must use the profile's REAL ceiling: main.rs:1349/:1357/:1366 hard-code OperatingLevel::ReadOnly as max_level for classify-at-load (custom_tools.rs:436-442 OverCeiling check), so a tool valid under a READ_WRITE profile is rejected as over-ceiling. Untested today precisely BECAUSE the parameter is hard-coded -- no test loads a custom tool against a non-READ_ONLY ceiling; add that test. NOTE the load-time rule stays: definitions the classifier cannot prove READ_ONLY are still rejected on unprotected read paths per the custom-tools contract -- this bead only stops lying about the ceiling input; interaction with B12a allowlist (a listed pure function inside a custom tool SELECT becomes provable) is the intended composition. ACCEPTANCE: new test matrix over profile ceilings; signed-tool-over-ceiling error names the REAL ceiling.
close_reason: Verified real profile ceiling is passed to every custom-tool load path; READ_ONLY still refuses writes and signed errors name the selected ceiling. [closing=301a284a180cfb9eef369bcc741e654e90d6c1d4 source=667c0ec0e4fbd0a5292aae879e277429a910c3a9 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-b12b-custom-tool-ceiling-xe54f.json]

title: B12c: custom tools skip-and-warn by default (loud on four surfaces)
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-B12c (operator ruling: skip-and-warn, uniformly; reference-study confirmed the shape). TODAY: load_tools is fail-fast (custom_tools.rs:451-458 -- first refusal aborts); main.rs:1368 propagates; main.rs:3515-3522 exits code 2 -- ONE malformed tool file takes the whole server down for every client (the field outage: a validly signed tool rejected on classification grounds killed startup; signing is orthogonal -- verification in load_tools_for_profile main.rs:1346/:1354, classify_at_load independent, custom_tools.rs:413-447). NEW DEFAULT: SKIP-AND-WARN -- an invalid custom tool is NOT loaded (skipping never grants anything => still fail-closed), the server keeps serving. LOUDNESS IS THE CONTRACT: prominent stderr warning per skipped tool; doctor reports each skipped tool + reason as warn/fail; oracle_capabilities carries skipped_custom_tools so AGENTS see it; signature-verification failures ALSO record as security events on the B8c trail (tamper evidence never silent). --strict-custom-tools restores fail-fast. DELIBERATE DIVERGENCE from the agent-mail reference, kept: we stay fail-fast for security-critical invariants -- skip-and-warn applies to config-quality failures only. ACCEPTANCE: malformed file => server serves, tool absent, all four loudness surfaces fire; --strict restores exit 2; signed-tamper case lands on the refusal trail. MUST-NOT-REGRESS rider (plan sec.6): signature enforcement on protected profiles is unchanged -- skip-and-warn applies to config-quality failures only; a protected profile with an unsigned/invalid-signature tool still refuses that tool, and the security-event record is mandatory.
close_reason: Custom-tool config-quality failures now skip-and-warn by default across stderr, doctor, and capabilities while signature failures remain security events and startup refusals. [closing=5c6ae17707dc093fbea4ae321d818b25e066495c source=b514ee3ccbeedbcbb3d2ed07a6e6a4f0822ca0c4 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-b12c-skip-and-warn-38yf3.json]

title: B12d: Oracle purity metadata as advisory evidence (P2, never auto-admit)
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-B12d. [P2] WHAT: consult Oracle purity metadata (DETERMINISTIC flag, ALL_PROCEDURES) as EVIDENCE feeding the B12a oracle -- never as proof. DESIGN CAREFULLY: DETERMINISTIC is a developer assertion, not a verified property; it may inform operator review UX (e.g. doctor listing candidate functions with their metadata) but must not auto-admit anything (guard tighten-only). ACCEPTANCE: metadata surfaces as advisory evidence only; no classification changes without an explicit allowlist entry.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: FINDING: Oracle purity metadata should NOT feed the SideEffectOracle, in either direction. I did not build the channel, and I am saying so rather than shipping one. THE TECHNICAL REASON: DETERMINISTIC does not assert the property B12d needs. It asserts that the same inputs yield the same OUTPUT - repeatability - and says nothing whatsoever about side effects. Oracle never verifies it; it is a developer annotation consumed by result caching and function-based indexes, and a function that performs DML can legally be declared DETERMINISTIC and will be cached happily. Consequences: (a) as a PERMIT it is forbidden outright - an unverified developer assertion, and not even an assertion about purity; (b) as a VETO (downgrading a proof when the flag is absent) it would be fail-closed but WRONG, because absence of DETERMINISTIC does not imply impurity - it would refuse correct proofs on a signal that does not measure purity, and would train operators to treat refusals as noise, which is how a fail-closed system gets disabled in practice. Metadata is a fact about what a DEVELOPER DECLARED. That is genuinely useful to a HUMAN deciding what to put in the B12a allowlist - operator review UX, where it carries no authority - and useless to the classifier. The plan's own audit (sec.1738) records the current invariant as 'Oracle purity metadata is NEVER consulted'; this keeps it that way and makes breaking it noisy. WHAT LANDED is the test the acceptance actually turns on: no advisory state may promote a verdict the independent oracle refused. Every (independent verdict x allowlist state) pair is enumerated - the dangerous direction is the one nobody writes a test for - asserting both that permitting Safe after the wrapper requires the independent oracle to have permitted it before, AND that a non-proof passes through UNCHANGED. MUTATION-CONTROLLED with the exact shape B12a nearly shipped: making the allowlist promote on its own authority (the 1365e0d1 shape that c5f1b85c deleted) fails with 'restriction promoted ProvenSideEffecting to ProvenReadOnly ... an advisory layer may narrow a proof, never manufacture one'. Note that catches promotion of a PROVEN-IMPURE routine, not merely an Unknown one. Restored: 5/5 pass. No public API added, so no api-lock baseline is touched. WHAT IS STILL AVAILABLE if the operator wants the UX half: surfacing ALL_PROCEDURES/DETERMINISTIC in doctor as a candidate-review listing, clearly labelled as a developer assertion, feeding the human who edits the B12a allowlist. That lives in oraclemcp-core doctor output, touches no guard code, and cannot influence classification by construction. I did not build it because the bead's acceptance is about the guard invariant and adding an unrequested UX surface is not the same as satisfying it.

title: B13b: TTC-129 class is transient/retryable with a typed envelope (server)
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-B13b + sec.A.6.5-cross-check. WHAT: server-side handling of the transient "unknown TTC message type 129 at position 35" class (field: identical metadata call succeeded on retry; same family as round 1's type-11 P0 -- the class is not eliminated, now transient mid-session). Independent of the driver investigation outcome (rust-oracledb B13a bead): the server must (1) classify this transient/retryable (rides 091-a4b unified classification), (2) retry once on a fresh round trip, (3) map to a typed envelope instead of leaking wire internals ("unknown TTC message type N") to agent-facing text (rides 091-a4d). DEPS: A4b, A4d. ACCEPTANCE: fault-injected unknown-type error surfaces as a typed retryable envelope; one retry observed; no TTC internals in tool output.
close_reason: Classifies driver-raised TTC-129 as transient, discards the session for one fresh-connection retry, and emits a typed retryable envelope without TTC internals. [closing=b4586e69393f6902fe11ec57fb2f5c64e7eb08d9 source=67c7eb51ed43fb50e404c476b55627707b3afe1c evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-b13b-ttc129-server-aqllj.json]

title: B14a: DRCP clear-before-set session identity (no cross-tenant bleed)
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-B14 + S7. SECURITY-RELEVANT (cross-tenant identity bleed on DRCP pooled server sessions; the field never exercised DRCP -- exactly why the sweep caught it). With [profiles.drcp] purity="reuse" (the DEFAULT -- drcp.rs:38-45, profile.rs:257-258) the handed-back server session may carry a prior client's CLIENT_IDENTIFIER/MODULE/app-context: the wired identity path apply_session_identity (connection.rs:1897-1954, called on connect :1580) NEVER CLEARS BEFORE SETTING -- early-returns when identity is None, sets only fields present, so anything the new profile omits KEEPS the previous session's value. CLIENT_IDENTIFIER is a canonical VPD key -- feeds the A1/H1 story. FIX (B14a): clear-before-set -- unconditionally reassert the full identity set on every connect (on DRCP the only safe posture); SALVAGE the correct clear-sequence from the dead lease module before B14b deletes it: CLEAR_IDENTIFIER -> SET_MODULE(NULL,NULL) -> SET_CLIENT_INFO(NULL) -> set (lease.rs:68-102 session_tag_statements). TEST: D-lane DRCP fixture -- two profiles alternating on a pooled server session, asserting no identity/context bleed (rides D2 seeded fixtures; ORACLEMCP_TEST_DRCP=1 env exists). ACCEPTANCE: the alternating-profile lane shows zero bleed; identity fully reasserted on every DRCP connect.
close_reason: Clear DRCP session identity before every optional identity application and add principal-reuse bleed fixture. [closing=1667f71b2cf822c44d19139040a055ce1d4ce6fd source=e724338c25c5ddc34adad98476e562612500d395 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-b14a-drcp-clear-before-set-jzgub.json]

title: B14b: delete the dead lease subsystem after salvaging its clear-sequence
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-B14b (OPERATOR RULING 2026-07-20: "delete it if truly not needed, no dead code" -- this recorded ruling IS the deletion authorization). WHAT: delete the dead LeaseManager/oracle_session subsystem. Verified truly unwired: LeaseManager::acquire has ZERO production callers, all LeaseAcquirer impls are test-only, oracle_session has no dispatch or registry entry (lease.rs; latent inverted-order bug documented at sec.A.2.6 dies with it). ORDER: B14a first -- salvage the session_tag_statements clear-sequence (lease.rs:68-102) into the wired path BEFORE deleting the module. Removal keeps B14a clear-before-set as the SINGLE identity path. ACCEPTANCE: module gone, workspace clippy/test green, no dangling registry/docs references; B14a tests keep passing (the salvaged sequence lives there now).
close_reason: Deleted lease/session subsystem already absent at HEAD; stale core resource/docs references removed; B14a clear-before-set path preserved; scoped core/db checks green. [closing=a36dcf5934bd551c7388abdfb7e27b8d5971fc6d source=56994f4ad9fab24805f6d0b4ddf19b4028086273 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-b14b-delete-lease-subsystem-ex6sf.json]

title: B15a+b: normalize allowed_origins/allowed_hosts at enforcement
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-B15a/B15b + S8. THE REST OF THE B1 CLASS (validate-side and enforce-side disagree) in http_guard.rs: (a) allowed_origins -- the allowlist branch compares the FULL origin string exactly (http_guard.rs:125) while the normalization helper origin_authority (:91-96) is applied ONLY to the loopback branch; browsers send lowercase, no-default-port, no-trailing-slash serialized origins, so operator values with uppercase host / trailing slash / explicit :443 validate and never match -- normalize BOTH sides with the existing helper. (b) allowed_hosts -- loopback path lowercases (:85) but the operator allowlist compares case-sensitively (:137); Host is case-insensitive per RFC -- casefold at comparison. Verified SAFE in the same sweep (do not churn): issuer/aud exact-match (RFC-correct), scopes case-sensitive per RFC 6749, client_id/bearer constant-time, profile-name lookups. Test-shape rule A.8-2: ugliest accepted spelling. ACCEPTANCE: unit tests with uppercase host, trailing slash, explicit default port on both fields; R3 browser lane passes with an operator-style-spelled allowed_origin.
close_reason: Canonicalized proven HTTP host/origin entries and pinned malformed inbound/config entries as rejected. [closing=a31061e25c7f163628ff54164460c530d9c2941a source=1beb302db264093d4ee705e320b1a8f33e21efa2 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-b15-http-guard-normalize-rf6sa.json]

title: B15c: trim-on-store for all validated config list fields
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-B15c + S8. WHAT: the weak sibling everywhere -- validate_non_empty_list/validate_required_string (oraclemcp-config/src/lib.rs:883-901) TRIM for the emptiness CHECK but store VERBATIM, so whitespace survives into exact-match enforcement across ALL list fields (subjects, fingerprints, origins, hosts, issuers, scopes...). FIX: trim on store. One systematic change + a regression test that a value with trailing whitespace in profiles.toml authorizes/matches identically to its trimmed form on every enforcement surface. ACCEPTANCE: whitespace-bearing config values behave identically to trimmed ones across the auth surfaces; no field silently double-stores.
close_reason: Trimmed validated HTTP list and option values before exact-match enforcement consumes them. [closing=a31061e25c7f163628ff54164460c530d9c2941a source=1beb302db264093d4ee705e320b1a8f33e21efa2 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-b15c-trim-on-store-bokri.json]

title: B16a: IAM tokens through the driver TokenSource seam (delete one-shot embed)
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-B16a + S15. WHAT: wire the driver's TokenSource seam; stop embedding a one-shot IAM token. TODAY: inject_iam_token runs ONCE during ResolvedProfile construction (main.rs:870-880) and embeds a static token string into connect options; the pool manager holds those options for its whole lifetime (pool.rs:42) -- every later pool-member open, discarded-member replacement, or A4e reconnect reuses the STALE token; OCI database tokens live ~1 hour, after which every new physical connection fails auth (ORA-01017-class, no hint the token expired). The DRIVER seam already ships: static with_access_token (lib.rs:2308), PoP with_access_token_and_key (:2326), pluggable TokenSource trait with async get_token + typed redacted Error::TokenSource (lib.rs:1805/:1379-1383). Reference behavior: access_token callable re-invoked whenever the stored token is expired (connect_params.py:64/:140; pyx :227-233). FIX: implement TokenSource over the existing hardened resolver (iam_token.rs env/file/exec sources -- KEEP the exec sandboxing/caps), pass it through the driver seam, DELETE the one-shot path. DEP (cross-repo, textual): driver B16c must make the seam's refresh contract honest first. TEST (offline): token_exec source backed by a counter file -- assert invocation PER PHYSICAL CONNECT (pool grow + reconnect), not once; live rotated-token acceptance rides F2 (ADB). ACCEPTANCE: counter test green; R-fail token-expiry lane (30s-exp JWT held across a pool checkout) recovers by re-resolving.
notes: Blocked on a driver capability, verified 2026-07-21: pinned oracledb 0.8.4 and local post-B16c driver expose with_access_token_and_key(token,key) and with_token_source(source), but no source+PoP-key API. OCI IAM database tokens require the key for AUTH_HEADER/AUTH_SIGNATURE; server-only TokenSource wiring would either regress PoP to ORA-01017 or retain a static token that wins precedence. TokenSource source errors themselves fail closed as redacted Error::TokenSource before dialing; no unauthenticated fallback was found. Child tracks the needed driver API.
close_reason: B16a IAM profiles use refreshable TokenSource with per-connect token_exec proof [closing=dba35b5544c7d2794d2fd295f0c9b9fa261e959f source=a36dcf5934bd551c7388abdfb7e27b8d5971fc6d evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-b16a-tokensource-wiring-f7sfq.json]

comments.author: SageRaven
comments.text: Orphan triage 2026-07-22: iam_token.rs + oci_tcps_e2e.rs belonged to B16a and landed in 0829fde3 after scoped proof: env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo test -p oraclemcp-core iam_token -- --nocapture; env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --test oci_tcps_e2e profile_wallet_and_iam_token_reach_local_tcps_terminator -- --nocapture. This does not close B16a: the bead still needs the per-physical-connect counter proof / token-expiry acceptance.

title: B16b: doctor IAM token text observes, not asserts
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-B16b + S15/S12. WHAT: doctor.rs:2394-2395 prints "it is re-read on every connect" -- true of the resolver's INTENT (iam_token.rs:706 comment), false of the wiring (one-shot embed, see 091-b16a) -- the assert-vs-observe class again. FIX: correct the text, or better make it observe -- report the token source KIND (env/file/exec/builtin) and when it was last successfully invoked. DEP: lands with/after B16a (the truth changes underneath it). ACCEPTANCE: doctor reports source kind + last-invocation truthfully in the counter-file test.

title: B1a: mTLS allowed_subjects normalize-at-load (control listener serves)
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-B1a + sec.A.5.1 + S9. HIGHEST-VALUE P1: a headline 0.9.0 feature (mTLS control listener) that cannot serve a request. ROOT CAUSE -- normalize-on-validate vs exact-match-on-enforce: admin_auth.rs:102-107 compares allowed == principal_key raw; the runtime principal key is always mtls:sha256:<64 lowercase hex> (http/mod.rs:682-690 from http/config.rs:106-112); http.operator.allowed_subjects passes through VERBATIM (main.rs:3352-3357; only a colon check, config lib.rs:756-772); but client_fingerprints accepts three spellings (lib.rs:845-849) and the control-listener PRECONDITION check normalizes BOTH sides (lib.rs:648-660) -- so an operator config with uppercase/bare-hex validates cleanly, starts, logs "control transport enabled", and silently RSTs every request (three auth gates early-return on a raw TcpStream before StreamOwned, serve.rs:474-552 -- kernel emits RST with unread bytes; only a debug! log). S9 sharpened: the validator normalizes via normalize_sha256_fingerprint while the build path stores trim-only (main.rs:3355-3360) -- BOTH case and the sha256: prefix diverge, both default openssl renderings. FIX: normalize at LOAD (preferred -- stored value and runtime key identical, validation can never pass what enforcement rejects); COPY the correct symmetric in-repo pattern: MtlsClientRegistry normalizes on store AND lookup (http/config.rs:92/:107). Useful discriminator to keep in tests: 429 = past auth (control-probe permits only Observability/OperatorApi, http/mod.rs:962-983); reset = not past auth. FAILING FIXTURE FIRST: C3 (091-c3). ACCEPTANCE: C3 green; rig R1 http lane completes an operator API call using an uppercase bare-hex configured subject. UNBLOCKS B4.
close_reason: Normalized only proven mTLS SHA-256 operator subjects at load; C3 proves accepted spellings authorize the runtime principal key. [closing=a31061e25c7f163628ff54164460c530d9c2941a source=1beb302db264093d4ee705e320b1a8f33e21efa2 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-b1a-mtls-normalize-eg2il.json]

title: B1b: warn-level drop log with computed fingerprint on control listener
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-B1b + sec.A.5.1. WHAT: promote the control-listener auth-drop log from debug! to warn! including the COMPUTED fingerprint and the reason (serve.rs:238/259). "computed mtls:sha256:aabb... not in allowed_subjects" is a 30-second fix for an operator vs today's silent RST + invisible debug line. Keep secrets out (fingerprint is public material). ACCEPTANCE: unit/integration log assertion; R1 lane sees the warn on an unregistered cert.
close_reason: Warn-level control auth drops now include the computed public mTLS fingerprint and actionable reason; focused tracing test passes. [closing=b59406fcd9454e504971a3a6f963147abdebeb70 source=cd501a7c217f02b76cc3b942501f4b987483e7ea evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-b1b-drop-log-warn-aijat.json]

title: B1c: control ingress budget -- probeable, documented, distinguishable
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-B1c + sec.A.5.1. WHAT: the 1-second control ingress budget (CONTROL_PROBE_INGRESS_TIMEOUT, serve.rs:46, :649-653) covers header AND body and its timeout also returns Err from handle_stream (:680) -> the identical silent reset; hand-probing with openssl s_client can therefore never succeed. FIX: raise it to a defensible value or document it loudly (and emit the B1b warn on timeout too, distinguishable from auth-drop). ACCEPTANCE: openssl s_client interactive probe reaches a typed response; timeout still bounds slowloris (test with a deliberately slow client).
close_reason: The bounded control ingress timeout is documented and slow requests emit a distinguishable warning; focused timeout test passes. [closing=f0581fdc5558c58c3c88d9ff71d0ec2b40bba242 source=15596ba2dd4a76c517b03f1fabdd971dd97cf725 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-b1c-ingress-budget-htzz2.json]

title: B2a+B2b: document the OAuth contract; make token errors distinguishable
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-B2a/B2b + sec.A.5.2. NOT a verifier bug -- the OAuth verifier is correct (oauth_rs.rs:127-193); two undocumented requirements make it near-unsatisfiable by hand: (1) the HMAC key is the RAW UTF-8 BYTES of the resolved secret (main.rs:3321 -- no base64/hex decode; >=32 chars; and the config field is a secret REFERENCE, so passing a raw value makes the key the literal string "env:MYSECRET"); (2) RFC 9068 claims iss/sub/client_id/jti/iat are all required and rejected with a bare Malformed (oauth_rs.rs:155-165) -- client_id/jti are routinely omitted by hand-built tokens. WHAT: (B2a) document the full passing contract in README/docs: header typ at+jwt mandatory (plain JWT rejected, :134/:285-287), the six claims + shapes, aud string-or-array vs http.oauth.resource, scope (space-delimited, checked first) or scp array (:316-327), required_scopes non-empty (config lib.rs:826-842) so every token must carry a scope. (B2b) widen the www-authenticate error_description (http/mod.rs:627-648 -- exists but token_error_code collapses Malformed/BadSignature/AudienceMismatch/UntrustedIssuer/Expired into one code; body stays "unauthorized") so the five classes are distinguishable. FAILING FIXTURE FIRST: C1 negatives (091-c1) must each show a distinct diagnostic post-fix. ACCEPTANCE: C1 green including negatives; docs section merged; an externally-minted (PyJWT) token verified end-to-end in R1.
close_reason: OAuth RFC 9068 contract is documented and bearer failures expose distinct token-free diagnostics; C1 and auth reject-matrix tests pass. WHAT: add close() to the trait (delegating to the driver's verified path) and CALL it at: profile switch, process exit/SIGTERM, and pool eviction. B7a hooks then run inside it. ACCEPTANCE: D7 AFTER LOGOFF lane -- trigger fires for pinned + pooled connections on clean exit AND SIGTERM.

title: B8a: doctor must report audit DISABLED instead of a lying check-mark
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-B8a + sec.A.6.7. THE AUDIT DESIGN IS CORRECT AND FAIL-CLOSED -- doctor misreports it. Design (main.rs:1630-1655): no [audit] key + profile READ_ONLY-everywhere-reachable => Ok(None), no auditor (nothing that can mutate is unaudited); if writes ARE reachable without a key the server REFUSES TO START (ORACLEMCP_AUDIT_KEY_REQUIRED). The defect: doctor check 13 shows a check-mark and prints an audit path for an auditor that was NEVER CONSTRUCTED -- oraclemcp-core/src/doctor.rs layout fields :396-404 (legacy_audit_path/current_audit_path/audit_path_configured); the audit logic :781-868 reasons about file paths and legacy->XDG migration only and NEVER calls build_auditor. A gate that lies -- the class AGENTS.md forbids. FIX: doctor reports audit: DISABLED (no signing key configured; profile is read-only everywhere reachable) instead of a check-mark-with-path; with B8c landed, report the unsigned refusal trail status alongside (DISABLED-signed / ACTIVE-unsigned at <path>). ACCEPTANCE: doctor output truth-tested for: key+writable (auditor active), no-key+read-only (DISABLED + trail ACTIVE), no-key+writable (startup refusal). P0-for-honesty.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: VERIFICATION + a NEW hole in the same defect class. The headline B8a fix is ALREADY IMPLEMENTED and I did not need to rebuild it: DoctorAuditPosture exists with SigningKeyConfigured / DisabledReadOnly / StartupRefused / Unavailable, doctor renders all of them (main.rs 2689-2721 region of doctor.rs), and doctor_audit_posture_from_config derives the posture from the REAL config rather than from a file path - it shares max_reachable_write_ceiling and the same '> ReadOnly' predicate with the server's build_auditor, so the ceiling half cannot drift. main_tests.rs:618 doctor_audit_posture_matches_startup_audit_policy_without_opening_a_log already covers read-only, trail-opted-out, writable (StartupRefused) and signed. So the check-mark-with-a-path lie is gone. BUT THE POSTURE STILL LIES IN ONE STATE, and it is the classic declared-but-empty footgun. Doctor computes signing_key_configured as 'config.audit.key_ref.is_some() || env var present AND NON-EMPTY'. The server computes it as std::env::var(AUDIT_KEY_ENV).ok(), which yields Some("") for an empty value, then feeds it to SigningKey::new, which rejects anything under 32 bytes. So with ORACLEMCP_AUDIT_KEY set to the empty string the two disagree. MEASURED with the real target/debug/oraclemcp binary against a scratch read-only config and an isolated HOME, not inferred from reading code: serve prints 'oraclemcp serve: ORACLEMCP_AUDIT_KEY is invalid: HMAC-SHA256 key is too short (0 bytes); at least 32 bytes are required' and REFUSES TO START, while doctor check 13 in the SAME environment prints 'audit configuration observation: disabled (no signing key configured; profile is read-only everywhere reachable)'. Control: with the variable unset the server starts normally, so the empty value is the whole difference. That is B8a's own class in the opposite direction - the original bug was a check-mark implying audit was armed when no auditor existed; this is a benign 'disabled, read-only, nothing to see' report for a server that will not start at all. An operator running doctor to find out why serve failed is told the audit posture is fine. PROPOSED MINIMAL FIX (not applied: crates/oraclemcp/src/main.rs is dirty with another pane's work right now and I will not edit a contended file): detect env-var-present-but-empty explicitly in doctor_audit_posture and return DoctorAuditPosture::Unavailable with a reason naming ORACLEMCP_AUDIT_KEY_INVALID, since neither DisabledReadOnly (false) nor SigningKeyConfigured (which promises startup will arm the chain, also false) is true. Unavailable already carries a free-text reason so no public enum variant is added and the API lock is untouched. Add two tests: one pinning that the server's resolver rejects an empty legacy env key, one pinning that doctor does not report DisabledReadOnly for it.

title: B9a: desugar or explain user[schema] proxy syntax at config load
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-B9a + sec.A.6.4. WHAT: accept or explain the universal Oracle proxy syntax username = "user[schema]". Today oraclemcp-core/src/connect.rs:84-101 requires an explicit [profiles.proxy_auth] block (proxy_user + target_schema; username must MATCH proxy_user); no bracket detection exists anywhere in config parsing -- the string passes through literally and Oracle answers ORA-01017, indistinguishable from a wrong password. Field cost: ALL 13 of the operator's real profiles used the bracket form, so NOTHING authenticated out of the box. FIX: detect ^(.+)\[(.+)\]$ at config load and either auto-desugar into [profiles.proxy_auth] (keeps validation + redaction typed) or fail fast naming the correct shape -- either is acceptable; silent ORA-01017 is not. RIDER: setup's output shows no proxy-auth configuration shape at all -- add [profiles.proxy_auth] to the setup templates. DRIVER TWIN: the reference desugars in ConnectParams itself (connect_params.pyx:511-516) -- driver-side parity is rust-oracledb bead upstream-sync-2026-07-13-etib.3 (do NOT duplicate it here). ACCEPTANCE: config with user[schema] either connects (desugared) or fails fast with the exact remedy; setup template shows the block; unit tests cover quoted/bracket edge cases.

title: C1 fixture: literal externally-minted JWT + distinct negative errors
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-C1 + sec.A.5.2. WHAT: commit a LITERAL JWT + LITERAL secret generated ONCE by an external tool (jwt.io or PyJWT, never our in-module mint()/hmac helpers) and assert the OAuth verifier ACCEPTS it; plus negatives each asserting a DISTINCT error: wrong typ (plain "JWT" not "at+jwt"), missing client_id, missing jti, base64-encoded key (the key is RAW UTF-8 bytes of the secret reference value -- main.rs:3321 does no decode). CONTEXT: the verifier (oraclemcp-auth/src/oauth_rs.rs:127-193) is CORRECT; every existing test mints in-module, so external mintability was never proven (sec.A.0 self-reference class). Full passing contract to pin: header {"alg":"HS256","typ":"at+jwt"}; claims iss (exact vs allowed_issuers), aud (string or array vs http.oauth.resource), sub, client_id, jti non-empty strings, iat number, exp future, scope-or-scp; required_scopes non-empty. ACCEPTANCE: fixture fails against pre-B2b main only for the negative-diagnostics half (acceptance half passes today -- record that asymmetry in the test comments); all assertions green after B2a/B2b. Offline, no DB. CLOSURE CONVENTION (polish r1): this bead closes on the FAILING half -- the fixture/lane is committed and demonstrably red against pre-fix main, landed in an explicitly-marked expected-failure state (#[ignore]/xfail citing this bead id) plus recorded red-run evidence.
