comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: LANE WIRED and committed locally (not pushed: checkout diverged, pane 2 owns the merge). scripts/rig/oracle_l1_vpd_fixture.sql + scripts/rig/verify_vpd_fixture.sh, loaded and run against free23. THE TRAP WAS ALREADY IN THE REPO: D4's oracle_l1_privilege_matrix.sql policy function returns the literal predicate '1=1' - attached, withholding nothing - so any assertion written against it is satisfied by a gate that does not work. D3 therefore ships its own genuinely restricting policy (classification = 'PUBLIC' over 4 rows, 2 of them RESTRICTED) and asserts the withheld rows are ABSENT, never that the query succeeded or that a policy exists. MUTATION-CONTROLLED against exactly the fail-open shape: --mutation-control swaps the predicate to '1=1', requires the assertions to FAIL, restores, requires them to pass. Measured: the fail-open yields 3 findings (blind principal reads 1,2,3,4; the synonym path leaks; COUNT=4 MAX=4), the real predicate yields 0. SIDE CHANNEL REPRODUCED (D3's failing half): the blind principal cannot SELECT ids 3 and 4, yet inserting primary key 3 raises ORA-00001 while inserting 99 (genuinely absent) succeeds - hidden keys are enumerable by probing, and the control means 'insert failed' cannot pass for an unrelated reason. On 23ai it is disclosure rather than inference: ORA-03301 prints 'row with column values (ID:3) already exists', naming the value of a row the caller is forbidden to read. Aggregates do NOT leak (COUNT and MAX are computed over the filtered set). THREE MEASUREMENT CORRECTIONS, each of which would have produced a lane that proved nothing. (1) Ground truth cannot come from SYSTEM or from the TABLE OWNER - both are subject to the policy; the owner sees 1,2 like everyone else, so a lane using the owner as truth compares 2 against 2 and cannot see a fail-open. Only SYS inside the PDB sees all four. (2) Blindness is not about roles: my first fixture withheld SELECT_CATALOG_ROLE and the 'blind' principal STILL reported a policy row, because ALL_POLICIES lists policies for every object ACCESSIBLE to the caller. Genuine blindness needs access via a VIEW whose owner holds the base privileges - which is also the field's silent-empty shape (filtered rows, 0 policies, 0 base tables, nothing to explain the short answer). (3) sqlplus 23ai decorates bare numeric columns with box-drawing characters, so matching ^[0-9]+$ silently produced the empty string and two assertions reported a 'leak' with empty values - a measurement bug wearing the costume of a finding; all values are now fetched as marked strings. A1e half is reachable: the sighted principal names ORACLEMCP_D3_VPD via ALL_POLICIES. NOT DONE: the server-side half of the acceptance (pre-fix main silently returning empty through the MCP tool path, and doctor naming the policy) is not yet driven through oraclemcp itself - this lane establishes the database-side ground truth those assertions need.

comments.text: Lane WIRED and committed locally (crates/oraclemcp-db/tests/live_idle_kill.rs, 317 lines, live-xe gated); NOT pushed because the shared checkout is diverged and pane 3 owns the merge. Both surfaces asserted separately. Anti-vacuity: the pool is pinned to max_size=1/min_idle=1 so the next checkout MUST be the killed connection - with default sizing the next checkout could return a different live connection and the test would pass while validate-on-checkout did nothing. The lane can fail on purpose: ORACLEMCP_D5_SKIP_KILL=1 runs everything except the kill and BOTH halves then fail (pooled: same sid/serial comes back, assert_ne fires; pinned: COMMIT succeeds, generation 1 to 1, panic fires). BEAD STAYS OPEN because the acceptance names a pre-fix failing half to record and IT DID NOT REPRODUCE on XE21. Measured: pooled victim 313/7915 replaced by 313/42101 with no error visible to the caller; pinned COMMIT refused with Quarantined outcome=CommitInDoubt message 'commit failed; lease discarded: Oracle connection lost: commit: I/O error: unexpected end of file'. Both are deliberate, not luck: OraclePool::with_conn already carries RetryPolicy::one_immediate_retry with ReconnectThenRetry plus discard when is_uncertain_session_state, and the same quarantine guards savepoint/preview_dml/enable_dbms_output/apply_session_statement on the lease. TWO CONSEQUENCES FOR THE FIX BEADS: (1) A4a is not 'add missing pooled recovery' - recovery exists as reconnect-THEN-REPLAY, which runs only after the caller's statement already failed once and is safe only for a replayable statement; A4a moves the check earlier. (2) A4e must not regress what already holds: the pinned path today refuses and discards rather than rebinding, and this lane now fails the moment a transparent reopen stops refusing a caller that was mid-transaction. The field's P0-5 pinned symptom therefore needs a trigger this lane does not yet reproduce (long idle before use, or a non-commit operation) - worth pinning down before A4e is designed.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: CORRECTION to my earlier comment on this bead - the pinned half of the lane tested the WRONG SURFACE, and my findings about it should not be relied on. I asserted against LeaseManager, which plan section B14 documents as having ZERO production callers and no tool registration ('constructed only in tests; LeaseAcquirer impls are test-only; oracle_session has no dispatch or registry entry'). Pane b5736dce has since removed that subsystem as planned B14 work, taking my pinned test with it - correctly. So the Quarantined/CommitInDoubt result I recorded was real behaviour of code no caller reaches, which is precisely the vacuity this swarm has been removing all day; I built one. What survives from that comment: the POOLED half and its finding stand (OraclePool::with_conn recovers via RetryPolicy::one_immediate_retry + ReconnectThenRetry + discard on is_uncertain_session_state, i.e. reconnect-THEN-REPLAY rather than validate-on-checkout, so A4a moves the check earlier rather than adding missing recovery), as does the anti-vacuity design (max_size=1/min_idle=1 so the next checkout must be the killed connection) and the ORACLEMCP_D5_SKIP_KILL control that makes the lane fail on purpose. THE REAL PINNED SURFACE, for whoever rebuilds this half: dispatch/mod.rs ProfileConnectionBundle.session - the per-profile long-lived Box<dyn OracleConnection> held in DispatcherState alongside an optional 'stateless' sibling, replaced only through PreparedProfileSwitch. That matches section A.6.9's description exactly ('a single long-lived connection with no liveness path, whose only recovery today is an explicit profile switch') and it is the path the field's P0-5 symptom ran on. The rebuilt test must kill THAT session and assert the caller is refused rather than silently rebound; it is a heavier test than the lease one because DispatcherState needs full profile/guard setup, which is likely why the lane reached for the lease shortcut in the first place. D5 remains open.

comments.author: SageRaven
comments.text: Wired the real pinned dispatcher surface in crates/oraclemcp/tests/live_dispatcher_idle_kill.rs and corrected crates/oraclemcp-db/tests/live_idle_kill.rs to keep only the pooled DB-crate lane. Verification on checkout target: cargo fmt --all -- --check PASS; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target cargo test -p oraclemcp --features live-xe --test live_dispatcher_idle_kill -- --nocapture PASS via explicit live skip because Oracle returned ORA-01017 invalid credential; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target cargo test -p oraclemcp-db --features live-xe --test live_idle_kill --no-run PASS. D5 cannot be guarded-closed honestly until a rig credential set that can connect and ALTER SYSTEM KILL SESSION is available to record the live kill half.

title: D6: local TCPS lane with synthetic wallets (B5/B6/P2-4/P-U4 validations)
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-D6. WHAT: local TCPS/TLS lane with synthetic wallets -- the driver already has synthetic-wallet + local rustls TCPS machinery; REUSE it (gvenzl cannot TCPS; the driver repo's local TCPS lane pattern from the 0.8.4 train covers real-query-over-TLS). Validates: driver B5 symptom (cert UnknownIssuer under stock retry_count=20 surfaces in ~1s, not a 20s call-timeout -- the evidence needed to close rust-oracledb-4sfc), driver B6 (synthetic "public" root injected via the SSL_CERT_FILE/DIR override B6b adds -- today's lane only exercises the self-signed-ADB-CA chain), P2-4 SNI default (stock ADB-shaped wallet works with the server default flipped), and P-U4 (3DES fixture through the SERVER wallet path). DEP: D1. ACCEPTANCE: all four validations runnable locally, each red-before/green-after its fix.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Grounding pass (no code yet). REUSE TARGET CONFIRMED: the driver's scripts/gen_test_wallets.sh generates fully SYNTHETIC material - fictional DN 'CN=oracle-test.invalid/O=Oracle Synthetic Test/C=US', 3650-day self-signed CA plus server cert, and it already emits every shape D6 needs: plaintext ewallet.pem, PBES2-encrypted PEM, modern PKCS12, and the legacy 3DES PKCS12 (OID 1.2.840.113549.1.12.1.3, requires openssl -legacy) that P-U4 needs. Its header says 'Fictional DN only - never use in production'. D6 will MODEL this generator in oraclemcp rather than reach across into the sibling checkout: the driver repo is frozen for the 0.9.0 publish, and a test that depends on a sibling working copy is not reproducible. No field or customer wallet material is involved at any point - the wallets are generated, never borrowed. The local rustls TCPS listener pattern is crates/oracledb/tests/tls_handshake.rs (gvenzl containers cannot do TCPS, so the lane terminates TLS locally rather than against the rig DB). EXISTING oraclemcp surface to build on: crates/oraclemcp-core/tests/oci_tcps_e2e.rs, doctor_wallet_posture.rs, doctor_wallet_resolve_crosscheck.rs, doctor_wallet_cert_expiry.rs, fixtures at crates/oraclemcp-core/tests/fixtures/wallet and scripts/e2e/fixtures/oci-wallet. SCOPE WARNING for planning: the acceptance asks for four validations each red-before/green-after, but two of them gate on fixes that have not landed - B6 needs the SSL_CERT_FILE/SSL_CERT_DIR override that B6b adds, and P2-4 needs the wallet-implies-SNI default flip. Those two can be wired and recorded red, but cannot go green from inside D6. B5 (UnknownIssuer surfacing in ~1s rather than a 20s call timeout under stock retry_count=20) and P-U4 (3DES fixture through the SERVER wallet path) are self-contained and can go green here.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Progress: P-U4 recipe VALIDATED locally, seam located, no code committed yet. (1) Legacy-3DES generation works on this machine's OpenSSL 3.5.5: 'openssl pkcs12 -export -legacy -certpbe PBE-SHA1-3DES -keypbe PBE-SHA1-3DES -macalg sha1' with the repo's existing fictional subject /CN=oracle-test.invalid/O=Oracle Synthetic Test/C=US. VERIFIED it really is legacy and not a silent modern-cipher fallback: openssl pkcs12 -info reports 'PKCS7 Encrypted data: pbeWithSHA1And3-KeyTripleDES-CBC, Iteration 2048' AND 'Shrouded Keybag: pbeWithSHA1And3-KeyTripleDES-CBC' - i.e. OID 1.2.840.113549.1.12.1.3 on both the cert bag and the key bag. Generated only into scratch so far. (2) CONSOLIDATION: oraclemcp already has the exact convention D6 needs at crates/oraclemcp-core/tests/fixtures/wallet/ with a PROVENANCE.md using the SAME fictional DN as the driver's generator, plus the standing rule that PKCS12/SSO containers are not byte-deterministic so the COMMITTED BYTES ARE AUTHORITATIVE and tests read them rather than regenerate. The 3DES fixture belongs there as a new scenario directory with a PROVENANCE.md row, not in a parallel tree. (3) SEAM: oci::classify_wallet/discover_wallet recognise ewallet.p12 BY NAME ONLY (has_p12) and never decrypt; the actual decryption on the server path runs through the doctor wallet probe over the driver's wallet loaders, which is what the existing undecryptable_* fixtures exercise via WalletError::KeyDecrypt. So P-U4's assertion is that the server-side probe DECRYPTS a legacy-3DES p12 rather than reporting KeyDecrypt - the mirror image of the existing negative fixtures, which also means the negative fixtures are the ready-made mutation control. Next concrete step: land the fixture + provenance row + that probe test as one unit.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: MAJOR SCOPE CORRECTION: most of the 'local TCPS lane' this bead asks for ALREADY EXISTS, and D6 should extend it rather than build a parallel one (the plan's own consolidation rule). crates/oraclemcp-core/tests/oci_tcps_e2e.rs is a working local rustls TCPS terminator driving a profile-backed, wallet-backed OCI path through the REAL driver adapter: it stands up a rustls ServerConfig with WebPkiClientVerifier, uses the same synthetic identity D6 requires (CN=oracle-test.invalid, DN CN=oracle-test.invalid,O=Oracle Synthetic Test,C=US), generates its wallet material rather than borrowing any, and already accepts that the DB outcome is a post-TLS connect failure because the terminator is not an Oracle protocol server - its acceptance signal is the listener's own evidence (completed mutual TLS, verified client cert, post-handshake Oracle Net bytes from the thin driver). That is exactly the shape B5 needs. REVISED REMAINING SCOPE for D6: (1) P-U4 is DONE and green, landed at 4e85647d with fixture, provenance and a wrong-password control - the server wallet path decrypts a legacy-3DES ewallet.p12 to PrimaryUsable. (2) B5 is a TIMING assertion layered onto the existing terminator: present a cert the client does not trust and assert UnknownIssuer surfaces in about a second rather than as a ~20s call timeout under stock retry_count=20. Self-contained, green-able here, and it is the evidence rust-oracledb-4sfc needs. (3) B6 needs the SSL_CERT_FILE/SSL_CERT_DIR override that B6b adds - wire it and record red; today's lane only exercises the self-signed-ADB-CA chain. (4) P2-4 needs the wallet-implies-SNI default flip - wire it and record red. So two of the four can go green inside D6 and two cannot, which the acceptance ('each red-before/green-after its fix') should be read against.

title: D7: AFTER LOGOFF lane (logical session close proof)
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-D7. WHAT: session-lifecycle lane: an AFTER LOGOFF trigger writing to a table; assert sessions close LOGICALLY (trigger fires) for pinned + pooled connections on clean exit AND SIGTERM. Validates B7 (server-side close path) and corroborates driver s0se (close_notify verified shipped -- sec.A.6.11). DEP: D1. ACCEPTANCE: pre-B7c main leaks (no trigger rows on drop-based shutdown -- failing half); post-B7 every session end produces exactly one trigger row. CLOSURE CONVENTION (polish r1): this lane bead closes when the lane is WIRED and the pre-fix failing half is recorded; the post-fix green half is owned by the fix beads and the E/R suites that depend on them.
close_reason: D7 AFTER LOGOFF lane wired and live-green for clean EOF, SIGKILL control, and SIGTERM logical close [closing=d31acca8829201f63300b4e75320074c5b1e5d0c source=7e20771b907bd3e6556a723f5128e5b3dc31acd8 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-d7-logoff-lane-25nil.json]

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: LANE WIRED + FAILING HALF RECORDED (committed locally; not pushed, pane 2 owns the merge). scripts/rig/verify_logoff_lane.sh, run against free23, requires the D9 overlay's ORACLEMCP_D9_AFTER_LOGOFF trigger. THREE MODES MEASURED, each attributed to the SERVER's own session: clean stdin EOF -> session live, +1 row (the database observed a LOGICAL CLOSE); SIGKILL -> session live, 0 rows (an abrupt end must look like this, and this is the control that gives the clean half meaning); SIGTERM -> session live, 0 rows = THE RECORDED LEAK. THE FINDING: the server closes logically on stdin EOF but NOT on SIGTERM, and systemd stops the installed service with SIGTERM, so every ordinary service stop leaves its session for PMON to reclaim instead of releasing it. That is exactly the pre-B7c failing half this bead predicts, now reproducible in one command. THE ANTI-VACUITY GUARD IS WHY THIS IS TRUSTWORTHY, and I needed it: 'no logoff row' is also what you observe when THERE WAS NEVER A SESSION. This server connects LAZILY and holds no Oracle session while idle - measured, v$session empty for its user while the process ran. My first attempt started and stopped the server without issuing any query and 'proved' a leak that way; that conclusion was WRONG and I withdrew it. The lane now drives a real oracle_query, reads back the server's own SID from sys_context, and REQUIRES that session to be visible in v$session before it is allowed to conclude anything from a missing row. TWO TRAPS ENCODED so they are not rediscovered: (1) SIDs are not identity - Oracle reuses them and this log already holds 39 rows for one SID, so rows are attributed by module, not sid; (2) the credential env var must NOT be named ORACLEMCP_* because the config loader claims that entire namespace for overrides and rejects ORACLEMCP_D7_PW as 'unknown field d7_pw' before the server starts. The lane exits non-zero on the recorded leak by design and goes green when B7 lands. NOT COVERED: the pooled-connection half of the acceptance ('pinned + pooled') - this drives the single-session profile only; and the installed-systemd-service path is inferred from SIGTERM rather than driven through systemctl.

description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-D8. WHAT: doctor-style preflight for the local environment: refuse to run with a clear message if Docker/images/ports/toolchain are missing (= rig doctor; R0 wires it as rig.sh doctor). DEP: D1. ACCEPTANCE: each missing prerequisite produces a named, actionable refusal; all-present run proceeds.
close_reason: D8 rig doctor shellcheck-clean with selftest and all-lane preflight evidence [closing=6b6b6261825e11adb4911cfd9eb596abae557271 source=681cbd53d48b43cb3df653c7ba92bc409380b0c0 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-d8-rig-doctor-ejuvj.json]

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Landed at 8300f6be, pushed. 456-line scripts/rig/rig_doctor.sh, wired into the CI lint job, shellcheck clean, secret_scan + sensitive_data_lint pass, R0 can wire it as 'rig.sh doctor'. 16 checks pass on a healthy machine; it collects ALL findings rather than exiting on the first, because one-refusal-per-run turns a five-minute fix into five round trips. MISSING vs PRESENT-BUT-WRONG are reported as different kinds: absent-from-PATH is MISSING, while docker-present-but-daemon-unreachable, docker-client-older-than-20.10 and busybox-timeout-without--k are WRONG - the rig's own 'command -v docker' passes in all three of those and then fails later and far more confusingly. The daemon check picks its fix hint from the actual error text (group membership vs start the service). A PASS MUST MEAN THE RIG WILL RUN, enforced mechanically rather than promised: --selftest EXTRACTS oracle_l1.sh's real prerequisites (its 'command -v' tools and its lane table) and fails if any lacks a check here, so adding a requirement to the rig without a doctor check breaks CI. PROVEN ABLE TO FAIL, not assumed: a rig mutated to require jq gives 'requires jq but rig_doctor has no check for it - a passing doctor would not mean the rig runs'; a rig with an extra xe11 lane gives 'defines lane xe11 which rig_doctor never preflights'; the real rig passes. Each individual check is also aimed at an environment it must reject, plus an accept case so a doctor that refused everything would fail too. FINDING against the rig (oracle_l1.sh:229): '[ -n $FIXTURE_PASSWORD ] || e2e_finish_fail' is VACUOUS and can never fire - the value comes from ${ORACLEMCP_RIG_L1_FIXTURE_PASSWORD:-${PYO_TEST_MAIN_PASSWORD:-testpw}} and ':-' treats empty exactly like unset, so it is always at least 'testpw'. Found only because I insisted my own equivalent check must be able to fail; it could not, for the same reason. The doctor now refuses declared-but-empty instead, which is the real footgun: export the variable empty meaning to clear it and the rig bootstraps a fixture user with a password you did not choose.

title: D9: vendor MIT sample schemas + governance fixture layer (rig L2)
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-D9 (RULING: VENDOR IT). WHAT: vendor oracle-samples/db-sample-schemas (VERIFIED MIT -- pin the upstream commit, copy its LICENSE; HR/CO/SH schemas) and LAYER the synthetic governance fixtures on top, since the samples ship NO VPD/RLS: D3's policy-protected table + synonym-over-protected-base + the two catalog-visibility principals, the proxy CONNECT THROUGH pair (bootstrap_live_schema.sh:60-63 already has one), and D7's AFTER LOGOFF trigger. Field-shaped data without a byte of customer data (constitution 9). Open item recorded: CO schema on xe18 unverified -- worst case the realistic-data lane runs free23/xe21 only; document whichever. DEP: D1. ACCEPTANCE: rig L2 loads HR/CO/SH + governance layer on free23 (+xe21 if compatible); LICENSE + pinned-commit provenance committed; R2 sweep runs against it.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Landed at 301a284a. VENDORED: oracle-samples/db-sample-schemas HR/CO/SH at tag v23.3, commit e3325a83e56c516815844025418a96ecaf219751, 19 files, 1.5 MB, under tests/fixtures/sample_schemas/upstream. LICENCE verified from the TEXT, not a badge: standard MIT grant, Oracle 2023 copyright, no field-of-use rider; LICENSE.txt vendored because MIT requires the notice to travel with copies. PROVENANCE is hash-based: every file was git hash-object'd and compared to the blob SHA GitHub reports for that path AT the pinned commit, 19/19 matched, hashes recorded in upstream/MANIFEST.json. EXCLUDED with reasons stated in PROVENANCE.md: the six SH CSVs, about 88 MB against 1.5 MB for everything vendored - bulk rows, not structure. Consequence recorded rather than hidden: SH arrives STRUCTURE-ONLY (fine for a catalog/tool-surface sweep, not for a data-volume lane), with the exact pinned-commit fetch command for anyone who needs them. GOVERNANCE is enforced, not just written: scripts/rig/verify_sample_schemas.sh refuses drift against the pinned hashes, a missing licence, a missing manifest file, and ANY file the manifest does not list - that last one is the point, since an unvetted schema dropped in here would otherwise inherit the vendored tree's implied 'this was checked'. Mutation-controlled: neutering fail() so findings stop counting makes all four detections report and the selftest exit 1; restored, it passes. Wired into the CI lint job. PROVENANCE.md documents how they stay current (pinned deliberately for run reproducibility; upgrading is six explicit steps INCLUDING re-reading the upstream licence, because a relicence is exactly what a version bump hides) and who may add (five requirements; licence verified from text; anything derived from a real or field-test environment is permanently out of scope). OVERLAY: synonym over the D4 VPD-protected base, proxy CONNECT THROUGH pair, D7 AFTER LOGOFF trigger - layered onto the D4 privilege-matrix names, not recreating them. RUN LIVE on free23: loads clean, all four objects exist. Two findings inside. (1) ORA-01031 on the database-level trigger: the OWNER must hold ADMINISTER DATABASE TRIGGER - creating it as SYSTEM, which already holds CREATE ANY TRIGGER and ADMINISTER DATABASE TRIGGER, still fails until the target user has it too. Not obvious from the error text; now commented in the fixture. (2) The D7 asymmetry was MEASURED, not assumed: clean sqlplus logoff wrote a row (0 to 1); a session killed server-side left NO row (still 1, with v$session confirming it was gone). ACCEPTANCE STATUS: licence + pinned-commit provenance committed = DONE; governance layer = DONE; rig L2 free23 load of the OVERLAY = DONE. NOT yet done: loading the vendored HR/CO/SH themselves into free23/xe21 (the L2 loader script), and the R2 sweep against it. The bead's open item about CO on xe18 remains unverified.

Runs inside the rig environment (D1/D2). ACCEPTANCE: every rung + every denial asserted across stdio AND Streamable HTTP; evidence artifacts logged via the e2e JSON-line logger.

title: E2: SEC-1 e2e -- every recovery path re-classifies at apply
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-E2 (SEC-1). WHAT: every recovery/apply path re-classifies and re-checks at apply time; a STORED verdict is never an authorization input. Assert on EACH recovery path individually, not once: change-proposal apply, source-history revert, write-intent recovery, profile-switch re-entry, A4e reconnect re-arm. DEP: D1. ACCEPTANCE: for each path, a tampered/stale stored verdict is ignored and the live classifier decision governs (negative test per path).

Write-intent no-replay covered by unit test build_write_intent_log_fails_closed_on_unresolved_restart_intent (main_tests.rs:1103). Sole remaining gap: A4e reconnect/re-arm has no implementation (091-a4e-session-recycle-uyujp OPEN). E2 cannot close honestly until A4e lands. mqmo9 evidence integrity fixed (f27f82c6).

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text:
2026-07-22 E2 progress (commit bcf96ca9): 7 negative tests now pass, covering 4 of the 5 named recovery paths:
1. change-proposal apply (direct + failed-retry + durable restart): stored READ_ONLY verdict ignored, live DDL refused at READ_ONLY, zero execute calls
2. source-history revert: tampered on-disk stored verdict ignored, live DDL refused
3. write-intent recovery: in-doubt intent survives restart unresolved, never auto-resolved or replayed (fail-closed by design; startup gate refuses writable startup with ORACLEMCP_WRITE_INTENT_IN_DOUBT)
4. profile-switch re-entry: pre-switch stored DDL confirmation refused after switch lowers live level
5. passive elevation expiry (bonus row): expired TTL confirmation refused on fresh classify

REMAINING GAP: A4e reconnect/re-arm (091-a4e-session-recycle-uyujp) is still open — no reconnect entrypoint exists to exercise. D5 is closed so A4e is unblocked, but it is dispatch-domain implementation work outside this agent's test scope. E2 cannot close until A4e lands and gets its negative test. Escalating to orchestrator.

All acceptance rows met. IMPLEMENTED BY the rig browser lane R3 -- build once there; this bead wires R3's assertions into the e2e suite inventory + evidence logging (no duplicate harness; consolidation rule). DEP: R3. description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-E5. WHAT: failure/recovery e2e: killed connections (D5/D10), refused optional features (D4 flashback), expired elevation windows, revoked credentials mid-session (B4 machinery). DEPS: D4, D5. title: E6: signed attestations for e2e runs
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-E6. WHAT: emit signed attestations from e2e runs (ties to the landed K1/K2 attestation machinery; K3 wiring is bead bp8ia.12.3) so an e2e result is evidence, not a claim. DEP: E1 (a suite to attest). ACCEPTANCE: e2e runs upload attestation artifacts consistent with the K-lane format; disabled-signer emits typed SKIP, never unsigned-lookalike.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: LANDED (committed locally; not pushed, pane 2 gating). I checked whether this could RUN before building it, and verified the audit against the live repo rather than trusting it: the GitHub API reports ZERO Actions variables, and the six secrets present are all OCI (no ORACLEMCP_TEST_ATTESTATION_KEY). So vars.ENABLE_TEST_ATTESTATION is never 'true', the signer has taken its operator-not-enabled branch every run since it landed, and it has never produced an attestation. WHICH OPTION I CHOSE: I could NOT wire it to genuinely sign - that needs a real key and a repo secret, which is the operator's to create and not something to invent - so I made the disabled state LOUD and closed the gap that actually mattered: NOTHING CONSUMED ATTESTATIONS AT ALL. verify_test_attestation existed only inside Rust unit tests; no CI job ever read an emitted artifact back, so nothing could tell a real attestation from an absent one. THE CONSUMER RUNS TODAY WITH NO SECRET, which is the whole point: the signature envelope records payload_sha256, and that digest BINDS the payload, so a tampered attestation is rejected even where the HMAC cannot be recomputed. Checking the HMAC itself still needs the key, and the script says so rather than implying it verified more than it did. INVARIANT: a lane must emit EITHER a well-formed signed attestation OR a typed SKIP naming a known reason - never silence, never a claim it cannot back. Rejections: E_NO_EVIDENCE, E_MALFORMED, E_UNSIGNED_LOOKALIKE (the acceptance names that case by that word), E_PAYLOAD_DIGEST_MISMATCH, E_CLAIMED_NOT_EMITTED, E_UNKNOWN_SKIP_REASON (so a silent behaviour change in the signer is noticed by something). PROVEN THE WAY rust-oracledb-2rz3 WAS, against the REAL committed artifact rather than a toy fixture: crates/oraclemcp-verifier/tests/fixtures/test-attestation-v1.golden.jsonl passes (exit 0); editing ONE field of its payload from 'kill=96.9%' to 'kill=99.9%' drives it red with E_PAYLOAD_DIGEST_MISMATCH naming both digests; restoring the file drives it green again. The selftest additionally accepts a typed SKIP and rejects the other five shapes, and CI runs the selftest immediately before the real check so a neutered consumer cannot pass quietly. THE INERT STATE IS SAID OUT LOUD: when every lane skipped and nothing signed, the check prints that the machinery is present but inert, names the missing variable and secret, and states 'Do not cite these runs as attested'. --require-signed turns that into a hard failure and is READY FOR THE DAY THE KEY EXISTS; it is deliberately not set now, because a repo with no signing key would fail every build. OPERATOR DECISION AVAILABLE: create ENABLE_TEST_ATTESTATION=true and the ORACLEMCP_TEST_ATTESTATION_KEY/_KEY_ID secrets, then flip --require-signed on, and the lane goes from honest-but-inert to genuinely enforcing. NOT DONE: emitting attestations from the e2e suite specifically (the bead's 'e2e runs upload attestation artifacts') - the signer is inert, so adding another inert call site would have added machinery without evidence; the consumer is wired to the coverage lane that already emits status, and extends to e2e the moment signing is enabled.

title: F4: real-ADB validation of B6 trust union + P2-4 SNI (zero-cost)
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-F4. WHAT: validate driver B6 (system-roots UNION wallet trust) against a REAL DigiCert-signed ADB endpoint -- the field's actual failure shape (ADB endpoints present either Oracle's self-signed ADB-CA chain, anchored by the downloaded wallet -- the free-tier lane that always passed -- or a publicly-signed chain whose root the wallet does not carry; a wallet-only store can never verify the second kind). Also verify P2-4 on the same endpoint (SNI default flip; predicate leads run driver-side). Zero-cost constitution 10: Always-Free only, AVAILABLE=0 asserted before and after (the authoritative check needs a control query -- the OCI CLI omits the data key on empty lists, so run a known-non-empty control in the same invocation); F5 confidentiality guard (bead bp8ia.10.3) applies to every artifact. DEPS: bp8ia.10.1 (provisioning harness), driver B6 landed. 19c RIDER lives in bp8ia.10.2 (F2). ACCEPTANCE: connect succeeds with the downloaded wallet on a publicly-signed endpoint using stock config; evidence scrubbed + committed; teardown-as-incident honored.

title: G11: close drained legacy epics (bookkeeping)
description: BOOKKEEPING, SINK-EXEMPT: close the legacy engineering-program epics whose children have fully drained (closed or explicitly deferred): bp8ia.3 (zero open children already -- close after review), .4, .5, .6, .7, .8, .9, .10, .12 as they drain. EXCLUDED: the root epic oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia and any epic containing the publish bead itself -- those close POST-publish (a container that includes the sink cannot precede it; this is why container epics as a class are sink-exempt, recorded in the root 091 epic). Cluster J (.11 + children) stays deferred and untouched per operator ruling. Use guarded closes; an epic close cites its drained children as evidence. ACCEPTANCE: every legacy epic whose children are fully closed/deferred is guard-closed citing its drained children; the root program epic and sink-containing epics remain open until post-publish; tracker shows zero drained-but-open legacy epics.
close_reason: G11: transitive drain check shows zero drained-but-open legacy epics; bp8ia.7 looks drained at depth 1 but has 9 live descendants, so nothing is closed [closing=a61513f0855be0494802dfbd628ca5911b5a53f6 source=cf53f83ce4fbaacadb089d296069f77d0238f6b7 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-g11-close-drained-epics-3dr5b.json]

title: H2: repin server to oracledb =0.9.0 + surface sync + semver record
description: Repin the server to the published driver: Cargo.toml oracledb =0.8.4 -> =0.9.0 everywhere it is pinned; scripts/release_surface_sync_check.sh and scripts/release_preflight.sh parse the pinned driver version -- keep them in sync; refresh doctor.rs wallet-variant comments citing the old driver (bead oraclemcp-izk5 rides this -- coordinate, do not duplicate); record the cargo-semver-checks result for BOTH repos (driver: documents the minor delta, must show no BREAKING change; server: GATES at patch -- rework or hold anything minor, plan sec.7). CROSS-REPO GATE (textual, br deps are repo-local): this bead cannot complete until driver 0.9.0 is live on crates.io (rust-oracledb tracker publish sink 5swy). Acceptance: full local gate green on the repin commit (feedback-full-ci-gate list: clippy x3, tests both feature sets, fmt, sync-check, drift regen); CI green; doctor shows 0.9.0.

title: H3: 0.9.1 release + migration notes (A1a, B11, B6, A5, wallets)
description: Release notes / migration notes for 0.9.1, written BEFORE the tag and shipped with the GitHub release. Must cover, at minimum: (1) A1a blast radius -- deployments whose DB principal lacks catalog visibility (ALL_POLICIES / ALL_TAB_COLS) change from silently-empty reads to explicit refusals; strictly more correct but user-visible; consider documenting a one-release warn-then-refuse posture if implemented. (2) B11 orientation caps -- oracle_capabilities gets a compact default; this changes the MANDATED FIRST CALL every fresh agent makes (the server instructions field sends them there); document detail_level="full" as the pre-0.9.1 shape escape. (3) B6 driver trust-union (system roots now unioned with wallet CAs -- reference parity; private-CA-only posture is controlled via the OS trust store). (4) P-U5 wallet truth-table refresh (cwallet.sso is a first-class working mode, not diagnostic-only). (5) A5 dashboard fetch-first + pairing page referrer change. Acceptance: notes file committed; release checklist references it; honesty grep passes.

(2) sh install.sh dies under dash with a cryptic "set -o pipefail" error -- detect a non-bash interpreter early and say "run with bash". title: P2-12: document that base= does not cap child max_level (no semantic change)
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-P P2-12 [doc]. base= inheritance does NOT cap a child at its parent's max_level, and a README sentence reads as though it does -- a reader could build a READ_ONLY base as a safety net that is not one. Probably intended (config is operator-owned): CLARIFY THE DOC; do NOT silently change inheritance semantics -- that would be a behavior change needing its own review. ACCEPTANCE: README/config docs state explicitly that a child may raise max_level above its base and why; example shows the correct way to enforce a fleet ceiling (protected=true).

FIX: add --write/--in-place that places the signature correctly. ACCEPTANCE: in-place signing of a multi-tool file with trailing params round-trips and verifies; help text updated.

title: P2-3: stop redacting current_schema/service_name on local transports
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-P P2-3 [fix]. current_database (alias of oracle_connection_info) redacts 19 fields INCLUDING current_schema and service_name -- the answers the tool exists to give. Field cost: the subagent passed owner explicitly on every call because "defaults to current schema when available" was never available. FIX: loopback/stdio opt-out or a narrower default redaction set; KEEP redaction-by-default for remote transports. ACCEPTANCE: stdio/loopback call returns current_schema + service_name; remote HTTP stays redacted; redaction tests updated deliberately.
close_reason: Local stdio and server-observed loopback HTTP now expose current_schema and service_name; remote transport retains both redactions. Scoped dispatcher, core-context, and real loopback HTTP tests passed. [closing=d8eb325bbc2d6930089fb28504c768ee95bedb2a source=ec0d4ab5a8c59124cd5a5d9ee156c3570edf51ab evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-p2-3-current-db-redaction-t0ufb.json]

title: P2-4: flip server wallet-implies-SNI default off; document the SNI parity limit
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-P P2-4 [verify] + sec.A.6.10 (OPERATOR RULING: python-oracledb parity, fully). The driver fix ALREADY SHIPPED in 0.8.4 (decide_sni tls.rs:732-755 sends SNI only when rustls-valid; OCI-ADB fallback sends descriptor host, is_oci_adb_endpoint :710-716; non-encodable token under use_sni=true fails typed UnsupportedSni) -- yet the field on 0.9.0 (pins =0.8.4) still got "use_sni=true cannot be honored... not a valid rustls DNS name". SERVER-SIDE SCOPE OF THIS BEAD (driver leads live in rust-oracledb tracker): (2 of the ruling) FLIP the server's wallet-implies-SNI default OFF -- connection.rs:1863-1867 force-defaults use_sni=true whenever wallet_location is set, more aggressive than BOTH the driver default and the reference's opt-in; flipping it is what makes a stock ADB wallet work out of the box; operators opt in explicitly. Structural truth to document (README/config docs): rustls ServerName cannot carry Oracle's routing token (reference sends S{len}.{service}...V3.{version} via CPython ssl server_hostname, transport.pyx:47-59/:182-183); host-as-SNI completes the handshake but forgoes Oracle's one-negotiation routing fast-path -- a documented performance nuance, not a functional gap. ACCEPTANCE: default flipped + doc sentence; D6 lane green with a stock synthetic ADB-shaped wallet; F4 verifies on a real ADB endpoint. No half state survives the train.

title: P2-5: config-load failures belong under a config doctor check
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-P P2-5 [fix]. Config-load failures are reported under doctor's "Connectivity" check, sending users to debug firewalls/DSNs for a TOML problem. FIX: file them under a config check. ACCEPTANCE: a broken profiles.toml shows under config, not connectivity; check text names the file+error.

Make the help text match the behavior. ACCEPTANCE: help text states both transports + the dev-only posture; docs consistent.

description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-P P2-7 [doc]. One serve instance per state directory at any port (the private runtime instance lock): reasonable, but undocumented -- "one profile per box" surprises operators. Document it where serve is introduced, with the multi-instance recipe (separate XDG state dirs). ACCEPTANCE: README serve section + docs/operations cover the lock and the recipe.

description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-P P2-8 [doc/fix] + sec.A.6.9. serve opens 2 Oracle sessions while reporting connection_strategy: single_session. Not a leak (proven in the field): it is the pinned + stateless pair. FIX: document the expected session footprint, point at the pool knob, and make the reported strategy honest (e.g. pinned_plus_stateless). ACCEPTANCE: connection_info/docs match observable v$session count in the D lane.

title: P2-9: capture the stale-lock kill stderr leak
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-P P2-9 [fix]. Stale-lock reclaim leaks a raw "kill: (<pid>): No such process" line to stderr. Capture it; keep the structured reclaim message. ACCEPTANCE: reclaim path test shows clean stderr.

[closing=aeae5d841283355f53134f0c1f22a6205688a764 source=a1041b03b5dca4883911443b511f807e18488665 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-p2-9-stale-lock-stderr-4vqcm.json]

title: P-U1: line-length cap for oracle_search_source
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-P P-U1 [fix]. oracle_search_source has max_rows but no max_line_chars/context_chars; source with 2000+-char generated lines produced ~25 KB of noise for one search. Add a line-length cap -- for a source-grep tool the most obvious missing parameter. ACCEPTANCE: long-line fixture truncates with a marker; default keeps responses bounded; R2 byte budget covers it.
close_reason: Bound oracle_search_source lines at the visible default and custom cap; each truncation now retains an explicit marker, flag, and original character count, with scoped regression proof. [closing=2106de73bd2dfe581dde6005de2e49eb0dc0a11b source=39859487e0dbbab7cb36c35962fc150718045afd evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-pu1-search-source-line-cap-9f9bp.json]

title: P-U2: from_line/to_line range fetch for oracle_get_source
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-P P-U2 [fix]. oracle_get_source cannot fetch a line range although oracle_search_source returns exact line numbers; max_chars truncates from the TOP, so reading a function deep in a large package body means guessing a byte budget. Add from_line/to_line -- the two tools are designed to be used together. ACCEPTANCE: range fetch returns exactly the requested lines with context metadata; docs cross-reference the pairing.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text:
READY TO CLOSE, not closed (pane 1.6 stopped under a clean-stop order 2026-07-22). Three commits, all on origin/main, carry this bead end to end:
  c7620595  shipped the from_line/to_line range fetch with a disclosed returned range
  2dfa1247  fixed a latent bind defect INSIDE that feature: the range SQL spelled (:4 IS NULL OR line >= :4), and the thin driver binds one value per placeholder OCCURRENCE, so the statement declared 7 slots against 5 values. Measured with the driver's own exported pre-flight: check_positional_binds -> PositionalCountMismatch { expected: 7, actual: 5 }. It had not bitten only because no lane had reached the range path yet; it would have shipped and every ranged get_source would have returned ORA-01008.
  4779991d  RangeSourceMock read binds 3 and 4 as from/to, which under the corrected layout made 'to' a second copy of 'from'. It now asserts the pairing (7 binds, 3==4, 5==6) so the stand-in refuses what Oracle refuses.
Also relevant: crates/oraclemcp-db/tests/positional_bind_shape.rs is a crate-wide lint against placeholder reuse, verified to fail on the old spelling.
Full gate green at cdd634ac (fmt, clippy x3, cargo test --workspace 5822 passed 0 failed, surface-sync, provenance, close-evidence 0 hard, boundary, api-lock).
TO CLOSE: author tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-pu2-get-source-range-9yvbk.json with source.sha = the tree the evidence is produced from (see docs/BEAD_CLOSE_EVIDENCE.md and the re-derivation in 73f6e15e for the pattern), commit it, then scripts/bead_tracker_guard.sh close. Note the in-scope paths must be clean at close time - run the guard with ROOT set to a clean worktree if the shared checkout is dirty.

title: P-U3: dashboard 403 becomes a structured envelope (rides A5)
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-P P-U3 [fix] (MEDIUM in the round; rides A5). The dashboard 403 is bare text while every other error is a structured JSON envelope. Unify -- rides the A5 dashboard work (same response-shaping layer). DEP: 091-a5. ACCEPTANCE: R3 asserts a JSON envelope (error_class + next_steps) on an unauthorized dashboard POST.

title: P-U4: verify legacy-3DES wallet through the SERVER path (D6 rider)
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-P P-U4 [verify] + sec.A.6.12. Legacy-3DES ewallet.pem (pbeWithSHA1And3-KeyTripleDES-CBC) still reported KeyDecrypt in the field although driver 0.8.4's scope included legacy-3DES decrypt with committed synthetic fixtures. Cosmetic in the field (cwallet.sso auto-login fallthrough works and is the intended path; only a doctor warning) but a truth-in-shipping question: EITHER the driver fixture is self-consistent-but-unreachable (sec.A.0's class again) OR the server's wallet diagnostics bypass the driver's decryptor. VERIFY by running the committed 3DES fixture through the SERVER's wallet path (doctor + connect), not only the driver's unit test. D6 rider. ACCEPTANCE: server-path 3DES fixture test exists and passes, or the divergence is diagnosed with a follow-up fix bead filed (discovered-from this).

title: P-U5: refresh the stale wallet truth table (cwallet.sso is first-class)
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-P P-U5 [doc] + S14. The wallet support table is STALE and UNDERSTATES shipped behavior: README.md:994/:1221 and docs/configuration.md:421 claim cwallet.sso is "recognized and reported with structured wallet diagnostics" (diagnostic-only), but oraclemcp-db/src/oci.rs treats it as a first-class working mode (mode: "cwallet.sso", oci.rs:340; required-files probe :33) and the field used cwallet.sso as THE working OCI path. Pre-0.8.4 wording -- refresh the truth table; re-check the ewallet.p12 row while there. Feeds 091-h3 release notes. ACCEPTANCE: table matches oci.rs behavior; honesty grep green.
close_reason: Aligned wallet documentation with the default thin-driver supported modes. [closing=b5d53f2ce29bd2075c19b51007d9bb95f674624f source=5259fba508886109837c2094c25d6f6b2183596c evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-pu5-wallet-truth-table-dst6s.json]

title: P-U6: HSTS on non-loopback native-TLS listeners
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-P P-U6 [fix/doc] (rig design review). No Strict-Transport-Security header is emitted anywhere. When native TLS is active on a NON-LOOPBACK listener, emit HSTS; NEVER on loopback HTTP (pinning localhost breaks local dev). One header, standard posture. ACCEPTANCE: header present on non-loopback TLS responses, absent on loopback; test both.
close_reason: Native TLS emits HSTS only on non-loopback listeners; buffered and streamed responses are covered by focused tests [closing=6180689cddec648b123f24386ed886275653afed source=6afbd1a57505dfa6b3a313aced698c81cfa9c137 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-091-pu6-hsts-ulizx.json]

title: R0: rig scaffold -- rig.sh, Tier A pseudo-home, Tier B container, hygiene assertion
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-R (layers L1-L6 + isolation contract). WHAT: the rig scaffold -- scripts/rig/rig.sh up|run|report|down + doctor (delegates D8). Tier A DEFAULT isolation: one throwaway root per run (target/rig-home-RUNID) with HOME + all XDG_* + PATH redirected in; the PRODUCT'S OWN config-discovery order makes redirection airtight; installer runs with --prefix inside it; serve runs as a rig-supervised foreground child on rig-chosen loopback ports, NEVER a host service; per-run state dirs mean the product's instance lock cannot collide with a real install; teardown kills the process group, removes the root, and runs a HOST-HYGIENE ASSERTION (real ~/.config/oraclemcp untouched, no new user systemd units, git status clean) so non-contamination is itself a failing-able test. Tier B (invasive lanes only: service install/uninstall/restart/backup/restore, self-update, uninstaller, doctor --fix): disposable systemd-capable oraclelinux9 container joined to the DB docker network; on the host those commands run only via --dry-run. L3 = THE ARTIFACT: installed via the real installer, configured via real setup --discover/--write, never cargo run. L4 harness: @modelcontextprotocol/inspector --cli + a ~120-line committed raw JSON-RPC probe sharing NO code with the server or e2e_harness.rs (enforced by a boundary lint), fed from hand-authored mcp.json over stdio AND Streamable HTTP. L6 report: field-shaped punch list on the existing scripts/e2e/lib.sh JSON-line logger, scrubbed by existing secret lints. Existing plumbing to reuse: installer_e2e.rs temp-prefix pattern; serve-attach live-test supervision. PLACEMENT: Tier-3 operator/nightly, NEVER required CI. Open item: inspector-CLI flag surface not build-verified -- fallback is the probe. DEPS: D1, D8. ACCEPTANCE: rig up/run/report/down works end to end on this machine; hygiene assertion green; boundary lint enforced in CI (cheap grep lint, not a live lane).

title: R1: external MCP client reachability over stdio + HTTP (two-sided proof)
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-R1. WHAT: external-client reachability -- inspector + the raw probe complete initialize, tools/list, and a governed read over BOTH transports against the INSTALLED binary, from literal externally-authored configs (the C9 snippets are the harness inputs). TWO-SIDED PROOF REQUIRED: must demonstrably fail against pre-fix main where the field said it fails. Also carries the B1d disposition: the http lane exercises the MAIN listener with registered and unregistered principals asserting typed 403/429, never silent resets (the field's main-listener reset claim stays unconfirmed unless this reproduces it). DEP: R0. ACCEPTANCE: both transports green post-fixes; failure-mode evidence recorded pre-fix; B1d matrix asserted. CLOSURE CONVENTION (polish r1): closes when both transports run end to end and the PRE-FIX failure evidence is recorded (the two-sided proof's first half); the post-fix green is asserted by the B-cluster fix beads and re-run at RC (sec.9.2 item 11).

title: R2: full tool-surface sweep with three wire assertions
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-R2. WHAT: tool-surface sweep with wire assertions: every advertised tool across the posture matrix {read_only, protected, proxy_auth, pooled, drcp}; write tools through the real preview->grant->apply gate. TOOL-COUNT TRUTH: derive from registry.rs:18-81 -- 34 canonical + 25 compat aliases (+9 oracle_plsql_* builds); the earlier "43+13" phrasing was the FIELD's sweep scope, not the registry; R2 asserts oracle_capabilities == TOOL_NAMES to pin runtime-vs-source drift mechanically. THREE WIRE-ONLY ASSERTIONS per response: (1) well-formed envelope / outputSchema validation -- every error a known-class ErrorEnvelope; (2) serialized-byte budget (catches S11/B11 incl. the get_ddl honest-truncation flag); (3) refusal-grammar uniformity -- every refusal carries structured_reason with a known category; same construct -> same category across tools. DEPS: R0, D9 (realistic schemas). ACCEPTANCE: full matrix run emits the R4 findings feed; byte budgets enforced; zero unknown-class envelopes. GREEN-HALF DEPS (polish r1): the byte-budget and refusal-grammar assertions require B10/B11a/B11b/A4d landed, so those are dependencies for CLOSURE; the sweep runs earlier in findings mode feeding R4 regardless.

title: R3: browser lane -- pair, act (200), SSE resume (implements C4/E4)
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-R3 [P0 -- C4 and E4 already commit to it]. WHAT: browser lane against live serve: Chromium (Playwright) pairs (POST -> 303 -> /), performs an authenticated operator action POST asserting 200 NOT 403 (the S2 dashboard-wide assertion), and holds an /operator/v1/events SSE subscription with Last-Event-ID resume. The existing web/e2e Playwright config targets vite preview and validates NONE of the Rust security surface -- this lane is NEW and drives the real listener. THIS IS C4's AND E4's IMPLEMENTATION -- build once here. DEP: R0. Note: deliberately NOT dependent on D9/D2 -- pairing + action POST need a live serve, not schemas. ACCEPTANCE: two-sided proof (fails on pre-A5 main; green after); SSE resume asserted; runs headless on this machine and in the Tier-3 lane. CLOSURE CONVENTION (polish r1): closes when the browser lane runs and records the pre-A5 failures (pair 403 / action-POST 403); A5's acceptance flips it green; the RC re-run is sec.9.2 item 11.

title: R4: round-N report + refusal-corpus regression gate
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-R4. WHAT: rig report -- scrubbed findings.jsonl/.md in the field punch-list shape (P0/P1/P2 + repro + envelope evidence), built on scripts/e2e/lib.sh JSON-line logging, scrubbed by the existing secret/sensitive-data lints (F5's scanner runs on rig output too). PLUS the refusal-corpus regression gate: diff the refusal-corpus export against the committed baseline -- a category change or a NEWLY-ALLOWED construct is itself a finding (guard tighten-only, made mechanical). DEPS: R1, R2, R3. ACCEPTANCE: rig report emits the punch list + refusal diff; a seeded category-flip fixture is caught; zero customer identifiers in any artifact.

title: R5-lite: Codex-Spark agent session against the RC (operator-gated)
description: Source: plan v8 sec.4-R5 (OPERATOR RULING: option 2 -- R5-lite in-train). OPERATOR-GATED at RC time; sec.9.2 item 11. WHAT: one codex exec -m gpt-5.3-codex-spark session (proven headless launch shape; near-zero Claude usage) drives the INSTALLED release-candidate server over stdio AND Streamable HTTP from a literal hand-authored config, through a fixed prompt list: orientation -> schema exploration -> governed read -> refusal -> error-recovery; full transcript feeds R4's round-N report. WHY: the only lane that discovers agent-ERGONOMICS defects (the 344 KB-orientation class) -- a deterministic sweep asserts what we thought to check; a real agent stumbles into what we did not. The FULL R5 matrix (claude -p, opencode, multi-harness) stays deferred to the next train. DEPS: R4 (report pipeline), RC build. ACCEPTANCE: transcript captured + scrubbed; findings triaged into the round report; no untriaged P0/P1 remains at tag time.

title: RC mutation seal (advisory, background; never blocks publish)
description: SINK-EXEMPT (operator ruling 2026-07-21, plan v8 sec.Z2-clarification): produce the fresh five-surface mutation seal (guard,audit,core,db,dispatch; scripts/mutation_safety_gate.sh + migrate_mutation_result.py) once the RC exists and the safety-crate churn has settled -- OFF the critical path, run in the background (nightly <=32-mutant shard rotation keeps accruing evidence meanwhile; a seal binds to an exact SHA and re-stales on every safety-crate fix, which is why per-push/pre-tag enforcement was wrong). The seal is ADVISORY this train: ALLOW_STALE_MUTATION_SEAL=1 is set on ci.yml AND the release path (be7577e) with a loud warning; do NOT remove those flags as part of this bead -- removing them is the NEXT train's decision once a fresh seal exists. Also re-add the seal-freshness check to the local pre-push gate at that point (plan sec.9.1 note). Honesty: mutation testing measures test-suite strength, not runtime safety; fuzz lanes are unaffected and keep gating robustness. This bead must NEVER block or stall the publish beads. ACCEPTANCE: a fresh enforcing five-surface seal exists for an RC-era SHA (produced in the background), migrate_mutation_result.py accepts every contributing shard, and the follow-up decision on removing the ALLOW_STALE_MUTATION_SEAL flags is recorded for the NEXT train -- all without ever having blocked a push or the tag.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: CAN-IT-FAIL VERIFIED, and a real hole closed (committed locally; not pushed). The seal is advisory and background, so before trusting it I measured whether it can still fail in the configuration CI ACTUALLY USES - ALLOW_STALE_MUTATION_SEAL=1, set on per-push CI and the release path. IT CAN, and the existing chain is sound: malformed ENFORCING marker + flag -> exit 1 (E_SEAL_SOURCE_MISMATCH); missing report + flag -> exit 1; genuinely stale marker + flag -> exit 0 with a loud warning, which is the intended deferral. And a deferred floor leg emits 'STATUS mutation-floor=deferred', which coverage_ratchet.sh refuses to guess about (it dies if the token is absent) and maps to SKIP rather than PASS, with its own self-test. So a stale seal does not silently become a green check - it becomes a visible SKIP. THE HOLE WAS IN WHAT NOTHING PINNED: the gate's self-test exercised the flag UNSET, so nothing asserted that the flag means 'defer STALENESS' rather than 'skip the check'. Had it ever widened into a global kill switch, the seal would report green while validating nothing and no test would notice. Three cases now pin its scope, and they are PROVEN LOAD-BEARING: rewriting cmd_check_report to return 0 whenever the flag is set fails the extended self-test with 'the flag is a kill switch, not a staleness deferral', while the UNMODIFIED self-test exits 0 against that same mutant. One fixture correction recorded in the commit: my stale-marker case first carried a bogus scope hash, and the integrity check fires BEFORE the staleness branch, so it failed for the wrong reason rather than exercising the deferral. NOTE FOR THE PRE-PUSH GATE this bead defers to the next train: the self-test cannot pass in a DIRTY working tree, because scope_state reads the working tree while the marker's source is HEAD. In CI's clean checkout it passes; in this shared checkout with concurrent panes it fails for reasons unrelated to the seal - verified by running the unmodified gate at HEAD in a clean worktree (exit 0) versus the shared checkout (exit 1). Re-adding a seal-freshness check to the local pre-push gate will need that addressed or it will be spuriously red for every agent. NOT DONE and NOT ATTEMPTED: producing the fresh five-surface seal itself. That needs an RC-era SHA and a day-scale background campaign, and this bead must never block a push or the tag. No ALLOW_STALE_MUTATION_SEAL flags were removed - that stays the NEXT train's decision, per the bead text.

title: 0.9.1/0.9.0 field-hardening train (PLAN_0_9_1 v8)
description:
ROOT EPIC for the coordinated 0.9.1 (server) / 0.9.0 (driver) field-hardening train. Source: docs/plan/PLAN_0_9_1_FIELD_HARDENING.md v8 (committed 9feffe1); every child bead is self-contained -- the plan citation in each body is provenance, not required reading.

VERSIONS (operator ruling 2026-07-20, plan sec.7): driver oracledb 0.8.4 -> 0.9.0 (MINOR, additive-only: public inventory already grew 908->915 with stage-aware TLS; B6/B7 add more; any breaking change waits for 1.0). Server oraclemcp 0.9.0 -> 0.9.1 (strict patch; cargo-semver-checks GATES the server, DOCUMENTS the driver). Server oracledb pin moves to =0.9.0 in the same train (bead 091-h2).

WORKSTREAM Z RECORD (landed pre-conversion, keep-green is standing): Z1 evidence-SHA/shallow-checkout fixed (5a52bf6+3f057e2, fetch-depth 0); Z2 mutation seal ADVISORY everywhere per operator ruling 2026-07-21 (ALLOW_STALE_MUTATION_SEAL on ci+release paths, commit be7577e; mutation!=fuzzing, fuzz lanes unaffected); Z3 Windows lane green (flake; vzui stays via backlog); Z5 fuzz musl --target pin (730957a); Z6 MUTATION_OOM_POLICY=warn void-shards on nightly rotation (be7577e). Both front pages fully green at conversion time (every check-run on HEAD).

STRUCTURE: workstream epics (ws-a p0-adoption, ws-b p1, ws-c wire-fixtures, ws-d live-env, ws-e e2e, ws-r rig, ws-p p2-tail, ws-g backlog-drain) each BLOCK-depend on their child tasks; the publish bead oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.13 (retitled) depends on all epics + H2/H3 -- so no non-deferred work bead can leave publish ready. SINK EXEMPTIONS (operator ruling 2026-07-21, plan v8 sec.10): bp8ia.8.2 live-nightly streak (elapsed evidence, may close post-publish), 091-rc-mutation-seal (advisory), 091-g11 close-drained-epics (bookkeeping; container epics as a class are exempt -- the root program epic can only close after publish). Driver-side exception: rust-oracledb-090-b13a-ttc129-desync-su8a converts to a pre-authorized operator deferral if unreproduced at RC.

RULES BINDING EVERY CHILD: (1) no bead closes without landed evidence via scripts/bead_tracker_guard.sh close; cited SHAs must be reachable from origin/main (Z1 rule). (2) Fix beads land their failing fixture FIRST (two-sided proof; plan sec.5 rule 1). (3) Consolidation rule: no new standalone live-test harness -- everything live runs through scripts/rig/rig.sh (plan sec.3). (4) Test-shape rules sec.A.8 (literal externally-authored fixtures; ugliest accepted spelling; observe-not-infer; empty catalog result is not absence; assert setup ordering per posture; validate on checkout; evidence reachable from origin/main). (5) Guard stays fail-closed and tighten-only; deferral is the operator's call alone. (7) CLOSURE CONVENTION for two-sided proofs: fixture/lane beads close on their committed FAILING half (expected-failure state citing the bead id + red-run evidence); the fix beads that depend on them own the green half and flip the fixture to enforced-green -- otherwise the fixture-first dependency edges would deadlock close order.

title: file_store OS-lock flake REPRODUCED: x1hr.5 closed it on a negative repro
description:
[MEDIUM] x1hr.5 ('Confirm/fix the file_store OS-lock flake left as an oral caveat') was CLOSED on a durable NEGATIVE repro — could not reproduce. It is still live: I hit it once while adding the em39 tests. Recording the POSITIVE repro so the caveat stops being oral.

OBSERVED (once, 2026-07-16, during cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --lib file_store):
  service_lock_initialization_failure_releases_os_lock FAILED
  panicked: 'partial initialization must not leave a permanent lock: Locked'
i.e. after acquire_service_owner_with_metadata failed its injected metadata write and dropped the descriptor, the very next acquire_service_owner returned Locked — the flock had NOT been released.

CHARACTER (measured, not guessed):
- Intermittent: 1 failure, then 18/18 green across 6 consecutive full-suite runs.
- Passes 5/5 in ISOLATION (--exact), so it is a parallel-execution interaction, not test logic.
- The failing test is PRE-EXISTING and untouched by siry/em39. My em39 tests likely only raised concurrent lock pressure enough to surface it — triggering a latent bug, not causing one.

HYPOTHESIS (explicitly a hypothesis, NOT a verified claim — do not close on it without evidence): flock ownership belongs to the open file description, and a fork DUPLICATES it. file_store tests spawn subprocesses (service_lock_subprocess_holder, service_lock_recovers_after_holder_process_is_killed) via Command. Between fork and exec the child transiently holds copies of every parent fd; O_CLOEXEC only closes them AT exec. So while test A drops its lock fd expecting release, a concurrently-forking test B can hold a transient dup of that OFD, keeping the flock alive for the fork->exec window and making A's re-acquire spuriously return Locked. That predicts exactly what was seen: intermittent, parallel-only, isolation-clean.

PRODUCTION IMPACT (probably benign, worth confirming): the server spawns helpers (token_exec, plugins via command-group). A helper's fork->exec window could briefly extend a lock's lifetime, so a competing process could see a spurious Locked and refuse to start. That is the FAIL-CLOSED direction (refuse, not double-own), so it is a nuisance rather than a safety hole — but it should be confirmed, not assumed.

NEXT: reproduce under load (loop the suite with high --test-threads, or stress acquire/release against a concurrent Command::spawn), confirm or refute the fork/OFD hypothesis, then fix (e.g. spawn helpers with the lock fd explicitly closed, or retry-with-timeout on the acquire path) and add the stress test x1hr.5's acceptance asked for. If the hypothesis is refuted, record THAT negative result with the reproduction attempt attached — do not re-close on silence.

DO NOT WEAKEN: single-writer exclusion must stay exact. A retry/timeout must never let two processes own the root, and Locked must remain a real refusal, not a warning.
close_reason: Reproduced the parallel-only flock race deterministically with a duplicated open file description: before the fix, a failed metadata initialization left the duplicate holding the flock and the next acquire returned Locked. Explicitly unlock the acquired descriptor on every post-lock initialization failure; the duplicate-FD regression now passes, as do all 19 FileStore tests and the full workspace gate.

title: [CI-RECOVERY-2026-07][secret-scan] Annotate masked SIEM fixture

title: [P1][live] 21c (xe21) live matrix FAILS on Oracle PGA-memory errors — diagnose infra-vs-code, fix or type it
description: 5c7i ran the real tri-state matrix: xe18 PASS, xe21 FAIL (documented Oracle PGA-memory errors), free23 PASS — so the release gate is NON-GREEN and all-versions-green is unmet. Determine whether the xe21 PGA failure is (a) the gvenzl oracle-xe:21-slim container being PGA/SGA-starved (infra: bump PGA_AGGREGATE_TARGET / container memory) or (b) a real driver/server memory-handling defect. If infra: fix the container/matrix config so xe21 goes green. If it cannot be made green, surface it as a TYPED, documented version-aware limitation per the evidence contract (never a silent skip). Blocks the coordinated publish (Rule 7 honest-green).
notes: POST-CLOSE ADDENDUM (PurpleRidge, same session) — a symptom that LOOKED like it contradicted the close, investigated, and it CONFIRMS it. While doing 6c0e I hit 'ORA-04036: PGA memory used by the instance exceeds PGA_AGGREGATE_LIMIT' on xe21 AGAIN, ~15 min after closing this green. Checked rather than assumed: xe21 total PGA allocated = 3 MB of a 2048 MB pga_aggregate_limit (0.15%), PGA inuse = 2 MB, 79 sessions; xe18 is 3 MB/66 sessions. The instance is nowhere near its PGA ceiling, so ORA-04036 here is NOT the instance exceeding PGA — it is Oracle failing to obtain memory from a host under pressure and reporting it in PGA terms. Host at that moment: free -g => free=7, shared=60, buff/cache=212 (available 156) while other panes were building. CONSEQUENCE, recorded honestly: the tri-lane matrix IS green (verified 20260716T131032Z-3169754) and xe21 has no database defect, but the lane is FRAGILE UNDER HOST MEMORY PRESSURE — it passes when the swarm is calm and can throw spurious ORA-04036/ORA-04030 whenever concurrent builds spike memory. That is the SAME root cause as gctl, not a second bug, and it is precisely why gctl's bounded-build-concurrency policy (docs/multi-agent-build-policy.md, commit 07f7c98) is load-bearing rather than housekeeping: all-versions-green holds only while build concurrency stays bounded. If xe21 goes red again in CI or a swarm run, check host memory FIRST (free -g, du on the build target) before touching Oracle — do not tune PGA; at 3MB/2048MB there is nothing to tune. Staying closed: no code/config defect exists to fix.
close_reason: PHANTOM — xe21 was collateral damage from oraclemcp-gctl (tmpfs build storm starving host RAM), NOT (a) container PGA-starvation and NOT (b) a driver/server memory defect. The FULL tri-state matrix is now GREEN: 'lanes: xe18=pass xe21=pass free23=pass', exit 0, artifacts target/e2e/oracle_version_matrix/20260716T131032Z-3169754 — same harness 5c7i ran, same per-lane users as the original failing run (xe18=TESTUSER, xe21=TESTUSER, free23=SYSTEM, read from the failing run's ladder_evidence owner fields), so this is a faithful replication and not an easier config. DIAGNOSIS: the recorded failure was 'ORA-00604 recursive SQL level 1 / ORA-04030: out of process memory when trying to allocate 568 BYTES (dga heap,ksfd dga fob consolidated)'. That is ORA-04030 (the OS refused an allocation to the Oracle server process), NOT ORA-04036 (PGA limit exceeded) as this bead's title implies — the title points a reader at PGA tuning, but a server process that cannot obtain 568 bytes is a host out of RAM. RULED OUT (a): xe18 (PASS) and xe21 (FAIL) are configured IDENTICALLY — pga_aggregate_limit=2147483648, pga_aggregate_target=536870912, shm=67108864, no docker memory limit on either; xe21 sits at 54MB of its 2GB PGA limit. No config difference could produce a PASS/FAIL split. RULED OUT (b): the failure is inside Oracle's OWN recursive SQL during EXPLAIN PLAN; the thin driver cannot make an Oracle server process fail a 568-byte malloc. PROVED (c): the failing matrix ran 12:01-12:03Z; /tmp measured 99G/124G with 11G free RAM (130G locked in tmpfs) at ~12:05Z, and plain sqlplus INSIDE the container (no driver in path) spontaneously hit ORA-04036 in the same window. With RAM freed (153G available) the lane passes — verified standalone twice (as system, then as TESTUSER) and now in the full 3-lane run. NO CODE OR CONFIG CHANGE MADE: nothing was broken, so nothing was fixed; PGA tuning would have been a change with no defect behind it. NOTE the typed-limitation clause in the acceptance did not apply — it can be made green, so no version-aware refusal was warranted. all-versions-green for the 5c7i gvenzl scope (18c/21c/23ai) is now MET; the separate OCI/ADB lane (oci-live-adb-harness-y1x7) remains its own gate. Matrix was run against a clean HEAD binary via ORACLEMCP_ORACLE_MATRIX_BINARY because the shared working tree currently does not compile (uncommitted Arrow IPC edit from 6sj8.7.11, reported to the swarm).

title: Console WIG polish backlog: focus-visible, confirm/misclick gates, dialog focus-trap, deep-linking, virtualization, ellipsis copy
description:
Consolidated Web Interface Guidelines backlog from the dashboard review pass. The two highest-value WIG items are tracked separately: aria-live gap (oraclemcp-uc3z) and Explorer keyboard access (oraclemcp-8fn2). This bead rolls up the remaining medium/low items so none are lost. All line numbers are web/src/app/App.tsx unless noted.

CONFIRM / MISCLICK (UX; note the backend step-up grant is still the real gate, so these are misclick protections, not security holes):
- 1913 Config "Rollback" fires immediately on click, no confirmation.
- 7486 Workbench "Discard workspace" (drops held/uncommitted statements) has no confirmation.
- 694 lane "Kill" uses native window.confirm(), inconsistent with the app's own dialog pattern (2320).
- 6968 / 5978 primary destructive buttons (Commit / Reviews Apply) enable on a non-empty confirm field; the grant auto-fills from the preview response, so consider surfacing the danger level and a deliberate acknowledgement on the primary button.

DIALOG:
- 2320 ClientCredentialConfirmationDialog has role=dialog/aria-modal but no focus trap and no backdrop; focus is not returned to the trigger on close.

FOCUS:
- OM_INPUT/OM_TEXTAREA (3353) and ~18 inline sites (1083, 1876, 4693, 4702, 4795, 4804, 4813, 4825, 4841, 7189, 7207, 7460, 7829, 7838, 7854, 7868) use outline-none with focus: instead of focus-visible:, so the ring shows on mouse click too.

ARIA PATTERN:
- 5863 role=tablist on plain Button groups with no role=tab/aria-selected/roving-tabindex on children (repeats 5894, 6870).
- 338 RootLayout has no skip-to-main-content link ahead of the 11-item sidebar nav.

PERFORMANCE:
- 4538/5168 Explorer "Rows" allows up to 5000 but ExplorerObjectsPanel renders rows.map() with no virtualization; same for global-search lists (5005/5037).

NAVIGATION / DEEP-LINK:
- 570 selectedLaneId + filters live only in useState, never the URL (no deep-linking a lane); same in Explorer (4140) and Reviews (5571).
- 1868 Config TOML draft and 6889 Workbench SQL editor have no unsaved-changes warning before navigation.

COPY / TYPOGRAPHY:
- literal "..." instead of the ellipsis character at 3368, 5193, 5291-5296, 5403-5405, 5420, 8211, 8899, 9031.
- 1133 confirm-field placeholder "preview grant" (repeats 5953, 6936) is not a recognizable example pattern.
- web/index.html:6 no meta name=theme-color alongside color-scheme; should match Carved Light --color-base (#0c0b09).

Severity: MEDIUM/LOW polish. Good patterns to preserve (from the same review): shared Button/Badge primitives use correct focus-visible; tabular-nums on numeric columns; Intl.NumberFormat centralized in formatNumber; prefers-reduced-motion/forced-colors correctly drop the 3D Orrery to the 2D board; styles.css handles color-scheme + reduced-motion + forced-colors globally.
notes: Reset to open 2026-07-16: orphaned in_progress claim (console WIG polish backlog).
close_reason:
ALL bead items done across 6 commits (d1a51a1, 857d0f2, bb9b733, 762cca7, d3a2259, fed3da0 + Workbench-ack). Verified by grep: bare 'focus:' sites = 0, window.confirm = 0, role=tablist = 0, literal '...' = 0, placeholder 'preview grant' = 0.

FOCUS: 15 inputs migrated focus: to focus-visible: (all shared one identical class string; the Button/Badge primitives were already correct).

DIALOG (2320): new useModalFocus hook traps Tab and returns focus to the trigger on close; dialog now renders over a backdrop. aria-modal=true was previously a promise to AT that the page was inert while it was not.

CONFIRM/MISCLICK: lane Kill (694), Config Rollback (1913), Workbench Discard (7486) all used native window.confirm — unstyleable, unfocusable, unassertable. Now one shared ConfirmDialog. NOTE the code had drifted since the bead was written: rollback/discard had since gained window.confirm, so the real remaining defect was the native-vs-app-dialog inconsistency the bead flags at 694.

DESTRUCTIVE PRIMARIES (6968/5978): both Reviews Apply and Workbench Commit enabled on a non-empty confirm field, and the grant AUTO-FILLS from the preview — so the destructive primary was permanently armed and clicking it was not a decision. Both now require an acknowledgement that NAMES the effect ('I reviewed this DML + DDL change against prod and intend to commit it'), reusing Config's existing sensitive-draft checkbox pattern. Each resets on selection/SQL/lane/mode change so it can never be inherited by a different statement. Misclick protection only — the server still re-classifies and the single-use grant is still the gate.

ARIA (5863/5894/6870): three groups declared role=tablist while controlling NO tabpanel, promising arrow-key/roving-tabindex semantics that do not exist. They are toggle groups, so now role=group + aria-pressed. Deliberately did NOT half-implement tabs — role=tab without tabpanels/arrow keys is worse than not claiming it.

SKIP LINK (338): added ahead of the 11-item sidebar nav, and made it part of the skin grammar — assertDashboardSkinConformance now rejects a skin whose skipLink is missing or never reveals on focus (2 negative tests).

VIRTUALIZATION (4538/5168 + 5005/5037): operator-approved @tanstack/react-virtual (same vendor as the 4 TanStack packages already present; pinned exactly per house style; +0 vulnerabilities; bundle 678 to 705 kB raw / 184.7 to 192.5 kB gz). One shared useWindowedRows hook covers the object table and BOTH global-search hit lists. Windows only above 150 rows so the default 100-row page is untouched. FAILS SAFE: returns every row until the virtualizer has measured a live scroll element, so a measurement failure degrades to a slow list rather than a blank one.

DEEP-LINK (570/4140/5571): /sessions?lane=, /explorer?lane=, /reviews?id= as typed TanStack Router search params; validateSearch is fail-soft (junk degrades to no-selection, never an error boundary) and is never an authorization input. Dropped the Sessions and Reviews auto-select effects — both were redundant (display already falls back to the first item) and against a URL would have written an unchosen item into a shareable link. Live free-text filters stay LOCAL on purpose: they drive queries per keystroke, so URL-syncing them would rewrite the URL on every keypress.

UNSAVED (1868/6889): Config draft + Workbench SQL now guard via the router blocker + ConfirmDialog, with enableBeforeUnload covering tab-close/reload. Never blocks same-route navigation. Dirty is honest: Config stops being dirty once applied; Workbench compares against its WORKBENCH_SQL_SEED so an untouched editor never nags.

COPY: ellipsis character (13 quoted + 2 template sites; the 26 spread operators untouched); placeholder now shows the grant's REAL shape (xgrant-<pid>-<n>.<tag>, traced through issue_confirmation_grant to exec_grant.rs) instead of the unrecognizable 'preview grant'; index.html theme-color matches Carved Light --color-base #0c0b09.

GATE: npm run check green (tsc --noEmit + vite build + dashboard_bundle_check + SBOM + 161 tests / 18 files). New wig-polish.test.tsx = 11 tests.

VERIFICATION BOUNDARY (stated, not hidden): this harness renders to static markup and has NO layout engine, so it covers the windowing threshold, the fail-safe fallback, the a11y wiring, the dialog structure and the skin contract — but NOT the measured windowing itself, nor the Tab-trap DOM behavior (the same limitation explorer-a11y.test.tsx already documents). Those need a browser; the served-console e2e is where they will be exercised. No jsdom/testing-library was added unilaterally.

title: Pinned driver cannot emit required OCI ADB service SNI
description: Live OCI Always Free acceptance proves this is driver-owned, not a harness or wallet-decryption issue. With the wallet HIGH full DESCRIPTION descriptor corrected to contain TRANSPORT_CONNECT_TIMEOUT=60 and profile-level connect_timeout_seconds removed, the pinned oracledb 0.8.3 fails before network I/O: use_sni=true cannot be honored because the Oracle service-form SNI S{len}.{service}.V3.{numeric} is rejected as a rustls DNS name. The no-SNI real-ADB path times out/resets at the OCI front end, so a harness cannot safely bypass it. Owner: sibling rust-oracledb, src/tls.rs decide_sni / TLS connector. Wallet crypto is independently verified clean: ewallet.pem PBES2/PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256/AES-256-CBC; ewallet.p12 pbeWithSHA1And3DES; cwallet.sso. Need a safe way to transmit Oracle service-form SNI or otherwise support OCI ADB without TLS downgrade. Evidence: target/e2e/oci_adb_terraform/20260716T211356Z-2293200 (runtime-only).
notes:

The plan audit classifies this SNI/ADB-S work as shipped; only he7t IAM subject mapping remains.

That proves the library masker but not profile policy loading plus the served MCP result envelope. Add a real served stdio or HTTP matrix using a masked live profile; assert whole-response plaintext absence, mask-unknown default, join-consistent tokenization, and audit-bound mask certificate re-derivation. Keep only synthetic fixture values, register the scenario, and record live evidence.
