title: [09x][M][P1] Resolve profile token salts in served egress
description: Arc M configured masking action=tokenize requires salt_ref, but the served policy adapter result_masking_policy_from_profile() currently drops salt_ref and deliberately creates ResultMaskingPolicy without with_token_salt(). The DB transformer therefore degrades tokenization to <masked>. Implement the documented per-profile state-file salt resolver/rotation seam, inject ProfileMaskingSalt with a non-secret salt_id into the served policy, and fail closed (reject/startup or mask with an explicit non-comparable certificate) when the referenced salt is unavailable. Add a real served-MCP E2E against a live test Oracle that uses action=tokenize and proves: same plaintext across a self-join maps to the same non-plaintext token; distinct plaintexts do not collide; raw response carries no fixture plaintext; certificate records the active salt_id; and salt rotation changes tokens without exposing either secret. Register it in run_all.sh. Do not weaken masking or make salt material observable.
close_reason: Landed c8d1faa: profile salt state is loaded fail-closed into served masking; a real FREE 23ai stdio MCP proof verifies stable client-join tokens across queries, scope separation, rotation, raw-wire plaintext absence, certificate salt_id, and missing-salt startup refusal.

title: E13: a struct field and its initializers are one commit (charter rule 17 + atomicity check)
description: Live incident 2026-07-21: one pane added a field to DoctorProfileContext while another pane owned the four initializers, breaking the build for every agent in the shared checkout. Encode the rule: a struct field and its initializers are ONE logical change, landed in ONE commit by ONE agent. Deliverable: AGENTS.md constitution rule + a static check that, for each struct field added by a commit or staged diff, finds struct-literal initializer sites the same change does not touch and refuses the split.
close_reason: Rule 17 + struct-atomicity: an added struct field is checked against the initializer and pattern sites the change leaves untouched [closing=5b9b65bfc54ed46652b943dc5d21ca8a8512d9f2 source=32122cb9857684ace414e03ef4a3ca6771dc0183 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-35l11.json]

title: E9: foreign mid-edit files are not defects (charter rule 13 + provenance check)
description: Live incident 2026-07-21: a pane read another pane's uncommitted mid-edit crates/oraclemcp-error/src/error.rs, concluded oraclemcp-db could not compile, filed a false build blocker and went idle. The symbol existed the whole time at HEAD. Encode the rule: check git status on a file before declaring it broken; a modified file that is not yours is another agent mid-edit, not a defect. Deliverable: AGENTS.md constitution rule + a mechanical check that answers 'is this path a foreign mid-edit?' from git state, so the question is never answered by reading the buffer.
close_reason: Rule 13 + foreign-edit: a worktree file differing from HEAD is reported as another agent mid-edit, not a defect [closing=5b9b65bfc54ed46652b943dc5d21ca8a8512d9f2 source=32122cb9857684ace414e03ef4a3ca6771dc0183 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-3shji.json]

title: [QA-IMPL][SEC] Subprocess plugin must not inherit parent environment
description: Verified security boundary bug in exported oraclemcp-core SubprocessPlugin: PluginProcessTree::spawn used std::process::Command without env_clear, so a plugin child inherited the server process environment. That environment commonly carries ORACLE_*/ORACLEMCP_* secret refs, OAuth/audit keys, IAM tokens, and live-test database passwords. Fix: spawn subprocess plugins with a sterile environment and adjust the public boundary docs to avoid overclaiming. Guard: subprocess_plugin_does_not_inherit_parent_environment proves a plugin cannot see parent HOME as a stand-in inherited env secret.
acceptance_criteria: SubprocessPlugin child environment is cleared before spawn; regression test proves a parent env variable is not visible in the child; fmt, clippy, cargo test --workspace, cargo deny, and git diff --check pass.
notes: Validated by focused test: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/oraclemcp-43xo-target cargo test -p oraclemcp-core subprocess_plugin_does_not_inherit_parent_environment -- --nocapture. Full gates also passed locally.
close_reason: Fixed by clearing the subprocess plugin child environment with env_clear and adding a regression test that verifies parent environment values are not inherited.

title: Dashboard modal shell is duplicated across ConfirmDialog and ClientCredentialConfirmationDialog
description:
[LOW] Found during the 2ekf fresh-eyes review; filed rather than fixed inline because it is a refactor, not a defect — both copies are correct today.

web/src/app/App.tsx: ConfirmDialog (~2511) and ClientCredentialConfirmationDialog (~2571) each hand-roll the same modal shell: the fixed inset-0 backdrop, mousedown-to-cancel on the backdrop only, the z-50 layer, role=dialog + aria-modal + aria-labelledby, the Escape handler, and the useModalFocus ref.

WHY IT MATTERS (drift risk on an a11y/security surface, not a present bug): the shell carries the focus trap and the modal semantics. Two copies must stay in sync; a future edit to one can silently leave the other without the Tab trap, the backdrop, or Escape — the exact defects 2ekf was filed to fix. Commit b319815 shows how quietly this surface regresses: autoFocus inside a dialog defeated focus-return because React applies autoFocus at commit, before the passive effect that records the trigger.

FIX: extract a ModalShell owning backdrop + focus trap + Escape + the labelled dialog box; both dialogs supply only body and actions. ClientCredential keeps its typed-client-id input and grant-derived confirm; ConfirmDialog keeps its plain body + confirmLabel.

GUARD — do NOT weaken: keep backdrop-cancel gated on !busy; keep autoFocus OUT of dialog contents (see b319815); keep focus returning to the invoker.

TEST: wig-polish.test.tsx already pins role=dialog/aria-modal/backdrop/labelledby and the no-autofocus property; extend it to assert BOTH dialogs share them so the shells cannot drift.
close_reason: Extracted shared ModalShell for both confirmation dialogs; preserved focus trap, Escape/backdrop cancellation, labels, and busy behavior. Web check passed.

Regenerate only this intended golden through UPDATE_GOLDENS, prove the exact golden test and full golden_behavior suite, then commit.

title: [09x][guard] SideEffectOracle bypassed: NestedJoin / PIVOT / UNPIVOT base objects were never collected
description:
query_base_objects::collect_factor handled only TableFactor::Table and ::Derived; TableFactor::NestedJoin (a parenthesized join, 'FROM (a JOIN b ON ..)') and ::Pivot/::Unpivot/::MatchRecognize fell into the '_ => {}' arm and named NO base objects.

classify_statement reads an EMPTY base-object list as Purity::ProvenReadOnly, so for those four spellings the engine's statement_purity consult - the trigger / VPD (DBMS_RLS) walk - was SKIPPED and the read was cleared Safe/READ_ONLY. An oracle that declares every object side-effecting still returned Safe, because it was never asked. This is a fail-open in the fail-closed core: it is not closable by any oracle, since the oracle is not consulted.

Found while writing tests to kill mutation survivors for bead .16 (the sibling walkers table_factor_carries_dml and collect_factor_qualifiers already descend both shapes, so this was an oversight in one walker, not a deliberate choice).

FIXED (tightening-only) in the same change: collect_factor now descends NestedJoin via a collect_table_with_joins helper and recurses through Pivot/Unpivot/MatchRecognize. Regression test: tests/classifier_metamorphic.rs::a_stricter_oracle_is_consulted_however_the_base_object_is_spelled asserts a strict oracle is reached for all 9 spellings (plain, parenthesized body, set-op, derived, CTE, nested join, doubly-nested join, PIVOT, UNPIVOT). Bead filed for the record/traceability; code and test land together.
close_reason: Fixed: query_base_objects::collect_factor now descends TableFactor::NestedJoin (via a collect_table_with_joins helper) and recurses through Pivot/Unpivot/MatchRecognize, so the SideEffectOracle statement_purity consult (trigger/VPD walk) is reached for every base-object spelling. Regression test a_stricter_oracle_is_consulted_however_the_base_object_is_spelled pins all 9 spellings against a strictest-possible oracle; full guard suite green (296 tests).

title: [09x][audit][mutation] Pin audit survivor invariants
description: Fresh 09x mutation-gate audit run at /var/tmp/oraclemcp-mutation-audit-20260713T001426Z reported oraclemcp-audit kill=87.9%, below the enforcing 90% gate. Survivors cluster around anchor temp suffix/reader errors, HMAC/key-id validation, WormFileForwarder path/flush/SD-param checks, durable shipping spool config/status/drop/recovery paths, and debug/verification proof surfaces. Proposed fix: verify each survivor cluster against source behavior, add focused unit/regression tests for true safety invariants, avoid production behavior changes unless a real semantic defect is reproduced, then rerun audit mutation gate to >=90 with status enforcing.

title: [09x][A] No SCN ever reaches a client — observed_scn is audited but never projected, and a flashback read never echoes the SCN it read at
description: SYMPTOM: the console cannot learn a single system change number from the server. Three separate omissions: (1) AuditRecord::observed_scn exists and IS recorded (crates/oraclemcp/src/dispatch/mod.rs append_audit_with_observed_scn -> Auditor::append_correlated_with_observed_scn; AuditRecord field in crates/oraclemcp-audit/src/record.rs) but redacted_audit_record() (crates/oraclemcp-core/src/http/operator.rs) does NOT project it; (2) QueryResponse (crates/oraclemcp-db/src/query.rs:48) has no SCN field, so oracle_query with as_of={scn|timestamp} never echoes the SCN it actually read at — for a TIMESTAMP target Oracle resolves the value internally (AsOf::resolve_to_scn) and the caller never learns the resulting SCN at all; (3) no route publishes the database's current SCN even though the read exists (AsOf::current_system_change_number, DBMS_FLASHBACK.GET_SYSTEM_CHANGE_NUMBER) and the console cannot obtain it itself, because a SELECT calling DBMS_FLASHBACK is refused by the fail-closed purity oracle (default Unknown). THE DASHBOARD IS SHOWING LESS PROOF THAN THE BACKEND HAS: for (1) especially — the audit chain durably records the exact snapshot every governed read observed, and the operator cannot see it. WHY IT MATTERS: SCN is the forensic replay handle. Without it an operator cannot correlate an audit record with a point in time, cannot reproduce what a read saw, and cannot anchor a time-travel UI to anything real. The SCN time-scrubber (bead …15.2, commit ead6c08) therefore renders data-scn-min/max='unknown' and builds its axis solely from snapshots it has itself confirmed, and states in the UI that the server publishes neither the current SCN nor the retention window. A FIX MUST PROVE: (1) redacted_audit_record projects observed_scn (it is a plain u64 — no redaction concern) and an http test asserts a read's record carries it; (2) an as_of read echoes the resolved SCN in its response, including for a timestamp target, with a live test showing timestamp -> SCN resolution is reported back; (3) if a current-SCN surface is added, it is read-only, governed, and does not require the caller to write PLAN_TABLE or hold FLASHBACK beyond what the read itself needs; (4) the scrubber can then anchor its axis to real values with no client change beyond consuming the new fields.
close_reason: Verified 1968cfb is at HEAD: audit-tail redaction projects observed_scn, flashback query responses echo the resolved SCN, and timestamp_as_of_echoes_oracles_resolved_scn_to_the_client passes.

title: Grant EXECUTE on SYS.DBMS_FLASHBACK to lab profiles so 18c/21c cover the POSITIVE flashback replay path
close_reason: DONE (commit ee79a4a). Granted + verified + documented. APPLIED: 'GRANT EXECUTE ON SYS.DBMS_FLASHBACK TO testuser' on the 18c and 21c lab lanes. Had to be issued AS SYSDBA — SYSTEM cannot grant on a SYS-owned package and fails ORA-01031 (found the hard way; now documented). VERIFIED at three levels, not asserted: (1) as testuser both lanes now open AND close a real flashback window (18c scn=3549775, 21c scn=4814082); (2) live_observed_scn_replays_the_committed_read_snapshot flips from 'REFUSED as contracted (capability_unavailable)' to the POSITIVE arm on both lanes — silent pass = an audited SCN replaying the same committed rows, 8.28s/13.17s including the 8s DDL settle, so the real flashback path executed; (3) free23 already had the grant. So the positive replay path is now covered on all three versions, which was this bead's whole point. DOCUMENTED in docs/operations.md beside the lab-lane setup so a fresh lab does not silently lose the coverage, recording the two facts that cost time: the grant must come from SYS (SYSTEM => ORA-01031), and PLS-00201 on a lane means the GRANT is missing, NOT that the version lacks DBMS_FLASHBACK (the package is present and VALID on 18c/21c/23ai — the misconception that generated the original xq3z bead). DESIGN NOTE: both postures remain asserted rather than granting away the refusal coverage — a granted lane proves the real replay, an ungranted lane proves the typed version-aware refusal. The xq3z test handles both arms, so the fail-closed safety contract keeps live coverage on any ungranted lab instead of becoming unreachable. NOT DONE: the grant is applied to THIS box's containers and documented for reproduction, but there is no lab-provisioning script in the repo to encode it into — if one is ever added, the grant belongs there.

title: No CI job compiles the Rust code for Windows; the target is built only at release-tag time
notes:
FOUND while fixing 7oaa (the Windows FileStore hardening) — this is the SYSTEMIC reason 7oaa's bug class exists and can ship.

THE GAP: the ONLY windows-latest job in ci.yml is "windows-installer" (PSScriptAnalyzer + install.ps1 dry-run). It never invokes cargo. Every cargo clippy/test job runs on Linux. So NOTHING compiles the Rust code for x86_64-pc-windows-msvc until release.yml builds the tag (release.yml:214). Consequences: (1) a Windows-only compile error is discovered while CUTTING A RELEASE, the worst possible moment; (2) every cfg(windows) block in the tree is effectively unverified — nobody can be sure it compiles; (3) cfg(windows) TESTS never execute anywhere, so Windows-only security refusals (7oaa: reparse-point + hard-linked service locks) have no automated proof at all, on a platform whose release assets we publish.

NOT HYPOTHETICAL — it bit me inside 7oaa: my first draft called options.custom_flags(FILE_FLAG_OPEN_REPARSE_POINT), but file_store.rs imports only the UNIX OpenOptionsExt (line ~20, cfg(unix)-gated), so custom_flags was not in scope for Windows. A hard compile error that NO existing gate would have caught; it would have surfaced as a broken release build. I only found it by cross-compiling deliberately.

FIX OPTIONS (note the caveat — the obvious one does not work today):
(a) Run "cargo check --workspace --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc" ON a windows-latest runner. Simplest, costs a runner minute; catches every Windows compile error.
(b) Cross-compile that same check on the existing ubuntu runner. CAVEAT I HIT: it FAILS on ubuntu today because ring and psm run C/asm build scripts needing an MSVC toolchain ('error occurred in cc-rs: failed to find tool "lib.exe"'). Needs xwin or clang-cl wiring first — cheaper per run, more setup.
(c) Run the existing cargo test job on windows-latest. Strongest: it additionally EXECUTES the cfg(windows) tests, and is the ONLY option that gives 7oaa's refusals real proof rather than compile-only assurance.
Recommend (c) if the runner budget allows, else (a). Do not assume (b) is the cheap default — it is currently broken.

WORKAROUND, reusable by anyone touching cfg(windows) code until this is fixed — compile the file in isolation:
  rustup target add x86_64-pc-windows-msvc
  # throwaway crate whose lib.rs is just:
  #   #![cfg_attr(windows, feature(windows_by_handle))]
  #   #[path = "<abs>/crates/oraclemcp-core/src/file_store.rs"] pub mod file_store;
  # with only that file's non-std deps (parking_lot / sha2 / thiserror), then:
  cargo check --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc
"cargo check" skips cfg(test), so crate:: refs in the test module do not matter, and ring/psm never enter the graph. That compiles the REAL production file against the REAL target, and is exactly what caught the import bug above.

RELATED / HONEST STATUS: 7oaa's three cfg(windows) tests are committed and compile-verified but have NEVER executed. They stay decorative until this bead is fixed. Recorded rather than implied.
close_reason: Added required Windows Rust workspace cargo test job and regenerated CI taxonomy; Windows cfg code now compiles and executes in CI.

title: CI Heartbeat reports five mutation shards and loom.yml not green
description:
The CI Heartbeat lane (the operator-never-discovers-red-first notification path) failed at run 29839712070 on 2026-07-21T14:34Z, reporting:

  not_green  MuhDur/oraclemcp  core shard offset 1      (run 29804146226)
  not_green  MuhDur/oraclemcp  db shard offset 0        (run 29804146226)
  not_green  MuhDur/oraclemcp  db shard offset 1        (run 29804146226)
  not_green  MuhDur/oraclemcp  dispatch shard offset 0  (run 29804146226)
  not_green  MuhDur/oraclemcp  dispatch shard offset 1  (run 29804146226)
  error      MuhDur/oraclemcp  loom.yml: no completed non-superseded scheduled run was found

The five shards are all from ONE run (29804146226), so this is likely a single mutation-campaign run that went not-green rather than five independent failures - triage it as one cause first.

The loom.yml entry is a different class: it is not red, it is ABSENT. The heartbeat cannot find any completed non-superseded scheduled run, so the loom lane's health is unknown rather than green. Unknown must never be reported as green (that is the heartbeat's whole purpose), so this needs either a scheduled run that actually completes or an explicit, documented exemption.

NOTE this bead is about the oraclemcp side only. The heartbeat also flags MuhDur/rust-oracledb driver_required:required.yml as not_green; that is separately tracked and is a known 0.9.0 release blocker being worked in the driver repo.

ACCEPTANCE: each of the five shards is either green or has a filed root-cause bead; loom.yml either produces a completed scheduled run or is explicitly exempted with the reason recorded; and a subsequent CI Heartbeat run concludes success. Cite the heartbeat run that goes green.

title: Epic: wire all currently supported thin-driver profile/config capabilities
description:
## Goal

Make oraclemcp's repo-local profile/config surface catch up with what the current pure-Rust thin `oracledb` driver already supports, without waiting on upstream features that are genuinely missing.

## Current evidence

- oraclemcp's strict config schema only exposes a subset of thin-driver connection options in `crates/oraclemcp-config/src/profile.rs`.
- `oraclemcp_core::profile_to_options` currently maps connect string, username/password, external/IAM flags, wallet location, a subset of session identity, call timeout, and session setup statements in `crates/oraclemcp-core/src/connect.rs`.
- `oraclemcp_db::connection::to_connect_options` currently only applies `wallet_location` and forces SNI when a wallet exists in `crates/oraclemcp-db/src/connection.rs`.
- The current `rust-oracledb` driver already supports additional public options: `ClientIdentity::{program,machine,osuser,terminal,driver_name}`, `with_edition`, `with_wallet_password`, `with_ssl_server_dn_match`, `with_ssl_server_cert_dn`, `with_use_sni`, `with_proxy_user`, `with_app_context`, and `with_sdu`.
- oraclemcp already contains partially stranded local support for `AuthAdapter::Proxy`, `DrcpConfig`, `PoolConfig`, `OraclePool`, and result placeholders for `QueryValue::Lob` and `QueryValue::Cursor`.

## Scope

Create and implement granular child beads for fields/capabilities that are wireable now because the driver or local code already supports them. Do not include Kerberos, RADIUS/native MFA, OCI IAM database-token auth, passwordless external/wallet auth, or ADT/object decoding here; those need upstream driver work and have separate upstream GitHub issues where applicable.

## Non-negotiables

- Preserve the fail-closed SQL guard and profile operating-level ceilings.
- Keep profile metadata and doctor output secret-safe: no connect strings, usernames, passwords, wallet passwords, token values, wallet paths, application-context values, or workstation/user identity leaks unless a tool is explicitly returning live Oracle session diagnostics.
- Keep config validation strict: new keys must be typed, documented, inherited correctly, and covered by tests.
- The default behavior must remain safe and compatible for existing profiles.
- Every child bead must include focused unit tests and, where feasible, live Oracle 23ai/FREE validation instructions.
close_reason: All dependency beads are closed: supported thin profile/config capabilities are wired through config, SessionContext, OracleConnectOptions, thin ConnectOptions, docs, doctor/setup, materialization, and live matrix coverage. Verified with full gates, Oracle 23ai live tests, and epic compliance audit 2026-06-18T10-25-21Z.

title: E10: close evidence must come from a tree clean of other agents' work (charter rule 14)
description: Live incident 2026-07-21: close evidence for b8a was generated while another pane had uncommitted work open, so source.tree_clean was recorded false and validate_evidence.py raised the HARD finding E_TREE_DIRTY. Encode the rule plus the mechanism: the evidence 'source' block (sha, branch, tree_clean) must be derived mechanically from git, scoped to the bead's in_scope paths, with foreign dirty paths reported separately instead of silently poisoning the boolean.

title: [09x][L] Cost badge under-reports: the profile max_query_cost is already on the operator wire and the dashboard ignores it
description:
SYMPTOM: the cost/gas badge (bead 15.1, commit eaab5e2) renders data-cost-ceiling="undisclosed" for any statement that was not refused, because it learns the ceiling only from a cost refusal (structured_reason.query_cost_refusal.max_query_cost). That was wrong about the wire. GET /operator/v1/config ALREADY returns each profile ceiling: ConfigOps::status() (crates/oraclemcp-core/src/config_ops.rs:513) fills ConfigOpsStatus.profiles from OracleMcpConfig::list_profiles(), whose element type ProfileMetadata (crates/oraclemcp-config/src/profile.rs:1037) is Serialize and carries max_query_cost: Option<u64> (line 1047), alongside call_timeout_seconds, cumulative_query_cost_budget and max_level. The dashboard type ConfigProfileMetadata in web/src/app/operator-client.ts simply does not declare the field, so the badge never reads it.

THE DASHBOARD IS SHOWING LESS PROOF THAN THE BACKEND ACTUALLY HAS, and this one is entirely the fault of the dashboard: the backend has published the ceiling all along.

WHY IT MATTERS: an operator cannot see how close a statement is to the ceiling until the gate has already refused it, which is exactly the moment the information stops being useful. With the config ceiling in hand the badge can render within_ceiling with a real ratio BEFORE the statement runs.

FIX (web/ only, Arc L lane): (1) add max_query_cost to ConfigProfileMetadata; (2) have the Workbench cost panel read the active profile ceiling from the config query and pass it as the ceiling input; (3) keep a refusal-disclosed ceiling as the authority when the two disagree, because a per-call max_query_cost meets the profile ceiling with min(), so a refusal can legitimately report a LOWER ceiling than the profile.

A FIX MUST PROVE: a test asserts the badge renders a numeric data-cost-ceiling from config alone with no refusal in play, and that a lower refusal-disclosed ceiling still wins over the config value.
close_reason:
Fixed in commit 42acae2 (web/ only, no Rust touched). The badge now learns the ceiling from the operator wire.

ROOT CAUSE (my own bug from bead 15.1): GET /operator/v1/config already publishes every profile max_query_cost — ConfigOps::status() fills ConfigOpsStatus.profiles from list_profiles(), whose ProfileMetadata (crates/oraclemcp-config/src/profile.rs:1047) serializes max_query_cost — but the dashboard type ConfigProfileMetadata never declared the field, so the badge could only learn the ceiling from a cost refusal, i.e. after the gate had already refused, which is exactly when the number stops being useful.

FIX: operator-client.ts adds max_query_cost (and call_timeout_seconds) to ConfigProfileMetadata; new profileCostCeiling(config, activeProfile) reads the ceiling for the active profile (falling back to default_profile) and new parseActiveProfile() reads connection.profile from oracle_connection_info. presentation-model.ts: CostBadgeInput gains ceilingSource ("refusal" | "config" | "unknown") and ungated; toCostBadgeViewModel now distinguishes THREE states that were previously collapsed into one — a numeric ceiling in force (from config or refusal), UNGATED (the config positively says the profile declares no max_query_cost, so oracle_query is not cost-gated: a fact, not a missing reading), and UNKNOWN (the console could not identify the active profile, so it claims nothing). A refusal-disclosed ceiling always wins, because the server has already met the profile ceiling with any per-call one via min(), so the refusal number is the effective ceiling and may legitimately be LOWER than the configured one. skin.tsx emits data-cost-ceiling-source and renders data-cost-ceiling="none" for ungated vs "undisclosed" for unknown. App.tsx CostGatePanel adds the config + connection useQuery consumers and feeds the configured ceiling in.

PROVEN: 7 new cost-gate.test.ts cases (ceiling read from config for the active profile; default-profile fallback; ungated vs unknown kept distinct; a numeric data-cost-ceiling with NO refusal in play; an estimate judged within_ceiling against the config ceiling BEFORE the gate refuses; a lower refusal-disclosed ceiling winning over the configured one; ungated reported as ungated) plus 2 rewritten conformance it() (renders data-cost-ceiling="50000" + data-cost-ceiling-source="config" with no refusal; never invents a ceiling and never confuses ungated ("none") with unknown ("undisclosed")). The two older tests that asserted the WRONG claim — that the ceiling is only ever disclosed on refusal — were corrected rather than left passing.

Gate green: tsc --noEmit clean, vitest 106/106 (10 files), npm run build + dashboard-bundle-check OK.

title: Rename the 0.9.1 release train to 0.10.0 across plan, beads and tracker root
description:
The workspace shipped 0.9.0 -> 0.10.0 in dfabe253 (pushed, on origin/main). The release train is still called 0.9.1 everywhere else, so the name and the artifact now disagree.

WHY 0.10.0 AND NOT 0.9.1. cargo semver-checks check-release failed all three locked crates against the published 0.9.0 with six major findings: oraclemcp-error enum_variant_missing (OracleMcpError::LeaseRequired); oraclemcp-db function_missing (require_lease_id), struct_missing (LeaseManager, LeaseInfo, PreviewImpact, LeaseId) and function_parameter_count_changed (compile_errors, describe_constraints, plscope_identifiers, plscope_statements 4->5; get_sources_by_name 5->7); oraclemcp-guard constructible_struct_adds_field (CorpusRecord.authenticity). The first five are b5736dce removing a dead lease subsystem and the metadata-bounding work adding max_rows - both intended. On a 0.x line a public-API removal forces the minor position. Measured, not assumed: with #[non_exhaustive] on CorpusRecord and the version left at 0.9.0, semver-checks still failed, having swapped constructible_struct_adds_field for struct_marked_non_exhaustive. Operator ruled 0.10.0 on 2026-07-22.

WHAT THIS BEAD IS. Every release-VISIBLE surface already moved together and is gate-verified (release_surface_sync_check.sh: OK version=0.10.0, 26 surfaces). What remains is the naming fallout, which is coordination rather than semver arithmetic:
  - docs/plan/PLAN_0_9_1_FIELD_HARDENING.md - filename and the ~10 in-text 0.9.1 references, including the OPERATOR DECISION (2026-07-20) line that fixed the train at driver 0.9.0 / server 0.9.1
  - bead titles and bodies naming the 0.9.1 train
  - the tracker release-sink bead bp8ia.13 (server 0.9.1)
  - the 091- bead id prefix is cosmetic; renaming ids is NOT proposed, only the human-facing text

NOT IN SCOPE. No code, no version metadata - that is done. Do not re-open the version decision here.

Discovered while making the public-API lock green; filed rather than done, because renaming a train the operator named is the operator's call.

title: doctor tests red at HEAD: parent-traversal refusal rejects doctor_tmp_dir's '..' path
description: Commit ee1b23ad ('fix(doctor): harden atomic audit migration') added a parent-traversal refusal to the atomic-install path. The test helper doctor_tmp_dir (crates/oraclemcp-core/src/doctor.rs:3048) builds its temp directory as CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR + '../../target/tmp/...', so the PathBuf it returns contains literal '..' components; create_dir_all succeeds but the new safety check refuses the path with 'is not a safe directory: parent traversal is refused'. Two tests fail as a result: doctor::tests::legacy_migration_refuses_symlink_swaps_at_atomic_install (added by the same commit) and doctor::tests::legacy_state_layout_detects_and_migrates_audit_jsonl_once (pre-existing, broken by the hardening). PROVEN independent of any concurrent work: both fail in a clean worktree at HEAD with no other changes present. Likely fix: canonicalize in doctor_tmp_dir (std::fs::canonicalize after create_dir_all) so the helper hands the production path a path with no '..' components, rather than relaxing the refusal - the refusal is the security property ee1b23ad landed and must not be weakened. Owner: the pane that landed ee1b23ad (CF3 / F-LOW 7.11.19).
close_reason: doctor tests green again: removed the '..' the helper handed production, then fixed the EBADF fchmod-on-a-Dir it was masking; the traversal refusal is unchanged [closing=0f3361b973221c5dc71e852d002d4311614f9169 source=f3650548bdfd7c970ec4d4c17797984b6d963796 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-7f4o9.json]

title: Wire driver-level application context triples from profiles
description:
## Current gap

`rust-oracledb` exposes `ConnectOptions::with_app_context(Vec<(namespace, key, value)>)`, and the protocol layer applies those triples during auth. oraclemcp currently has no profile schema for driver-level application contexts. Operators can sometimes approximate this with `trusted_session_statements`, but that is broader, later, and less structured than the driver-supported logon app-context path.

## Required change

Add a strict profile config section for driver-level application context triples and map it through:

- `ConnectionProfile`
- `profile_to_options`
- `OracleConnectOptions`
- `RustOracleConnection::to_connect_options`
- `oracledb::ConnectOptions::with_app_context(...)`

## Design requirements

- Use a typed list of triples, not string parsing.
- Validate that namespace/key are non-empty and bounded.
- Treat values as potentially sensitive tenant/session context: do not include them in profile metadata or ordinary debug output.
- Preserve `trusted_session_statements` for cases that really need arbitrary operator-authored session setup.

## Acceptance criteria

- TOML examples can express at least two app-context triples.
- Config inheritance merges or replaces the app-context list according to the documented profile inheritance rule; tests must pin that behavior.
- The mapped `OracleConnectOptions` preserves ordering.
- Unit tests prove config parsing, validation failures, metadata redaction, and final driver option mapping.
- A live test or documented 23ai/FREE script verifies the context through `SYS_CONTEXT(namespace, key)` when the database has an application context available.
close_reason: Implemented typed profile app-context wiring and verified with full Rust gates plus single-bead compliance audit 2026-06-18T08-14-08Z; deterministic missing-test item manually calibrated as false positive against concrete test evidence.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Verification before close: implemented typed [[profiles.app_context]] triples with strict validation, shallow inheritance semantics, redacted Debug/profile metadata, ordered mapping through profile_to_options -> OracleConnectOptions -> RustOracleConnection::to_connect_options -> oracledb::ConnectOptions::with_app_context, README/robot docs examples, and optional live SYS_CONTEXT test via ORACLEMCP_TEST_APP_CONTEXT. Gates passed: cargo fmt --all -- --check; git diff --check; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace; cargo deny check. Single-bead audit pass beads_compliance_audit/passes/2026-06-18T08-14-08Z scored 985/1000; deterministic missing-test item was manually calibrated as a false positive because focused tests exist for parsing/validation/order, inheritance, metadata redaction, core mapping, driver mapping, and live round trip.

title: Fail closed on Windows reparse-point and hard-linked FileStore service locks
description: [HIGH] Cross-review finding after 8c26968. The Unix hardening correctly opens .service.lock with O_NOFOLLOW and validates the opened descriptor as one regular inode. On Windows the cfg(unix) protections are absent: std::fs::OpenOptions follows an existing symlink/reparse point, and no nlink check rejects hard links. A same-user actor who can modify the FileStore state directory can therefore redirect acquire_service_owner() and its set_len(0)+metadata write into another writable file. Windows symlink privilege defaults are not a security boundary (Developer Mode and reparse points exist), and Windows release assets are published. Implement a safe no-follow/reparse-point open using a maintained safe abstraction or a platform-specific safe API while preserving #![forbid(unsafe_code)], reject non-regular/reparse and multi-link sidecars atomically where the platform permits, add Windows-gated adversarial tests, and return UnsafePath before any truncate/write. Do not weaken Unix behavior, crash recovery, or replacement-lock semantics.
close_reason:
FIXED in 1df04ae. Windows half of siry/em39: both Unix defences were cfg'd out on Windows, so a same-user actor able to write the FileStore state dir could still redirect acquire_service_owner's set_len(0) — or the JSONL append carrying audit-chain records — into any file that user can write.

FIX (mirrors the Unix shape, does not invent one):
1. FILE_FLAG_OPEN_REPARSE_POINT is the O_NOFOLLOW analogue and is what makes the refusal possible AT ALL: it returns a handle to the reparse point ITSELF. Without it the open silently follows, authenticate_private_file then stats the VICTIM — a perfectly ordinary lone regular file — and we truncate it. The flag is ignored for non-reparse files, so the normal path is unaffected.
2. authenticate_private_file also rejects FILE_ATTRIBUTE_REPARSE_POINT and number_of_links() != 1, stat'ing THE HANDLE WE HOLD, never the path — no inspect-then-use window. is_file() covers neither: a file symlink opened no-follow still reports as a file, and a hard link IS a regular file, just the victim's.
Both run before try_lock and before any truncate/append, and refuse as UnsafePath, not Io.

SCOPE WIDER THAN THE BEAD TEXT, deliberately: 7oaa was filed before em39 landed and names only .service.lock, but em39 added two MORE unix-only-no-follow opens (tail-recovery set_len at ~line 367, and open_append_private_file) with the identical Windows gap. Fixing only the lock would have left those live. All three now carry the flag. create_new_private_file needs none — create_new is O_EXCL and refuses any existing path, link included. (4 open sites audited, 3 needed it.)

WINDOWS-SPECIFIC INSIGHT worth keeping: a HARD LINK NEEDS NO PRIVILEGE on Windows, unlike a symlink (SeCreateSymbolicLinkPrivilege / Developer Mode). So the hard link, not the reparse point, is the REACHABLE attack, and its test does not skip. The reparse test skips when the runner cannot create a symlink — that privilege default is NOT the boundary, which is exactly why the hard-link test carries the load. This also refutes siry's parting note ("Windows symlink creation requires privilege by default"), which was true but not the whole surface.

number_of_links() is unstable (windows_by_handle, rust-lang/rust#63010) so lib.rs gains a cfg_attr(windows)-gated feature. Adds NO nightly requirement — the line is already nightly-pinned for asupersync. None => REFUSE: File::metadata documents Some, so None means the descriptor could not be authenticated, and an unverifiable sidecar must not be written.

CONSTANTS are documented literals, a deliberate DEPARTURE from siry's libc precedent: O_NOFOLLOW genuinely differs per Unix (0x20000 Linux vs 0x100 macOS/BSD) so hardcoding it there is fragile; the Win32 flags are one frozen ABI identical on every Windows target, and windows-sys already sits at TWO versions in the lock — a third copy for two integers is the worse trade. std's OpenOptionsExt/MetadataExt remain the platform-specific safe API the bead asked for. forbid(unsafe_code) holds; zero unsafe added; no new dependency.

VERIFIED: the REAL file_store.rs cross-compiles clean for x86_64-pc-windows-msvc (via a #[path] include probe — the full crate cannot be checked here because ring/psm need an MSVC toolchain). That caught a GENUINE BUG IN MY OWN FIRST DRAFT: the file imports only the UNIX OpenOptionsExt, so custom_flags was not in scope for Windows and would have broken the RELEASE build — the only place it could have surfaced. Linux untouched: fmt clean, clippy -p oraclemcp-core --all-targets -D warnings clean, file_store 18/18 green.

HONEST LIMIT — the reason this is not as strong as siry: the three cfg(windows) tests are compile-verified but have NEVER EXECUTED. This box has no Windows and, as I discovered doing this, NO CI JOB COMPILES OR TESTS THIS CRATE FOR WINDOWS AT ALL (filed as oraclemcp-6fyp; the only windows-latest job is the installer PSSA). siry proved its tests load-bearing by mutation; I cannot. The tests mirror the unix ones that do pass, and both Windows-only primitives they use (fs::hard_link, os::windows::fs::symlink_file) were compile-checked against the real target. Until 6fyp lands, the Windows refusals rest on code review + cross-compilation, not on execution. Stated rather than implied.

NOT WEAKENED: Unix behaviour, crash recovery, and replacement-lock semantics untouched — no cfg(unix) path changed; 18/18 file_store tests green including service_lock_recovers_after_holder_process_is_killed and old_lock_drop_does_not_unlink_replacement_lock.

title: e2e harness robustness: run_all skip-accounting hardened; version_matrix explicit-live-missing-creds should hard-fail; ladder query_rows top-level isError; audit key-propagation brittleness; no per-lane wall-clock timeout
description:
e2e harness robustness items from the 2026-07 multipass bug hunt (harness audit). Two P2 items were FIXED in this pass (scripts/e2e/run_all.sh); the rest are P3 follow-ups left as this bead.

FIXED in this pass (run_all.sh):
- Skip-accounting green-wash: skip detection grepped stderr for the scenario_complete/skipped JSON event that lib.sh only emits under E2E_LOG=1, so a skip WITHOUT --log (e.g. the version matrix with no ORACLEMCP_LIVE_XE / creds) fell through to passed++. Now checks both the JSON event AND the plain 'SKIP <scenario>:' stderr sentinel (emitted only without --log), keeping the scenario_complete filter so --dry-run's command_dry_run/skipped events do not false-match.
- Async tee flush race: '> >(tee out) 2> >(tee err >&2)' did not guarantee err was flushed before the skip grep. Replaced with plain redirects to files then cat-back after the exit code is captured (real failures were always status-based, unaffected).

REMAINING P3 follow-ups (left as this bead; NOT changed because they touch the live e2e harness and could not be re-verified end-to-end against the live lanes in this session):
(a) scripts/e2e/oracle_version_matrix.sh:133-135 - when ORACLEMCP_LIVE_XE=1 is EXPLICITLY set but a SELECTED lane lacks creds, it currently e2e_finish_skip. An explicit live opt-in should hard-fail (e2e_finish_fail) for a selected lane so a misconfigured explicit request cannot silently skip. (Whole-suite skip when LIVE_XE is unset stays correct.)
(b) oracle_version_matrix.sh run_lane (setup region ~182-206) runs with errexit OFF (the caller wraps it in set +e + a subshell), so an unchecked setup failure (mkdir/heredoc/export) would not abort the lane. Add set -e at the top of run_lane (its local set +e/set -e pairs around doctor/ladder still toggle correctly).
(c) scripts/e2e/oracle_ladder_session.py:199 query_rows reads isError from structuredContent's embedded copy; query_refused at :209 reads it from the TOP-LEVEL result. Make query_rows read the top-level result for consistency (benign today, brittle).
(d) The python audit verifier requires signature/key_id in every record, but AuditRecord skip-serializes them when unsigned; works as wired (the script exports ORACLEMCP_AUDIT_KEY) but brittle if the key does not reach the binary.
(e) No overall per-lane wall-clock timeout: a hung lane (e.g. a stuck DB) could block the matrix indefinitely; add a timeout around the ladder session.

Live-run tip from the audit: run the ladder WITH --log; the most likely spurious live failure is the test user lacking SELECT on v$version - if read_only_banner fails, GRANT SELECT ON v_$version (or SELECT_CATALOG_ROLE) to the lane user rather than weakening the assertion.
close_reason: Harness robustness landed: ladder fails with a clear message when audit records are unsigned (key-propagation), per-lane wall-clock timeout added to oracle_version_matrix.sh so a hung lane fails the lane instead of hanging run_all, plus the skip-accounting residue. Verified via ladder e2e 18/18 pass across xe18/xe21/free23.

title: Explorer object rows use tr onClick with no keyboard handler; keyboard/SR operators cannot select an object
description:
Web Interface Guidelines / accessibility finding from the dashboard review pass (independently confirmed).

web/src/app/App.tsx:5172 — Explorer object rows are a `<tr onClick={() => onSelect(row)}>` with `cursor-pointer` but no `onKeyDown`, no `tabIndex`, and no `role="button"`. Keyboard-only and screen-reader operators cannot select an Explorer object — the primary interaction of that panel is mouse-only. Every other list in Explorer (App.tsx:5006, 5038, 5096) correctly uses a `<button>` for the same select interaction, so this table is the outlier.

Trigger: tab through the Explorer objects panel; the rows are never focusable and Enter/Space does nothing.

Severity: MEDIUM (a11y keyboard access; not data-loss). Fix direction: make each row a real button (or add role=button + tabIndex=0 + onKeyDown handling Enter/Space), matching the sibling lists.
close_reason: Fixed in fd8f9b9 (web only). Explorer object rows (web/src/app/App.tsx ExplorerObjectsPanel) are now keyboard-operable: each selectable <tr> got role=button, tabIndex=0, onKeyDown activating on Enter/Space (preventDefault + onSelect), aria-pressed reflecting selection, aria-label='Select <objectName>', and a focus-visible outline ring. Matches the keyboard access the sibling <button> lists already had. Test web/src/app/explorer-a11y.test.tsx (2 SSR cases) pins the a11y wiring: role=button + tabindex=0 + aria-label + aria-pressed=false on an unselected row, aria-pressed=true on the selected row. Note: the keydown handler behavior itself is not unit-testable in this harness (vitest environment=node, no jsdom/testing-library — a dependency surface the project deliberately keeps minimal), so the test pins the static a11y attributes the handler depends on; the handler is standard Enter/Space activation. Gate: web vitest 149 passed / 7 skipped, tsc --noEmit clean, dashboard_skin_lint OK.

title: [BUG][P0] A READ_ONLY lane is frozen at the snapshot of its first read
description:
FOUND LIVE by the Arc I e2e (scripts/e2e/reversible.sh) against Oracle 23ai Free: an independent READ_ONLY MCP session never observes commits made after its first read.

MECHANISM. The A1 read-only backstop (crates/oraclemcp/src/dispatch/read_only_backstop.rs) is LAZY BY DESIGN: ensure_armed() issues ROLLBACK + SET TRANSACTION READ ONLY once and then no-ops while 'armed' (its own test pins 'issued exactly once across many reads', and its doc calls this 'repeated reads in the SAME read-only transaction pay no extra round trip'). Nothing on the pure-read path ever ends that transaction. But Oracle gives a READ ONLY transaction TRANSACTION-LEVEL read consistency: 'all queries refer to the same snapshot of the database' (Database Concepts, Read-Only Transactions). The transaction ends only at COMMIT/ROLLBACK. So a lane that only reads keeps one read-only transaction open forever and is pinned to the snapshot taken at its FIRST read.

IMPACT. Any read-only agent - the default and recommended posture, and the whole monitoring/inspection use case - sees stale data indefinitely. oracle_query, the dictionary tools, and oracle_db_health all serve the frozen snapshot. The staleness is silent: no error, no label. A write-capable lane is unaffected (its writes end the transaction), which is why the deterministic tests never caught it: they assert the statement is issued once, which is exactly the bug.

REPRO (live, 2 sessions on one DB): session W = READ_ONLY MCP lane; run oracle_query 'SELECT V FROM T WHERE ID=1' (arms the backstop). From another session, commit an UPDATE to that row. Re-run the same oracle_query in W: it still returns the OLD value, forever.

FIX SPACE (needs a deliberate call - this is a security control, so the A1 property must be re-proved by whatever lands): (a) end the read-only transaction after each read (ROLLBACK) so the next read arms a FRESH read-only transaction and a fresh snapshot - costs one extra round trip per read, which is precisely what the lazy design was avoiding; (b) keep laziness but re-arm on a staleness bound (time / on demand); (c) drop SET TRANSACTION READ ONLY for a per-statement mechanism that does not carry a snapshot. Whatever lands must keep layer B intact (a MISCLASSIFIED write must still hit ORA-01456) and must come with a live test that proves a read-only lane observes a commit made by another session.

Not an Arc I regression: the reversible workspace neither introduced nor worsened it. The Arc I e2e worked around it by making its witness session READ_WRITE-but-never-writing (statement-level consistency), with a pointer to this bead.
close_reason: Fixed the READ_ONLY snapshot freeze by starting a fresh engine-enforced READ ONLY transaction before each guarded read; added unit and two-session Oracle 23ai regression coverage. Scoped fmt, clippy, and tests passed; live Oracle credentials were unavailable locally.

title: Refresh profile docs, setup templates, and doctor for thin-supported fields
description:
## Current gap

The README, setup templates, profile examples, profile metadata, and doctor output still describe a thin profile surface that is behind the current driver capabilities. For example, the README says `os_user` and `program` remain backend-reported unless the driver exposes setters, but the current driver does expose `ClientIdentity` setters. Profile docs also need to reflect that thin edition selection is now driver-supported through `ConnectOptions::with_edition(...)`, while unsupported IAM/external auth paths remain explicit unsupported diagnostics.

## Required change

Refresh user-facing profile/config documentation and diagnostics after the supported thin-driver fields are wired.

## Must cover

- Explicit driver identity fields vs DBMS session attributes.
- Edition selection through the thin driver, including connection-time failure behavior for invalid/unauthorized editions.
- Username/password over TCPS wallet vs unsupported passwordless external/wallet auth.
- Wallet password references and TLS DN/SNI options.
- Proxy auth as supported, distinct from unsupported Kerberos/RADIUS/external auth.
- Application context triples.
- SDU and DRCP routing knobs.
- `[profiles.pool]` behavior after it is either wired or made explicitly non-operative.
- Result materialization improvements for LOB locators and REF CURSORs.

## Security requirements

- `oracle_list_profiles` remains non-secret and does not leak identity values, connect strings, usernames, credential references, wallet paths, wallet passwords, app-context values, proxy target details, or arbitrary edition names unless explicitly justified.
- `oraclemcp --json setup --profile ...` must generate safe templates with placeholders, never real local secret values.
- `doctor` should classify unsupported auth separately from malformed profile config and real connection/auth failures.

## Acceptance criteria

- README profile section is accurate for the thin-only driver.
- `oraclemcp --json setup --profile demo` includes safe examples for the newly wired fields.
- Doctor output reports actionable next steps for supported vs unsupported thin profile modes.
- Tests cover profile metadata redaction and setup template contents.
- Search for stale claims such as "driver lacks setters", unsupported edition selection, or unsupported wallet/password statements and update them.
close_reason: Refreshed thin-only profile docs, setup templates, and doctor guidance for supported fields, safe examples, redaction, pools, and LOB/REF CURSOR materialization; verified focused tests, full gates, and compliance audit 2026-06-18T10-15-36Z.

title: [CI-RECOVERY-2026-07][installer] Preserve npm dispatch outputs under GitHub Actions
description:
CI run 29208371445 fails installer_e2e::npm_publish_dispatch_validator_rejects_injection_and_maps_dist_tags on pinned and latest nightly because the test runner inherits GITHUB_OUTPUT. validate_npm_publish_input.sh then writes only to the Actions output file and suppresses stdout, so the same valid invocation has an environment-dependent diagnostic contract.

Proposed fix: validate into a single rendered payload, always emit it to stdout for direct/operator callers, and additionally append the exact payload to GITHUB_OUTPUT when present. Preserve fail-closed SemVer, auth-mode, injection, and dist-tag checks.

title: Resolve Asupersync license policy before adoption
description: cargo deny rejects asupersync 0.3.4, asupersync-macros 0.3.4, and franken-* 0.3.4 because their Cargo metadata declares LicenseRef-MIT-OpenAI-Anthropic-Rider. The LICENSE text is MIT with an OpenAI/Anthropic rider, not an SPDX-permissive license. Before W3/W4/W6a can land, the operator must choose an approved path: obtain legal/owner approval and update deny.toml with an explicit custom-license policy, use a permissively licensed fork/relicense, or change the runtime/driver plan. Do not silently whitelist this license as MIT.

title: Classifier: WITH-CTE-smuggled DML body tiered READ_ONLY (sqlparser SetExpr::Update/Delete/Merge/Insert)
description:
CONFIRMED live on READ_ONLY lane (xe18, plain TCP lab container) via oracle_preview_sql, then FIXED with tests.

SYMPTOM: On a READ_ONLY profile, oracle_preview_sql for
  WITH a AS (SELECT 1 x FROM dual) UPDATE t SET x=1
  WITH a AS (SELECT 1 x FROM dual) DELETE FROM t
  WITH a AS (SELECT 1 x FROM dual) MERGE INTO t USING a ON (1=1) WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET x=1
returned gate_decision=allow, required_level=READ_ONLY (Safe). A statement whose text carries a top-level UPDATE/DELETE/MERGE must never be tiered READ_ONLY (fail-closed invariant).

ROOT CAUSE: sqlparser 0.62 parses WITH <cte> {UPDATE|DELETE|MERGE|INSERT} ... as a single Statement::Query whose query.body is SetExpr::Update/Delete/Merge/Insert (the trailing DML is absorbed as a query body, not surfaced as Statement::Update). classify_statement's Statement::Query arm assumed the body is always a read and fell through to Safe/ReadOnly. Verified by dumping the sqlparser AST (body: Update(...) / Delete(...)). The comment at classifier.rs ~886 already noted DML SetExpr bodies exist but wrongly assumed they are 'classified separately'.

EXPLOITABILITY: none in practice — Oracle rejects the syntax with ORA-00928 (WITH cannot precede UPDATE/DELETE/MERGE), confirmed via oracle_query on the live lane (isError, error_class=SYNTAX_ERROR, no write). This is a fail-closed-SPIRIT / defense-in-depth verdict bug, not a live write bypass. The genuinely dangerous variants were already blocked: WITH FUNCTION autonomous-transaction DML (blocked), WITH cte DROP/TRUNCATE/GRANT (parse-fail -> buried/leading-verb scan -> blocked).

FIX (crates/oraclemcp-guard/src/classifier.rs): added set_expr_carries_dml() that recurses query.body through SetExpr::Query and SetExpr::SetOperation and returns true on Insert/Update/Delete/Merge. The Statement::Query arm now returns guarded_rw (Guarded/READ_WRITE) when it fires. Purely STRUCTURAL AST inspection - NOT text scanning - so it has zero false-positive surface (unlike the buried-verb scan, which would false-positive on non-reserved identifiers like purge/audit/flashback and on FOR UPDATE lock clauses). Match is exhaustive (SetExpr is not non_exhaustive) so a future sqlparser bump that adds a body variant breaks the build and forces review.

TESTS added: cte_smuggled_dml_body_is_never_read_only (4 variants -> not Safe, not READ_ONLY); legitimate_cte_reads_stay_safe_after_dml_body_tightening (legit CTE read, SELECT purge/audit/flashback identifiers, UNION ALL body stay Safe; SELECT ... FOR UPDATE stays Guarded/READ_WRITE not over-tightened to Forbidden). Post-fix live re-verify: WITH cte UPDATE now blocked/READ_WRITE; legit CTE read still allow/READ_ONLY. cargo test -p oraclemcp-guard green (93 lib tests), fmt+clippy clean.
close_reason: Fixed in classifier.rs (set_expr_carries_dml structural guard in Statement::Query arm) + 2 tests; verified live on xe18 READ_ONLY lane pre/post; guard suite + fmt + clippy green

title: [P1][oci-harness] wallet SSL_SERVER_CERT_DN extraction fails on real free-tier ADB wallet
description: OCI-live re-run 21:29: provision+wallet-download+DESTROY all PASSED (zero cost, ADB destroyed), but the harness FAILED extracting SSL_SERVER_CERT_DN from the downloaded ADB wallet ('downloaded ADB wallet has no SSL_SERVER_CERT_DN'), so the TCPS probe could not configure ssl_server_dn_match. This is a HARNESS wallet-parsing bug, NOT the driver's OCI code nor creds. Modern ADB wallets carry the DN in tnsnames.ora (ssl_server_cert_dn / ssl_server_dn_match=... in the connect descriptor) and/or sqlnet.ora, not always as a separate metadata field. FIX: extract the server cert DN from the wallet's tnsnames.ora connect descriptor (parse ssl_server_cert_dn="...") with a fallback, and only require it when the probe needs strict DN match; verify against the wallet fixtures + a live re-run. Keep the exit-trap destroy + free-tier guard intact. Do NOT apply to cloud yourself; fix + validate + wallet-fixture test only.
close_reason: Fixed by 198f00c: parse explicit wallet SSL_SERVER_CERT_DN with sqlnet fallback; modern no-literal wallets keep strict host/SAN matching. Fixture and Terraform harness checks pass.

title: ci_taxonomy flags _quality.yml, a non-workflow that yg4x.2 parks in .github/workflows/
design: Correct and supersede the prior triggerless-retention close after the retro observed eleven no-job failures, then move _quality.yml outside .github/workflows and update the fail-closed parser and tests atomically.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The original close is explicitly corrected, GitHub workflow discovery no longer sees the projection, and required-local parsing still fails closed on missing or drifted data.
notes:
SCOPE ESCALATED — this is CROSS-REPO, do not fix it from one repo. New evidence since filing:

(1) ALL THREE implicated files are MIRRORED in rust-oracledb: .github/workflows/_quality.yml, scripts/ci_taxonomy.py, scripts/verify_required_local.py. Both candidate fixes therefore change a mirrored contract, so this needs the .2/.8 owners plus whoever owns the mirror (GoldenGorge). NOTE: _quality.yml already DIFFERS between the two repos (cmp), so the mirror has drifted already — worth checking whether that drift is intentional before anyone edits either copy.

(2) I RETRACT the 'check the Actions tab' suggestion from my earlier note as currently untestable: _quality.yml is NOT PUSHED. origin/main does not contain it and there are 45 unpushed commits. 'gh workflow list' shows 7 registered workflows (ci/docker/kani-safety/mutation-safety/publish-mcp/publish-npm/release) and _quality.yml is absent — but that proves NOTHING, because GitHub has never seen the file. Whether GitHub reports 'Invalid workflow file' for an on:-less yml in .github/workflows/ stays UNVERIFIED until the orchestrator pushes. Do not treat its absence from the list as evidence it is ignored. Re-check after the next push: if GitHub does flag it, option (a) becomes clearly correct rather than merely tidier.

(3) OPTION ANALYSIS, refined. (a) move _quality.yml out of .github/workflows/ and re-point verify_required_local.py: fixes the taxonomy false positive AND any invalid-workflow risk, but changes a mirrored path in BOTH repos. (b) teach ci_taxonomy that a workflow is a file GitHub would actually run (has an 'on:' trigger): contained and arguably the more honest definition, BUT it silently stops flagging a REAL workflow that lost its 'on:' by mistake — such a file is dead and should be reported as 'not runnable', not skipped; so (b) done naively trades one blind spot for another. Prefer (b) implemented as a distinct 'not a runnable workflow' classification rather than a skip. (c) add timeout-minutes/permissions to _quality.yml: still recommend AGAINST — it greens the gate while asserting nothing true about a file GitHub never runs.

STATUS: returning to open, unclaimed. I hold no lock on it and I have not edited either tool. Everything needed to decide is in this note; the decision is the owners'.

title: Codex: independent Round-2 verification of PLAN_0_4_0_PRODUCTION_HARDENING.md
description:
Independent Round-2 VERIFICATION of /home/durakovic/projects/oraclemcp/PLAN_0_4_0_PRODUCTION_HARDENING.md (the oraclemcp 0.4.0 production-hardening plan). Handed off from the Claude planning session.

WHY: The plan went through a 4-agent Claude review (Round 1) + cross-validation vs rust-oracledb/docs/ROAD_TO_1_0.md v3.1 and the asupersync skill (Rounds 1.2/1.3), all logged in the plan's section 14 ledger. DO NOT TRUST those rounds — verify INDEPENDENTLY with fresh eyes against the actual sources. Be adversarial; do not rubber-stamp.

SOURCES (cite file:line):
- oraclemcp crates: dispatch/mod.rs, registry.rs, oraclemcp-db/src/{connection,pool,lease,query,awr,auth_adapter}.rs, oraclemcp-audit/src/*, oraclemcp-guard/src/levels.rs, oraclemcp-core/src/{server,http,connect}.rs, oraclemcp-telemetry/src/*.
- /home/durakovic/projects/rust-oracledb/docs/ROAD_TO_1_0.md: ADR-0001/0002/0003; W1-T3 (four operation-specific request types, NOT a mega-builder); W1-T5 ProtocolLimits; W1-T6 typed errors + connection_disposition() + redacted observability; W2-T1 (oracledb 0.3.0 migration names oraclemcp); W3-E3 DPOR; W3-E7 direct oraclemcp contract suite; R3/R10 (cargo public-api + cargo-semver-checks run under nightly-2026-05-11); verified baseline counts.
- asupersync 0.3.4 (~/.cargo/registry/src/*/asupersync-0.3.4): observability::otel OtlpHttpExporter (Tokio-free?); cap model + SubsetOf; Budget/Outcome; LabRuntime/DporExplorer; net surface maturity (lane-specific?).
- /home/durakovic/projects/plsql-intelligence: plsql-mcp deps core/error/guard@0.1.0 only; own plsql_catalog::OracleConnection.

VERIFY SPECIFICALLY:
1. Section 2 oracledb-0.3.0 reframe: cut-over + oraclemcp first-party migration land at oracledb 0.3.0/W2-T1; ADR-0002 turns on a blocking cargo-semver-checks gate at 0.3.0 so oraclemcp can pin ^0.3 (additive-only after).
2. "Consume, don't rebuild": typed errors / connection_disposition() / ProtocolLimits+ResourceLimit / redacted driver metrics are actually in oracledb's plan as the plan claims.
3. B5: cargo public-api AND cargo-semver-checks genuinely run under the pinned nightly (R3/R10).
4. A8 (CRITICAL): the served oracle_execute/execute_approved path is genuinely UN-audited today (binary has no oraclemcp-audit dep; oracle_query_execute not in TOOL_NAMES; record.rs hash is unsigned SHA-256).
5. A9: asupersync cap/SubsetOf compile-time capability narrowing is real and usable as described.
6. A1 level-aware read-only backstop semantics; B1 full async migration realism (drop BlockingConnection); B6 Budget.
7. D1 OTLP via asupersync `metrics` feature is Tokio-free; the OUTBOUND HTTP client maturity risk is real.
8. Any residual contradiction, overclaim, wrong assumption, scope/dependency error, or place the dual-"0.3.0" (oraclemcp vs oracledb) is ambiguous.
9. Bead-readiness: is each WP (A-F) self-contained enough to convert to beads via `br` with clean dependency edges?

DELIVER: Append findings to the plan's section 14 as a "Round 2 - Codex" block (ID | severity | location | issue | evidence(file:line) | proposed fix). Apply fixes that are clearly correct by editing PLAN_0_4_0_PRODUCTION_HARDENING.md in place; flag judgment calls for the operator. End with a GO / NO-GO verdict for bead conversion. Do NOT commit or push without explicit operator go-ahead (AGENTS.md).
close_reason: Codex Round-2 review complete (GO); folded into plan §14

title: [CI-RECOVERY-2026-07][semver] Advance breaking guard API line to 0.9.0
description: Fresh CI run 29208371445 reports cargo-semver-checks breaking changes against published oraclemcp-guard 0.8.0: GuardDecision gained public non_transactional_effect and query_effect_requires_fetch fields. These are required safety metadata and cannot be removed or hidden without another construction break. Under pre-1.0 SemVer, the honest repair is a 0.9.0 workspace/release-surface candidate, not an allowlist. Bump every workspace/internal pin and release-visible surface in lockstep, add a 0.9.0 changelog entry explaining the break, regenerate dashboard lock/SBOM, run release-surface/preflight, semver checks, full gates. Do not tag or publish without explicit operator authorization; driver remains at newest published exact pin until rust-oracledb 0.8.3 is published.
close_reason: 0.9.0 release preparation revalidated after CI-driven nested golden and README install-surface corrections; all release surfaces, semver, preflight, golden 9/9, and installer 12/12 now pass locally. No tag or publish performed.

The driver half is plan sec.25.2 P0.1-P0.4 and is NOT covered by any oraclemcp bead: split _quality.yml into a parallel job matrix with fail-fast false across all four callers (required/canary/soak/release-qualification), keeping verify_required_local.py's shape contract in the same change; shard fuzz 4 ways and cache the ASan build, adding crates/oracledb-protocol/fuzz to the rust-cache workspaces; stop running the suite twice by dropping 'needs: release-qualification' from emit-required-proof or assembling the proof from per-job evidence; take the live matrix off the synchronous critical path by consuming version-matrix.yml's exact-SHA results artifact and converting release_matrix_gate.sh's serial 5-lane loop into a job matrix. This belongs to whoever owns rust-oracledb; filed here only so the cluster's 'both repos' scope is not silently dropped when the server side closes.

comments.author: durakovic

Current committed state already landed most of this tranche: ab8abc4 splits _quality.yml into validate/core/contracts/features/release-surface/musl/perf/fuzz fan-out plus a fail-closed aggregate; 4f1eb3 adds four fail-fast=false fuzz shards, ASan-cache workspace crates/oracledb-protocol/fuzz -> target, and shape-contract validation in verify_required_local.py.
