title: connection.rs stays a single-file driver seam (justified monolith) — leave alone
description: De-monolith loop Pass 8 (2026-07-03): crates/oraclemcp-db/src/connection.rs (4,751 lines) was evaluated and correctly LEFT INTACT. It is a deliberately single-file Oracle driver adapter, mechanically ENFORCED by three independent locks: (1) scripts/oraclemcp_driver_seam_lint.sh hardcodes ADAPTER_ALLOWLIST to exactly the single path crates/oraclemcp-db/src/connection.rs (any sibling with an oracledb:: path fails it); (2) the in-file test pin_is_0_5_1_and_seam_intact asserts that allowlist const equals exactly that one path; (3) all three test modules (tests, lob_tests, driver_seam) embed real oracledb:: driver calls, so none can move. git mv connection.rs to connection/mod.rs is therefore forbidden (path would stop matching the allowlist). The only driver-free block is the structured-decode subsystem (~680 lines, ~1501-2183) but it pattern-matches five non_exhaustive driver value enums; extracting it would erode the documented 'driver value types live in one file' invariant and is a deliberate maintainer decision, NOT a mechanical behavior-identical refactor. Do not attempt an autonomous split. If ever revisited: it requires relaxing the seam convention + moving its lob_tests decode tests + updating the seam doc together, as a reviewed change.
close_reason: Decision record — connection.rs stays a single-file driver seam, correctly LEFT INTACT. Mechanically enforced by three independent locks: driver-seam lint ADAPTER_ALLOWLIST=exactly that one path, the in-file pin-contract test asserting the allowlist const, and all three test modules embedding real oracledb:: calls so none can move. Not an autonomous split (would erode the documented 'driver value types live in one file' invariant); any future revisit is a reviewed change relaxing the seam convention. No action needed — closing as acknowledged.

title: De-monolithize oraclemcp-core/src/http.rs (11.5k B1 god-file) — coordinated, review-gated
description:
De-monolith PLAN from the 2026-07-02 isomorphic-refactor loop (Pass 5, plan-only outcome). http.rs is 11,495 lines and is the HTTP security surface (auth/OAuth/mTLS/dashboard pairing/CSRF/operator protocol/SSE). No module-level statics (no B5 shared-state entanglement), so per-cluster splits are mechanically safe.

BLOCKER (why this is NOT a conservative autonomous change): http.rs's source PATH and source TEXT are part of the tested contract. Splitting requires http.rs to become http/mod.rs, which breaks: concurrency_contract.rs:276 (include_str! of src/http.rs, compile-time), dashboard_e2e.rs (8 runtime read_repo_file sites scanning the source text for security substrings), plus path-pins in tests/conformance/COVERAGE.md, scripts/e2e/hardening_acceptance.sh:70, docs/threat-model.md, docs/behavior-inventory.md. Any autonomous pass forbids touching tests/, so this needs its own reviewed bead where updating those references is in scope.

STEP 1 (prerequisite, unblocks all): atomically convert http.rs -> http/mod.rs AND update every path-pin above (include_str! path, the 8 read_repo_file sites, the script, COVERAGE.md, the two docs). Reviewed; crosses tests/.

THEN per-cluster facade-preserving extractions (mod.rs pub(crate) use so oraclemcp_core::http::X still resolves; one git mv-friendly move per commit; full-workspace build+test+goldens gate each):
2. request_target.rs (lines 6714-6773: split_request_target/parse_query_string/percent_decode_query — PROVEN clean leaf, zero back-refs, 1 caller).
3. serve.rs (11038-11495: serve_http*/handle_connection/read_http_request/write_http_response — leaf-ish).
4. sse.rs (10726-11000: HttpSseStream/sse_response + chunk writers).
5. config.rs (80-535: HttpTransportConfig + rate-limit).
6. stores.rs (542-1116: session/result/event/idempotency stores).
7. operator/ subtree (~558-793 + 7572-10169, ~2600 LOC: /operator/v1 handlers — largest, most coupled, do last).
8. tests.rs (1122-6567, the inline 5.4k-line test module — B9; keep scanned security substrings where the source-scanning tests expect them, or update scanner expectations in the same bead).

Each extraction bead needs a proof-gate dep (cargo build+test --workspace same pass count, goldens byte-identical, clippy no-grow) and a docs dep (module //! docs). Do NOT weaken any auth/CSRF/pairing logic; forbid(unsafe).
close_reason: Verified after e96b1b3 repaired the split test-source contract. HEAD contains the isomorphic migration/extractions 09d9c4b, 54cd83b, 6213b15, a2ed5f2, ecde279, 12fb3c2, 43ba7ca, ddc64c3, and e96b1b3: original 11,495-line http.rs is now a 2,348-line http/mod.rs facade with serve (657), config (475), stores (715), operator (3,794), SSE (594), leaf modules, and a 833-line test facade plus eight flat seams (~9.8k test LOC). The facade preserves the moved public re-exports. No test was lost: pre-ddc64c3 parity is exact at 132 HTTP test attributes and 132 test names; the restored compile-time contract scans every seam and B13-LANE-007 passes. Scoped fmt and clippy for oraclemcp-core passed; the full -p oraclemcp-core suite passed (622 unit tests plus all integration/compile-fail/doc suites); -p oraclemcp --test dashboard_e2e passed 12/12, retaining auth, CSRF, TLS, route-precedence, and fail-closed source contracts. Per the shared-tree Charter, the workspace-wide quiet-tree gate is orchestrator-owned and is not falsely claimed from this dirty tree.

title: B6a: extract serve/listen/bind lifecycle from http/mod.rs
description:
De-monolithize oraclemcp-core/src/http/mod.rs (5.9k LOC). CONTEXT: the risky atomic rename + path-contract migration is ALREADY DONE (http.rs -> http/mod.rs, with request_target.rs / sse_writer.rs / wire.rs / tests.rs already split out). This bead extracts ONE further module from the remaining mod.rs. Each extraction is gated on build + test + BYTE-IDENTICAL goldens + clippy-no-grow + forbid(unsafe), and MUST preserve the tested path/text contract (include_str!, dashboard_e2e.rs scanning, the threat-model / behavior-inventory pins). Coordinate with B3/B4 HTTP edits to avoid churn. repo: oraclemcp.

MODULE: extract the serve/listen/bind lifecycle (socket bind, listener setup, graceful shutdown, the serve loop) into http/serve.rs. TEST/DoD: goldens byte-identical; dashboard_e2e.rs pairing flow green; clippy no-grow; the loopback/allow_remote bind contract (B3.2) unchanged; detailed logging on the extraction-verification run.
close_reason:
Extracted the serve/listen/bind lifecycle into crates/oraclemcp-core/src/http/serve.rs (657 lines): serve_http/serve_http_until/serve_https/serve_https_until/serve_control_https_until, the accept loops, reap_finished_workers, listener_config, close_stateful_sessions_for_shutdown, close_http_principal_sessions, reap_idle_stateful_sessions, handle_connection/handle_tls_connection/handle_control_tls_connection, complete_tls_handshake + its deadline helpers, handle_stream, the two DeadlineRead impls, and the connection/TLS/ingress timeout consts. http/mod.rs 8323 -> 7734 lines.

ISOMORPHISM PROVED (not asserted): (1) BEHAVIOR - 683 passed / 0 failed / 1 ignored in -p oraclemcp-core, with PER-BINARY pass counts diffed identical against the pre-change baseline; (2) PUBLIC API - mod.rs pub-uses the six moved pub fns so oraclemcp_core::http::serve_http and the whole lib.rs re-export list resolve unchanged, and service_app.rs (the real consumer) builds untouched; (3) SOURCE-TEXT CONTRACT - dashboard_e2e's security-substring scanner globs http/*.rs (12/12 pass), concurrency_contract's include_str! targets http/tests.rs (untouched), hardening_acceptance.sh only requires http/mod.rs to exist; (4) CODEGEN - a same-crate module move is pure namespacing: no new indirection/dynamic dispatch, and handle_stream stays generic over impl DeadlineRead + Write, still called intra-crate; (5) clippy -p oraclemcp-core --all-targets -D warnings clean, forbid(unsafe) untouched.

Move was VERBATIM: a mechanical diff of the moved code against the original region shows the only differences are the new import header, the timeout consts relocated to the top of the file, rustfmt re-wrapping two signatures that pub(super) pushed past 100 cols, and 3 pub(super) visibility markers for items the inline test module reaches via 'use super::*'. tests.rs was NOT edited (it is the contract); the four imports whose production users moved are retained under #[cfg(test)] in mod.rs instead.

NO FAIL-CLOSED DEFAULT WEAKENED: transport permit still taken before a worker spawns; the dedicated control ingress still completes certificate verification + fingerprint registration + operator authorization BEFORE any HTTP byte is parsed and only then migrates from the separately bounded pre-auth handshake ledger into the authenticated control reserve; every ingress deadline (TLS handshake / request header / request body, incl. the shorter control-probe windows) unchanged. Landed 6213b15.

title: B6b: extract HTTP config assembly from http/mod.rs
description:
De-monolithize oraclemcp-core/src/http/mod.rs (5.9k LOC). CONTEXT: the risky atomic rename + path-contract migration is ALREADY DONE (http.rs -> http/mod.rs, with request_target.rs / sse_writer.rs / wire.rs / tests.rs already split out). This bead extracts ONE further module from the remaining mod.rs. Each extraction is gated on build + test + BYTE-IDENTICAL goldens + clippy-no-grow + forbid(unsafe), and MUST preserve the tested path/text contract (include_str!, dashboard_e2e.rs scanning, the threat-model / behavior-inventory pins). Coordinate with B3/B4 HTTP edits to avoid churn. repo: oraclemcp.

MODULE: extract the HTTP config assembly (HttpConfig wiring, CSP/header setup, TLS/pairing config) into http/config.rs. TEST/DoD: goldens byte-identical; the strict CSP at the tested line contract holds (http/mod.rs:2027-2029); clippy no-grow; detailed logging.
close_reason:
Extracted the HTTP transport configuration into crates/oraclemcp-core/src/http/config.rs (475 lines): HttpTransportConfig (+ its Debug/Default), MtlsClientRegistry, ObservabilityState + ReadinessProbe, EffectiveHttpScheme, the request-rate limiter cluster (HttpRequestRateLimitConfig / HttpRequestRateBuckets / HttpRequestRateLimiters / HttpRequestRateLimitRejection) with its three public DEFAULT_HTTP_REQUEST_RATE_* constants, and default_transport_admission/default_sse_admission. http/mod.rs 7734 -> 7288.

ISOMORPHISM PROVED: (1) BEHAVIOR - 683 passed / 0 failed / 1 ignored, per-BINARY pass counts diffed identical to the pre-refactor baseline; (2) PUBLIC API - a mechanical diff of every 'pub' item across the module before/after shows NONE lost and NONE added; mod.rs pub-uses the ten moved public names so oraclemcp_core::http::HttpTransportConfig et al resolve unchanged; (3) THE DoD's CSP CONTRACT - dashboard_csp() and the response path that emits the strict CSP header were not touched (they are request-path, not config); (4) SOURCE-TEXT CONTRACT - dashboard_e2e's scanner globs http/*.rs: 12/12 pass; (5) clippy -p oraclemcp-core --all-targets -D warnings clean.

Six items private *within one file* became pub(super) because a module wall now separates them from the parent that still calls them (EffectiveHttpScheme::is_https, MtlsClientRegistry::principal_key_for_fingerprint, HttpRequestRateLimiters::{try_admit_at,bucket_count,metric_bucket_names}, and HttpRequestRateLimitRejection + its fields). pub(super) reproduces exactly their existing reach and keeps them invisible outside 'http' - which is why the public-API diff is empty. tests.rs was NOT edited.

NO FAIL-CLOSED DEFAULT WEAKENED - they moved intact and are now stated in one place: stateful off, allow_remote off, OAuth enforcement absent unless configured, an EMPTY mTLS fingerprint registry (an unregistered client cert never becomes a principal), and the limiter's bounded resident-bucket cap. Module opens with 'use super::*' (the inline test module's own idiom) so the moved code resolves every name in exactly the scope it was written in - HttpTransportConfig's field types alone reach 30+ names, and hand-deriving that import list is precisely how an 'isomorphic' move silently rebinds a type. Landed a2ed5f2.

title: B6c: extract file-backed stores from http/mod.rs
description:
De-monolithize oraclemcp-core/src/http/mod.rs (5.9k LOC). CONTEXT: the risky atomic rename + path-contract migration is ALREADY DONE (http.rs -> http/mod.rs, with request_target.rs / sse_writer.rs / wire.rs / tests.rs already split out). This bead extracts ONE further module from the remaining mod.rs. Each extraction is gated on build + test + BYTE-IDENTICAL goldens + clippy-no-grow + forbid(unsafe), and MUST preserve the tested path/text contract (include_str!, dashboard_e2e.rs scanning, the threat-model / behavior-inventory pins). Coordinate with B3/B4 HTTP edits to avoid churn. repo: oraclemcp.

MODULE: extract the file-backed stores (proposals / snapshots / audit-tail readers, the files-first persistence access) into http/stores.rs. TEST/DoD: goldens byte-identical; no DB introduced (files-first invariant); clippy no-grow; detailed logging.
close_reason:
Extracted the transport's two stores into crates/oraclemcp-core/src/http/stores.rs (715 lines): HttpSessionStore (stateful mcp-session-id table, principal binding, global + per-principal caps, idle reaping) and HttpResultStore (the bounded per-session replay ring behind Streamable HTTP GET, cursor/Last-Event-ID resume, typed stream_cursor_expired refusal), with their limits/entries/buffered+retained events and helpers. http/mod.rs 7288 -> 6615.

ISOMORPHISM PROVED: 683 passed / 0 failed / 1 ignored with PER-BINARY counts diffed identical to baseline; mechanical diff of every 'pub' item shows NONE lost, NONE added; dashboard_e2e source-text scanner 12/12; clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean.

SCOPE HONESTY: the bead says 'file-backed stores (proposals/snapshots/audit-tail)'. That does not match the code - http/mod.rs never held those. The file-backed persistence lives in its own crate modules (change_proposal.rs, source_history.rs, file_store.rs) and is reached FROM the /operator/v1 route handlers, i.e. bead .4's scope. What http/mod.rs actually held is the session/result store cluster, which is exactly what the parent epic's plan assigns to stores.rs (orig. lines 542-1116), and that is what I extracted. FILES-FIRST INVARIANT HELD trivially and is now easier to audit: both stores are in-memory by construction, the module opens no file and imports no database (grep for sqlite/rusqlite/redb/sqlx/diesel = 0 hits).

Every fail-closed bound moved unchanged and is now stated in one place: global + per-principal stateful-session ceilings with retry_after_ms, and the four buffered-event ceilings (per-session event count, per-event bytes, per-session bytes, global byte budget) that stop one client consuming the whole transport's replay memory; an aged-out cursor still returns a typed refusal rather than silently replaying a truncated suffix.

Methods/helper types that were private *within one file* became pub(super) because a module wall now separates them from the parent that still calls them; pub(super) reproduces their exact reach and keeps them invisible outside 'http' (HttpSessionStore/HttpResultStore are publicly re-exported types whose methods were, and remain, unreachable from outside) - hence the empty public-API diff. tests.rs was NOT edited; the two names only it needs are imported under #[cfg(test)]. Landed ecde279.

title: B6d: extract /operator/v1 route handlers from http/mod.rs
description:
De-monolithize oraclemcp-core/src/http/mod.rs (5.9k LOC). CONTEXT: the risky atomic rename + path-contract migration is ALREADY DONE (http.rs -> http/mod.rs, with request_target.rs / sse_writer.rs / wire.rs / tests.rs already split out). This bead extracts ONE further module from the remaining mod.rs. Each extraction is gated on build + test + BYTE-IDENTICAL goldens + clippy-no-grow + forbid(unsafe), and MUST preserve the tested path/text contract (include_str!, dashboard_e2e.rs scanning, the threat-model / behavior-inventory pins). Coordinate with B3/B4 HTTP edits to avoid churn. repo: oraclemcp.

MODULE: extract the /operator/v1 route handlers (health, schema, active-lanes, events(SSE), session/set-level, change-proposals, source-history, audit-tail) into http/operator/. TEST/DoD: every /operator/v1 route contract-tested unchanged (feeds b4-contract-tests); goldens byte-identical; Subject still derived server-side (never browser-supplied); clippy no-grow; detailed logging.
close_reason:
Extracted the entire /operator/v1 surface into crates/oraclemcp-core/src/http/operator.rs (3773 lines): the route table + every handler (health, metrics, capacity, active-lanes + lane-cancel, vsession, config, change-proposals incl. source-snapshot capture, schema-diff, source-history, client-credentials, audit-tail with redaction + proof bundle, classifier verdicts, events/SSE replay, guarded action routes), the hash-chained audit bracketing each route, the idempotency ledger, and the per-subject/per-lane event store. http/mod.rs 6615 -> 2904 (from 8323 at the start of this bead series, and from the 11,495-line god-file the epic opened against).

ISOMORPHISM PROVED: (1) BEHAVIOR - 683 passed / 0 failed / 1 ignored, PER-BINARY counts diffed identical to the pre-refactor baseline; operator-protocol conformance 24/24; (2) PUBLIC API - mechanical diff of every 'pub' item: NONE lost, NONE added; OperatorEventStore/OperatorIdempotencyLedger re-exported from mod.rs so the lib.rs list resolves unchanged; (3) SOURCE-TEXT CONTRACT - verified DIRECTLY by replicating dashboard_e2e::read_http_source (non-recursive read_dir over http/*.rs) and checking all 52 security substrings it pins: every one is still inside http/*.rs; (4) clippy -p oraclemcp-core --all-targets -D warnings clean.

DEVIATION FROM THE BEAD, deliberately: the bead says 'into http/operator/'. I used a FLAT http/operator.rs because that scanner's read_dir does NOT recurse - a subdirectory would have silently escaped the very contract the bead's own DoD requires me to preserve, turning a security scan into a no-op that still passes. A flat module keeps the operator source in the scanner's view.

NO SECURITY PROPERTY RELAXED: Subject still server-derived, never browser-supplied; operator authority still a config allow-list checked above the subject, failing closed on a non-allow-listed route; audit still brackets every route and a broken audit sink still fails the request closed; guarded actions still forward through MCP tools/call, so they meet the same fail-closed classifier and operating-level gate as any agent request. Items crossing the new module wall are pub(super) - the exact reach they already had, invisible outside http, hence the empty public-API diff. tests.rs was NOT edited; names only it needs are imported under #[cfg(test)].

NOTE for the orchestrator: the oraclemcp crate (not mine) is currently mid-edit by another agent and does not compile (E0004, non-exhaustive match), so its dashboard_e2e test binary could not be RUN; the substring check above is an independent, equivalent proof that does not depend on that crate. Landed 12fb3c2.

title: [P1] Restore compile-time HTTP test-contract coverage after seam split
description: The ddc64c3 test-seam split moved stateful_idle_reaper_closes_by_timeout_and_clears_buffers from http/tests.rs to http/tests_stores.rs, but crates/oraclemcp-core/tests/concurrency_contract.rs still defines the http_runtime artifact as include_str!(src/http/tests.rs). At HEAD, omcpb test -p oraclemcp-core runs 622 unit tests plus the compile-fail and chaos suites, then fails concurrency_contract::wp_n_edge_negative_catalog_names_every_b13_proof because B13-LANE-007 cannot find that named proof. Restore the compile-time source contract so it covers the facade and all eight flat test seams (or preserve the required names in the facade) without weakening or deleting the B13 catalog; keep the non-recursive dashboard scanner contract intact. close_reason: Landed e96b1b3. Restored http_runtime's compile-time source contract to concatenate the tests.rs facade plus all eight flat test seams; B13-LANE-007 is found without weakening, deleting, or renaming the assertion. Verified omcpb fmt -p oraclemcp-core -- --check, clippy -p oraclemcp-core --all-targets -- -D warnings, and test -p oraclemcp-core green (622 unit tests plus compile-fail, chaos, concurrency-contract, integration, and doc suites). Pre-ddc64c3 parity is exact: 132 HTTP test attributes and 132 test names before/after.

title: Materialize generic LOB locator cells with thin driver reads
description:
## Current gap

`rust-oracledb` already exposes public LOB locator reads through `Connection::read_lob` / blocking `read_lob`, and oraclemcp already has capped LOB-reading logic for explicit `oracle_read_clob` style workflows. Generic query serialization still emits placeholders for `QueryValue::Lob` in `crates/oraclemcp-db/src/connection.rs`:

`<unsupported LOB locator size=...>`

That makes ordinary `SELECT clob_col FROM ...` or PL/SQL output returning LOB locators less useful to agents even though the driver can read the locator.

## Required change

Materialize LOB locator cells during generic query/result conversion when the driver returns `QueryValue::Lob`, using the driver's public locator-read API and the existing oraclemcp caps/serialization policy.

## Safety requirements

- Preserve hard caps for text characters and binary bytes.
- Preserve type-specific output: CLOB/NCLOB as capped text, BLOB/BFILE as capped binary/base64 or existing binary representation, with truncation metadata.
- Do not read unbounded LOBs by default.
- Respect call timeout/cancellation handling where available.
- Keep errors structured: unsupported LOB subtype or failed locator read should be explicit, not silently replaced with null.

## Acceptance criteria

- Generic query rows no longer emit unsupported placeholders for supported LOB locators.
- Existing explicit LOB tools keep working.
- Unit tests cover CLOB, BLOB, null LOB, truncation, and locator-read failure paths.
- Live 23ai/FREE tests cover selecting CLOB/BLOB values through `oracle_query` with caps.
- Performance is bounded: tests or benchmarks prove a large LOB cannot force unbounded allocation.
close_reason: Implemented bounded generic LOB locator materialization for CLOB/BLOB/BFILE, wired oracle_query serialization caps through the backend, added unit tests and bound live 23ai coverage; gates, full workspace tests, cargo deny, and single-bead audit passed.

title: [P1][cqn] public EMON receiver opening bypasses subscription caps
description:
Confirmed CQN subscription-cap bypass.

After one privileged CqnRegistrationGate::register_query succeeds, the public CqnRegisteredQuery::open_emon_receiver(&self, cx, connection) may be called repeatedly. It takes no SubscriptionRegistry, owner, URI, reservation token, or cap proof. Each invocation directly calls OracleConnection::open_cqn_notification_receiver and can create another independently authenticated EMON connection.

The C1.3 registry's documented invariant is that every admitted owner/URI pair consumes one EMON connection and total active connections never exceed the profile/database ceiling. That invariant is not enforced at the actual EMON effect point: a caller can register once, skip SubscriptionRegistry::subscribe, and open unbounded receivers. This is an availability/resource-governance fail-open, not a cosmetic API concern.

Acceptance: make an atomically acquired registry reservation (or opaque capability) mandatory for each EMON open, bind it to the server-derived owner and registered query/URI, and release it exactly once on receiver teardown/failure. Add a regression that attempts open_emon_receiver without admission and then beyond both principal and DB ceilings; no driver receiver call may occur.
close_reason: Closed P1 CQN EMON cap bypass: every receiver open now requires an atomically acquired registry reservation bound to the server-derived owner, registered URI, and query identity; reservations release on driver failure and receiver teardown. Regression covers no-admission, principal/DB caps, N+1 refusal, and release.

title: FileStore JSONL append and tail-recovery follow links (same class as siry)
description:
[HIGH] Found by fresh-eyes review immediately after fixing siry: siry hardened .service.lock, but the SAME link-following class survives on the FileStore DATA path, one function over in the same file.

TWO SITES (crates/oraclemcp-core/src/file_store.rs):
1. append_jsonl -> open_append_private_file: OpenOptions.append(true).create(true) FOLLOWS an existing link. Path segments are validated (no traversal), but a same-UID actor who can write the state dir can pre-plant <root>/<collection>/<id>.jsonl as a symlink (or hard link) to any file this UID can write, and the store then APPENDS to it. This is the audit / write-intent / metrics append path, so it is both an arbitrary-append primitive AND a way to divert the audit chain off its own file.
2. recover_jsonl_tail (the OpenOptions::new().read.write.open(&path) near the repair block): same follow, and it then calls set_len(valid_len) — an arbitrary file-shortening primitive.

WHY nlink==1 IS SAFE FOR DATA FILES (checked, not assumed): no production code hard-links store files (only tests do); store files are created via create_new_private_file (O_EXCL, nlink 1); and service restore ALREADY refuses a hard-linked bundle (ORACLEMCP_SERVICE_RESTORE_INVALID_BACKUP). The house posture already treats a hard link here as invalid.

FIX (reuse the siry pattern, do not reinvent): O_NOFOLLOW on both opens so a symlinked target is refused IN THE OPEN (no inspect-then-open window), then authenticate the OPEN DESCRIPTOR via fstat (file.metadata(), never the path) for is_file() + nlink()==1. Generalize siry's authenticate_lock_file into one shared authenticate_private_file(file, path). Refuse as UnsafePath, not Io — ELOOP is an attack signal. OpenOptionsExt::custom_flags is a safe API so forbid(unsafe_code) holds; libc is already a unix-gated dep of this crate after siry.

TESTS: mirror siry's discipline — assert the VICTIM'S BYTES SURVIVE, not merely that an error came back (refusing while still appending or shortening would be a passing test and a live bug). Cover symlinked append, hard-linked append, and symlinked tail-recovery. Mutation-verify: reverting the defence must make them fail.

DO NOT WEAKEN: append_jsonl's created/fsync_dir semantics, the torn-tail repair contract, or the InvalidJsonlRecord line-terminator guard.
close_reason:
FIXED in 2942e7c. Same class as siry, found by reviewing the siry fix itself.

BOTH SITES HARDENED (crates/oraclemcp-core/src/file_store.rs):
1. append_jsonl / open_append_private_file — appended service records (audit chain, write-intents, metrics) through a planted link into an attacker-chosen file.
2. recover_jsonl — followed a link then set_len(valid_len), an arbitrary file-shortening primitive.

FIX: O_NOFOLLOW refuses a symlinked target IN THE OPEN (no inspect-then-open window), then the OPEN DESCRIPTOR is authenticated by fstat (file.metadata(), never the path) for is_file() + nlink()==1. siry's authenticate_lock_file/lock_open_error were GENERALIZED to authenticate_private_file/private_open_error, so all THREE sites (lock + append + recovery) now share ONE contract instead of three hand-rolled ones. 3x O_NOFOLLOW + 3x authenticate, verified by grep.

nlink==1 CHECKED, NOT ASSUMED, for data files: no production code hard-links store files (only tests), store files are created via create_new_private_file (O_EXCL, nlink 1), and service restore already refuses a hard-linked bundle (ORACLEMCP_SERVICE_RESTORE_INVALID_BACKUP). The house posture already treats a hard link here as invalid, so requiring nlink==1 breaks nothing.

TESTS (3 new, unix-gated): symlinked append, hard-linked append, symlinked tail-recovery. Each asserts the VICTIM'S BYTES SURVIVE, not merely that an error was returned.

MUTATION-VERIFIED (and the first attempt caught my own sloppiness): my initial mutation was imprecise — it stripped O_NOFOLLOW from the LOCK opener (byte-identical text) instead of the APPEND opener, so the symlink-append test passed and looked non-load-bearing. Re-mutated precisely against the unique  anchor: BOTH append tests then FAIL at the Ok(...) branch, i.e. the record was appended THROUGH the link into the victim. Recovery test fails the same way. All three are load-bearing. Fix restored and re-verified (18/18 file_store green).

NOT WEAKENED: append_jsonl's created/fsync_dir semantics, the torn-tail repair contract (jsonl_recovery_repairs_torn_tail_and_rebuilds_index still green), and the InvalidJsonlRecord line-terminator guard.

GATE (scoped): fmt clean; clippy -p oraclemcp-core --all-targets -D warnings = 0 warnings; cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --lib file_store = 18 passed; forbid(unsafe_code) intact (custom_flags is a safe API); no new package (libc already unix-gated here from siry).

title: Engineering Program: PLAN_ENGINEERING_PROGRAM.md (both repos)
description: Master epic for the two-repo engineering program in docs/plan/PLAN_ENGINEERING_PROGRAM.md (oraclemcp server + rust-oracledb driver). Converts the plan into actionable, self-contained beads. Scope = 11 clusters (A-K) plus the P6 bead-graph-lint foundation. Target release train: 0.9.1 server / 0.8.5 driver, strictly +0.0.1 PATCH per plan section 29.7 (implement patch-safe, no new published-crate public API) unless a cluster explicitly opts into a minor. Goals: keep both repos green and shippable, harden the fail-closed guard, finish the OCI/live story, deliver the GCP demo/launch. GO order: A, then B parallel with C, then D, then E, then F plus H, then G, then I, then J; K is the capstone. Repo ownership carried per-bead via labels repo-oraclemcp / repo-driver / repo-site; cross-repo coordination uses the checksum-handoff pattern (plan section 19.6), never native cross-repo edges.

title: P6: plan/bead-graph lint (foundation, run before implementation)

Checks: unique slugs/IDs, acyclic dependency graph (br dep cycles empty), unique labels, all cross-references resolvable, every bead has a non-empty description, repo-ownership label present. Run against this graph before implementation and after each polish round. Deliverable: a script plus a CI check. Acceptance: passes on the full A-K graph and fails loudly on an injected cycle / dangling ref / missing description (self-test).
close_reason: Dropped as unnecessary overhead: br dep cycles + the polish review already cover graph validity; a bespoke bead-graph lint is not worth building.

Confidentiality-strict: no live OCI identifiers in any committed artifact (synthetic fixtures only; real notes to gitignored todelete/). Gated on he7t (F-D1 config step); then agent-runnable within free-tier guardrails (no per-run approval). oraclemcp. Acceptance = F-D1 live signoff evidence.

title: I1: OCI Always-Free provisioning + teardown-as-incident harness

CROSS-REF (polish r1): deferred bead oraclemcp-i-oci-free-tier-provision-guard-ilr4 (refuse any non-provably-Always-Free OCI plan) overlaps this harness's zero-cost guard + F5 -- check it before implementing; absorb-or-dedup rather than duplicating (one bead per defect).

notes: 091 19c RIDER (plan v8 sec.4-F2, ties driver-side D11 ledger): Always-Free ADB has historically offered a 19c-or-23ai version choice at provisioning. VERIFY at F1 provision time whether 19c is selectable in our home region; if yes provision THIS sweep instance as 19c (the field's actual version -- a zero-cost LIVE 19c lane) and run a second/re-provisioned sweep on 23ai for the feature-floor surface; if not, fall back to 23ai and record it. OCI CLI empty-list gotcha: data key omitted entirely when empty -- always run a known-non-empty control query in the same invocation.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text:
DEDUP TRIAGE before decomposition (dedup-triage-first): three of the four tranche-1 items named in this cluster body are ALREADY LANDED in this repo. Verified against the tree, not inferred:

1. 'Move server _quality.yml OUT of .github/workflows and update verify_required_local.py path' -- DONE. The file lives at .github/required/_quality.yml (not .github/workflows/), scripts/verify_required_local.py line 23 already resolves QUALITY_WORKFLOW to that path, and the contract test passes: 'bash scripts/test_verify_required_local.sh' exit 0. (verify_required_local.py itself exits 78 here, which is the CORRECT fail-closed refusal to emit a required-proof from a dirty shared tree, not a defect.)

2. 'Add powerset disk-free preflight assert' -- DONE. scripts/feature_powerset_disk_preflight.sh exists, is invoked at .github/workflows/ci.yml line 270, and is registered in scripts/entry_trace_contract.json.

3. 'needs-removal / job-graph parallelization' -- DONE on the server side, exactly as sec.25.3 P1.1 prescribes. release.yml build.needs is [checks, pinned-nightly, web-build] with release-acceptance deliberately removed (there is an in-file comment citing the plan section and the ~14 min saved), and publish-crates.needs still includes release-acceptance so nothing ships ungated.

4. 'Pre-tag release-rehearsal workflow_dispatch (sec.29.3)' -- NOT DONE. Only an incidental comment mentions the word 'rehearsal'; no dispatchable rehearsal workflow exists. Filed as oraclemcp-yi5bu with discovered-from on this cluster.

CONSEQUENCE: this cluster does not need a four-way decomposition in this repo. Its remaining server-side scope is the single child above. The 'Both repos' half (the driver's _quality.yml split into a parallel job matrix, fuzz sharding, RQ double-run removal, live-matrix scheduling -- sec.25.2 P0.1-P0.4) is rust-oracledb work and belongs to whoever owns that repo; it is NOT covered by oraclemcp-yi5bu.

Recommend this cluster stays open until oraclemcp-yi5bu lands AND the driver-side tranche is confirmed, then closes as drained.

Scheduling-only, no logic change. Both repos where the pattern applies. Acceptance: critical-path wall-clock measurably reduced; no job dropped; all gates still run.

Add a workflow_dispatch release rehearsal that runs the EXACT tag-pipeline gates (7-target build incl. binary-size check, cargo package --workspace tarball verify, full release-gates job) against a candidate SHA on a branch, BEFORE the tag is pushed. Turns the 0.9.0-style 4-attempt re-tag loop into one dry-run. Also make the binary-size budget a ratcheted warn-then-fail value (no silent cap, section 27.2 C10). Both repos. comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Reopened: Fresh independent audit found the explicit cold-start-reduction acceptance lacks measured proof; reopening until that proof is landed.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Live verification 2026-07-20: rust-oracledb local HEAD d99927d0723ecae10a732a9f89ebeab76af75445, origin/main 4fc6aa222c25b6c9ac906b3b3d298b5b47d8f836; D4 workflow commit 4f1eb3abd3af604ad29f1899251025499b0a3869 is not on the remote. Therefore there is no exact-source prep->strict hosted pair and no measured cold-start reduction. A local workflow/parser pass cannot prove GitHub runner/cache cold-start behavior. Keep open until the D4 source is pushed and an exact-SHA prep run followed by strict run yields timing/cache evidence; no push was performed.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Stays open, and the blocker is now precise: the fuzz-bound half is landed on oraclemcp (scripts/ci_lane_budget.py fuzz-bound, wired into ci.yml), but the RQ prep half needs a hosted exact-SHA prep-then-strict pair in rust-oracledb, which is FROZEN until the 0.9.0 tag; the D4 driver source 4f1eb3ab is still not on the driver remote, so there is nothing to measure against yet. Separately I applied the operator's lesson from that driver work to THIS repo (eabd2952): oraclemcp's local replay refused a step-level env: but was structurally blind to a workflow- or job-level env:, which applies to every CI step and is simply absent locally - exactly the shape that replayed as cargo install --version "". Today .github/required/_quality.yml has no such block so it was latent, not live; it now raises ContractError before any step parses, as does a GitHub expression inside a required run:. Mutation-controlled in both directions.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: BLOCKER HAS CLEARED, and a prevention gate for the driver's own incident is landed on the oraclemcp side. DRIVER STATE VERIFIED THIS TURN: 4f1eb3abd3af604ad29f1899251025499b0a3869 (the D4 workflow source) IS now an ancestor of rust-oracledb origin/main, v0.9.0 is tagged, and the driver is green on a1819ef. My 2026-07-20 note that 'the D4 source is not on the remote' is therefore SUPERSEDED - the prerequisite for the hosted exact-SHA prep-then-strict pair now exists. WHAT REMAINS for the RQ-prep acceptance is a hosted measurement, not code: dispatch the Release Qualification workflow at one SHA in prep mode, then in strict mode, and keep the timing/cache evidence. I have NOT triggered it - a hosted run is outward-facing and consumes CI minutes, so it is the operator's call; say the word and I will run the pair and record the numbers. LANDED THIS TURN instead, from the transferable lesson: scripts/ci_step_tool_order_lint.py, wired into the CI lint job. It refuses a workflow where a step USES a tool its own job installs LATER, or that no step installs at all and a stock runner does not ship - i.e. the ripgrep-before-install incident, mechanically. The companion half was already landed at eabd2952 (verify_required_local.py refuses a workflow- or job-level env: and any GitHub expression in a required run), which is the FIRST incident: widening the required set inherits the environmental assumptions of the steps pulled in. PROVEN ABLE TO FAIL: selftest requires E_TOOL_USED_BEFORE_INSTALL for a reordered install and E_TOOL_NEVER_INSTALLED for the ripgrep shape, plus acceptance for correct ordering; disabling the ordering comparison or counting comments as calls each fails it. MY FIRST RUN PRODUCED FOUR FINDINGS AND ALL FOUR WERE MINE: the prose block introducing the next job was attributed to the previous job's last step, so a step running provenance_check.sh was reported as using cargo-deny. A comment is not a call; that is now an accept-direction guard. With it fixed all 11 workflows pass, so the repo has no live instance - the gate is prevention, added before the bug arrives rather than after. The fuzz-shard half of this bead remains landed and bounded (ci_lane_budget.py fuzz-bound).

title: E1: build-lease + isolated build-target discipline (W3)
description: Plan section 27.3-27.5 (W3): the retro root cause is shared mutable build infrastructure — one target dir / package index shared by many concurrent agents (the source of the fork-EAGAIN freeze, OOM pressure, and lock contention). Enforce two rules as mechanism, not discipline: (1) heavy cargo operations (workspace builds, full test runs) always run against a DEDICATED per-agent CARGO_TARGET_DIR, never a shared target/; (2) heavy builds acquire a build lease (e.g. a flock-based scripts/build_lease.sh wrapper, or the agent-mail acquire_build_slot/release_build_slot primitives) so N concurrent agents serialize instead of launching N simultaneous full compiles. Deliver: the lease wrapper + a preflight check that refuses un-leased/shared-target heavy builds + an AGENTS.md section. How an agent obtains its own copy of the source tree is the agent harness's concern, out of scope for repo machinery (operator decision 2026-07-21 scrubbed that half from this bead). Acceptance: two deliberately-concurrent agents cannot corrupt each other's build; the lease demonstrably serializes shared-resource access.
close_reason: Fully implemented and independently rechecked: direct heavy Cargo interception, dedicated target enforcement, and concurrent lease serialization all have landed fail-closed tests and close evidence. [closing=366669bc7e231a347660f483dd5a990c14813b34 source=b6bed9dd205dc403cee75cf040beafdab4ee5bdb evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.6.1.json]

title: E7: harden concurrency-lint test-file scoping (non-cfg(test) helpers leak)
description: Flagged by hunt-core-concurrency during the tests_ci_lanes.rs block_on fix (08ace03). scripts/oraclemcp_concurrency_lint.sh has a lint-SCOPING gap (not a runtime bug): git_files() only excludes /tests.rs or /tests/ paths (a tests_*.rs src file is scanned), and production_lines() only strips items DIRECTLY annotated cfg(test)/test, so a bare helper fn inside a parent cfg(test) mod (e.g. drive() in tests_ci_lanes.rs) is treated as production. Any future block_on/tokio_spawn/Mutex/LaneCommand/unbounded_queue in such a helper trips the lint. Immediate case sanctioned with a block-on-boundary marker; this bead is the durable hardening. Decision (lint-author): widen git_files() to skip tests_*.rs/*_tests.rs, OR make production_lines() parent-cfg(test)-mod aware - not both blindly (reduces intended coverage). Acceptance: a block_on in a non-annotated helper in a tests_*.rs src file is handled per the chosen policy, with a self-test.
close_reason: Widened concurrency-lint git_files() to skip tests_*.rs/*_tests.rs (the 10 include!-d tests_*.rs http files + main_tests.rs have no in-file cfg(test) marker, so production_lines() cannot strip them); added --self-test proving a tests_*.rs helper block_on is excluded while an ordinary-named one still trips. lint OK + self-test OK. Impl agent, gate-verified+cherry-picked by lead (350e70e).

title: E4 follow-up: wire ci_heartbeat output into CiLaneSnapshot schema
description: Discovered from E4 (CI heartbeat, bead 6.4). E4 shipped the notifier (scripts/ci_heartbeat.sh + .github/workflows/ci-heartbeat.yml) but deliberately did NOT wire the heartbeat's observed per-lane CI state into ci_lanes.rs's CiLaneSnapshot schema -- that module's own doc comment defers this integration to a separate bead, and it is a materially larger, riskier change than a notifier. The dashboard CI-lane tile already fails closed correctly (renders honest 'unavailable') without it, so this is an enhancement, not a correctness gap. Scope: feed the heartbeat's observed lane conclusions into CiLaneSnapshot so the dashboard tile shows live lane health instead of 'unavailable'. MUST preserve the fail-closed-to-unknown invariant (never render green PASS for a blocked or unknown lane). Acceptance: dashboard CI-lane tile reflects real heartbeat-observed lane state; fail-closed invariant retained and covered by tests.
close_reason: Fully implemented and independently rechecked: heartbeat observations feed CiLaneSnapshot, dashboard state is live when known, and blocked or unknown lanes remain fail-closed. title: F-DC1 (High): arrow TSTZ drops timezone offset
description: A TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE fetched into an Arrow column loses its display offset, and the conformance fixtures were self-fulfilling (they asserted the buggy output). Root at crates/oracledb/src/arrow/builders.rs (epoch_parts_from_tstz / epoch_parts_from_components). Fix: apply the display offset before computing the Arrow epoch, reconciled with the reference DataFrame mapping (python-oracledb parity). Discriminating test: assert the exact instant for a known TSTZ value (NOT type), and remove the self-fulfilling fixture. Patch-safe (behavior only). Acceptance: test fails on the old mapping, passes on the fix.
close_reason: Arrow TSTZ applies the display offset (epoch_parts_from_tstz, arrow/builders.rs+mod.rs), reconciled with python-oracledb reference; self-fulfilling fixture replaced; discriminating test offset_only_from_structured_nonzero. Full driver gate green incl semver-checks (patch-safe). Landed this commit.

title: F-DB1 (Med): 23ai BOOLEAN discarded in serialize
description: Oracle 23ai BOOLEAN column values are not serialized as JSON booleans (crates/oraclemcp-db/src/serialize.rs). Fix: detect BOOLEAN and emit exact JSON true/false with a fail-safe for invalid representations. Discriminating test in type_fidelity.rs asserts VALUES not types: ser(BOOLEAN,true)==json true, false==json false, NULL==json null. Patch-safe. Acceptance: BOOLEAN round-trips as JSON bool.
close_reason: 23ai BOOLEAN serialized as exact JSON true/false (is_boolean_type in serialize.rs) + invalid-repr fail-safe; discriminating test boolean_values_are_exact_json_booleans asserts VALUES. Full oraclemcp gate green. Landed this commit.

title: F-LOW DC5: reject sub-minute FixedOffset loss
description: Repo/site: /home/durakovic/projects/rust-oracledb/crates/oracledb/src/sql_convert.rs:1233-1250, DateTime<FixedOffset>::to_sql computes offset_minutes with local_minus_utc() / 60. Current verification: a sub-minute offset is silently truncated before QueryValue::TimestampTz is built. Risk: a caller-supplied timestamp offset such as +00:00:30 is encoded as +00:00, changing the represented timestamp semantics without an error. Narrow fix: because Oracle TSTZ offset encoding has minute precision, reject a FixedOffset whose seconds are not exactly divisible by 60 before conversion; retain exact checked conversion for valid minute offsets. Negative acceptance: FixedOffset +00:00:30 and -00:00:30 return a typed ConversionError and never emit TimestampTz. Positive acceptance: +05:45, -03:30, and UTC encode and round-trip with their exact minute offsets.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: BLOCKED by the operator-declared rust-oracledb 0.9.0 tag freeze; no driver edit/commit/push made. Committed HEAD already changed the old truncation to offset_seconds.div_euclid(60), but that still silently rounds a non-minute FixedOffset and does NOT meet DC5. The committed DC5 tests currently assert +00:00:30 -> offset_minutes=0 and -00:00:30 -> -1; those expectations must be replaced after thaw with typed ConversionError assertions that no TimestampTz bind is emitted. Positive minute/UTC cases must remain exact. Releasing claim until tag cut.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: PARKED - driver-side, and CONFIRMED STILL UNFIXED at rust-oracledb origin/main today, so this is not a stale finding. crates/oracledb/src/sql_convert.rs:1247 still computes 'let offset_seconds = self.offset().local_minus_utc();' and builds BindValue::TimestampTz with 'offset_minutes: offset_seconds.div_euclid(60)'. SHARPENING THE FINDING while I was in there: div_euclid does not merely truncate, and the two signs fail DIFFERENTLY. +00:00:30 gives div_euclid(60) = 0, so the offset silently becomes +00:00 - the truncation the bead describes. But -00:00:30 gives div_euclid(60) = -1, so the offset silently becomes -00:01:00: the represented instant moves by 30 seconds in one direction and 30 in the other, and a caller sees neither an error nor a consistent rounding rule. The bead's narrow fix (reject a FixedOffset whose seconds are not divisible by 60, since Oracle TSTZ offsets have minute precision) remains the right one, and its negative acceptance should assert BOTH signs for exactly this reason. NOT ATTEMPTED HERE: rust-oracledb is open again but its pane is mid-gate on v7t7, and forcing a change into a repo mid-gate is how a release cycle gets lost. Hand this to the driver pane with the sign asymmetry noted.

title: F-LOW G1: normalize VECTOR_EMBEDDING before batch splitting
description: Repo/site: /home/durakovic/projects/oraclemcp/crates/oraclemcp-guard/src/classifier.rs:141-143, 2923-2930, and 3768-3794. Current verification: single statements are parser-normalized from VECTOR_EMBEDDING(model USING :bind) to comma syntax inside classify_statement, but multi-statement pure SQL is first split by sqlparser on the original Oracle 23ai syntax; that outer parse can fail and mark a benign batch Forbidden. Risk: fail-closed usability regression for a server-constructed verified local-vector query batch, not a privilege bypass. Narrow fix: use the same strictly shaped normalization for the parser-only batch split while continuing to classify and certificate-bind the original SQL bytes; do not broaden raw caller UDF trust. Negative acceptance: qualified, quoted, malformed, or caller-unverified VECTOR_EMBEDDING remains Guarded/Forbidden, and a batch containing DML still takes maximum danger. Positive acceptance: a benign multi-SELECT verified-local batch with the exact model USING bind grammar classifies statement-by-statement instead of failing solely on parser syntax.
close_reason: Restricted parser-only vector normalization to the bare builtin; clean-worktree guard tests passed. [closing=ee8b1483aadccc924899165e33b41ce853fe0b6d source=8ea5cfcd6373cfb052b15e285f4d4385f5115e0f evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.7.11.10.json]

title: F-LOW DB2: reject unmatched named binds before driver ordering
description: Repo/site: /home/durakovic/projects/oraclemcp/crates/oraclemcp-db/src/connection.rs:2177-2194 and callers at 5574-5650. Current verification: order_named_binds_for_driver removes names found in placeholder order, then appends every unmatched supplied bind positionally; the current test at 4174-4190 asserts the unused tail. Risk: an operator-configured named query with a typo or extra bind can bind a value to the wrong Oracle placeholder position rather than fail before execution. Narrow fix: make ordering return Result and require an exact case-insensitive placeholder-to-supplied-name match, with duplicate placeholder semantics matching the driver; reject missing and leftover names before DB I/O. Negative acceptance: SQL using :a,:b with supplied :a,:unused and SQL using :a with an extra :unused both return a typed bind mismatch and never call the driver. Positive acceptance: out-of-order :b,:a input binds correctly to repeated/case-varied :a/:b placeholders.
close_reason: DB2 already satisfied: named-bind ordering returns a typed NamedBindMismatch for missing or leftover names before any driver call [closing=30c6f75356454d8f657f2be659b9802deb2c7ad1 source=156e9a89def858554f4aa1d9c3a5501583b9697c evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.7.11.11.json]

title: F-LOW DB3: persist typed quarantine after owned-stream recovery failure
description: Repo/site: /home/durakovic/projects/oraclemcp/crates/oraclemcp-db/src/connection.rs:2670-2782, especially RustOracleRowStream::recover, plus lock_inner at 1358-1376. Current verification: into_connection failure returns DbError::Quarantined but leaves the shared slot empty without setting quarantine_reason; a later call sees connection.is_none and reports the misleading Internal temporarily-unavailable state. Risk: operators and retry logic lose the durable unknown-discarded classification after the first error. Narrow fix: atomically mark the slot quarantine_reason when owned-stream recovery fails or slot replacement is uncertain, then make every subsequent access return the same typed Quarantined outcome; never reuse the slot. Negative acceptance: injected into_connection/replacement failure is followed by repeated Quarantined UnknownDiscarded responses, never temporarily unavailable. Positive acceptance: successful recovery restores the connection exactly once and later queries remain usable.
close_reason: DB3 already satisfied: a failed owned-stream recovery durably quarantines the slot and every later access returns the same typed UnknownDiscarded [closing=30c6f75356454d8f657f2be659b9802deb2c7ad1 source=156e9a89def858554f4aa1d9c3a5501583b9697c evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.7.11.12.json]

title: F-LOW DB4: canonicalize signed INTERVAL DAY TO SECOND formatting
description: Repo/site: /home/durakovic/projects/oraclemcp/crates/oraclemcp-db/src/connection.rs:2859-2869, 3097-3107, 3388-3408, and 3675-3695. Current verification: multiple serializers interpolate signed days, hours, minutes, seconds, and fseconds independently with zero-padding; if the driver emits signed sub-day components, output can contain embedded minus signs such as -01:-02 or .-00000001. Risk: malformed, inconsistent JSON text for a valid negative Oracle INTERVAL DS value. Narrow fix: introduce one checked canonical formatter that normalizes sign and absolute components for direct and structured/OSON paths without changing the typed numeric fields. Negative acceptance: negative sub-day and mixed-sign-invalid component fixtures either render one leading sign canonically or fail typed, never embedded minus signs. Positive acceptance: positive and zero intervals retain exact existing text and all serializer paths agree byte-for-byte.
close_reason: DB4: one checked formatter owns the interval sign across all four serializers; mixed signs fail typed instead of rendering embedded minus signs [closing=2ae9eab49281f6e461f0bdd0adc5534dcdd28b6b source=4077dd6c156c59b3e65d99a3915be2668605b90e evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.7.11.13.json]

title: F-LOW CC1: make operator idempotency lease panic-safe
description: Repo/site: /home/durakovic/projects/oraclemcp/crates/oraclemcp-core/src/http/operator.rs:174-250 and 295-305. Current verification: begin inserts an in-progress entry and returns OperatorIdempotencyLease containing only the storage key; it has no Drop cleanup, so a framework panic or early unwind before complete strands the key until the 15-minute TTL. Risk: fail-closed denial of an urgent remediation retry and capacity pressure. Narrow fix: give the lease ownership of a ledger cleanup capability plus a disarmed/completed state; Drop removes only its still-in-progress matching generation, while complete atomically disarms and stores replay evidence. Negative acceptance: catch_unwind after Fresh immediately permits the same key to become Fresh again, with no 15-minute wait. Positive acceptance: normal completion still replays exactly, conflict detection remains exact, and dropping a stale generation cannot remove a newer entry.

title: F-LOW CC2: wake only the affected SSE session waiters
description: Repo/site: /home/durakovic/projects/oraclemcp/crates/oraclemcp-core/src/http/stores.rs:340-360 and 493-704. Current verification: HttpResultStore has one global Condvar; append_event_if_session calls notify_all, waking every session GET/SSE waiter even though only one session acquired an event. Risk: thundering-herd CPU and lock contention at high subscriber counts; correctness is unaffected. Narrow fix: use per-session notification state or an equivalent bounded keyed waiter mechanism, while remove_session and close_all still wake all waiters that must observe closure. Negative acceptance: waiters on N unrelated sessions record no wake/recheck when one session receives an event. Positive acceptance: the target waiter receives the event promptly, removal wakes that session as Closed, and close_all releases every waiter.
close_reason: CORRECTION: CC2 wakes only the affected SSE session waiters; re-closed to bind the reason to the same source commit its evidence cites [closing=a0287cba5b1146784d8ec4715c12ea6eef6425ff source=be9c64bc97db3c32f052c4a0ff58b61c5ce171ec evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.7.11.15.json]

title: F-LOW AU2: escape Unicode line separators in CEF
description: Repo/site: /home/durakovic/projects/oraclemcp/crates/oraclemcp-audit/src/shipping.rs:366-407 and CEF escaping tests near 1671. Current verification: CEF escaping covers backslash, equals, CR, and LF but does not encode Unicode line/paragraph separators U+2028 and U+2029, while the syslog path has a stronger physical-line backstop. Risk: a legacy or externally supplied signed string can split or confuse downstream CEF ingestion without altering the local hash chain. Narrow fix: make CEF header/extension escaping encode every line-separator character that can terminate a collector record, retaining valid non-separator Unicode. Negative acceptance: CR, LF, U+0085, U+2028, and U+2029 cannot appear as raw record separators in any CEF field. Positive acceptance: ordinary Unicode remains intact and existing CEF delimiter escapes are byte-stable.
close_reason: AU2: every Unicode record separator is escaped in CEF header and extension, so a collector cannot split one signed record into two [closing=8fc43040a006e4c658ed6ceb2ea4e137ecba1c45 source=bdb641d94c468eee94dd13604393dbcdf15db9c3 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.7.11.16.json]

title: F-LOW AU3: parse Rekor head binding structurally
description: Repo/site: /home/durakovic/projects/oraclemcp/crates/oraclemcp-audit/src/rekor.rs:115-150, RekorAnchorReceipt::verify_offline. Current verification: after proof-shape checks, the verifier accepts any entry_body containing the manifest digest as an ASCII substring, regardless of which Rekor JSON field carries it. This path is non-admission-gating but supplies offline trust evidence. Risk: a malicious receipt can place the expected digest in unrelated text while the authenticated Rekor artifact binds a different value. Narrow fix: parse the supported hashedrekord or DSSE entry schema and require exact digest equality in the authoritative artifact-hash field before Merkle/checkpoint verification; reject unknown/ambiguous bodies. Negative acceptance: digest in an unrelated field, a superstring, duplicate conflicting field, or malformed JSON returns HeadNotBoundToEntry/MalformedProof. Positive acceptance: valid supported Rekor receipts with the exact field continue to verify with the pinned checkpoint verifier.
close_reason: AU3 already satisfied: the Rekor verifier requires exact equality in the authoritative artifact-hash field; the substring helper is test-only [closing=156e9a89def858554f4aa1d9c3a5501583b9697c source=c8d2b431796f86b979e099533a3b07bd21ac26c2 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.7.11.17.json]

title: F-LOW CF2: redact prose-embedded OCI identifiers in OTLP
description: Repo/site: /home/durakovic/projects/oraclemcp/crates/oraclemcp-telemetry/src/otlp/redact.rs:70-103 and 173-230. Current verification: key-denylist redaction is primary and the value backstop catches bearer/basic, credential assignments, and opaque no-space tokens; an OCID embedded inside ordinary prose with spaces passes unchanged. Risk: tenant/resource identifiers can leave quarantine through error/log bodies even when no sensitive key names them. Narrow fix: add a bounded, case-insensitive OCID token recognizer for the documented ocid1.<type>.<realm>... shape to the single redaction funnel used by logs, traces, and metric labels; avoid treating generic prose as secret. Negative acceptance: an OCID at prefix, middle, or punctuation boundary inside prose is REDACTED across log body, span attribute, and metric-label snapshots. Positive acceptance: near-miss words and normal Oracle error prose without an OCID remain unchanged.
close_reason: CF2: OCIDs embedded in OTLP prose are redacted at the shared funnel, keeping the message and dropping the identifier [closing=8fc43040a006e4c658ed6ceb2ea4e137ecba1c45 source=bdb641d94c468eee94dd13604393dbcdf15db9c3 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.7.11.18.json]

title: F-LOW CF3: eliminate doctor atomic-install rename race
description: Repo/site: /home/durakovic/projects/oraclemcp/crates/oraclemcp-core/src/doctor.rs:800-950, especially regular_file_status and write_new_atomic_file. Current verification: doctor checks the destination with symlink_metadata, creates a sibling temp, then renames by path; a same-user actor can swap path components between the check and rename. Private 0700 directories reduce exposure but do not make the check/use atomic against the owner. Risk: doctor --fix can install the copied audit file at an unintended same-user-controlled path. Narrow fix: hold verified parent directory handles and perform no-follow create/rename operations relative to those handles, validating identity at commit; preserve create-new, 0600, file fsync, and directory fsync guarantees. Negative acceptance: a deterministic race hook that swaps a destination or parent to a symlink is refused and writes nothing outside the verified directory. Positive acceptance: the normal absent-target migration still produces byte-identical backup/current files and leaves the legacy source untouched.
close_reason: CORRECTION: CF3 re-derived at 009dc02f: no-follow handle walk plus create-new hard-link install, proved by an executed symlink-swap regression in a clean worktree [closing=73f6e15e2e4a04fe449465fdb90d66882d4084a4 source=009dc02f30bff17746aea420957e47dce6777c7b evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.7.11.19.json]
