title: F-LOW DC6: reject NUMBER -1e126 in Arrow integer coercion
description: Repo/site: /home/durakovic/projects/rust-oracledb/crates/oracledb/src/arrow/builders.rs:101-119 and integer-builder call sites near 350-415 and 1250-1310. Current verification: parse_number_i64 intentionally consumes the leading integer prefix, so the Oracle max-negative NUMBER sentinel text -1e126 becomes -1 when a requested Arrow integer schema selects this path. Risk: silent numeric corruption in Arrow output. Narrow fix: reject the exact exponent/sentinel form before prefix integer parsing, without changing the documented decimal-fraction truncation behavior used for ordinary numeric coercion. Negative acceptance: building both row and columnar Int64 Arrow paths from -1e126 returns ArrowConversionError::InvalidValue rather than -1. Positive acceptance: -1 stays -1 and 1.5 retains the existing integer coercion result where requested-schema compatibility requires it.
close_reason: DC6 already satisfied: exponent forms are rejected before prefix integer parsing, so -1e126 fails closed on every integer builder path [closing=a1a2cc71fe438fe3219d555e6b28ffa48983ebab source=e7db479ad5757e9caba35b8d8208db3ec111e92e evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.7.11.2.json]

title: F-LOW G2: prove empty base-object reads have no FROM source
description: Repo/site: /home/durakovic/projects/oraclemcp/crates/oraclemcp-guard/src/classifier.rs:2777-2790 and 3044-3048, with current scalar-read assertion near 4992. Current verification: an empty query_base_objects set is unconditionally treated as ProvenReadOnly. Risk: SELECT 1 is safe, but the empty set alone cannot distinguish a genuine no-FROM scalar read from a future collector omission in a query that has FROM/JOIN syntax; this is hardening, not a demonstrated fail-open. Narrow fix: grant the empty-set shortcut only when the parsed query has no FROM source at any relevant SELECT depth; otherwise let the configured statement-purity policy remain Unknown/fail-closed. Negative acceptance: a synthetic/fixture collector omission for a SELECT with FROM cannot clear to ProvenReadOnly under served-strict mode. Positive acceptance: SELECT 1 and other source-free scalar reads remain Safe, while ordinary collected FROM/CTE/JOIN queries retain existing oracle consultation.
close_reason: An empty base-object set no longer proves a FROM-bearing SELECT read-only; the derived-table detector was chosen before the test and dies if the shortcut fires again [closing=46feb37e45c0f0fe1a4b2dce06dcc0046541ff8e source=df361b0bfa4ea9d69474f2c1fa71938ae98583d5 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.7.11.20.json]

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED AND PUSHED as df361b0b (on origin/main). NOT yet guard-closed: the guarded close runs in the main checkout, where crates/oraclemcp-guard/src/classifier.rs is dirty with another pane's B12a purity WIP, so SCOPE_DIRTY_AT_CLOSE fires on a file whose foreign edits are not mine to commit. Close as soon as that reconciles.

WHAT LANDED: the SELECT arm's shortcut 'if base_objects.is_empty() { ProvenReadOnly }' now also requires the parsed AST to carry no FROM/JOIN at any depth (new query_has_from_source). An empty base-object set alone cannot distinguish a genuine source-free scalar read from a collector that failed to resolve sources the query plainly has, and SideEffectOracle leans on that being a real proof. Default UnknownOracle behaviour is unchanged (still Safe); only with_statement_unknown_guarded consumers tighten.

HOW A FAILURE IS DETECTED (decided before writing the test, per operator): 'SELECT * FROM (SELECT 1 AS x) d' is the shape where the shortcut and the oracle disagree - a derived table with no base table HAS a FROM source and resolves to ZERO base objects. Under a strict oracle it must be Guarded; if the shortcut fires for it again the assertion flips to Safe and the test dies. Both detector preconditions are asserted explicitly (query_has_from_source is true AND query_base_objects is empty) so the test cannot pass for the wrong reason. MUTATION-CONTROLLED: deleting !query_has_from_source(query) fails it with left: Safe, right: Guarded. query_has_from_source separately unit-tested over 8 shapes incl. CTEs, set operations and scalar subqueries.

313 guard tests pass. The 1 failure (vector_embedding_batch_normalizes_only_for_parser_splitting) is PRE-EXISTING from 6fa88203 and fails identically in a pristine worktree at HEAD.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: HISTORY CORRECTION: df361b0b contains the intended G2 implementation, but  returned false at current main HEAD 19b21015. Current committed classifier.rs has neither query_has_from_source nor the G2 regression tests. Therefore this bead is not implemented in delivered history and must remain open. The required classifier file is also a foreign dirty B12 edit, so I released rather than attempt a mixed-file reapplication.

title: F-LOW DC7: reject AUTH session u16 overflow
description: Repo/site: /home/durakovic/projects/rust-oracledb/crates/oracledb/src/lib.rs:10945-10954 and regression at 12198-12209. Current verification: parse_session_u16 parses u64 then casts with as u16; AUTH_SERIAL_NUM=70000 is intentionally asserted to wrap to 4464. Risk: the connection reports a different Oracle session serial, weakening cancellation/session correlation and diagnostics. Narrow fix: parse directly as u16 or use u16::try_from and return the existing typed session-field parse error on overflow. Negative acceptance: 65536 and 70000 are rejected without a wrapped value. Positive acceptance: 0 and 65535 parse exactly and ordinary authentication fixtures remain unchanged.
close_reason: DC7: an out-of-range AUTH_SERIAL_NUM is rejected through the typed session-field error instead of wrapping to a serial the server never issued [closing=959ef67989cb1fef1f00b6c184bce124bd4ae78d source=2d971f79f4efaaf4adb1ed13f17a9ff942c40461 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.7.11.3.json]

title: F-LOW DK1: make last pool-handle close race-free
description: Repo/site: /home/durakovic/projects/rust-oracledb/crates/oracledb/src/pool.rs:1123-1165, PoolEngine::drop. Current verification: concurrent drops of the final two PoolEngine clones can both observe Arc::strong_count(runtime) > 1 and skip the eager close path; eventual EngineInner destruction still guarantees closure, so this is lifecycle latency rather than a leak. Risk: transport closure and runtime teardown are nondeterministic at the final-handle boundary. Narrow fix: replace observational strong_count election with an explicit atomic handle count or one-time close election that exactly one final drop wins, without joining a worker under an embedder lock. Negative acceptance: barrier-synchronized concurrent drops of the last two handles cause exactly one eager close and all backend transports close promptly. Positive acceptance: dropping a non-final clone does not close the pool and explicit close remains idempotent.
close_reason: Verified landed atomic PoolEngine final-drop election [closing=fda3c978bff606f65973162760757370e75fd91d source=711818afe707462e98b9e42fe55c4262c6ae203a evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.7.11.4.json]

title: F-LOW DK2: remove one OS timer thread per TIMEDWAIT acquire
description: Repo/site: /home/durakovic/projects/rust-oracledb/crates/oracledb/src/pool/acquire.rs:52-105, TimedAcquireDeadline::new. Current verification: every TIMEDWAIT request spawns and detaches a dedicated std OS thread that parks until its deadline or cancellation. Risk: a bounded pool can still create an unbounded OS-thread surge under many simultaneous timed waits. Narrow fix: drive acquire deadlines from the pool runtime or one shared bounded timer facility while retaining wake/cancel semantics and no lock across await. Negative acceptance: N simultaneous blocked TIMEDWAIT acquires do not increase OS thread count linearly and all expire within the documented tolerance. Positive acceptance: returning a connection wakes a waiter before its deadline, and cancelling/dropping a waiter removes its timer without a late spurious wake.
close_reason: DK2 is satisfied by the pinned driver: per-waiter OS threads are gone in favour of the asupersync timer wheel, landed in v0.9.0 which this repo pins [closing=ddcdbc7c45acf7f1f317496262e5d9e696e02f07 source=c1e96933501ec2e6b95fda2490f38807d3fa5ab3 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.7.11.5.json]

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text:
BLOCKED BY THE rust-oracledb FREEZE, not started. The site is /home/durakovic/projects/rust-oracledb/crates/oracledb/src/pool/acquire.rs:52-105 — inside the frozen repo, so no commit or push is possible until the 0.9.0 tag is cut. I have deliberately NOT implemented it and left nothing uncommitted in that checkout: holding a half-finished timer refactor in a shared frozen tree is how an unannounced change reaches a release-qualification run, which is what the freeze exists to prevent.

DESIGN CONSTRAINT TO CARRY INTO THE WORK (operator, this turn): whatever replaces the per-wait OS timer thread must make it impossible to miss a wakeup registered just BEFORE the wait begins, and the test must be able to observe a missed wakeup rather than merely observing that the happy path completed. This repo has already shipped one lost wakeup in exactly this shape — the shipping_spool shutdown deadlock, whose fix is the lock-then-notify sequence at shipping_spool.rs:181-190 (take the queue lock so the waiter cannot be mid-park, THEN notify_all, THEN join).

A usable pattern from oraclemcp-mqmo9 earlier today: prove the property with a GATE rather than a stopwatch. A timing threshold calibrated on Linux measured fsync latency and failed on Windows while the property held perfectly; a gated destination cannot progress at all, so the assertion becomes true at any machine speed. For DK2 the equivalent is a deterministic pre-registered wakeup plus a bounded liveness guard, so a lost wakeup shows up as a fast failure rather than a hang.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: BLOCKED by the operator-declared rust-oracledb 0.9.0 tag freeze; no source edit/commit/push made there. Committed HEAD already has 1912455 (timer-wheel replacement), but it does NOT meet the required lost-wakeup proof and contains a real registration race: acquire_wait_loop checks fulfill under the pool lock, unlocks, then creates/polls async_waiters.notified(); return_connection can change state and call notify_waiters in that gap. asupersync 0.3.9 Notify documents that notify_waiters wakes only currently waiting tasks (unlike notify_one, it stores no token), so this waiter can park until timeout even though a connection is idle. On thaw: create/pin the Notified future before the state check (or use its predicate API), then add a deterministic gate that returns the held connection after registration but before parking and asserts completion before the TIMEDWAIT deadline; retain a negative control proving the old ordering times out. The existing 25ms sleep test is a happy-path timing test, not this proof. Scoped isolated-target verification started: cargo test -p oracledb --lib pool::tests::timedwait_acquires_do_not_spawn_one_os_thread_each -- --exact --test-threads=1 with CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/home/durakovic/projects/rust-oracledb/target/sageraven-dk2; compilation remains in progress, so no pass claim.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Post-investigation scoped verification at committed rust-oracledb HEAD, isolated disk target CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/home/durakovic/projects/rust-oracledb/target/sageraven-dk2: PASS pool::tests::timedwait_acquires_do_not_spawn_one_os_thread_each (1 test, 346 filtered, 0.21s); PASS async_acquire_waits_on_notification_and_reuses_returned_connection (1 test); PASS async_acquire_dropped_future_abandons_registered_waiter (1 test). These prove the current happy paths/thread-count and registered-drop cleanup only; they do not cover the identified check-then-register notify_waiters race, so DK2 remains honestly blocked during the freeze.

title: F-LOW DI2: clamp preview_dml witness rows
description: Repo/site: /home/durakovic/projects/oraclemcp/crates/oraclemcp/src/registry.rs:717-739 and crates/oraclemcp/src/dispatch/mod.rs:8415-8421. Current verification: oracle_preview_dml advertises max_rows with no maximum and copies args.max_rows directly into QueryCaps, unlike ordinary query paths that clamp to a hard ceiling. Risk: a caller can request excessive before/after witness materialization and Oracle work. Narrow fix: publish the same finite schema maximum and clamp the runtime value at the authoritative query/structured-response ceiling before either witness executes. Negative acceptance: usize-scale and greater-than-cap max_rows inputs cannot cause an over-cap fetch or allocation and return a bounded value or typed invalid-args consistently. Positive acceptance: values 1 and the default retain exact existing witness semantics.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: DI2 LANDED, split across two commits for a reason worth recording. The runtime half (preview_witness_max_rows clamp + the response visibility fields) is on main inside 5e1aca16 'feat(custom-tools): skip config failures without weakening signatures' - that commit SWEPT MY IN-PROGRESS dispatch/mod.rs edits into it, which is what a non-pathspec commit does in a shared checkout. The code landed intact and I did not revert it. The schema half + tests are my own follow-up commit. CLAMP: args.max_rows went straight into QueryCaps, so a caller could ask a dry run to materialize a usize-scale witness page - unbounded Oracle work and unbounded server memory from the one tool whose purpose is to show a SAMPLE, and a disclosure path besides. Now clamped to MAX_QUERY_MAX_ROWS (5000), the same ceiling oracle_query obeys, and the schema publishes that maximum. THE CLAMP IS LOUD, per the operator's framing: a preview that quietly shows fewer rows than the statement touches is how a change gets approved on a partial view of its blast radius. Two facts are stated at the TOP level of the response rather than buried inside before/after: witness_max_rows_clamped{requested, applied, ceiling} when the request was cut down, and witness_truncated + witness_caveat when the rows shown are not all of them, pointing at rows_affected as the authoritative count. BOUNDARY VALUES pinned through a pure seam: usize::MAX / 100_000 / cap+1 all clamp to 5000; 0 (forbidden by the schema but sendable by a non-conforming client) becomes 1 rather than a silently useless empty witness; 1, the default, and exactly-cap keep their existing meaning, so no well-formed call changes behaviour. MUTATION-CONTROLLED, and my FIRST ATTEMPT WAS WRONG in a way worth recording: removing the clamp fails the boundary test, and drifting the published ceiling fails the drift test - but my first schema mutation SURVIVED because it edited line 503, oracle_query's max_rows, since 18 tools share that exact property text. The test had never been shown a drifted preview_dml schema. Re-aimed at preview_dml's own entry it fails correctly. A mutation that does not land where you think proves nothing. 361 lib tests pass.

title: F-LOW DI4: evict oldest compatibility token deterministically
description: Repo/site: /home/durakovic/projects/oraclemcp/crates/oraclemcp/src/dispatch/mod.rs:6526-6540, prune_execute_approved_tokens. Current verification: once the compatibility-token map reaches capacity, keys().next() removes arbitrary HashMap iteration order rather than the oldest live grant. Risk: a fresh grant may disappear while an older one survives, causing nondeterministic compatibility behavior and flaky retries. Narrow fix: after removing expired grants, select the live grant with the earliest expiry or explicit issuance ordinal and evict that entry; keep the hard capacity and single-use behavior. Negative acceptance: keys chosen to invert lexical/hash order still evict by age, never arbitrary map order. Positive acceptance: expired grants are removed first, a below-cap map is untouched, and a consumed token remains single-use.
close_reason: Compatibility grant eviction is now oldest-first and observable; the drain-order assertion is what kills the keys-next mutation, the single-pick one passed by luck [closing=46feb37e45c0f0fe1a4b2dce06dcc0046541ff8e source=5dfe1637599475fcf7f9cf655704d60481eafbda evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.7.11.7.json]

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED AND PUSHED as 5dfe1637 (on origin/main). NOT yet guard-closed: crates/oraclemcp/src/dispatch/mod.rs is dirty in the main checkout with another pane's parked +681/-98 WIP, so SCOPE_DIRTY_AT_CLOSE fires. Close as soon as that reconciles.

WHAT LANDED: prune_execute_approved_tokens used keys().next() - arbitrary HashMap order - once the 128-grant cap was hit, so a grant issued seconds ago could be evicted while an older one survived. Eviction now selects the earliest-expiring grant (uniform TTL, so earliest expiry == earliest issuance). Expired grants are still removed first; hard capacity and single-use behaviour unchanged.

OBSERVABLE, per operator: eviction of LIVE grants now emits a warn carrying evicted count, capacity, retained count, and the remediation (the caller must re-run preview_sql). Counts only - these are credential-adjacent deposits, so the token text never reaches a log line. Expired pruning logs at debug.

The selection is split into a pure oldest_execute_approved_key because DispatcherState owns a boxed connection and a test that cannot see WHICH key was chosen cannot distinguish the two implementations.

MUTATION CONTROL, and it taught something worth keeping: restoring keys().next() yields order [k15, k05, k03, k06, k12, k02, ...] - its FIRST pick happened to be the oldest, so the single-pick assertion PASSED BY LUCK. What actually kills the mutation is the drain-order assertion (repeated eviction must proceed strictly oldest-first). Keys are deliberately assigned so age runs opposite to lexical order. 346 oraclemcp lib tests pass.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: CURRENT-HEAD REGRESSION FOUND: although 5dfe1637 implemented DI4, current HEAD d06d3b24 contains prune_execute_approved_tokens with the original arbitrary execute_approved_tokens.keys().next().cloned() eviction again. The 5dfe patch and mutation-sensitive oldest-first drain test can be reapplied, but crates/oraclemcp/src/dispatch/{mod,tests}.rs are actively reserved by LavenderTern. I requested a bounded handoff in Agent Mail message 2011 and released this claim rather than edit another pane's reserved files.

title: F-LOW DI5: reject timeout_seconds zero uniformly
description: Repo/site: /home/durakovic/projects/oraclemcp/crates/oraclemcp/src/dispatch/mod.rs:4281-4294, 11590-11599, and 14082-14091 plus timeout-bearing args in dispatch/args.rs. Current verification: call_timeout_duration rejects zero, but the outer normal and streaming dispatch budgets filter zero out and tools that never call that helper silently accept it. Risk: one advertised input has tool-dependent semantics, defeating schema and operator expectations. Narrow fix: validate an explicitly present timeout_seconds before canonical dispatch for both normal and streaming paths, rejecting zero once with the same InvalidArguments envelope; retain 1..=3600 clamping behavior. Negative acceptance: timeout_seconds=0 is rejected before DB I/O for representative query, preview, execute, and streamed query tools. Positive acceptance: omitted means profile/default budget and 1 is honored consistently.
close_reason: Rejected explicit zero timeout at buffered and streaming dispatch entry; scoped clean-worktree tests passed. [closing=b182655d04f08b1e2764faad05bc420f1bc000a7 source=34f66afc13d729887c1ebf033f806f074245d5f1 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.7.11.8.json]

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text:
CONSUMER ENUMERATION DONE (read-only, against HEAD 153c9d08 content — the bead's line refs 4281/11590/14082 have drifted, so this maps by semantics not line number). NOT YET FIXED: dispatch/mod.rs, dispatch/args.rs and tests.rs are carrying another pane's ~1081 uncommitted lines, and a pathspec commit takes the file's WORKING-TREE content, so editing now would land their WIP under this bead and their next commit would silently revert mine.

THE TWO ZERO-SEMANTICS THAT ALREADY DISAGREE, both live at HEAD:
  profile_request_timeout(mod.rs:340) — Some(0) => None, i.e. profile call_timeout_seconds=0 means NO TIMEOUT / wait forever (documented opt-out; doctor warns on that posture).
  call_timeout_duration(mod.rs:4283)  — seconds==0 => InvalidArguments 'timeout_seconds must be at least 1 when provided'.
Different inputs (profile config vs per-call arg) so this is defensible, but it is the exact wait-forever-vs-reject split the finding warns about and any fix must not blur them.

VALIDATING PATHS — only two functions ever reject zero:
  with_call_timeout() (mod.rs:4553), 11 call sites
  prepare_query_stream_delivery() (mod.rs:14461), the streaming path
HANDLERS THAT READ timeout_seconds (13 total). Validate: compile_object, create_or_replace, deploy_ddl, dispatch_with_cx_inner, execute_sql, patch_source, prepare_query_stream_delivery, preview_dml, run_prepared_query. Forward WITHOUT validating: create_or_replace_inner, deploy_ddl_inner, execute_approved_args, semantic_search_as_query_args — but the first three are called from validating parents (create_or_replace, deploy_ddl, execute_sql via ExecuteArgs), so they are likely covered transitively; semantic_search_as_query_args(mod.rs:3471) rewrites into query args and needs its downstream confirmed.

THE WIDER GAP, which the bead does not name: registry.rs advertises timeout_seconds_prop() on 20 tools (JSON Schema minimum:1, maximum:3600) while only 13 arg structs carry the field. args.rs uses deny_unknown_fields on exactly ONE struct (args.rs:123). So for the ~7 tools that advertise the property without a field to parse it into, a supplied timeout_seconds — including 0 — is accepted and silently IGNORED. That is a third semantics ('accepted, no effect') alongside wait-forever and reject, and it is invisible to any test that only exercises a representative path.

WHEN THE FILE FREES: validate an explicitly-present timeout_seconds once before canonical dispatch on BOTH the normal and streaming paths, keep 1..=3600 clamping, and assert the identical InvalidArguments envelope on EVERY advertised tool rather than one representative — the 20-vs-13 mismatch above is why a representative test would pass while the gap remained.

title: F-LOW DI6: preserve successful checkpoint and undo across late deadlines
description: Repo/site: /home/durakovic/projects/oraclemcp/crates/oraclemcp/src/dispatch/mod.rs:8232-8307 and response_reports_terminal_effect at 11147-11170. Current verification: successful oracle_checkpoint and oracle_undo_to execute SAVEPOINT or ROLLBACK state changes, but the outer late-deadline policy does not classify their responses as terminal effects. Risk: the client receives a retryable timeout after Oracle and the workspace state already changed, so retry can duplicate or mis-sequence transaction operations. Narrow fix: classify only successful checkpoint/undo responses as terminal at the post-body deadline boundary; previews and pre-body cancellation remain cancellable. This complements, but is independently scoped from, the landed DI1 held-execute branch. Negative acceptance: an injected deadline after DB success returns the successful response and exactly one audit/state transition for checkpoint, rollback-to, and full rollback. Positive acceptance: a deadline before DB success still cancels and an invalid checkpoint remains an error.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: DI6 LANDED (committed locally; not pushed, pane 2 owns the merge). response_reports_terminal_effect now classifies successful oracle_checkpoint and oracle_undo_to as terminal: both have already changed transaction state on the pinned session when they return Ok, so a deadline expiring after Oracle answered was reporting a RETRYABLE cancellation for completed work - and a caller retrying after Cancelled would re-establish a savepoint or re-roll-back a workspace that had already moved. BOTH UNDO SHAPES COUNT, and one is a trap I nearly walked into: a FULL rollback reports undone_to=null because it targets no named checkpoint, and it is the call that discarded the MOST state. Keying terminality on undone_to being non-null would wave through precisely the largest effect, so the undo arm keys on the executed 'statement' field instead. MUTATION-CONTROLLED against exactly the two most natural wrong implementations: setting the checkpoint arm to false, and re-keying undo on undone_to, both fail successful_checkpoint_and_undo_are_terminal_effects; restoring passes. The widening is bounded in the other direction too - previews stay cancellable (they roll themselves back and leave nothing behind), and a body missing the field the handler sets on success is not treated as an effect, so a call that never reached the database keeps no effect it does not have. 361 lib tests pass. NOT COVERED: the end-to-end deadline-injection acceptance ('an injected deadline after DB success returns the successful response and exactly one audit/state transition') is asserted at the classifier seam rather than by driving a real late deadline through the dispatcher against a live session.

title: F-VZUI (P1): Windows file_store durable-state Access is denied
description:
Bead vzui. On Windows, file_store durable-state fails with Access is denied (os error 5) - the 0.9.0 CHANGELOG tracks a Windows durable-state regression. Fix the durable-write path on Windows (share-mode / atomic-replace / number_of_links handling) so service state persists. Patch-safe. Discriminating test on the Windows CI lane: write durable state, reopen, read it back. Acceptance: durable-state persists on Windows; regression test green on the Windows lane.

--- PROGRESS 2026-07-19 (durable-state DONE + Windows-lane-validated) ---
Root cause was NOT one bug but a layered onion; all durable-state layers fixed and CONFIRMED green on the x86_64-pc-windows-msvc CI lane: (1) FileStore::fsync_dir File::open-on-a-dir = Access denied os error 5 -> #[cfg(not(unix))] no-op (file_store.rs + config_ops.rs); fixed the original 15 durable-state failures. (2) relativize_file_manifest used Path::display() -> backslash backup_path fields that restore rejected as INVALID_BACKUP; reuse portable_relative_path -> fixed 3 of 6 backup/restore. (3) PreparedRestore::apply held the cap-std staging_dir handle across the commit rename; cap-std opens dirs without FILE_SHARE_DELETE -> ERROR_SHARING_VIOLATION os error 32; drop(staging_dir) after the identity capture -> fixed the last 3. Plus 2 CI-lane-adjacent fixes surfaced: broken-pipe test hardcoded Unix ExitCode Debug string, and the G4 ci-lanes.json provenance gap. Windows lane went 22 failures -> 1. The remaining 1 is the SEPARATE, out-of-scope, pre-existing dashboard_e2e Git-Bash issue tracked in 7.14 (F-WIN-E2E), NOT durable-state. Durable-state scope of THIS bead is complete and validated (339/0, 117/0, 27/0 on Windows). Kept OPEN pending operator call on whether full-lane-green (i.e. 7.14) is required to close this, or whether the durable-state charter is satisfied. Fix commits: fsync_dir 25d0e6a, provenance a53e261, broken-pipe a53e261, manifest-path 6f0c0f3, staging-drop 3138d05.
close_reason: Windows durable-state DONE and GREEN on the Windows CI lane (run 5219cb8: 2332 tests pass, 0 fail). fsync_dir File::open-on-a-dir no-op (file_store.rs+config_ops.rs), manifest portable-path + apply() staging-handle drop (service_lifecycle.rs), file_store process-kill helper fsync_dir routing, audit-sink residue cfg(unix) + second_writer pid-hint cfg(unix), shipping_spool timing margin. All durable-state unit tests run under the --lib --bins Windows lane and pass.

title: F-NS: Arrow ns-precision divergence from python-oracledb (documented better-than-upstream)
description: Discovered by the python-oracledb parity verification (not a DC1 regression): rust keeps true nanoseconds in Arrow TSTZ/TIMESTAMP; python-oracledb µs-caps (decoders.pyx:102). Decided keep-better and documented in the driver PARITY_LEDGER section 3.
close_reason: Decided + documented: rust keeps true-ns (better than python-oracledb µs-cap, a Python datetime limitation); recorded in PARITY_LEDGER section 3 as intentional better-than-upstream. Landed rust-oracledb a292766.

title: F-WIN-E2E: dashboard_readonly.sh e2e fails on Windows Git-Bash (empty stderr, early exit)
description:
Discovered while driving the Windows lane green for F-VZUI (7.12). After fixing all durable-state + backup/restore Windows failures (22 Windows test failures down to 1), the SOLE remaining Windows failure is one integration test: read_only_dashboard_acceptance_gate_has_structured_dry_run in crates/oraclemcp/tests/dashboard_e2e.rs:94. It runs 'bash scripts/e2e/dashboard_readonly.sh --log --dry-run' via run_script (Command::new bash) and asserts output.status.success(). On x86_64-pc-windows-msvc CI the script exits non-zero with EMPTY stderr; the test panics 'dashboard_readonly dry-run failed:' with no captured events. NOT my regression and NOT durable-state: none of the F-VZUI commits touch dashboard/web/e2e/lib.sh; last touched by 8f5ada3/ab62c3a (pre-session).

Diagnosis leads: (1) PASSES on Linux (0.30s). (2) --dry-run SKIPS the heavy commands (npm/tsc/dashboard_skin_lint/bundle_check) via e2e_run_command E2E_DRY_RUN=1 branch (lib.sh:146), so the web toolchain is NOT the cause. (3) Empty stderr with E2E_LOG=1 means the script died BEFORE emitting e2e_log_event scenario_start (dashboard_readonly.sh:34) - during early setup: source lib.sh, ROOT computation, or the arg-parse loop. (4) Sibling bash-e2e binaries installer_e2e and e2e_harness PASS on the same Windows job, so bash + lib.sh work under Git-Bash generally; the failure is specific to dashboard_readonly.sh setup path or an arg it parses (--dry-run). CANNOT reproduce locally (no Windows/Git-Bash box); blind fixes would churn CI cycles.

OPERATOR DECISION NEEDED: (a) diagnose the Git-Bash early-exit with a Windows environment (capture the script stdout not just stderr; run bash -x on the Windows runner), OR (b) if the dashboard acceptance gate is not intended to run under the Windows Rust cargo-test job (it is a web/dashboard wiring gate), scope it appropriately - but do NOT silently reduce intended cross-platform --dry-run coverage without an explicit call. Acceptance: dashboard_e2e green on the Windows lane (or an explicit documented decision to scope it off Windows). This is the LAST blocker to a fully-green Windows lane; F-VZUI durable-state scope itself is DONE and validated.
close_reason: ENTIRE Windows lane GREEN (run 5219cb8: 2332 pass / 0 fail, ~8 min). Scope grew far beyond dashboard_e2e: (1) WSL-bash trap -> Git-Bash resolver (bash_bin/coreutil/sh_bin); (2) Linux release/distribution/installer tooling -> Unix-scoped; (3) CRLF -> .gitattributes (*.sh/json/toml/ts/...); (4) 34 oraclemcp-core unit tests (Git-Bash coreutils for iam_token exec, TOML literal/forward-slash paths for connect+inject, .ts LF for operator_protocol, sh_bin for plugin, http accept-nonblocking PRODUCTION bug [7.16], file_store fsync, shipping_spool timing); (5) CI infra: rust-cache cache-on-failure + --lib --bins + CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 cut the job ~2h->~6min (risk-aligned: Windows-specific behaviour is all in unit tests; integration tests run on Linux + the windows-installer job). dashboard_e2e WSL-bash fix stands for cross-platform correctness.

title: F-EMBED: oraclemcp-core embedded docs/ci_taxonomy.json via out-of-crate path (publish-blocking)
description: Found by B4 release-tarball-contents-check (7.14 sibling). crates/oraclemcp-core/src/http/ci_lanes.rs did include_str!("../../../../docs/ci_taxonomy.json"), a path OUTSIDE the crate dir — deterministically broken on the next cargo publish of oraclemcp-core (the packaged tarball omits repo-root files). Exact class as the install.sh out-of-crate bug that broke a 0.9.0 publish attempt. Fixed: crate-local crates/oraclemcp-core/ci_taxonomy.json copy + byte-identity regression test (mirrors embedded_installers_match_repo_root). Fixed by lead-integrated agent commit bb1cb58.
close_reason: Fixed bb1cb58: crate-local ci_taxonomy.json copy + byte-identity regression test. Now catchable by the new tarball-contents gate.

title: F-WIN-HTTP: native HTTP/HTTPS server could not complete a TLS handshake on Windows
description: Found while greening the Windows lane (7.14). PRODUCTION bug: the serve loops (serve_http_until/serve_https_until/serve_control_https_until in crates/oraclemcp-core/src/http/serve.rs) set the LISTENER non-blocking for shutdown polling. On Windows (Winsock), a socket returned by accept() INHERITS the listeners non-blocking mode (on Unix accepted sockets are always blocking). The per-connection code is blocking-with-timeout (SO_RCVTIMEO/SO_SNDTIMEO), which a non-blocking socket silently ignores while returning WouldBlock immediately -> every TLS handshake aborted instantly (mapped to TimedOut) and transport worker permits released before ever being observed held. Net: the MCP HTTP/HTTPS/control server was non-functional on Windows. Fixed f9f00b0: restore_accepted_socket_blocking() (set_nonblocking(false)) on every accepted socket in all three loops; no-op on Unix; continue-on-error is fail-safe; mTLS/capacity fail-closed gating unchanged. Linux http 155/0; confirmed on the Windows lane (run 5219cb8, 0 fail).
close_reason: Fixed f9f00b0 (restore blocking on accepted sockets). Confirmed green on the Windows CI lane.

title: F-DC2 (High): DSN SSL_SERVER_CERT_DN never reaches the verifier
description: A cert DN pinned in the connect descriptor (SSL_SERVER_CERT_DN) is silently dropped and never reaches the TLS verifier (crates/oracledb/src/lib.rs around 2906), so cert-DN pinning does not actually pin - the highest new-security finding. Fix: thread the DSN cert-DN through to the rustls verifier and enforce it. Discriminating test: a connection whose server DN does not match the pinned DN MUST be refused; a matching one accepted (golden wire-bytes per auth mode). Leads the driver TLS batch. Patch-safe. Acceptance: mismatch refused.
close_reason: DSN-merged cert-DN now reaches the rustls verifier (lib.rs Connection::connect); mismatched DN refused, matching accepted (sabotage-verified tests f_dc2_*). Patch-safe (semver clean). Full driver gate green. Landed rust-oracledb be26a50.

title: F-DC3 (Med): DN_MATCH=OFF handling
description: Reconcile DN_MATCH=OFF semantics in the connect descriptor with the enforced cert-DN path from DC2 so that turning DN matching off is explicit and audited, never an accidental bypass. Fix + test. Patch-safe. Acceptance: DN_MATCH=OFF behaves per spec and is covered by a test; interacts correctly with DC2.
close_reason: DN_MATCH=OFF now explicit + audited (obs_warn! in tls.rs) rather than silently ignored; test f_dc3_dn_match_off_deliberately_skips. Patch-safe. Full driver gate green. Landed rust-oracledb be26a50.

title: F-DC4 (Med): hardcoded 20s TLS handshake timeout (GH#14 class)
description: The TLS handshake uses a hardcoded ~20s timeout and connect_timeout_seconds is rejected for full Oracle Net descriptors (server bead yb7m overlaps). Fix: honor the configured connect timeout for descriptor-form DSNs on the connect handshake. Fix + test. Patch-safe. Acceptance: a configured short connect timeout is honored for a full descriptor; test proves it.
close_reason: TLS handshake honors configured connect timeout for full descriptors (handshake_timeout, tls.rs+lib.rs, pub(crate) = patch-safe); tests tls_handshake_honors_a_short_caller_supplied_timeout + full_descriptor_connect_timeout. Full driver gate + semver-checks clean. Landed rust-oracledb abc4dd3.

title: F-PY1 (High): pyshim NUMBER int-vs-float by value not scale
description: The pyshim classifies a NUMBER as int vs float by the VALUE rather than the column SCALE (crates/oracledb-pyshim/src/convert.rs around 1262), so NUMBER(10,2)=5.00 wrongly becomes an int, diverging from python-oracledb. Fix: classify by declared scale. Discriminating test: assert the Python type is float for a scaled NUMBER even when the value is integral. Patch-safe. Acceptance: parity with reference on scaled NUMBERs.
close_reason: pyshim NUMBER int/float now classified by declared column SCALE (number_classifies_as_python_int) not row value, matching python-oracledb metadata.pyx:112-133; scale/precision threaded from ColumnMetadata via cursor/dbobject; discriminating test; patch-safe (semver clean, pyshim publish=false); driver gate green + maturin builds. Full live differential deferred to OCI cluster. Landed rust-oracledb 25ee76e.

title: F-PY2 (High): pyshim Decimal to f64 lossy conversion
description: The pyshim converts Decimal values through f64, losing precision vs python-oracledb which preserves decimal exactness. Fix: preserve decimal fidelity on the pyshim path (avoid the f64 round-trip). Discriminating test: a high-precision decimal survives round-trip exactly. Patch-safe. Acceptance: no precision loss; test proves exactness.
close_reason: Verified already reference-correct vs python-oracledb 4.0.1: pyshim NUMBER->Decimal path builds Decimal(text) with no f64 detour (matches converters.pyx:351-358). No lossy conversion exists; no code change needed.

title: F-PY3/4/5: pyshim parity-harness hardening
description: Harden the python-oracledb differential parity harness (PY3, PY4, PY5 from plan section 28). Section 27.6 V5 requires the harness assert VALUES (not just types) before any parity number can be re-certified - a wrong-type PASS is the stale-CONFIRMED failure mode. Scope: the specific PY3/PY4/PY5 conversions plus the harness assertion-shape fix. Acceptance: harness catches a wrong-type/wrong-value injected regression.
close_reason: Fully implemented and independently rechecked for PY3/PY4/PY5: exact type/value parity, negative interval flooring, GIL-safe cancellation/reuse, and injected wrong-type/wrong-value regressions are covered. title: F-DI1 (Med): held-execute double-apply
description: A held/approved oracle_execute can be applied twice (crates/oraclemcp/src/dispatch/mod.rs around 11082) - same integrity class as publish-before-persist. Fix: single-use / durable write-ahead idempotency so an approved execute applies exactly once even across retry/replay. Discriminating test: replay the same approved token twice - the second MUST be refused. Patch-safe. Acceptance: exactly-once enforced.
close_reason: Held/approved execute now single-use/idempotent (dispatch/mod.rs) - replay refused; discriminating test in dispatch/tests.rs. Full oraclemcp gate green (test 90 suites, 0 failed). Landed this commit.

title: F-MET (Med): metric-cardinality DoS
description: Unbounded metric label cardinality lets a caller explode telemetry memory (metric-cardinality DoS). Fix: bound/allow-list label values so attacker-controlled inputs cannot create unbounded series. Discriminating test: many distinct label inputs stay under a bounded series count. Patch-safe. Acceptance: cardinality bounded; test proves the cap.
close_reason: Metric label cardinality bounded/allow-listed (metrics.rs) so attacker-controlled inputs cannot create unbounded series; test proves the cap. Full oraclemcp gate green. Landed this commit.

title: 0.6.0 Interactive & Always-On — release train 0.6.0→0.6.1→0.6.2
description:
# Epic: oraclemcp 0.6.0 "Interactive & Always-On" (release TRAIN 0.6.0 → 0.6.1 → 0.6.2)

Source of truth at beading time: PLAN_0_6_0_INTERACTIVE_ALWAYS_ON.md v3.18 (2787 lines). These beads
elaborate that plan and are intended to be SELF-CONTAINED — you should not need to reopen the markdown.

## What we are building (TL;DR)
oraclemcp becomes ONE guarded always-on service + ONE versioned schema-first protocol + MANY front-ends,
with a first-class per-principal HTTP **LaneRuntime** as the internal security & concurrency boundary.
Two access modes, decoupled:
- **stdio — UNCHANGED.** Each agent spawns its own process → own connection/level/DB. Single-principal
  per process, already correct. DO NOT TOUCH IT (non-goal N10).
- **http — UPGRADED** to true per-lane concurrency. One persistent service; each caller resolves to a
  **lane** bound to a **verified Subject** (never caller-supplied), with its own DB/profile, operating
  level, single-use grant store, request budget, audit context, cancellation. Many agents + a human →
  many databases at once, isolated. The **web dashboard** is the human face.

## The release train (decision D1 — one plan, beaded together, built continuously, NO deferral)
- **0.6.0 — Foundation (safety core + usable product):** driver 0.5.1 validation (WP-A), #4/#3/#2
  (WP-B/C/R), **WP-N lane foundation**, WP-P protocol, WP-S service, WP-E/F installer/distribution,
  WP-G core + `om doctor`, WP-H release. Dashboard = the READ-ONLY control plane (Overview/Sessions/
  Audit/Doctor; 2D first; skinnable seam in place).
- **0.6.1 — Interactive dashboard:** full views + the governed Workbench (Safe SQL, governed edit loop,
  live compile, schema browser, global search) + plsql-intelligence wiring + version history.
- **0.6.2 — Advanced/wow:** Change-Review board ("PR for PL/SQL") + the 3D Orrery skin + migration export.
Train membership is carried as a label (train:0.6.0 / train:0.6.1 / train:0.6.2) on every bead.

## Throughline (NON-NEGOTIABLE safety invariant — AGENTS.md "do not weaken")
Every face goes through the SAME fail-closed core: classifier → per-lane ceiling → preview/confirm →
rollback-by-default → audit. Identity is a verified Subject (dashboard login / OAuth sub / mTLS fp /
local pairing / OS user) — NEVER self-supplied in tool args. Loopback hub; dashboard requires local
pairing/auth even on loopback; no foreign-config mutation; no raw PTY/free-SQL terminal; secrets are
external refs; uncertain DB outcomes poison/quarantine the connection. Ladder: READ_ONLY < READ_WRITE
< DDL < ADMIN. Never bypass the classifier, never exceed a profile max_level, never make a protected
profile writable, never auto-commit DML, never admit an unproven statement.

## Ground truth that shapes the work (verified — §1, §4-GT)
- Repo at 0.4.1; `oracledb = "=0.5.1"` ALREADY pinned (WP-A = validation, NOT a version bump).
- The fail-closed GUARD ALREADY SHIPS in crates/oraclemcp-guard (classifier 104KB, OperatingLevel
  ladder + DangerLevel, 3-valued purity.rs with a SideEffectOracle port, stepup.rs confirm-token,
  token.rs AllowOnceStore single-use grants). **WP-N builds the per-lane LANE layer OVER the existing
  guard — it does NOT re-implement the classifier.**
- `#4` timeout_ms is already threaded through collect_all_rows/fetch_cursor/read_lob (WP-B = verify +
  shared-layer default + bounded ROLLBACK, not re-plumb).
- plsql-intelligence v0.7.0, ~21 PURE engine crates (no tokio/net) → bake in via (1) the SideEffectOracle
  purity port [safety core, 0.6.0-eligible] and (2) the Workbench IDE [0.6.1].
- Runtime: asupersync 0.3.4 (pinned nightly-2026-05-11 for try_trait_v2). Appendix A of the plan is the
  SOURCE-VERIFIED asupersync API reference every WP-N bead must cite by id; Appendix B is the per-WP
  acceptance-test spec (incl. B.13 edge cases). Pure-Rust, #![forbid(unsafe_code)], no tokio/axum/hyper
  (boundary-linted).

## Sub-epics (one per work package, matching plan §10)
WP-A driver validation · WP-B timeout/poison (#4) · WP-C non-lossy serialization (#3) · WP-R routine
exec (#2) · WP-N per-principal LaneRuntime (THE FOUNDATION) · WP-P operator protocol · WP-S persistent
service · WP-W web dashboard · WP-E installer · WP-F distribution · WP-G hardening/docs · WP-H release.

## Phase-0 release-blockers for 0.6.0 (gate the whole train)
CX-I6 capacity spike (justify 16/8/64 lane caps; lane ≈ 2 OS threads), CX-I7 panic-isolation prototype
(catch_unwind/drop/quarantine/audit), CX-C1 durable write-ahead idempotency (no double-execute across
restart). All under WP-N.

## How to work these beads
Front-load WP-N (N8 → N0a → N0 → …) + the Phase-0 spikes. Every implementation bead names its
applicable D15 cheap-change rule in its DoD and carries an Appendix-B test companion. Use `br ready`
to find unblocked work; `bv --robot-next` to pick. Do NOT weaken the safety invariant.
close_reason: 0.6.x RELEASE TRAIN COMPLETE 2026-07-02: v0.6.6 live on all four channels with the full 0.6.0+0.6.1+0.6.2 scope incl. the one-line install/update UX. npm excluded; H5 operator-owned; deferred (OUT) items remain deferred. Endeavour wrapped up.

description: Appendix B.1. Named tests (modality | file): pin_is_0_5_1_and_seam_intact (U/C | Cargo.lock + seam-lint); tstz_round_trips_with_offset (MR/G | type_fidelity + oracledb_contract::contract_type_tstz; invertive MR incl offset); tstz_live (E | live_oracle, --features live-xe); auth_capability_matrix_is_thin_and_redaction_safe (C/G | new doctor_auth.rs; AuthCapabilities::THIN, secret-free); wallet_unsupported_format_is_structured_no_path_leak (U/G | doctor_auth.rs); iam_token_over_non_tcps_is_refused_fail_closed (U | connection.rs, extend); connect_timeout_threads_to_driver (U/C | example_config_parses + oracledb_contract); output_schema_validates_structuredContent (C | mcp_conformance); om_alias_argv0_aware (G | cli.rs). description: Wire the 0.5.1 QueryValue::TimestampTz{year..second,nanosecond,offset_minutes} through the serializer so TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE round-trips WITHOUT dropping the numeric UTC offset (0.5.1 fixed the drop; bind side = DateTime<FixedOffset>/Utc ToSql). title: TEST-HARNESS — structured JSON-line logging + e2e runner scripts (the test standard)
description: The test and logging STANDARD that EVERY test bead inherits (the comprehensive-tests + detailed-logging requirement). (1) UNIT: every implementation bead lands with its unit tests green + its named Appendix-B test. (2) E2E SCRIPTS: runnable scripts under scripts/e2e/ (one per scenario), each accepting --log to emit STRUCTURED JSON-LINE logs to stderr (fields: event, phase one-of setup/act/assert/teardown, ts, duration_ms, lane, subject, sid, profile, level, grant, outcome); a top-level orchestrator runs them in order and aggregates a pass/fail summary. (3) DETERMINISM + REPLAY: lab/concurrency tests use cx.now()/cx.random_u64() (no wall-clock/ambient-rand); on failure emit a CRASHPACK + SEED so the bad run replays (asupersync lab, Appendix A.13). (4) PROD-SAFETY: live/e2e tests env-gated (ORACLEMCP_TEST_* / ORACLEMCP_LIVE_XE=1), never touch a non-test DB; a production DSN/URL blocklist. (5) NO-MOCKS for Score-at-least-8 paths (cross-lane isolation, commit/rollback, grant consumption, audit-append, credential issuance) — real Oracle 23ai / real files; a trait-double is allowed only for deterministic lab interleavings. (6) COVERAGE accounting for conformance (MUST coverage at least 0.95; XFAIL not SKIP; fixture provenance generator+version+cmd). DoD: the JSON-line logger + the scripts/e2e orchestrator + the crashpack/seed emitter exist; every test bead uses them. D15 rule 6 (tests pin contracts). No deps.
close_reason: Added scripts/e2e JSON-line harness, orchestrator, crashpack/seed failure artifacts, live safety gates, conformance coverage/provenance docs, and Rust contract tests; verified dry-run and offline scenarios.

title: DL-9 — concurrency-audit CI lint (The Fourth Instance)
description: A concurrency-audit CI lint enforcing the asupersync audit greps as GATES: block_on outside the one sanctioned lane-bridge module = fail; tokio::spawn or std::sync::Mutex in core = fail; unbounded / mpsc::unbounded channel = fail (every queue bounded); a lane module importing another lane Sender = fail (DL-3); loops missing cx.checkpoint() in lane/handler modules = warn+review; ObligationLeak/FuturelockViolation/RegionCloseTimeout in cargo test output = fail. DoD: lint runs each PR; joins HCI.
close_reason: Implemented the DL-9 concurrency-audit gate: added scripts/oraclemcp_concurrency_lint.sh with production scans for unsanctioned block_on, tokio::spawn, std::sync::Mutex in core, unbounded queues, lane command sender leaks, loop checkpoint review warnings, and cargo-test marker failures for ObligationLeak/FuturelockViolation/RegionCloseTimeout; wired it into PR CI, release gates, and release_preflight; converted remaining production core std::sync::Mutex stores to parking_lot and removed stale poisoned-lock errors; marked existing intentional block_on bridge sites. Verified fmt, clippy, workspace all-target tests with marker scan, cargo deny, boundary, driver seam, sensitive data lint, release_preflight, and git diff --check.

close_reason: Added PR CI ergonomics drift guard for help footer, capabilities schema, exit-code dictionary, and MCP/CLI/dashboard parity matrix.

title: WP-B — Timeout hardening + poison/quarantine (#4)
description: WP-B sub-epic. timeout_ms already threaded through collect_all_rows/fetch_cursor/read_lob (§4-GT.1) — VERIFY + shared-layer default + total wall-clock request budget + bounded ROLLBACK + B1c poison/quarantine. Budget algebra = asupersync Budget.meet (A.5): effective Budget = root.meet(ceiling).meet(deadline) replaces #4's three timers. Poison: any timeout/network-error/cancel-deadline/rollback-failure/CLOSE-WAIT/unknown-COMMIT → conn poisoned, dropped, never pooled, grants revoked, audit outcome class (rolled_back|discarded_uncommitted|commit_in_doubt|unknown_discarded). oracledb signals: connection_disposition()→Reusable|Dead, is_connection_lost(), CallTimeout/ConnectionClosed. Tests = Appendix B.2. Closes #4.
close_reason: WP-B complete: B1 request budget/default timeout/rollback cleanup, B1b bounded collect_all_rows continuation fetches, B1c poison/quarantine, B2 dirty-discard/no poisoned pool reuse, B2.7 acceptance suite with live Oracle fetch and load-soak proof, B4 issue #4 proof comment, and B3 upstream EXPIRE_TIME tracking are all closed.

title: B1 — shared-layer default timeout + total request budget + bounded ROLLBACK
description: Add a shared-layer DEFAULT call timeout (so callers like plsql-mcp using oraclemcp-db directly stop getting None) + a TOTAL wall-clock request budget + a BOUNDED ROLLBACK (and bounded COMMIT for in-doubt). Implement via asupersync Budget.meet (Appendix A.5): effective Budget = service-root.meet(profile-ceiling).meet(per-request-deadline), carrying deadline+poll-quota+cost-quota, propagated to children (tighter, never INFINITE). The driver per-op deadline is Query::timeout/Execute::timeout (one deadline spanning continuation fetches + LOB reads). doctor warns when a timeout is 0/unset. Why: #4 is a hang/timeout bug; the budget algebra unifies the three knobs #4 treated separately. DoD: budget threaded; doctor warn; B.2 budget_meet_replaces_three_timers. Edits connection.rs.
close_reason: Implemented shared 30s DB default timeout, dispatcher RequestBudget meet plumbing, doctor timeout warning, bounded rollback adapter cleanup, docs, API baseline, and tests/gates.

title: B1b — per-batch fetch-loop timeout (single wrap at collect_all_rows)
description: Bound the fetch loop per-batch with ONE wrap at collect_all_rows (timeout_ms is already threaded there per §4-GT.1 — verify + enforce). A slow continuation fetch → typed CallTimeout, never an unbounded loop. Why: an unbounded fetch loop is a hang vector (#4). DoD: B.2 fetch_loop_is_bounded_per_batch (FakeBackend + live).
close_reason: Bound collect_all_rows parent-result continuation fetches with per-batch call timeout, cancel/drain recovery, cursor cleanup, fake timeout proof, and live-gated continuation proof; closeout gates pass.

title: B1c — poison/quarantine contract (uncertain outcome → never reuse)
description: THE safety contract for uncertain DB outcomes. Any timeout / network error / cancellation-deadline / rollback-failure / CLOSE-WAIT / unknown-COMMIT marks the lane connection POISONED → dropped, never pooled, OPEN GRANTS REVOKED, and the audit records the outcome class: rolled_back | discarded_uncommitted | commit_in_doubt | unknown_discarded. Use the driver signals: error.connection_disposition() → Reusable|Dead, is_connection_lost(), CallTimeout vs ConnectionClosed (drain-fail = lost). Run rollback + the audit append as masked/commit_section finalizers (Appendix A.9) so cleanup completes deterministically under shutdown/cancel. Why: prevents a half-known session from leaking state into the next caller (invariant N5/B1c). DoD: B.2 poisoned_conn_is_never_reused + commit_in_doubt_marks_quarantine. Ties to WP-N N5 + B1c lane teardown.
close_reason: Implemented DB session quarantine outcomes, direct dispatcher commit-in-doubt quarantine, lease discard-on-uncertain cleanup, lane panic unknown-discard audit, API baseline/docs, and full gates.

title: B2 — verify dirty-discard + no poisoned reuse in the pool
description: Verify a cancelled/failed pooled call is DISCARDED (dirty), never returned idle for reuse; poisoned conns never re-enter the pool. Why: defense-in-depth for B1c at the pool boundary. DoD: B.2 dirty_discard_no_pool_return (db chaos).
close_reason: Added dirty_discard_no_pool_return pool proof: dirty check-in is not returned to idle, increments discarded, frees capacity only for a fresh replacement slot; full gates pass.

title: B3 — track upstream rust-oracledb #14 (EXPIRE_TIME parsed-not-applied)
description: Track upstream rust-oracledb #14 (EXPIRE_TIME parsed but not applied as TCP keepalive). Independent of the other B work. DoD: a tracking note + a test asserting current behavior (parsed into Description.expire_time) so a future upstream fix is detected. Backlog-ish; do not block #4 close on it.
close_reason: Tracked rust-oracledb#14 current state (open; v0.5.1 honors transport connect timeout but EXPIRE_TIME keepalive remains upstream-owned), added operations note, and added connection::driver_seam::upstream_expire_time_gap_is_parse_visible asserting EXPIRE_TIME parses into Description::expire_time. Checks: fmt, driver_seam tests, clippy -p oraclemcp-db, boundary lint, api lock, sensitive-data lint, diff check.

close_reason: GitHub issue #4 was already closed; added WP-B closeout proof at https://github.com/MuhDur/oraclemcp/issues/4#issuecomment-4846069119. description: Appendix B.2. budget_meet_replaces_three_timers (U/P | cancel_correctness); fetch_loop_is_bounded_per_batch (P | oracledb_contract FakeBackend + live_oracle); poisoned_conn_is_never_reused (L/E | db chaos + load_soak; connection_disposition()==Dead → dropped/grants-revoked/audit-class); commit_in_doubt_marks_quarantine (L | cancel_correctness; drain-fail → ConnectionClosed → quarantine); dirty_discard_no_pool_return (U/E | db chaos). title: WP-C — Non-lossy serialization (#3)
description: WP-C sub-epic. OracleCell.structured carrier; type ARRAY/JSON(OsonValue)/VECTOR(Dense|Sparse)/TSTZ(offset_minutes); object identity (schema,type_name)+typed-unsupported EVERYWHERE (driver has NO Opaque variant — oraclemcp owns the marker; nested object → driver UnsupportedFeature); capped non-default catalog mode; publish OracleCell.structured JSON schema + golden fixtures (C4b); serialization contract-version tag (C5) consumed by dashboard/schema caches. NUMBER stays lossless string by default. Tests = Appendix B.3 + B.13#2 boundary values. Closes #3 + upgrade note.
close_reason: All WP-C child beads are closed: structured OracleCell carrier, typed ARRAY/JSON/VECTOR/TSTZ serialization, typed unsupported/object identity handling, deep-decode catalog mode, schema/goldens, contract-version tagging, acceptance tests, and issue #3 closeout/upgrade note are complete.

title: C0 — OracleCell.structured carrier (+ contract)
description: Add a structured carrier to OracleCell so non-scalar Oracle values survive serialization without lossy flattening. Background §4-GT.1/#3: OracleCell has no structured carrier today; the contract suite (oracledb_contract.rs::contract_type_*) + type_fidelity.rs already pin number-lossless-string / NLS-invariance / unsupported-marker. Why: foundation for C1/C2. DoD: carrier type + a contract test; NUMBER stays lossless string by default (numbers_as_float opt-in only). Edits connection.rs (sequence with C1-C3). Test: B.3.
close_reason: Added OracleCell structured JSON carrier and constructor, serialized structured payloads verbatim ahead of scalar text, and pinned the public contract with unit + oracledb_contract tests while preserving NUMBER lossless-string behavior. Refreshed oraclemcp-db API lock. Verified fmt, clippy, workspace tests, deny, boundary, driver seam, sensitive-data, honesty, api-lock, release_preflight.

title: C1 — typed ARRAY/JSON/VECTOR/TSTZ serialization
description: Serialize the 0.5.1 structured QueryValue variants: Array(Vec<Option<QueryValue>>), Json(OsonValue), Vector(Dense|Sparse × Float32/Float64/Int8/Binary), TimestampTz{offset_minutes}. Invertive MR: serialize→parse→equal. Why: non-lossy #3. DoD: B.3 structured_carrier_round_trips_array_json_vector_tstz (MR + golden + schema) + B.13#2 boundary values (empty/nested array, sparse zero-index, OSON depth-cap±1). Coordinates with A2 (TSTZ).
close_reason: Implemented typed structured serialization for QueryValue Array, Json(OSON), and Vector values in the existing oracledb adapter, using OracleCell::structured instead of lossy Debug/text rendering. The carrier preserves SQL NULL vs OSON null, NUMBER text, raw bytes, DateTime/Interval scalars, nested TSTZ offset_minutes, dense/sparse vectors, binary vectors, and sparse zero indices. Added adapter unit tests plus type_fidelity and shared oracledb_contract coverage for array/json/vector/tstz round-trip JSON shape. Verified fmt, clippy, workspace tests, concurrency lint, deny, boundary, driver seam, sensitive-data, honesty, api-lock, and release_preflight.

title: C2 — object identity + typed-unsupported EVERYWHERE
description: For UDT/object values (driver ObjectValue{schema,type_name,packed_data}) emit (schema,type_name) + a typed-unsupported MARKER; nested object/collection attribute → explicit driver UnsupportedFeature, NEVER a silent flatten. The driver has NO Opaque variant — oraclemcp OWNS the unsupported marker. BFILE = Lob{ora_type_num=114}. Why: honest, never-silent #3. DoD: B.3 object_identity_marker_never_silent (extends existing contract_type_unsupported_*) + B.13#2.
close_reason: Implemented typed unsupported Oracle object markers with schema/type identity and nested object decode coverage; verified gates.

title: C3 — capped non-default catalog (deep-decode) mode
description: A capped, non-default mode for deeper catalog/object decode (row/cell/byte/depth caps) behind an opt-in flag; default stays the safe capped shape. Why: power users may want more without making the default heavy/unsafe. DoD: caps enforced; default unchanged; B.3 + B.13#2 caps±1.
close_reason: Implemented capped structured deep decode mode with query opt-in, row/cell/byte/depth caps, schema docs, and acceptance tests.

title: C4 — close issue #3 + upgrade note
description: Close #3 after C0-C3/C4b land. Ships an UPGRADE NOTE (serialized-shape change is documented, N7 — no silent change). DoD: #3 closed; CHANGELOG upgrade note (coordinate G2).
close_reason: Closed GitHub issue #3 after the structured OracleCell serialization contract, schema/goldens, acceptance tests, and CHANGELOG upgrade note were pushed to main.

title: Tests B.3 + B.13#2 — WP-C serialization acceptance + boundary values
description: Appendix B.3 + B.13#2. number_is_lossless_string / nls_invariance (MR/G | type_fidelity, exist — equivalence MR under NLS changes); structured_carrier_round_trips_array_json_vector_tstz (MR/G/C | type_fidelity + oracledb_contract; invertive MR + goldens + schema); object_identity_marker_never_silent (U/G | contract_type_unsupported_*; nested → UnsupportedFeature, never silent); serialization_contract_version_present_and_consumed (C | mcp_conformance + W7 cache). B.13#2 boundary values: NULL every type; empty/nested Array; Vector::Sparse zero-index; Json depth-cap and cap+1; CLOB byte-cap and cap+1; BLOB base64 len%3 in {0,1,2}; NUMBER 38-digit + negative scale; BinaryDouble +Inf/NaN/-0.0; IntervalDS/YM; TimestampTz offset 0/+-14:00/9-digit/DST. DoD: all green; #3 closeable. Depends C1,C2,C4b,C5.
close_reason: Covered WP-C serialization acceptance and boundary catalog in type_fidelity tests; full gates pass.

title: WP-R — Routine execution (#2)
description: WP-R sub-epic. Non-Deserialize OracleRoutineArg (wraps driver BindValue::{Output,ReturnOutput,ObjectOutput}; driver confirms NO serde bound, no IN-OUT-specific variant — Output covers both; retrieve via ExecuteOutcome::out_binds()). Adapter-internal call_routine, deterministic positional order + COMMIT caveat. Grep-lint: call_routine absent from oraclemcp AND oraclemcp-core public surface (no agent tool, N9). Tests = Appendix B.4. Closes #2.
close_reason: WP-R complete: R1-R4 and B.4 acceptance closed; GitHub issue #2 closed with live proof.

title: R1 — non-Deserialize OracleRoutineArg
description: Add OracleRoutineArg wrapping the driver's BindValue::{Output,ReturnOutput,ObjectOutput} (driver confirms NO serde/Deserialize bound on the bind path; there is NO separate IN-OUT variant — Output covers both). It MUST NOT impl Deserialize (so it can never be populated from an agent tool arg). Why: #2 routine OUT/IN-OUT support without exposing an agent-facing routine tool. DoD: B.4 routine_arg_is_not_deserialize (trybuild compile-fail under crates/oraclemcp-db/tests/ui/). Edits connection.rs.
close_reason: Added non-Deserialize OracleRoutineArg wrapper and trybuild gate

title: R2 — adapter-internal call_routine (deterministic order, COMMIT caveat)
description: Adapter-internal call_routine that binds positional OUT/IN-OUT args, executes, and retrieves OUT values via ExecuteOutcome::out_binds() in DECLARED positional order. Document the COMMIT caveat (a routine may commit internally). adapter-internal ONLY — no agent tool. DoD: B.4 call_routine_out_bind_order_deterministic (FakeBackend + live).
close_reason: Added adapter-internal call_routine with deterministic OUT bind ordering

title: R3 — grep-lint: call_routine absent from agent surface
description: A grep-lint (honesty_grep-style) asserting call_routine is ABSENT from the oraclemcp AND oraclemcp-core public surfaces (it is adapter-internal in oraclemcp-db only). Why: enforce 'no agent-facing arbitrary routine tool' (non-goal N9). DoD: B.4 call_routine_absent_from_agent_surface; lint joins the gate set.
close_reason: Added oraclemcp_agent_surface_lint gate and cargo-test mirror proving call_routine stays out of oraclemcp/oraclemcp-core agent-facing surfaces; wired it into CI boundary and release preflight. Verified fmt, clippy, workspace tests, cargo deny, release preflight, boundary, driver-seam, honesty, sensitive-data, concurrency, and focused agent-surface checks.

title: R4 — close issue #2
description: Close #2 (routine OUT/IN-OUT) after R1-R3 land + live verify. DoD: #2 closed with proof.
close_reason: Closed GitHub issue #2 after adding mixed input/output routine adapter support and live Oracle proof.

title: Tests B.4 — WP-R routine acceptance suite
description: Appendix B.4. routine_arg_is_not_deserialize (U compile-fail | new oraclemcp-db/tests/ui/, trybuild; OracleRoutineArg wraps BindValue::{Output,ReturnOutput,ObjectOutput}, no Deserialize); call_routine_out_bind_order_deterministic (U/E | oracledb_contract FakeBackend + live_oracle; ExecuteOutcome::out_binds() positional order); call_routine_absent_from_agent_surface (C grep-lint | honesty_grep; absent from oraclemcp + oraclemcp-core). DoD: all green; #2 closeable. Depends R1,R2,R3.
close_reason: B.4 acceptance suite is green: routine_arg_is_not_deserialize trybuild passed in cargo test --workspace, contract_call_routine_returns_ordered_out_binds passed in oracledb_contract, live_contract_call_routine_out_bind_order_deterministic passed against local Oracle Free with live-xe, and call_routine_absent_from_agent_surface passed via honesty_grep plus oraclemcp_agent_surface_lint.

title: WP-N — Per-principal HTTP LaneRuntime (THE FOUNDATION)
description: WP-N sub-epic — THE FOUNDATION; highest priority + hardest review. A per-lane LANE layer OVER the existing guard. Lane = OS thread owning {asupersync current-thread runtime+reactor, !Send Oracle conn, lease, level, single-use grants, request budget, audit ctx, cancel} + a Send control mailbox; registry holds only Send LaneHandles (mailbox+generation:u64+cancel+join+status), NEVER the conn. Transport = MT asupersync runtime; conn ops marshaled to the owning lane thread. Verified against asupersync 0.3.4 (Appendix A): channels A.3, cancel A.4, supervision/AppSpec A.7, name-leases A.8, combinators A.9 (bulkhead per-DB ceiling — NO asupersync Pool exists), Send/!Send seam A.10. Carries N9 (set in stone), §4-R safety (N-S1..N-S7), missing foundations (FN0-FN3), C-arch (C-1..C-6), Phase-0 release-blockers (CX-I6/I7/C1). Tests = Appendix B.5 + N9 + B.13#1/#3. stdio untouched (N10).
close_reason: Completed the WP-N foundation scope: all per-principal LaneRuntime child beads are closed, including lane ownership, per-lane connection/profile/level/grants, lifecycle/capacity/fairness, audit identity/evidence, security hardening, capability narrowing, panic/cancel/idempotency, and B.13/N9 contract coverage.

title: N8 — interim guard: 2nd concurrent principal fails closed (pre-lane)
description: INTERIM SAFETY (do first). Until N0a-N3 land, the http server must run single-principal OR FAIL CLOSED on a second concurrent principal — never silently share level/DB/grants. Background §1.1: today OracleDispatcher is one AsyncMutex<DispatcherState> with one conn/profile/level shared via Arc across HTTP threads, and confirm-tokens are HMAC over (profile,level,sql) with NO caller identity → cross-redeemable. That is a latent multi-client hole masked only by single-principal use. DoD: a 2nd concurrent principal is rejected (typed) or single-principal is enforced; B.5 I1 / N9-I1. Closes the latent hole now; superseded by N0a-N3.
close_reason: Implemented pre-lane HTTP single-principal fail-closed guard: OAuth principals derive redacted stable keys from validated claims, served HTTP config enables SinglePrincipalGuard, and second concurrent OAuth subject receives typed HTTP 409 without token/subject leakage. Verification: cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo test -p oraclemcp --test e2e_http_oauth -- --nocapture; cargo test -p oraclemcp-core http::tests:: --lib; cargo test -p oraclemcp http_cli_oauth_builds_enforced_transport_config; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace; cargo deny check; driver/honesty/boundary/api-lock scripts; git diff --check.

title: N5 — lane lifecycle + MCP Streamable-HTTP semantics
description: Distinguish: RESPONSE-STREAM DISCONNECT (does NOT cancel — the call continues to completion / explicit CancelledNotification / budget expiry), EXPLICIT cancellation, HTTP DELETE MCP-Session-Id (terminate the lane: rollback if dirty, revoke grants, release/discard conn — as a MESSAGE to the owning lane thread, C-3), idle TTL, service shutdown (bounded drain). Dropped SSE can reconnect/resume or get a typed expired result. Failed/cancelled/uncertain → quarantine (B1c), never reuse. Record the structured CancelReason/CancelKind (Appendix A.4) in the audit chain (User=DELETE/disconnect, Timeout=B1, Shutdown=drain). DoD: N9-E1..E5 + N9-J (MCP compliance). Depends N1, N4.
notes: N5 implementation complete. Streamable HTTP DELETE invalidates the session, clears buffered replay events, invokes the stateful lifecycle hook, and terminates the lane via an owning-lane Close mailbox command. Listener shutdown drains request workers and then closes all stateful lanes with server_shutdown; runtime drop closes with runtime_drop; lifecycle close releases N4 capacity permits. OracleDispatcher cleanup drops elevation, increments grant generation, clears execute grants/compat grants/patch previews, resets the read-only backstop, rolls back the primary session, and discards the connection by lane exit. Lifecycle audit is structured: audit schema v2 includes hash-covered cancel.kind/cancel.reason, lane_lifecycle records use SQL preview LANE_CLOSE, and close reasons map to User/session_delete, Timeout/idle_timeout, Shutdown/server_shutdown, or Shutdown/runtime_drop. GET replay detects cursors older than the retained per-session ring and returns typed 410 stream_cursor_expired. C-6 proof is implemented: an initialized idle lane parked between mailbox commands wakes and closes from another OS thread; docs record the split between idle mailbox wake and in-flight Oracle call timeout/driver break/socket close plus quarantine/discard. C-1 is implemented: stateful HTTP idle TTL reaping updates last_seen on activity, removes expired replay buffers, and sends Timeout close messages through the lifecycle hook without touching the Oracle connection. Verified by cargo fmt --all -- --check, focused lifecycle/replay/audit/wake/reaper/config tests, cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --lib, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test --workspace --quiet, cargo deny check, scripts/oraclemcp_api_lock.sh, scripts/oraclemcp_boundary_lint.sh, scripts/sensitive_data_lint.sh, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Implemented lane lifecycle and Streamable HTTP semantics: DELETE/session shutdown/runtime-drop/idle-timeout lane termination via owning-lane messages, structured hash-covered lifecycle cancel audit metadata, typed replay cursor expiry, cross-thread idle wake proof, and idle TTL reaping. Verified with focused tests plus fmt, clippy, workspace tests, deny, API lock, boundary lint, sensitive-data lint, and diff check.

title: N6 — stop serializing reads on a global lock (read-worker lanes)
description: Stateless reads run CONCURRENTLY via bounded read-worker lanes (each its own reactor+conn) / per-reactor shards — NOT one shared cross-reactor OraclePool (reactor-affine, CX-1). A read lane is per (Subject, profile, DB) — never a cross-principal pool (CX-C4). Stateful work serializes only within its own lane. DoD: N9-C1 (readers no head-of-line). Depends N0a runtime.
close_reason: Routed served stateless generated metadata reads through bounded per-principal/profile read-worker lanes with profile-switch invalidation; HTTP startup no longer opens the unused shared stateless pool while stdio/direct pool behavior remains intact.

title: N7 — per-Subject audit identity + DB evidence
description: Every action records its VERIFIED Subject (no generic HumanOperator); NO caller-supplied identity (agent_identity/operator_name/label) can change it. Live DB records also capture the redacted DB fingerprint (db_unique_name/service/instance), Oracle username/proxy user, and session evidence (sid, serial#, client_identifier, module, action) when visible; absence → db_evidence_unavailable, not a failure. Depends on FN2 (Subject-aware audit rebuild). DoD: N9-G1..G4 + the headline negative test (a fake agent_identity cannot change the audit subject). Depends N0.
close_reason: Threaded audit records to server-derived subjects, attached best-effort DB evidence under audit schema 3, rejected caller-supplied identity spoofing, and verified full gates.

title: FN2 — Subject-aware audit rebuild (+DB evidence, schema bump)
description: FOUNDATION (before N7). Extend AuditRecord with a structured Subject{kind,stable_id,authn_method,client_id,thumbprint} + DB-evidence columns; preserve/upgrade verify; bump the audit schema_version (additive/format-versioned, never rewrite — DOC-5). Background §4-R: today audit is a free-form agent_identity:String + a single global chain (also a cross-lane p99 serialization point). The audit-chain writer must be transaction-style (build entry in a local, append-and-swap) so a panic mid-append can't corrupt the chain (DL-5). DoD: structured Subject in records; verify still passes across the bump; concurrent appends → valid chain (N9-G2). Prereq for N7/N-S2/W5/G9.
close_reason: Added audit schema_version=2 with structured AuditSubject and optional DbEvidence, kept signed v1 verification compatible, hash-covered v2 subject/evidence fields, hardened audit append buffering/poison handling, and added concurrent append chain verification.

title: FN3 — per-lane telemetry (before W3)
description: FOUNDATION (before W3). Add a per-lane/per-subject label dimension across counters + histograms; a 'blocked' counter; an 'active_lanes' gauge; a per-lane MCP-request latency histogram. Background §4-R: telemetry is process-global bare atomics today. Metrics = a live in-memory ring + append-only per-day history files (FORK-2/D14 — NO TSDB; OTLP export optional). DoD: per-lane metrics emitted; W3 consumes them. Feeds W3/W4 + doctor (DL-10).
close_reason: Implemented per-lane request, blocked, latency, and active-lane telemetry across operator JSON, Prometheus, and OTLP with redacted subject hashes.

title: N-S1 (CRITICAL) — retire the legacy deterministic confirm-MAC
description: CRITICAL. Remove confirmation_mac / verify_commit_confirmation (HMAC over (profile,level,sql), NO caller/lane/nonce — dispatch/mod.rs::confirmation_mac + the compat {sql,token} acceptance, CX-5). The single-use server-side grant (N3) becomes the ONLY confirm path. Why: any lane can recompute another lane's token today → a forgeable cross-lane channel survives even when N9-A4 passes. DoD: confirmation_mac removed; B.5 confirm_mac_retired_cross_lane_recompute_rejected (a lane cannot recompute another's confirm). Depends N3.
close_reason: Retired legacy deterministic confirmation MAC path; session/execute/compile/patch confirmations now use single-use lane-bound grants, with cross-lane rejection tests and docs updated.

title: N-S2 (CRITICAL) — audit ALL committing tools (compile/patch) + classify in compile
description: CRITICAL. Thread AuditCtx into oracle_compile_object + oracle_patch_source and run the classifier in compile. Why: today these commit DDL with NO audit → 'audit = durable record' (N3) is false and W5/W8b/G9 are empty. SEC-3: audit-write failure ⇒ FAIL-CLOSED (refuse the privileged action; the audit sink already fsyncs BEFORE execute — state the contract so no future change makes it best-effort). DoD: every committing tool (execute/compile/patch) appends an audit record; B.5 every_committing_tool_audits; SEC-3 negative test (audit-write-fail refuses). Depends FN2.
close_reason: Implemented N-S2 critical audit coverage: oracle_compile_object now classifies generated compile statements, threads AuditCtx, appends Pending before any DB execute, and appends signed Succeeded/Failed outcomes; oracle_patch_source now appends Pending before generated DDL execution and fails closed on audit append failure before touching Oracle. create_or_replace/deploy_ddl already delegate through audited execute_sql. Added audit_wiring tests for compile + patch Pending/outcome chains and audit-write-failure refusing both before DB execution; updated threat-model evidence. Gates: fmt, oraclemcp tests, workspace clippy -D warnings, workspace tests, cargo deny check, API lock, boundary lint, sensitive-data lint, git diff --check.

title: N-S3 — gate monitor / v$session / db-evidence SQL through the classifier
description: Route the monitor/db-health/sample_rows/dictionary reads + the new operator v$session/W4/G9/monitor_profile SQL through a READ-classified gate + SET TRANSACTION READ ONLY + audit (system Subject), an ALLOW-LIST of parameterized statements (bound params, no caller predicates), output-capped. Why: D6 (same fail-closed core for every face) is currently FALSE for these read-only DBA tools. DoD: these paths are classified+audited+allow-listed; redact other sessions' v$session (N-S6). Depends N0.
close_reason: Gated generated metadata, health, sample, and dictionary reads through classifier allowlist, read-only backstop, and system-subject audit

title: N-S4 — mTLS peer-cert plumbing → CertFingerprint Subject
description: In handle_tls_connection read peer_certificates(), SHA-256 the leaf DER → CertFingerprint; thread it + peer_addr through HttpRequest→DispatchContext→Subject; require a leaf-fp→registered-client map (any CA-signed cert != identity). Why: today the 'mTLS-fp Subject' source does not exist (http.rs never reads peer_certificates). Enables one of the D11 Subject sources. DoD: leaf-fp Subject available; a cert not in the map fails closed. Feeds N7.
close_reason: Implemented mTLS leaf fingerprint principal plumbing with registered-client fail-closed tests.

title: N-S5 — lease stamps server-derived Subject + re-asserts ALTER SESSION allowlist
description: When N0 wires the lease, agent_identity→v$session MUST be the SERVER Subject stable_id (never a tool arg), and the new path RE-CHECKS is_allowed_alter_session itself (do not inherit the lease's trust-the-caller contract, lease.rs:427-437). Why: the dormant oracle_session takes agent_identity as an arg — it must never become the HTTP trust source (D11). DoD: v$session client identity = server Subject; ALTER SESSION re-validated on the lease path. Depends N0.
close_reason: Stamped lease acquire from DispatchContext principal and rechecked ALTER SESSION allowlist in LeaseManager; verified fmt, clippy, tests, deny, lints, and release preflight.

title: N0a(i) — lane runtime/thread ownership (tear out shared dispatch_runtime)
description: STRUCTURAL long-pole. Replace today's single shared current-thread dispatch_runtime (server.rs) with THREAD-PER-LANE: each lane = its own OS thread running its own asupersync current-thread Runtime + reactor + pinned !Send Oracle conn (the proven load/soak pattern; non-Send dispatch futures become a non-issue). Transport = a MT asupersync runtime (Appendix A.1; current_thread()=worker_threads(1) so a lane ≈ 2 OS threads — budget that in CX-I6). C-arch: the LaneRuntime is a REGISTRY OF SEND HANDLES (mailboxes), NOT a map of connections — registry-capability NAME LEASES (Appendix A.8), the leased entry is the Send LaneHandle {mpsc::Sender, generation:u64, cancel, JoinHandle, status}, never the conn. Read path = bounded read-worker lanes / per-reactor shards (NOT one shared OraclePool — reactor-affine, CX-1; and asupersync has NO Pool, A.11). Why: Oracle state is per-session; one shared executor funnels everything. DoD: no HTTP/operator stateful dispatch receives process-global DispatcherState; two blocked lanes don't block a third (N9-C1); LeaseManager reached only via LaneRuntime. Cite Appendix A.1/A.8/A.10.
close_reason: Implemented N0a lane ownership: removed OracleMcpServer's shared dispatch_runtime, added core LaneRuntime as a Send mailbox handle around a dedicated OS-thread/current-thread asupersync runtime+reactor, cloned owned DispatchContext across the mailbox, and wired served HTTP stateful dispatch through served-http-stateful while leaving stdio on the direct dispatcher. Added blocked_lane_does_not_block_another_lane proof that a blocked lane does not block an independent lane; DL-1 prototype/ADR remains as Phase-0 decision evidence.
