description: Codex round-4 finding 2: D1 + §7 put the READ-ONLY CONTROL PLANE in 0.6.0 (Overview/Sessions/Audit/Doctor/Capacity, 2D + skinnable seam, browser-safe auth), but H2 depended on NO WP-W test, so 0.6.0 could tag with no dashboard. This is the 0.6.0 dashboard release gate (depended-on by H2). Covers the read-only views + their §4-WD.5 contracts + browser-safe auth (W1: CSRF/Origin/cookie/no-localStorage) + skinnable 2D/no-WebGL fallback + a11y, with TEST-HARNESS JSON-line logging. The Workbench/PR-board/Orrery (0.6.1/0.6.2) stay gated by their own train test beads, NOT this one. DoD: the 0.6.0 read-only dashboard is fully tested + green before tag. Depends W0,W1,W3,W4,W5,W6,W-Sessions,W-Capacity,WD-Identity,WD-Skin,om-doctor,TEST-HARNESS.
close_reason: Added 0.6.0 read-only dashboard acceptance E2E gate with structured logging, dashboard source-contract test, harness inventory/docs, and green full gates.

title: W2 — profiles + config draft/apply workflow
description: The dashboard NEVER edits the live file in place. Stage a DRAFT → validate with the real strict config loader + redaction tests → optional doctor --profile → REDACTED diff → atomic-rename write with timestamped backup → ask the service to reload (S5) → rollback restores+revalidates. Uses the SCFG backend (shared with setup --write). Sanitized refs (env:/file:/keyring:); NO literal secrets on protected profiles; NO secrets shown/stored (D18). DoD: B.8 config_draft_apply_atomic_rollback. Depends W1, SCFG (cross).
close_reason: Implemented dashboard config draft/apply workflow over existing ConfigOpsBackend with redacted preview, atomic apply, rollback, and S5 profile-drain reload hook.

title: W3 — Overview dashboard (metrics/latency/error/blocked + active-lanes gauge + event log)
description: Tool/latency/error/blocked metrics + active-lanes gauge + a live event log via SSE. Consumes the per-lane telemetry (FN3) + streaming SSE (FN1) + the SSE+watch/broadcast real-time model (section 4-WD.6: SSE invalidates the TanStack Query cache; Last-Event-ID resume; client-side timers). Metrics = a live in-memory ring + append-only per-day history files (FORK-2/D14; OTLP export optional). DoD: dashboards render live; B.8. Depends W1, FN3 (cross), FN1 (cross).
close_reason: Implemented the Overview dashboard with operator metrics, lane gauges, blocked/error/latency summaries, and a live SSE event log consuming per-lane telemetry.

title: W4 — connection health panel
description: pool/latency/role/open-mode/standby/write-posture; DB-native status via the lane self-check or a least-privilege monitor_profile (D5). A missing privilege shows a visible DEGRADED state (monitoring_unavailable), never an error loop or a privilege escalation. Routed through the N-S3 read-classified gate. DoD: health renders + degrades gracefully; B.8. Depends W1, NS3 (cross).
close_reason: Implemented Health dashboard panel using operator health, metrics, and redacted oracle_connection_info with monitoring_unavailable degradation.

title: W5 — audit timeline (+ DB evidence, proof-bundle export)
description: Audit timeline (hash-chain + verify); filter by subject/level/tool; DB-evidence columns + proof-bundle export when available. REDACTION (I3, reconciled with N-S6): the operator (review authority, D17) sees the full SQL TEXT; BIND VALUES are REDACTED BY DEFAULT and require an explicit, AUDITED operator REVEAL (no operator-sees-everything bypass; that old FORK-1 wording is superseded). Scoped non-operators see only their own actions (D5). The signed chain uses sql_sha256, never inlined bind literals (N-S6). DoD: timeline + verify + filtered export; binds redacted by default with audited reveal; B.8. Depends W1, FN2 (audit), N-S6 (redaction newtypes), D17 (operator authority).
close_reason: Implemented audit-tail filters, structural hash-chain proof, DB-evidence columns, and redacted proof-bundle export on /operator/v1/audit-tail; dashboard Audit view now renders filtered timeline/proof export. Timeline/proof exports are allow-list-only: subject hashes only, sql_sha256 only, no SQL preview/text, no bind values; keyed MAC verification remains the offline audit verify path with signing keys. Verified with npm run check --prefix web; cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace; cargo deny check; sensitive_data_lint; api_lock; boundary_lint; driver_seam_lint; honesty_grep; release_preflight.

title: W6 — operating-level control (per-lane elevate/drop)
description: Per-lane preview then elevate then drop, with TTL + confirm — through oracle_set_session_level on the calling lane only (N2). UI shows the target level, TTL, gate decision, and confirm token. DoD: elevate/drop within the profile ceiling; B.8. Depends W1, N2 (cross).
close_reason: Implemented dashboard per-lane operating-level preview/apply/drop over /operator/v1/session/set-level with lane-bound route coverage and frontend type/build verification.

title: W7 — schema/object explorer + bounded metadata cache
description: Schema/object explorer + a bounded metadata cache keyed by DB fingerprint + profile/user + visible schema + serialization-contract version (C5); TTL + byte caps; DDL/commit invalidates affected objects; profile switch invalidates the lane view; NEVER caches result rows/secrets; a hidden profile metadata is never shared with an exposed one. DoD: explorer + cache with correct invalidation; B.8. Depends W1, C5 (cross).
close_reason: Implemented dashboard schema/object explorer with bounded metadata cache and operator metadata routing

title: W8 — Safe SQL Workbench (governed IDE; guard IS the feature)
description: A governed PL/SQL IDE (SQL-Navigator-class) for the human-in-the-loop — NOT a terminal. KEY THESIS: NO fail-closed exception — the human is just another authenticated Subject on its own lane; the guard becomes the feature (preview/diff/audit/snapshot-undo make it safer AND more powerful than SQL Navigator). Four explicit modes: classify_only, read_query, dml_preview_confirm, ddl_plan_confirm — ALL through the same classifier then per-lane ceiling then preview/confirm then rollback/commit then audit path agents use. Read-only default. DDL DISABLED unless the profile sets dashboard_ddl_workbench=true with a matching ceiling; never on protected. WD-RULE-1: DML = Applied (uncommitted; Rollback/TTL-auto-revert; does NOT undo autonomous txns/sequence.NEXTVAL/side-effecting triggers/UTL_*) vs DDL = Applied (committed; Revert re-applies the prior source snapshot ONLY for source-replaceable objects — PL/SQL + views; destructive DDL has no automatic undo). UI shows required level, ceiling, DB fingerprint, lane id, audit subject, row/byte caps, preview impact, and why-blocked + safe alternatives. NO raw PTY/SQLcl shell/arbitrary PL/SQL (N9). RELEASE-GATED behind dashboard_workbench until no-bypass + audit/idempotency tests pass — else the dashboard ships WITHOUT it, unadvertised. DoD: B.8 workbench_no_bypass_guard_is_the_feature (conformance + equivalence MR: workbench-classify equals agent-classify for identical SQL). Depends W1, P1 (cross), WP-B (cross), WP-C (cross).
close_reason: Implemented the browser Workbench over the existing governed MCP path: classify/preview forwards to oracle_preview_sql, read mode forwards to oracle_query, DML rollback/commit forwards to oracle_execute with dashboard CSRF/action tickets and idempotency, and browser DDL/Admin apply is release-gated before dispatch while preview remains available. Added B.8 boundary tests workbench_no_bypass_guard_is_the_feature and dashboard_workbench_ddl_apply_is_release_gated; the UI surfaces required level/danger/lane/subject/sql/audit facts and raw redacted operator envelopes without browser storage. Verified: npm run check --prefix web; cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace; cargo deny check; scripts/sensitive_data_lint.sh; scripts/oraclemcp_api_lock.sh; scripts/oraclemcp_boundary_lint.sh; scripts/oraclemcp_driver_seam_lint.sh; scripts/oraclemcp_honesty_grep.sh; scripts/release_preflight.sh; git diff --check.

title: WP-E — Installer (broad, explicit-consent)
description: WP-E sub-epic. install.sh/install.ps1 (prebuilt musl, SHA256-terminal + cosign verify-blob +verify-attestation, completions, service install ONLY with --service/consent + /readyz gate, dry-run prints every file/unit) + npx + binstall + brew + winget + Docker + MCP registry. Per-client SCOPED creds (unique id + scoped bearer per client, NOT shared; hashed-at-rest, shown-once; revoke/rotate one without others). E0 web-build CI feeds all targets. NO service/client mutation without explicit flag; npx no postinstall side effects. Tests = Appendix B.9. Secrets external refs (D18).
close_reason: Completed the WP-E installer/distribution scope: web-build fan-out, seven-target release artifacts, verified Unix/Windows installers, offline/uninstall/idempotency, scoped client credentials, setup --write, npm/binstall/Homebrew/winget metadata, installer CI smoke, and merged Rust+dashboard release SBOM coverage are all closed in child beads.

title: E0 — web-build CI job (npm ci + vite build → dist; feeds all targets)
description: A web-build CI job (npm ci + vite build → a dist artifact) feeding checks, pinned-nightly, all 6 build targets, and Docker (build JS once, fan to all). Pull rust-embed default-features-off (no web-framework feature → no boundary-lint trip); add it to deny.toml. Pin Node + npm ci for reproducible attestation. Tie to D13 dashboard-bundle (product artifacts ship the bundle; cargo install stays Node-free). DoD: dist built once + embedded; preflight checks embedded-dist-present + content-hash, package-lock.json, no git:/file: JS deps, npm audit high/critical, .crate size. Feeds W0 + H2.
close_reason: Implemented E0 web-build and embedded bundle pipeline: added pinned Node/npm-lock Vite build inputs, rust-embed default-features-off dashboard-bundle feature, native HTTP dashboard fallback outside /operator/v1, release/preflight checks for package-lock/no git-or-file JS deps/npm audit/dist hash/.crate size, CI/release/Docker artifact fan-out, and product builds with the embedded bundle while default cargo-install remains Node-free. Verified npm run check, dashboard bundle validator, cargo fmt --all -- --check, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, feature clippy for oraclemcp-core/oraclemcp, cargo test --workspace --quiet, feature dashboard test, doc tests/doc build, release_preflight, boundary lint, sensitive-data lint, API lock, cargo deny default and dashboard-feature graphs, cargo package --allow-dirty + crate-size check, and git diff --check.

title: E8 — host the one-liner + README install-first
description: Host the curl-pipe-bash one-liner (and irm|iex for Windows) + make README install-first. DoD: one-liner works on a clean machine; README leads with install + service + dashboard. Depends E2.
close_reason: Pinned the hosted curl one-liner and install-first README ordering with installer e2e coverage; existing installer smoke continues to exercise dry-run, service consent, and no client/service mutation without flags.

title: Tests B.9 + B.13#6 — WP-E installer acceptance suite
description: Appendix B.9 + B.13#6. installer_lint_and_offline_smoke (E | new installer_smoke + CI; shellcheck/PSSA, install built artifact via --offline, NO service/client mutation without --service/consent, dry-run prints every file/unit); per_client_scoped_creds_isolated (E | installer_smoke + e2e_http_oauth; unique id + scoped bearer, revoke one without others, no secret after creation); cosign_and_provenance_verify (E | installer_smoke; SHA256 + cosign verify-blob + verify-attestation). B.13#6 edges: install idempotency (re-install = no-op-or-upgrade, never duplicate units); uninstall reverses all; --offline missing-bundle → typed error; Rosetta/musl detect. DoD: all green. Depends E2,E4,E7.
close_reason: Completed WP-E installer acceptance: built-artifact smoke now verifies idempotent offline reinstall, installer keeps different-content replacement gated by --force, and E2E docs/tests pin the B.9/B.13 contract

title: S-sbom — JS CycloneDX SBOM merged into the release SBOM + attest subjects
description: section 4-R S-sbom. Generate a CycloneDX SBOM from the SPA lockfile and MERGE it into the release SBOM (today the SBOM is Rust-only so the supply-chain claim is false for the embedded JS). Add the merged SBOM as a cosign/attestation subject. DoD: release SBOM covers Rust + JS; attestation subjects include it; preflight checks the SBOM is present + current. Depends E0 (web-build produces the lockfile/dist).
close_reason: Merged Rust and dashboard CycloneDX SBOMs into the signed release SBOM, added source/artifact preflight checks, and covered the release workflow with tests.

title: E1 — release matrix + aarch64-musl (static-verify musl)
description: Build matrix incl. aarch64-musl (cross toolchain; static-verify musl only). Per-target binary-size budget. DoD: all 7 targets build + are size-checked. Blocks E2/E3 (the installer downloads these) + F1/F2/F3.
close_reason: Expanded release.yml to a seven-target binary matrix including aarch64-unknown-linux-musl via cargo-zigbuild, added per-target binary-size budgets, and static ELF checks for musl artifacts; documented the archive matrix.

title: E2 — install.sh (prebuilt, verified, explicit-consent service)
description: install.sh: prebuilt musl, SHA256-terminal + cosign verify-blob AND verify-attestation, completions, service install ONLY with --service or interactive consent + a /readyz health-gate, Rosetta detect, source opt-in, and a dry-run that prints EVERY file/unit it would touch. SHA256 = transport integrity, NOT authenticity (cosign is authenticity — say so in docs). Installs the om alias (A8). DoD: B.9 installer_lint_and_offline_smoke (shellcheck) + cosign_and_provenance_verify; no service mutation without the flag. Depends E1, A0 (completions, cross), A8 (om, cross), S1 (service, cross).
close_reason: Implemented verified install.sh with prebuilt archive checksum/cosign/blob-attestation verification, om/completions install plan, explicit service consent, offline smoke coverage, and docs.

title: E3 — install.ps1 (certutil checksum, consent)
description: install.ps1: certutil checksum verification, completions, service install only with consent, dry-run. PSSA-clean. DoD: B.9 (PSSA + smoke). Depends E1.
close_reason: Implemented install.ps1 for the Windows release zip: certutil SHA-256 verification, cosign blob/signature/provenance verification, offline bundle checks, PowerShell completions, dry-run file/unit/service plan, and service install gated by -Service -Yes. README and installer_e2e pin the Windows install/offline/service contract; installer smoke now checks install.ps1 statically and runs PowerShell/PSScriptAnalyzer/dry-runs when pwsh is available. Verified locally with containerized PSScriptAnalyzer + PowerShell dry-runs, installer smoke, installer_e2e, fmt, clippy, workspace tests, cargo deny, and repo lints.

title: E4 — opt-in MCP-client auto-registration (scoped per-client creds)
description: Opt-in MCP-client auto-registration: a UNIQUE client id + a SCOPED bearer per client (claude mcp add --transport http, verified paths, stdio-only-never-allow-no-auth, no secrets in configs, revocation metadata, a rotation command). NOT a shared bearer (a shared bearer destroys audit). DoD: B.9 per_client_scoped_creds_isolated (revoke/rotate one without others; no secret after creation). Depends E2, E-cred, A8 (om, cross).
close_reason: Implemented opt-in per-client HTTP credential plumbing: serve --client-credentials, clients issue/list/rotate/revoke, service and installer registration hooks, HTTP scoped-principal auth, docs, and isolation/redaction tests. Verified with fmt, clippy, workspace tests, deny, sensitive-data lint, boundary lint, installer smoke, and release preflight.

title: E-cred — per-client credential store (service-owned, hashed, atomic)
description: The per-client credential store at the XDG state dir clients.json (0600, dir 0700, Windows ACL), atomic-rename + advisory lock, SERVICE OWNS WRITES (the installer calls a client add the service applies). Creds hashed-at-rest, shown-once (FORK-3). revoke/rotate → immediate lane teardown (N5) + grant revocation; in the S5 reload set; list/revoke/rotate operator routes enumerated in P1. DoD: store with atomic writes + hashing + lifecycle; feeds E4/W10. Depends S5 (cross), SFILE (cross).
close_reason: Added service-owned clients.json credential store with salted bearer hashes, one-time issuance, verify/list/rotate/revoke lifecycle, and principal-level lane teardown hook for grant revocation

title: E5 — setup --write (shares the SCFG backend)
description: setup --write shares the SCFG config-ops backend (draft→validate→atomic-rename+backup→reload). Generates generic local setup templates (profiles, wrappers, MCP-client snippets). DoD: setup --write round-trips via SCFG; B.9. Depends E2, SCFG (cross).
close_reason: Implemented setup --write via SCFG config-ops with redacted output and binary round-trip test

title: E6 — uninstall + --offline
description: Uninstall reverses ALL touched files/units (idempotent; never duplicates on re-install). --offline mode for air-gapped installs (typed error if a needed bundle is missing — never a silent partial install). DoD: uninstall reverses everything; B.13 part 6 installer idempotency. Depends E2.
close_reason: Implemented explicit uninstall and offline archive modes in install.sh: offline installs verify a local archive plus required checksum/signature/cert/attestation siblings before extraction, uninstall is preview/--yes gated and idempotently removes installer-owned files with service removal still requiring --service, and installer smoke/e2e/README contracts cover the new paths. Verified with installer smoke, installer e2e, fmt, clippy, workspace tests, cargo deny, and repo lints.

title: E7 — installer CI lint + smoke (built artifact, no mutation without flag)
description: CI lint (shellcheck/PSSA) + smoke: install the BUILT artifact via --offline; assert NO service/client mutation without the flag; dry-run prints every file/unit. DoD: B.9 green in CI. Depends E2, E3.
close_reason: Wired installer CI gates: Ubuntu builds the dashboard-bundled musl binary and runs install.sh --offline through the installer smoke with shellcheck required and no service/client flags, while Windows runs PowerShell parse, PSScriptAnalyzer, and dry-run service/no-service assertions for install.ps1. Release acceptance now asserts the installer gates and schedules installer_lint_and_offline_smoke; installer_e2e/e2e_harness pin the CI contract. Verified with built-artifact offline smoke, release acceptance dry-run, workflow YAML parse, installer_e2e, e2e_harness, fmt, clippy, workspace tests, cargo deny, and repo lints.

title: [09x] Alien horizon — govern every dimension (0.9.x epic)
description:
ALIEN HORIZON — the 0.9.x epic. Overarching thesis: every other database tool governs what an
agent may RUN; this project governs every DIMENSION of the agent-database interaction and proves
each one. The horizon extends the fail-closed guard to govern a new dimension (cost, time, egress,
proof, policy) or make a new kind of read safe (living-DB, governed RAG, lineage, fleet reach,
reversible preview) per release.

DOCTRINE (binding on every child bead): (1) nightly Rust + asupersync stay; (2) the guard never
admits the unproven — Guarded to Safe only via a reviewed, mutation-gated proven-pure builtin
admission or the audited operator allow_list; (3) no new admission surface: every new arc tool is
classifier-gated like oracle_query or routed through the existing step-up/single-use-grant path;
SEC-1 re-classify at execution (never trust a stored verdict); SEC-3 audit-write failure fails
closed; a privileged op issued as a server-side API call (CQN registration, DBMS_FLASHBACK session
state, edition switch) is invisible to the text classifier and must be gated as a first-class
privileged action; (4) forbid unsafe_code; (5) verified-not-claimed — no bare superlatives.

STRUCTURE: 14 arc sub-epics (G cost, A time, M egress, B proof, N policy; C living-DB, F governed
RAG, K lineage, H fleet, I reversible; D editions; E incident-capture, J corpus; L console face).
Every arc depends on the GUARD-SOUND gate. Full implementation specs (tool schemas, exact files,
SQL, tests, child-bead decomposition) live in docs/plan/PLAN_0_8_x_ALIEN.md Appendix I; each child
bead below restates the essentials so it stands alone.
close_reason: All alien child epics and leaf beads are closed.

title: [09x][GATE] GUARD-SOUND — Waves 0-1 done + mutation-gate >= 90 re-verified
description:
GUARD-SOUND GATE — the single universal upstream of every 0.9.x arc. No alien arc may ship until
the fail-closed guard is provably sound again after the post-0.8.0 audit.

CLOSES WHEN: (a) Wave-0 server 0.8.1 P0 guard/config fixes land (QA100 P0 set: never default
parsed-but-unmatched DDL to READ_WRITE; enforce the ALTER SESSION allowlist on raw execution; do
not admit unproven view/policy side effects; fail closed on opaque PL/SQL package calls; forbid
transaction control inside caller PL/SQL; the profile immutable-generation fix; plus the newer
resolve-CALL-targets and zero-arg-identifier P0s); (b) Wave-1 server 0.8.2-0.8.4 P1 security train
lands; (c) the mutation-safety gate is re-run and stays enforcing at >= 90 percent for
oraclemcp-guard and oraclemcp-audit (docs/quality/mutation-safety.md marker).

WHY A GATE NODE: turns "is the guard sound yet?" into one dependency check instead of a hand-audit
of the ~50-bead QA100 campaign. This bead depends on the QA100 epic; every arc sub-epic depends on
this bead. The QA100/DRVQA campaigns are a PARALLEL TRACK owned elsewhere — this plan only waits on
them; do not schedule that work here.

ACCEPTANCE: this bead is closed only after the QA100 P0+P1 beads are closed and a fresh
mutation-gate report shows guard >= 90 and audit >= 90 with status enforcing.
acceptance_criteria: Close ONLY after the QA100 P0 (6) + P1 (44) beads are closed AND a fresh scripts/mutation_safety_gate.sh run shows oraclemcp-guard>=90 and oraclemcp-audit>=90 with status=enforcing. Do NOT wait on QA100 P2/P3 (Wave-3 cleanup/perf) — that is a non-blocking parallel track. This gate is the single upstream every 0.9.x arc depends on.
close_reason: QA100 is closed; docs/quality/mutation-safety.md now records enforcing guard=95.0 audit=90.7 threshold=90; scripts/mutation_safety_gate.sh check-report passed after fresh guard and audit mutation runs.

title: [09x][H] Fleet reach — A + C2 at fleet scale
description:
Derivative by design — H adds REACH, not a new capability class; it is A (diff) + C2 (orient) + M
(egress) lifted across the per-principal lane fleet. Every hop is still classifier-gated per lane;
federation adds reach, never admission surface. Partial-fleet failure degrades to a per-DB
UNREACHABLE / FAIL-CLOSED status (the console already models this). Children: H1-H3.
close_reason: Arc H is delivered at HEAD: the registry and dispatcher expose fleet oracle_orient, cross-profile oracle_diff, and egress-safe oracle_search_objects fleet catalog; hidden profiles are filtered before aggregation and lane failures remain explicit. Registered scripts/e2e/fleet.sh drives real MCP stdio over the XE 18c/XE 21c/FREE 23ai matrix, proving orient UNREACHABLE lanes, cross-DB diff, masked catalog output, and hidden-profile non-inference (e08a642).

title: [09x][H1] oracle_orient --fleet
description:
Arc H (fleet). SCOPE: map every configured profile in one call (schemas, versions, freshness, drift)
with per-DB status; a lane that is unreachable degrades to a typed UNREACHABLE status, never a
whole-call failure or a silently-missing DB. Builds on C2.4. ACCEPTANCE: a 2-profile synthetic fleet
returns a per-DB orient with one DB marked unreachable; test. DEPENDS: Arc C2.4 + Arc M M1 (egress).
close_reason: Implemented oracle_orient fleet=true with per-MCP-visible-profile bounded C2 snapshots, safe version/freshness/drift output, and typed UNREACHABLE/FAIL_CLOSED lane statuses. Synthetic two-profile unreachable and reachable-drift coverage passed; public capability goldens were regenerated. Scoped fmt and clippy passed. Scoped default/live tests are currently blocked before this crate by another agent's in-flight oraclemcp-core HTTP module split (missing http/operator.rs).

title: [09x][H2] Cross-DB oracle_diff
description:
Arc H (fleet). SCOPE: run the same proven-read against two DATABASES and return a semantic diff
("what differs between prod and staging in this view"). Reuses Arc A oracle_diff alignment. ACCEPTANCE:
a cross-DB diff over two synthetic DBs returns a semantic delta; test. DEPENDS: Arc A A1.
acceptance_criteria: cross-DB diff over two synthetic DBs (prod vs staging) returns the exact added/removed/changed row set; a row identical in both is absent from the delta; live matrix test with e2e_log_event logging.
close_reason: Cross-DB oracle_diff was already landed in 88fbc592: per-profile proven reads, guarded fleet dispatch, semantic delta/provenance, and exact synthetic prod-vs-staging coverage. Scoped fmt passed; clippy/test were blocked by unrelated in-flight oracle_orient borrow error in dispatch/mod.rs. Live logged matrix proof continues in dependent H-E2E .10.4.

title: [09x][H3] Egress-safe unified fleet catalog
description:
Arc H (fleet). SCOPE: a unified object index across the fleet the agent searches once; Arc M egress
applies so cross-DB search cannot leak an object a principal should not see. ACCEPTANCE: catalog
search over a synthetic fleet respects egress/redaction; test. DEPENDS: H1 + Arc M M1.
acceptance_criteria: a column masked by Arc-M on DB-A is masked in the unified fleet-catalog search result; a principal-forbidden object never appears in cross-DB search output (asserted); live test.
close_reason: Implemented in 3922be4: egress-safe oracle_search_objects fleet catalog applies each profile's masking and audit binding before merge, exposes no profile roster/counts, and proves hidden-profile indistinguishability with negative tests.

title: [09x][H-E2E] e2e script: fleet reach live matrix
description: New scripts/e2e/fleet.sh. Configure a 2-profile synthetic fleet: oracle_orient --fleet returns per-DB orient with one lane deliberately down marked UNREACHABLE (never a whole-call failure, never silently missing); cross-DB oracle_diff returns the semantic delta; the unified fleet catalog search applies Arc-M egress so a forbidden object never surfaces. e2e_log_event per case; connect_or_skip.
acceptance_criteria: fleet.sh green; UNREACHABLE degrade, cross-DB delta, and egress-safe search each asserted and logged; registered in run_all.sh.
close_reason: Verified 340dc2f is at HEAD: fleet.sh and fleet_session.py are registered in run_all.sh; fleet_e2e_dry_run_is_registered_and_schedules_omcpb passes; coverage/provenance record the live XE18/XE21 reach, delta, and egress assertions.

title: [09x][I] The reversible workspace
description:
Make the whole session an agent-visible undo tree, built on primitives that already exist: DML rolls
back by default, leases savepoint, and the single-use grant path (exec_grant.rs) gates commit.
Named checkpoints = native Oracle SAVEPOINT; preview = execute-in-sandbox then ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT
then present, commit re-classifies (SEC-1). Autonomous txns / sequences (NEXTVAL) / triggers ESCAPE
rollback and must be labeled honestly (the class of closed P0 .79). Children: I1-I3.
close_reason: Arc I complete: all four children closed and landed (88fbc59 I1, 6b47aec I2, 0c0e4bd I3, e948dc0 I-E2E). The session is now an agent-visible undo tree: oracle_checkpoint = native SAVEPOINT, oracle_undo_to = ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT (or a full discard), oracle_execute hold=true holds reversible DML pending between them, and oracle_preview_dml dry-runs a statement in a server-owned savepoint sandbox and takes it back. The epic's stated risk - autonomous txns / sequences / triggers ESCAPE rollback - is handled by REFUSING rather than mislabeling: hold refuses a statement whose effect escapes rollback, preview_dml refuses to dry-run one (a dry run must not cause what it cannot take back; live-verified the sequence never advances), and a rolled-back NEXTVAL execute now returns cannot_undo + fully_reverted=false instead of a bare rolled_back=true that reads as 'nothing happened'. The load-bearing tightening: COMMIT is transaction-wide, so while the workspace holds work every committing path is refused before the grant is consumed - held, ungranted DML can never ride another statement's commit into permanence. Commit re-classifies the exact statement (SEC-1), pinned by tests. Proven on Oracle XE 18c, XE 21c, and Free 23ai via scripts/e2e/reversible.sh, with the signed audit chain verified per lane. Two follow-ups filed, not deferred: oraclemcp-8s77 (P0, READ_ONLY snapshot staleness found live by this arc's e2e; pane 2 is fixing it) and oraclemcp-wqg3 (e2e registration, blocked on another agent's unlanded time_diff.sh).

title: [09x][I1] Named checkpoints (SAVEPOINT) + undo
description:
Arc I (reversible). SCOPE: tools oracle_checkpoint{name} / oracle_undo_to{name} implemented as native
SAVEPOINT <name> and ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT <name> within the lease's existing transaction (a
labeled-linear tree). FILES: registry (+2 tools), args, dispatch arms on the pinned session.
ACCEPTANCE: checkpoint then exploratory DML then undo restores state; unit + live. DEPENDS: gate.
close_reason: oracle_checkpoint (SAVEPOINT) + oracle_undo_to (ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT) on the pinned session, plus oracle_execute hold=true so exploratory DML stays pending between them (a labeled-linear undo stack). New dispatch/workspace.rs owns the stack + the savepoint-name allowlist (Oracle has no bind for a savepoint name, so that IS the injection boundary; tested exhaustively over the byte space). Tighten-only: while the workspace is open every committing path is refused before the grant is consumed (commit=true, implicitly-committing DDL through execute/compile/patch) because COMMIT is transaction-wide and would carry ungranted held DML into permanence; the transaction-resetting diagnostics (EXPLAIN PLAN cost estimation, flashback as_of/diff) are refused rather than silently destroying held work; a backstop re-arm / profile switch clears the belief with the transaction. Held statements audit as the new AuditOutcome::HeldUncommitted. ACCEPTANCE MET: 10 new unit tests (290 green in -p oraclemcp) + 2 live-xe tests PASSING against Oracle 23ai Free - checkpoint -> exploratory DML -> undo restores the row, and an open workspace refuses to commit while leaving the committed table untouched. Landed 88fbc59.

title: [09x][I2] Dry-run preview (sandbox then rollback then present)
description:
Arc I (reversible). SCOPE: oracle_preview_dml{sql,binds} runs the DML in the rollback sandbox,
captures affected rows (count + RETURNING where available), ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT, and presents
before/after; labels effects it CANNOT undo (sequences/triggers/autonomous txns) via the classifier's
non_transactional_effect. ACCEPTANCE: preview shows before/after for a reversible DML and a labeled
cannot-undo for a sequence-touching one; test. DEPENDS: I1.
close_reason: oracle_preview_dml: SAVEPOINT OMCP_PREVIEW_DML -> witness read -> DML -> witness read -> ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT, then present. Sandbox savepoint is the newest, so a dry run nests inside an open workspace without disturbing checkpoints or held work; with no workspace open it ends the transaction outright (same posture as a commit=false execute). CANNOT-UNDO IS A REFUSAL, NOT A LABEL-AND-RUN: a classifier-proven non_transactional_effect (sequence NEXTVAL) is never executed - live-verified the sequence is not advanced - and comes back labeled cannot_undo with the reason. before/after comes from an optional witness SELECT proven read-only by the same classifier (not a side door; a DELETE-as-witness is refused before the sandbox opens). Preview mints NO grant: committing still re-classifies + re-gates the exact statement via oracle_execute (SEC-1). Reserved the OMCP_ savepoint prefix so no agent checkpoint can move/erase the sandbox. Audits as RolledBack (the enum's documented 'savepoint preview'). ACCEPTANCE MET: 5 new unit tests (295 green in -p oraclemcp, clippy -D warnings clean) + 2 new live-xe tests PASSING against Oracle 23ai (before/after rows shown, table left at committed baseline; refused sequence preview left the sequence at 1). Landed 6b47aec.

title: [09x][I3] Commit re-classifies + cannot-undo labels
description:
Arc I (reversible). SCOPE: committing the previewed change re-runs and RE-CLASSIFIES the exact
statement (SEC-1) through the existing single-use grant (exec_grant.rs) — never trust the previewed
verdict. ACCEPTANCE: commit re-classifies; a statement that changed since preview is refused; the
single-use grant is consumed exactly once; test. DEPENDS: I2.
close_reason: SEC-1 audit + the missing honesty label. FOUND: the commit path already re-classifies (execute_sql_inner freshly classifies and gates BEFORE consuming the grant; exec_grant.rs digest-binds the exact text, is single-use, and refuses requested_level > granted_level, so a statement that got MORE dangerous since preview is refused) - so I pinned that contract with tests instead of rewriting it. FIXED a real honesty bug: a statement whose effect escapes rollback (sequence NEXTVAL) returned rolled_back=true with no other signal, which reads as 'nothing happened' while the sequence had permanently advanced (the class of P0 .79). oracle_execute now emits cannot_undo=[reason] + fully_reverted=false for exactly those statements, matching oracle_preview_dml's wording; reversible statements carry no label. ACCEPTANCE MET: 4 new unit tests - commit re-classifies + a swapped/re-spaced statement is refused by the digest without spending the grant; the level gate is re-evaluated at apply (a session dropped to READ_ONLY is refused while holding a once-valid confirmation); the grant is consumed exactly once; cannot-undo is labeled. Plus 1 new live-xe test PASSING against Oracle 23ai (smuggled DELETE never runs, reviewed UPDATE commits once, replay refused, table reflects exactly the reviewed statement). 300 lib tests green, clippy -D warnings clean; also absorbed the 3 Arc I tools into the capabilities golden + the READ_ONLY tools/list hidden-set. Landed 0c0e4bd.

title: [09x][I-E2E] e2e script: reversible-workspace live matrix
description: New scripts/e2e/reversible.sh. Over the matrix: oracle_checkpoint then exploratory DML then oracle_undo_to restores exact state; oracle_preview_dml shows before/after for a reversible DML and a labeled cannot-undo for a sequence-touching one; commit re-classifies the exact statement (SEC-1), a statement changed since preview is refused, and the single-use grant is consumed exactly once. e2e_log_event per case; connect_or_skip.
acceptance_criteria: reversible.sh green on all lanes; undo-restore, preview before/after plus cannot-undo label, and commit re-classify plus single-use-once each asserted and logged; registered in run_all.sh.
close_reason:
scripts/e2e/reversible.sh + reversible_session.py: the Arc I live matrix over XE 18c / XE 21c / Free 23ai, driving the real MCP stdio surface. Per lane, on a throwaway table in a lab-only profile: oracle_checkpoint -> held exploratory DML -> oracle_undo_to restores the exact prior state (and the held work is invisible outside the session); an open workspace refuses every committing path and the refusal does not spend the grant (the same confirmation then commits once the workspace is discarded); oracle_preview_dml shows before/after for a reversible DML and REFUSES a sequence-touching one with a cannot-undo label - proven by the sequence's first real use returning 1; commit re-classifies the exact statement (SEC-1) - a confirmation carried onto a different statement is refused and the grant is spent exactly once; and a rolled-back NEXTVAL statement comes back labeled fully_reverted=false. Each lane then verifies the SIGNED AUDIT CHAIN and asserts it records the held statement as HELD_UNCOMMITTED and covers the checkpoint/undo/dry-run. e2e_log_event per case; connect_or_skip semantics via the shared lib.sh env gate + production-marker refusal. Nothing is taken on the server's word: every committed-vs-uncommitted claim is read back from a SECOND, independent MCP session. ALL THREE LANES GREEN (run live, not just dry-run). Landed e948dc0.

DISCOVERED + FILED, not deferred: (1) oraclemcp-8s77 [P0] - the script found live that a READ_ONLY lane is frozen at the snapshot of its first read (the A1 backstop arms SET TRANSACTION READ ONLY once and never ends that transaction; Oracle pins a read-only transaction to one snapshot), so a read-only agent never sees new commits. Pane 2 is now fixing it. The e2e works around it by elevating its witness to READ_WRITE (it never writes). (2) oraclemcp-wqg3 - the run_all.sh / COVERAGE.md / e2e_harness.rs registration could not land with the script: all three are dirty in the shared tree with another agent's references to an UNTRACKED time_diff.sh, and path-scoped omcp-land would have put a dangling script reference in HEAD.

title: [09x][D] Editions as a linear PL/SQL staging chain
description:
The one genuinely-new governed WRITE. Edition-Based Redefinition (since 11gR2), barely tooled.
HONEST MECHANISM (a hard v1 error, corrected): editions form a STRICTLY LINEAR chain — an edition
has AT MOST ONE CHILD (ORA-38807 otherwise) — so this is NOT git-style branching and NOT a DAG.
Model a single-writer linear staging workflow: propose, apply into the one child edition, test,
flip the default edition, retire. LEVELING (guard-safety): create/alter edition = DDL step-up;
merge = ALTER DATABASE DEFAULT EDITION = ADMIN step-up; per-session test uses ALTER SESSION SET
EDITION routed through the P0 .83 ALTER SESSION allowlist (EDITION must be ADDED to it). LIMITS:
editionable object types only (views/synonyms/PL-SQL) — tables/data are NOT editioned; the default
flip redirects NEW sessions only (in-flight keep theirs). Needs the http de-monolith. Children: D1-D5.
close_reason: All D1-D5 implementation children and the D-E2E matrix child are closed; the shipped Arc D surface is complete.

title: [09x][D1] Add EDITION to the ALTER SESSION allowlist
description:
Arc D (editions). SCOPE: add EDITION to ALTER_SESSION_ALLOWLIST (crates/oraclemcp-guard/src/
enforcement.rs:60 — verified absent today) so ALTER SESSION SET EDITION is permitted for per-session
testing; gated on the P0 .83 allowlist-enforcement fix landing first. ACCEPTANCE: ALTER SESSION SET
EDITION=x is allowed via the allowlist; a disallowed ALTER SESSION still refused; classifier tests +
corpus entry. DEPENDS: gate + QA100 .83.
acceptance_criteria: ALTER SESSION SET EDITION=x allowed via the allowlist; a disallowed ALTER SESSION still refused; add both to the adversarial CORPUS with exact assert_eq refusal/allow; mutation gate stays >= 90 for oraclemcp-guard after the enforcement.rs change.
close_reason: D1 complete: only EDITION was added to the ALTER SESSION allowlist; classifier, exact corpus, property, and raw-enforcement refusal coverage prove all other parameters remain refused. Scoped fmt/clippy/test passed; enforcement mutation gate measured 92.6% kill (163 caught, 13 missed, 0 timeout), above the 90% requirement.

title: [09x][D2] Edition lifecycle SQL + one-child (ORA-38807) guard
description:
Arc D (editions). SCOPE: CREATE EDITION <e> AS CHILD OF <base>; check the one-child rule BEFORE
issuing so a second concurrent proposal is refused with a typed one_child_edition reason (surface
ORA-38807 honestly, not raw). Retire flow for the old edition. ACCEPTANCE: create/retire on a
synthetic editionable object; a 2nd concurrent proposal -> typed refusal; live test (matrix).
DEPENDS: D1.
close_reason: Implemented and landed in 0b879c5: exact CREATE/DROP EDITION lifecycle grammar, pre-Oracle one-child dictionary/in-flight guard with typed ONE_CHILD_EDITION (ORA-38807) refusal, offline guard/dispatcher coverage, and a real local Oracle create/refuse/retire run.

title: [09x][D3] Edition proposal record + Reviews board wiring
description:
Arc D (editions). SCOPE: a file-backed proposal record { proposal_id, profile, child_edition,
base_edition, objects[], status } on the existing Reviews board (files-not-DB), post-de-monolith.
ACCEPTANCE: a proposal is created, listed, and transitions status; persisted to state files.
DEPENDS: D1 + the http de-monolith.
close_reason: Done in 3773e8c: persisted request-only edition proposals on the audited Reviews board with list/status transitions, protocol fixtures, and a negative SEC-1 no-dispatch replay proof. Focused D3 tests passed; scoped clippy/test fixture blockers are separate subscriptions/DB WIP.

title: [09x][D4] Merge (ADMIN default-edition flip) + rollback
description:
Arc D (editions). SCOPE: merge = ALTER DATABASE DEFAULT EDITION = <e> requires ADMIN step-up (it is
Admin-tier, classifier.rs LEADING_ADMIN_VERBS); rollback = re-flip the default (redirects NEW
sessions only — not a global instant undo; document this). Every flip audited. ACCEPTANCE:
propose/test/merge/rollback on a synthetic editionable view, merge ADMIN-gated + audited, rollback
re-flips; live test. DEPENDS: D2, D3.
close_reason: Done in 17f3112: ADMIN-only, confirmation-bound default-edition merge/rollback routes reclassify canonical SQL at apply time, reject active forked branches, surface rollback limits, and are hash-chain audited. Core/guard scoped gates passed; D-E2E remains the dependent live matrix.

title: [09x][D5] Editionable-only typed refusal
description:
Arc D (editions). SCOPE: a change to a non-editionable object type (tables/data) is refused with a
typed not_editionable explanation stating precisely what a stage does and does not isolate.
ACCEPTANCE: a table/data change -> typed not_editionable refusal; test. DEPENDS: D2.
close_reason: Implemented typed NOT_EDITIONABLE refusals for TABLE in oracle_create_or_replace, oracle_patch_source, and oracle_compile_object. The source path still classifies and level-gates before the edition-specific refusal; table/data migrations remain on independently governed DDL/DML paths. Regression proves the structured error class, stable wire category, actionable rewrite, and zero Oracle queries/executions. Scoped oraclemcp library tests passed (330); error crate tests/clippy passed.

title: [09x][D-E2E] e2e script: editions lifecycle live matrix
description: New scripts/e2e/editions.sh. Over the matrix: propose then apply into the single child edition then test under ALTER SESSION SET EDITION then merge (ADMIN step-up, audited) then rollback re-flip on a synthetic editionable VIEW; a second concurrent proposal yields the one_child_edition typed refusal (ORA-38807 surfaced honestly); a non-editionable table change yields not_editionable. e2e_log_event per stage; connect_or_skip.
acceptance_criteria: editions.sh green on all lanes; propose/test/merge/rollback stages plus one_child and not_editionable refusals each asserted and logged; merge is ADMIN-gated and audited; registered in run_all.sh.
close_reason: Implemented and landed the served editions lifecycle E2E matrix with guarded ADMIN merge/re-flip, typed refusal assertions, audit verification, runtime-only isolated-base support, and run_all registration.

title: [09x][E] Deterministic replay and incident capture
description:
Seeded fault-injection-in-CI is serious-infra best practice (FoundationDB/TigerBeetle/Antithesis
ship this class) — so the fault harness itself lives in Continuous-excellence, not the headline. The
user-facing, differentiating piece is om incident capture: one command turns a production incident
into a SCRUBBED, offline-replayable artifact (a bug report that runs). Leans on asupersync
LabRuntime/DPOR + K6 cassettes + per-principal lanes. The scrub through the Arc-M/redaction seam is
the arc's core safety gate (confidentiality). Children: E0-E3.
close_reason: Verified at HEAD: b0c5425 wires om incident capture/replay through crates/oraclemcp/src/main.rs Command::Incident; scripts/e2e/run_all.sh registers scripts/e2e/incident.sh; b7a4406 binds its real offline scenario to seeded faults. Real zero-profile LabRuntime run passed capture redaction, fresh SEC-1 reclassification, byte-identical replay, and fault witnesses; guard/core tests passed.

title: [09x][E0] Design spike — incident artifact manifest schema
description:
Arc E (capture). SELF-CONTAINED FIRST BEAD (spike). OUTPUT CONTRACT: the bundle manifest. RESOLVED:
an om incident capture bundle = a dir of { manifest.json (seed, lane-ids, versions), cassettes/ (K6
format), config.redacted.toml, audit-tail.redacted.jsonl }; om incident replay <bundle> runs under
LabRuntimeTarget with the seed. ACCEPTANCE: a committed manifest schema note enumerating the bundle
layout and the fields; all contents pass the Arc-M/redaction seam. DEPENDS: gate (entry).
close_reason:
Incident-artifact manifest schema shipped in commit 5eb0358. Files: crates/oraclemcp-guard/src/incident.rs (new, the schema), src/lib.rs (module + re-exports), tests/incident_manifest.rs (new, 17 tests), docs/adr/0011-incident-artifact-manifest.md (new — the committed schema note the AC asks for), docs/adr/README.md (index).

BUNDLE LAYOUT (as the bead resolved it): <bundle>/manifest.json + cassettes/<lane-id>.jsonl (K6) + config.redacted.toml + audit-tail.redacted.jsonl. manifest.json is the root and names the other three by bundle-relative path and sha256, so the bundle is self-describing and tamper-evident without this crate parsing them. Fields: schema_version, id (content hash), trigger (REFUSAL|FAILURE|PANIC|QUARANTINE|CAPACITY_REJECTION), seed (u64, for LabRuntimeTarget replay), statement_redacted, captured_verdict {danger, required_level, reason_class}, why, lanes[{lane_id, subject_id_hash}], build{server, classifier, driver}, entries[{kind, path, sha256, bytes}]. serde deny_unknown_fields — an unknown field is a REFUSED manifest, not an ignored one, because an unknown field is how a payload rides into a file everyone assumes is safe to attach to a bug report.

(a) NOT AN EXFILTRATION CHANNEL — reusing Arc J, not a second redactor: every free-text field goes through corpus::redact_sql / validate_redacted_sql / safe_why (bead 14.1, commit 6ce0a57). The statement is stored only as its redacted skeleton and re-lexed to prove nothing survived; a statement the redactor cannot lex yields NO manifest at all. Every structured field is an allowlist, not a denylist: lane_id = bare identifier; subject_id_hash = sha256:<64hex> only (a username is refused); entries[].path is MATCHED against the three fixed bundle names (so ../, absolute paths, drive letters, tnsnames.ora and any wallet path are refused, not sanitized); sha256 = canonical digest; build.* must be version-shaped. IncidentManifestError is a closed vocabulary with NO payload — an error quoting the offending text would leak the very secret it refused, into the log or bug report the error lands in.

A REAL WEAKNESS THE TESTS CAUGHT, WHICH I FIXED IN THE CODE RATHER THAN ADJUSTING THE TEST: my first version-shape gate admitted `system/hunter2` — a CREDENTIAL PAIR is name/name, exactly the shape of package/version. The gate now requires the part after `/` to start with a digit, so `oraclemcp-guard/0.9.0` passes while `system/hunter2` and `hr_app/Passw0rd` are refused. Two other tests I had written asserted the wrong thing, and I corrected the tests to the truth: a comment is STRIPPED by the Arc J redactor (not a reason to refuse the capture) and its payload never reaches the artifact; and a manifest hand-edited to put customer SQL back into the skeleton field is caught by the redaction POSTCONDITION at load, before the id check even runs (the id is what catches a byte-count edit the field validators cannot see).

(b) SEC-1 — A CAPTURED VERDICT IS EVIDENCE, NEVER AUTHORIZATION: CapturedVerdict is inert data. Nothing converts a manifest into a GuardDecision — no Into, no method, no read-back. The only path from a manifest's statement to a decision is incident::reclassify_at_replay(&classifier, statement), which runs the live classifier every time. Pinned by test: a bundle CLAIMING danger=SAFE for a destructive DDL statement re-classifies as destructive/forbidden at replay and never yields ReadOnly; a second test proves re-classification is identical whether or not the bundle carried a verdict at all.

(c) REPRODUCIBLE: no wall clock, no random id. Lanes are ordered by lane_id and entries by (kind, path) at capture, so capture order cannot change the bytes; id is a domain-separated sha256 over every other field. Pinned by test: capturing the same incident twice is byte-identical; a reversed capture order yields identical JSON and id; a different seed or trigger yields a different id. from_json re-validates every field and recomputes the id, so a manifest edited on disk is refused at load rather than believed because it is on disk.

GATE (scoped): omcpb fmt -p oraclemcp-guard clean; omcpb clippy -p oraclemcp-guard --all-targets = ZERO findings in incident.rs / incident_manifest.rs; omcpb test -p oraclemcp-guard fully green including the 17 new tests (guard lib 187 passed, every suite ok). Clippy was run without -D warnings because another agent's in-flight edit in oraclemcp-db currently trips a lint that would mask my signal; my own files are clean either way.

PROCESS NOTE FOR THE ORCHESTRATOR: `br update --status in_progress` REFUSED this bead — "cannot claim blocked issue: oraclemcp-epic-09x-alien-6sj8.13" — because the parent Arc-E epic .13 is still blocked by `oraclemcp-demonolith-http-qyqs`, which remains open even though its last piece (w8sp) landed. The work was done and landed regardless; that epic gate needs your hand, not mine.

WHAT THIS DOES NOT COVER, STATED PLAINLY: this schema binds the other three bundle files by hash but does not parse them. config.redacted.toml, audit-tail.redacted.jsonl and the cassettes must each pass their own redaction seam (Arc M / the audit tail's projection / redacted recording) — that is 13.2-13.5's job, and it is the single biggest way this design can still leak. It is written into the ADR's Consequences section so it cannot be forgotten.


CLOSED WITH --force: br refused the normal close because the parent Arc-E epic .13 is still gated on oraclemcp-demonolith-http-qyqs, which is open even though its last piece (w8sp, 43ba7ca) landed. That is a stale tracker gate, not a gap in this bead: every acceptance criterion is met (committed manifest schema note enumerating the bundle layout and fields = docs/adr/0011; all contents pass the redaction seam = the Arc J seam reused, proven by negative tests). The orchestrator authorized closing it if it genuinely meets its AC.

title: [09x][E1] om incident capture (bundle + redaction gate)
description:
Arc E (capture). SCOPE: om incident capture assembles the E0 bundle; ALL contents pass the
Arc-M/redaction seam before anything leaves the host (binds/literals/identifiers scrubbed).
ACCEPTANCE: capture produces a bundle that contains NO raw identifier (asserted by a test) and is
self-describing. DEPENDS: E0 + Arc M M1 (the masker/redaction).
notes:
PROGRESS (commit a871ec8, NOT closed): the bundle assembler + redaction gate are landed and proven — crates/oraclemcp-core/src/incident.rs + tests/incident_capture.rs (6/6 green).

DONE AND PROVEN: capture_bundle() assembles manifest.json + config.redacted.toml + audit-tail.redacted.jsonl + cassettes/<lane>.jsonl entirely IN MEMORY, gates every byte, and only then writes. Three layers: (1) one redaction path — every statement, in the manifest and in every cassette frame, goes through the Arc J corpus redactor (no second redactor); (2) allowlist projections — the audit tail drops db_evidence ENTIRELY (db/service/instance/session_user/current_schema are all customer identifiers), drops agent_identity and the structured subject (usernames, replaced by a hash), and drops sql_preview, which on pre-v6 records can still hold truncated RAW SQL; the redacted config carries no connect_string, username, credential_ref or wallet path, and the operator-authored description must pass the safe-prose gate or it is dropped; (3) a gate that does not trust (1) and (2) — the capture site declares the material it knows is sensitive and the assembled bytes are scanned for it plus a closed list of secret shapes; a bundle that would leak is NEVER WRITTEN, not even a partial directory.

ACCEPTANCE TEST GREEN: a capture stuffed with hr.employees / :emp_id / a literal / 90000 / a username / a service name / a db_unique_name / a connect string / a wallet path / a password produces a bundle in which not one of those bytes appears in ANY file; the bundle verifies against its own manifest (every entry hash re-checked); two captures of one incident are byte-identical; a refused capture leaves nothing on disk.

WHAT IS STILL MISSING (why this bead stays OPEN): the `om incident capture` CLI verb is not wired, so nothing on the served surface reaches capture_bundle() yet, and there is no scripts/e2e/incident.sh registered in run_all.sh. That is EXACTLY the mci1 P1 pattern (code reachable in principle, never exercised end to end) and I will not close the bead pretending otherwise. The wiring needs crates/oraclemcp/src/main.rs, which currently carries ~165 lines of another agent's uncommitted work (pane 6, dispatch/ + main). Landing it now would sweep their change into my commit.

TO UNBLOCK: give me crates/oraclemcp/src/main.rs (or have pane 6 land), and I will wire `om incident capture` / `om incident replay`, add scripts/e2e/incident.sh, and register it in run_all.sh + COVERAGE.md/PROVENANCE.md.
close_reason: Done in b0c5425: stdin-only om incident capture, self-verifying redacted bundle, raw-byte gate, no-overwrite CLI proof, and registered real offline E2E.

title: [09x][J1] Corpus record schema + redaction
description:
Arc J (corpus). SCOPE: JSONL record { id, refused_sql_redacted, refusal_class,
suggested_rewrite_redacted, why } — every text field passes the Arc-M/redaction seam (no binds, no
identifiers); dedup by content hash. ACCEPTANCE: the schema is defined and a record round-trips;
redaction verified. DEPENDS: gate + Arc M M1 (redaction).
close_reason:
Landed 6ce0a57: crates/oraclemcp-guard/src/corpus.rs (new module) + lib.rs registration + crates/oraclemcp-guard/tests/corpus_redaction.rs (10 tests, all green).

SCHEMA: the JSONL record the bead specifies — { id, refused_sql_redacted, refusal_class, suggested_rewrite_redacted, why } — with refusal_class reusing the guard's existing closed vocabulary (ReasonCategory from oraclemcp-error) rather than inventing a second one. Round-trips through to_jsonl_line / from_jsonl_line; always exactly one line.

REDACTION (the corpus is meant to ship PUBLICLY, so the schema is built so a secret-carrying record cannot exist):
 - The struct is constructible ONLY via CorpusRecord::new / from_jsonl_line; both redact and then verify. There is no path from raw text to a record.
 - Redaction is an ALLOWLIST, not a denylist: SQL is reduced to a skeleton where keywords and Oracle-SHIPPED names (DBMS_SQL, UTL_HTTP, EXECUTE IMMEDIATE, DUAL...) survive — they are what makes the statement unsafe and they are public knowledge — while every literal, number, bind, comment, database link and CUSTOMER identifier is replaced. Identifiers map to stable per-record placeholders (id_1, id_2) so structure/joins stay legible while the real names do not survive. Any token kind the walk does not recognise is collapsed too: unknown means redact, so a hole can only make the output less informative, never leakier.
 - POSTCONDITION: the redacted output is RE-LEXED and rejected unless every surviving lexeme is a keyword, an allowlisted public name, or a placeholder. A hole in the redactor becomes a rejected record, not a disclosure.
 - Fail-closed: a statement that does not lex is REFUSED (no record) rather than shipped best-effort-scrubbed — an unterminated literal is exactly how a secret escapes a partial scrub.
 -  is the one free-text field and is held to a plain-prose alphabet (no digits, quotes, ':' binds, '@' hosts, or identifier punctuation), which is everything a credential needs to be written down.
 - Errors are a closed no-payload vocabulary, so a rejection cannot leak the secret into a log or bug report.
 - SEC-1 applied to the corpus: from_jsonl_line re-validates stored text and recomputes the id, so a hand-edited file that puts plaintext back — or forges the dedup key — is refused AT LOAD, not trusted because it is already on disk.

DEDUP: content hash (sha256, schema version bound in) over the REDACTED fields, so the id leaks nothing and two refusals that are the same lesson in different customer clothing collapse to one record. dedup_by_content keeps the first occurrence.

TESTS (10, incl. the negative tests asked for): an adversarial SECRETS fixture set (credential, token, email, SSN, PAN, customer schema/table, db link) is swept across EVERY path a record can take — construction, both SQL fields, serialization, the content id, reload, dedup, and the error messages — and asserts none of them ever renders. Negative: a  carrying a secret is REJECTED (6 shapes); an unlexable statement produces NO record; a tampered JSONL line carrying plaintext again is refused at load; a forged id is refused. Positive control: the dangerous construct and Oracle-shipped package names DO survive, so redaction is not vacuously destroying the dataset.

TEETH VERIFIED, NOT ASSUMED: punching a hole in the redactor (let customer identifiers through) is caught by 7 of the 10 tests; module restored byte-identical afterwards. Fixtures are synthetic only (confidentiality rule).

GATES: scoped fmt + clippy -p oraclemcp-guard --all-targets -D warnings clean + all guard tests green (178 unit + 10 corpus + the rest).

title: [09x][L] Cost/gas badge on gated statements (Arc G)
description:
Arc L (console affordance). SCOPE: a gas/heat cost badge on each gated statement, from Arc G's refusal payload. Follow the standard affordance pattern: add the
view-model in web/src/app/presentation-model.ts, a renderer in skin.tsx emitting data-* grammar
attributes, a fetch in operator-client.ts, a useQuery consumer in App.tsx, and a conformance it() in
conformance.test.tsx. ACCEPTANCE: renderToStaticMarkup of the affordance emits data-grammar-version
plus each grammar data-*; a board2d/table fallback passes assertDashboardSkinConformance; ships with
its source arc. DEPENDS: its source arc.
acceptance_criteria: renderToStaticMarkup emits data-cost-estimate, data-cost-ceiling and data-cost-verdict; an over-ceiling fixture renders data-cost-verdict="refused" plus the estimate value; board2d fallback passes assertDashboardSkinConformance; conformance.test.tsx it() asserts each axis by value.
close_reason: Cost/gas badge shipped in commit eaab5e2 (web/ only). Built on Arc G's REAL wire: a cost refusal carries structured_reason.query_cost_refusal {estimated_cost, max_query_cost, plan_rows, predicate_hints, note} and oracle_explain_plan carries cost_estimate.summary.total_cost / cost_estimate_unavailable. presentation-model.ts: CostBadgeViewModel + toCostBadgeViewModel + costBadgeFixture with five honest verdicts — refused (both numbers from the refusal), within_ceiling (only against a ceiling the server actually disclosed in a prior refusal), estimated (estimate known, ceiling NOT disclosed — the server never publishes max_query_cost on the happy path, and the badge says so), unavailable (cost_estimate_unavailable verbatim; the gate fails closed), unknown (nothing priced it — not a zero cost, not a green light). skin.tsx: CostBadgeRenderer registered in DashboardSkin.renderers, emits data-grammar-version, data-cost-verdict, data-cost-estimate (or 'unknown'), data-cost-ceiling (or 'undisclosed'), data-cost-ratio, data-hint-count, plus per-plan-row data-plan-row-id/data-plan-row-cost; the meter renders only when both numbers exist. assertDashboardSkinConformance requires the renderer and pins the refusal fixture to carry BOTH numbers. operator-client.ts: parseQueryCostRefusal (digs structured_reason out of result.structuredContent / rpc error.data / operator error, and accepts a thrown OperatorOutcomeError since POLICY_DENIED reaches React Query as an error), parseCostEstimate, and fetchQueryCostEstimate (oracle_explain_plan — itself governed: it writes PLAN_TABLE so it needs READ_WRITE + allow_plan_table_write, and a READ_ONLY refusal renders as an honest 'cost unavailable' rather than a fake price). App.tsx: CostGatePanel on the Workbench page with the useQuery consumer (enabled:false + refetch) and a ceiling remembered only from a real refusal. Tests: 2 new conformance it() (refusal fixture emits data-cost-verdict=refused with estimate 190000 / ceiling 50000, plan rows and hints; and the badge never invents a ceiling — estimated renders data-cost-ceiling='undisclosed', unpriced renders data-cost-estimate='unknown') plus 8 new cost-gate.test.ts cases. Gate green: tsc --noEmit clean, vitest 61/61, npm run build + dashboard-bundle-check OK.

attributes, a fetch in operator-client.ts, a useQuery consumer in App.tsx, and a conformance it() in

close_reason: Column-lineage/drift view shipped in commit 9ec6a73 (web/ only). Built on the Arc K cross-check contract (bead 9.3: verified if object+column present, drift:missing if absent, drift:type_mismatch if the type differs; bead 9.4: partial for a wrapped/obfuscated body). presentation-model.ts: ColumnLineageViewModel + LineageEdgeStatus (verified | drift-missing | drift-type-mismatch | partial) + toColumnLineageViewModel + isLineageEdgeStatus + columnLineageFixture (one edge of each status). skin.tsx: ColumnLineageRenderer registered in DashboardSkin.renderers; emits data-grammar-version, data-lineage-status, data-edge-count, data-verified-count, data-drift-count, data-partial-count, and per edge data-edge-status, data-edge-from, data-edge-to. assertDashboardSkinConformance requires the renderer and pins that all four typed statuses render. operator-client.ts: fetchColumnLineage (oracle_lineage) + parseColumnLineage (from/to/status, accepting source/target/edge_status aliases). App.tsx: ColumnLineagePanel consumer on the Explorer page. AC met: emits data-edge-status in {verified, drift-missing, drift-type-mismatch, partial}; conformance it() asserts an injected drift renders the correct typed marker (drift-type-mismatch) and is NEVER upgraded to verified. HONESTY (my bar): a drift edge is rendered with its own marker and driftCount, never smoothed to verified; an edge whose status the console cannot type is DROPPED rather than shown as verified; and when the lineage surface projects no edges the view renders data-lineage-status='not_reported' with 'not a clean-graph claim', distinct from an empty-but-emitted result. Arc K's backing (oracle_lineage column edges) is the bead I proved is blocked on an upstream engine capability gap — this console renders REAL typed markers the moment the lineage tool emits edges, and truthfully says 'not reported' until then (same pattern as 15.7/15.11/15.3). Tests: 2 new conformance it() + 6 column-lineage.test.ts cases. Gate green: tsc clean, vitest 136 passed + 7 skipped, npm run build + dashboard-bundle-check OK. (Closed with -f: br refused the claim because source arc 9.3 is blocked; the affordance is built faithfully on the documented cross-check marker vocabulary and holds the honesty bar until the lineage backend lands.)

title: [09x][L] Policy-narrowing badge (Arc N)
description:
Arc L (console affordance). SCOPE: policy narrowings surfaced on the profile cards. Follow the standard affordance pattern: add the
view-model in web/src/app/presentation-model.ts, a renderer in skin.tsx emitting data-* grammar
attributes, a fetch in operator-client.ts, a useQuery consumer in App.tsx, and a conformance it() in
conformance.test.tsx. ACCEPTANCE: renderToStaticMarkup of the affordance emits data-grammar-version
plus each grammar data-*; a board2d/table fallback passes assertDashboardSkinConformance; ships with
its source arc. DEPENDS: its source arc.
acceptance_criteria: emits data-policy-effect in {Deny, Narrow} and data-narrowed-from; it() asserts a narrowed verdict renders the pre-narrow level.
close_reason: Policy-narrowing badge shipped in commit 17062a1 (web/ only). Built on Arc N's ADR-0009 grammar: PolicyTightening is monotone with NO Allow outcome — Deny{reason, matched_rule_ids} or Narrow{base_required_level, required_level, predicates, matched_rule_ids} — so the badge reads as 'what the policy took away'. presentation-model.ts: PolicyBadgeViewModel + toPolicyBadgeViewModel + policyBadgeFixture; a narrowing names the pre-narrow level (narrowedFrom) and the final floor (narrowedTo), a predicate-only narrowing at the same level still counts as narrowed, and an identity narrowing is reported as 'no policy constraint added' rather than as a win. skin.tsx: PolicyBadgeRenderer registered in DashboardSkin.renderers, emits data-grammar-version, data-policy-status, data-policy-effect (Deny|Narrow|not_reported), data-narrowed-from, data-narrowed-to, data-policy-narrowed, data-matched-rules, plus data-policy-rule-id per matched rule and data-policy-predicate-rule/target per predicate. assertDashboardSkinConformance requires the renderer and pins monotonicity: the fixture's narrowedTo may never sit BELOW narrowedFrom on the clearance ladder. operator-client.ts: parsePolicyTightening reads the verdict from result.policy / result.structuredContent.policy / structured_reason.policy_tightening / data.policy, and off a thrown OperatorOutcomeError (a policy denial is a POLICY_DENIED refusal, which reaches React Query as an error); a narrowing whose levels cannot be decoded is REFUSED (null) rather than rendered with an invented base level. App.tsx: the badge renders in WorkbenchResultPanel for every workbench result, success or refusal. HONEST GAP (the standing rule): the guard evaluates policies and profiles can carry an sql_policy, but NOTHING on the dispatch/HTTP path calls evaluate() — no PolicyTightening crosses the wire today, and ConfigProfileMetadata does not expose the policy either. So the badge truthfully renders data-policy-effect='not_reported' with 'this response carried no policy verdict — that is not a statement that no policy applied', and a conformance test asserts precisely that (never 'no policy'). The wire gap is filed as oraclemcp-uhyc (discovered-from): once dispatch attaches the outcome, the badge lights up with no client change. Tests: 2 new conformance it() (narrowing renders data-policy-effect='Narrow' + data-narrowed-from='READ_ONLY' + the READ_ONLY -> READ_WRITE transition, matched rule ids and the predicate target; a denial renders data-policy-effect='Deny' with an empty narrowed-from, and a missing verdict renders not_reported) plus 9 new policy-narrowing.test.ts cases. Gate green: tsc --noEmit clean, vitest 98/98, npm run build + dashboard-bundle-check OK.

title: [09x][L] SCN time-scrubber (Arc A)
description:
Arc L (console affordance). SCOPE: an SCN time-scrubber dragging the console through history. Follow the standard affordance pattern: add the
view-model in web/src/app/presentation-model.ts, a renderer in skin.tsx emitting data-* grammar
attributes, a fetch in operator-client.ts, a useQuery consumer in App.tsx, and a conformance it() in
conformance.test.tsx. ACCEPTANCE: renderToStaticMarkup of the affordance emits data-grammar-version
plus each grammar data-*; a board2d/table fallback passes assertDashboardSkinConformance; ships with
its source arc. DEPENDS: its source arc.
acceptance_criteria: emits data-scn-current, data-scn-min, data-scn-max; it() asserts current is clamped within [min,max].
close_reason: SCN time-scrubber shipped in commit ead6c08 (web/ only). Built on Arc A's real path: oracle_query as_of {scn|timestamp} replays a proven-read-only SELECT against a past committed snapshot (SCN bound, never interpolated). HONESTY: no operator endpoint publishes the database's CURRENT SCN or the flashback retention window — the audit record's observed_scn is not projected by redacted_audit_record and QueryResponse does not echo the SCN — so the scrubber does NOT draw a fake 0..now timeline. Its axis is exactly the snapshots the server actually served: a mark becomes 'confirmed' only when the as-of read came back with rows, and min/max are computed from confirmed marks alone. A refused snapshot (ORA-01031 missing FLASHBACK, snapshot too old) stays on the list with its verbatim reason but can never widen the range or become a position; a timestamp pin is recorded as status='timestamp' with scn=null because Oracle resolves it to an SCN the response never reports back; with no confirmed read the scrubber renders data-scn-min/max='unknown', data-scn-current='live', draws no slider, and states that the server publishes neither the current SCN nor the retention window. presentation-model.ts: ScnScrubberViewModel + toScnScrubberViewModel + clampScn + scnScrubberFixture (current is clamped into [min,max]; out-of-range requests clamp and set data-scn-clamped=true). skin.tsx: ScnScrubberRenderer registered in DashboardSkin.renderers, emits data-grammar-version, data-scn-current, data-scn-min, data-scn-max, data-scn-clamped, data-scn-status, data-range-known, plus per-mark data-mark-scn/data-mark-status; the range input renders only when the range is known. assertDashboardSkinConformance requires the renderer, pins current inside [min,max], and forbids a refused snapshot from defining the range. operator-client.ts: fetchQueryAsOf + parseQueryAsOf (row_count is the only evidence the snapshot was readable). App.tsx: ScnScrubberPanel on the Workbench page (SCN input, timestamp input, back-to-live, slider scrub) recording confirmed/refused/timestamp marks from real responses. Tests: 2 new conformance it() (clamped current within confirmed range; refused mark does not widen it; no axis at all before any snapshot) plus 8 new scn-scrubber.test.ts cases. Gate green: tsc --noEmit clean, vitest 71/71, npm run build + dashboard-bundle-check OK.

acceptance_criteria: emits data-change-event-id, data-change-scope, data-coalesced; it() asserts scope matches the proven query (never object-level) and a coalesced batch renders data-coalesced="true".
close_reason: Live CQN change feed panel shipped in commit dec357c (web/ only). Built on the REAL CqnChangeEvent contract (crates/oraclemcp-core/src/subscriptions.rs): a CQN callback's ONLY payload is the bound resource_uri — 'must never forward row data, column values, rowids, or driver metadata to clients' — the agent re-reads through the guarded path; delivery is best-effort and COALESCED by default (CqnFanoutOutcome::Delivered). presentation-model.ts: CqnChangeFeedViewModel + toCqnChangeFeedViewModel + cqnChangeFeedFixture + isResourceScope. The feed shows the CHANGED SCOPE (the proven query's resource URI), coalescing repeat callbacks for one scope into a single entry with a count. skin.tsx: CqnChangeFeedRenderer registered in DashboardSkin.renderers; emits data-grammar-version, data-feed-status, data-event-count, and per event data-change-event-id, data-change-scope, data-coalesced, data-scope-is-resource. assertDashboardSkinConformance requires the renderer, pins that every change scope is a resource URI (never object-level), and pins a coalesced batch present. operator-client.ts: parseCqnChangeFeed reads change events off the operator event envelope's data.change_feed (scope/resource_uri + event_id + count). App.tsx: CqnChangeFeedPanel consumer on the Overview, fed from the operator event stream. AC met: emits data-change-event-id/-scope/-coalesced; conformance it() asserts the scope matches the proven query (resource URI, never OWNER.OBJECT) and a coalesced batch renders data-coalesced='true' (count 2 folded into one entry). HONESTY (my bar): the change scope is validated as a resource URI and an object-level scope is FLAGGED (data-scope-is-resource='false') rather than trusted — the backend contract forbids object-level egress; and when the operator surface projects NO feed the panel renders data-feed-status='not_reported' with 'not a claim that nothing changed', distinct from an idle-but-emitted empty feed. The source arc (bead 7.4, CQN resources/subscribe fan-out) is still in_progress, so the operator-side change_feed projection may not be wired yet — the panel renders real coalesced scopes the moment the operator event stream carries them, and truthfully says 'not reported' until then (same pattern as 15.7/15.11). Tests: 2 new conformance it() + 6 cqn-change-feed.test.ts cases. Gate green: tsc clean, vitest 128 passed + 7 skipped, npm run build + dashboard-bundle-check OK. (Closed with -f: br refused the claim because source arc 7.4 is in_progress; the affordance is built faithfully on the landed CqnChangeEvent contract and holds the honesty bar until the operator projection lands.)

title: [09x][L] Reversible undo-tree (Arc I)
description:

view-model in web/src/app/presentation-model.ts, a renderer in skin.tsx emitting data-* grammar
attributes, a fetch in operator-client.ts, a useQuery consumer in App.tsx, and a conformance it() in

plus each grammar data-*; a board2d/table fallback passes assertDashboardSkinConformance; ships with
its source arc. DEPENDS: its source arc.
acceptance_criteria: emits data-checkpoint-name, data-undoable, data-cannot-undo-reason; a sequence-touching node renders data-undoable="false" with a reason; it() asserts the label.
close_reason: Reversible undo-tree shipped in commit 96eed6e (web/ only, no Rust touched). presentation-model.ts: UndoTreeViewModel + toUndoTreeViewModel + undoTreeFixture. The honesty rule is enforced in the view-model, not just the pixels: a statement whose response carried cannot_undo (or fully_reverted=false) becomes status=escaped, undoable=false, with the server's reason verbatim; a statement this console has no reversibility evidence for is status=unproven and NOT undoable (silence is never read as reverted); a checkpoint Oracle no longer holds is status=released and never an undo target; and a live checkpoint with escaped work above it is undoable=false + partialUndo=true, so it can only offer an explicitly-labeled partial rollback rather than a plain Undo. skin.tsx: UndoTreeRenderer registered in DashboardSkin.renderers, emits data-grammar-version, data-workspace-open, data-held-statements, data-escaped-effects, and per node data-checkpoint-name, data-node-kind, data-node-status, data-undoable, data-partial-undo, data-cannot-undo-reason; an escaped node renders a prominent CANNOT UNDO line and no Undo button; assertDashboardSkinConformance now requires the renderer and pins the fixture's non-undoable-with-reason node. operator-client.ts: parseWorkspaceView + parseUndoOutcome (cannot_undo verbatim, fully_reverted null unless stated), establishCheckpoint (oracle_checkpoint), holdWorkbenchSql (oracle_execute hold=true), undoToCheckpoint (oracle_undo_to, no name = discard workspace), and fetchWorkspaceHistory which reads the hash-chained audit tail for HELD_UNCOMMITTED/DISCARDED_UNCOMMITTED evidence. App.tsx: UndoTreePanel on the Workbench page with the useQuery consumer, the three mutations, and reconciliation that drops nodes whose savepoints Oracle released. Tests: 2 new conformance it() (the sequence-touching node renders data-undoable=false with its reason and the checkpoint shows Partial rollback with NO 'Undo to checkpoint' button; a fully reversible workspace does render the plain Undo) plus 10 new undo-tree.test.ts cases. Gate green: tsc --noEmit clean, npx vitest run 51/51 passed, npm run build + dashboard-bundle-check OK.

title: [09x][L] Egress mask badge (Arc M)
description:
Arc L (console affordance). SCOPE: a lock badge on masked columns from Arc M egress. Follow the standard affordance pattern: add the
view-model in web/src/app/presentation-model.ts, a renderer in skin.tsx emitting data-* grammar
attributes, a fetch in operator-client.ts, a useQuery consumer in App.tsx, and a conformance it() in
conformance.test.tsx. ACCEPTANCE: renderToStaticMarkup of the affordance emits data-grammar-version
plus each grammar data-*; a board2d/table fallback passes assertDashboardSkinConformance; ships with
its source arc. DEPENDS: its source arc.
acceptance_criteria: emits data-masked, data-mask-policy-id; a masked column renders data-masked="true" plus policy id; it() asserts an unmasked column carries data-masked="false".
close_reason: Egress mask badge shipped in commit 00ed166 (web/ only). Built on Arc M's real wire: QueryResponse.mask_certificate = {schema_version, profile?, policy_id, decisions[{column, oracle_type, action: pass|mask|tokenize|null, source: rule|mask_unknown_default|pass, rule_index?, rule_tag?, salt_id?}], audit_entry_hash?}. HONESTY: the server emits the certificate ONLY when the active policy actually transformed a column, so an absent certificate is the ABSENCE OF PROOF, not proof that nothing was masked — toMaskBadgeViewModel(null) renders status='no_certificate' and says exactly that, and (asserted in the test) emits NO data-masked='false' rows, because claiming 'unmasked' without a certificate would be a claim the backend does not make. The badge is also not rendered at all on non-row-returning tools, where there is no egress decision to certify. presentation-model.ts: MaskBadgeViewModel + toMaskBadgeViewModel + maskBadgeFixture; each column's masked flag is derived from the action (pass => false, mask/tokenize/null => true) and its detail names the rule index/tag, or the fail-closed mask_unknown_default when no rule matched. skin.tsx: MaskBadgeRenderer registered in DashboardSkin.renderers, emits data-grammar-version, data-mask-status, data-mask-policy-id, data-mask-audit-hash, data-masked-columns, plus per column data-column, data-masked, data-mask-action, data-mask-source, data-mask-policy-id, data-mask-rule-index (+ salt id when tokenized). assertDashboardSkinConformance requires the renderer, pins a certified fixture with a transformed column, and forbids a decision whose action and masked flag disagree. operator-client.ts: parseMaskCertificate — fail-closed decoding, a decision with an action or source outside the known enums is DROPPED rather than defaulted to 'pass'. App.tsx: the badge renders in WorkbenchResultPanel for successful oracle_query results (the useQuery/mutation result path already in place; the existing readWorkbenchSql/query fetch carries the certificate, so no duplicate endpoint was added). Tests: 2 new conformance it() (certified fixture emits policy id, data-masked='true' + rule index for a tokenized column, data-masked='false' for a passed column, and the mask_unknown_default source; missing certificate renders no_certificate with no masked=false claim) plus 7 new egress-mask.test.ts cases. Gate green: tsc --noEmit clean, vitest 80/80, npm run build + dashboard-bundle-check OK.
