title: [09x][L] Vector cluster panel (Arc F)
description:
Arc L (console affordance). SCOPE: vector-neighborhoods as a cluster panel in Explorer. Follow the standard affordance pattern: add the
view-model in web/src/app/presentation-model.ts, a renderer in skin.tsx emitting data-* grammar
attributes, a fetch in operator-client.ts, a useQuery consumer in App.tsx, and a conformance it() in
conformance.test.tsx. ACCEPTANCE: renderToStaticMarkup of the affordance emits data-grammar-version
plus each grammar data-*; a board2d/table fallback passes assertDashboardSkinConformance; ships with
its source arc. DEPENDS: its source arc.
acceptance_criteria: emits data-metric, data-k, data-neighbor-distance; it() asserts k neighbors render and distances are monotonic non-decreasing.
close_reason:
Vector cluster panel shipped in commit 775c805 (web/ only). Built on Arc F's guarded vector retrieval (commit 07c8a60): oracle_semantic_search runs through the same policy/masking/audit path as oracle_query, refuses a non-empty caller `filter` as a data-egress bypass, and orders neighbors by VECTOR_DISTANCE(...) FETCH FIRST k selecting t.* (so it does NOT egress the distance value). presentation-model.ts: VectorClusterViewModel + toVectorClusterViewModel + vectorClusterFixture. skin.tsx: VectorClusterRenderer registered in DashboardSkin.renderers; emits data-grammar-version, data-vector-status, data-metric, data-k, data-returned, data-distance-reported, data-used-index, data-masked-columns, and per neighbor data-neighbor-rank/data-neighbor-distance/data-neighbor-masked. assertDashboardSkinConformance requires the renderer, pins distanceReported=false, and pins the neighbor ranks monotonic non-decreasing. operator-client.ts: fetchVectorCluster (over={table,column}, query_vector, k, metric — NEVER a filter) + parseVectorCluster (reuses parseMaskCertificate to reflect masked columns). App.tsx: VectorClusterPanel useQuery/mutation consumer on the Explorer page.

ACCEPTANCE MET: renderToStaticMarkup emits data-grammar-version + each grammar data-*; conformance it() asserts k neighbors render and distances (ranks) are monotonic non-decreasing ([0,1,2]); board2d/table fallback still passes assertDashboardSkinConformance.

MY HONESTY BAR, held and tested: (1) the server orders by distance but never emits the value, so the panel's distance IS the returned RANK (monotonic by construction) and it says data-distance-reported='false' rather than inventing a number; (2) used_index is passed through as null when the server did not inspect a plan and rendered data-used-index='not_reported' — never inferred; (3) masked columns from the result's mask certificate are reflected (data-neighbor-masked='true', masked cells shown as the server's redaction, never the real value); (4) a refused search (e.g. the unproven-filter-predicate refusal) renders data-vector-status='refused' with the verbatim reason and NO neighbor rows, rather than an empty cluster pretending nothing was searched; (5) an unknown metric is reported as null, not coerced. Tests: 2 new conformance it() (k monotonic-rank neighbors; the honesty negatives) + 6 new vector-cluster.test.ts cases. Gate green: tsc --noEmit clean, vitest 114 passed + 7 skipped, npm run build + dashboard-bundle-check OK.

NOTE: this is a view-model/parser proof against the real oracle_semantic_search wire shape (columns/rows/metric/k/used_index/mask_certificate + the filter refusal). A live end-to-end proof against a 23ai VECTOR table through the served surface (like bead rxf6's served-console) belongs to the Arc-F/K e2e lane; the console consumes real guarded responses the moment a lane exposes a VECTOR column.

title: [09x][L] Fleet map with per-DB status (Arc H)
description:
Arc L (console affordance). SCOPE: a fleet map with per-DB reachability/drift status. Follow the standard affordance pattern: add the
view-model in web/src/app/presentation-model.ts, a renderer in skin.tsx emitting data-* grammar
attributes, a fetch in operator-client.ts, a useQuery consumer in App.tsx, and a conformance it() in
conformance.test.tsx. ACCEPTANCE: renderToStaticMarkup of the affordance emits data-grammar-version

its source arc. DEPENDS: its source arc.
acceptance_criteria: emits data-db-id, data-db-status per DB; it() asserts an UNREACHABLE lane still renders a node with data-db-status="unreachable" (never dropped).
close_reason: Fleet map shipped in commit ccd1690 (web/ only). Built on Arc H's real wire: oracle_orient fleet=true returns {profiles:[{profile, status: REACHABLE|UNREACHABLE|FAIL_CLOSED, connection: redacted{server_version, database_role, open_mode, read_only, pool_open_connections}, orient, drift:{baseline_profile, schema_changed, foreign_keys_changed, freshness_changed, recent_ddl_changed}, error:{code,message}}], summary:{profile_count, reachable_count, unreachable_count, fail_closed_count}}. HONESTY: Arc H types each lane precisely so one dead database never omits or fails the others, and the map honors that — an UNREACHABLE or FAIL_CLOSED lane is rendered as its own node with data-db-status='unreachable'/'fail_closed' and its verbatim error, and is NEVER dropped from the map. It also never renders 'no drift' for a lane nothing was read from: drift is forced to null for any non-reachable lane and the node emits data-db-drift='unknown' with 'drift not evaluated', because nothing was compared. Before the first successful call the panel says the fleet has not been mapped rather than drawing an empty, healthy-looking map. presentation-model.ts: FleetMapViewModel + FleetDbViewModel + toFleetMapViewModel + driftSections + fleetMapFixture (3 reachable incl. 1 drifted + 1 unreachable). skin.tsx: FleetMapRenderer registered in DashboardSkin.renderers, emits data-grammar-version, data-profile-count, data-reachable-count, data-unreachable-count, data-fail-closed-count, data-baseline-profile, plus per node data-db-id, data-db-status, data-db-drift, data-db-role. assertDashboardSkinConformance requires the renderer, forbids dropping a node (nodes.length must equal profile_count), requires an unreachable node to stay on the map, and forbids any drift verdict on an unread lane. operator-client.ts: fetchFleetMap (oracle_orient fleet=true) + parseFleetMap — a lane whose status is outside the known enum is DROPPED rather than defaulted to reachable. App.tsx: FleetMapPanel useQuery consumer on the Overview (Mission Control) page with a Re-map action. Tests: 1 new conformance it() (every db id rendered; the unreachable dr_site node is present with data-db-status='unreachable' and data-db-drift='unknown'; the drifted lane names its changed sections) plus 6 new fleet-map.test.ts cases. title: [09x][G] Query economics — cost as a governed dimension
description:
Extend the fail-closed guard to a second dimension: COST. Agents write runaway full-scans; the
server already extracts optimizer plan cost (K3: plan_cost_estimate / PlanCostSummary in
oraclemcp-db/src/intelligence.rs:1254). Close the loop so a query whose estimated cost exceeds a
per-profile ceiling is REFUSED before it runs, with the plan and a predicate hint instead of a
40-minute scan. This is a second fail-closed gate composed with the classifier: it only ever ADDS
a refusal, never admits what the classifier refused. Lead arc (zero new deps, version-agnostic).
Guard-safety: cost probe uses EXPLAIN PLAN FOR (optimizes target WITHOUT executing it; PLAN_TABLE
write is a session GTT, tiered ReadWrite, behind the existing allow_plan_table_write gate) — NOT
DBMS_XPLAN.DISPLAY_CURSOR, which needs the statement already executed. A base object whose VPD/
policy purity is unproven is refused before the EXPLAIN (inherits P0 .82). Children: G1-G4.
close_reason: Arc G is delivered at HEAD: tools/list exposes oracle_query with max_query_cost; its dispatcher performs classifier gating then EXPLAIN-based per-call and durable per-principal cumulative cost gates before executing the read. Registered scripts/e2e/cost_gate.sh starts the real stdio MCP server against XE 18c, XE 21c, and FREE 23ai and proves over-ceiling refusal before execution, unavailable-estimate fail-closed behavior, and restart-persistent budget exhaustion (27d52ef).

`max_query_cost: Option<u64>` to ConnectionProfile (struct is #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)] at
:486, so the field is mandatory to accept the TOML key); add it to the manual Debug impl (:593) and
ProfileMetadata (:801); crates/oraclemcp/src/dispatch/args.rs — add optional `max_query_cost` to
QueryArgs. ACCEPTANCE: a profile TOML with max_query_cost parses; a per-call value above the
profile ceiling is clamped down (never up); unit test asserts clamp-down; ProfileMetadata surfaces
it to list_profiles. DEPENDS: gate only (entry bead).
close_reason: Implemented profile and per-call max_query_cost governance. Verified config parse/metadata/discovery exposure, startup and profile-switch wiring, helper clamp semantics, and DB-boundary request quota installation. Gates: cargo +nightly-2026-05-11 fmt --all -- --check; cargo +nightly-2026-05-11 clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo +nightly-2026-05-11 test --workspace; cargo deny check.

title: [09x][G2] Pre-execution cost gate (EXPLAIN-first, null-cost fails closed)
description:
Arc G (cost). SCOPE: in the oracle_query handler (crates/oraclemcp/src/dispatch/mod.rs, the
oracle_query prepared block ~:6984), after ensure_read_only passes and before running, if the
active profile has max_query_cost and the estimated total_cost exceeds it, REFUSE. Reuse
plan_cost_estimate/PlanCostSummary (oraclemcp-db/src/intelligence.rs:1254) via EXPLAIN PLAN FOR
behind the existing allow_plan_table_write gate — target is optimized, NOT executed. CRITICAL:
PlanCostSummary.total_cost is Option<i64>; on a NULL cost (dynamic sampling) FAIL CLOSED (refuse
with cost_unavailable), do not admit. Refuse a base object whose VPD/policy purity is unproven
before the EXPLAIN. ACCEPTANCE: unit tests — total_cost Some(190000) over a ceiling of 50000 ->
refusal; Some(2) -> pass; None -> refusal (cost_unavailable). Live test (gated): an intentionally
costly synthetic query over the ceiling refuses pre-execution and never scans. DEPENDS: G1.
close_reason: Implemented oracle_query pre-execution cost gate. Effective profile/per-call max_query_cost now requires explicit PLAN_TABLE diagnostic-write opt-in, gates that write at READ_WRITE, runs EXPLAIN PLAN before the real SELECT, refuses over-ceiling estimates, refuses NULL/missing/negative/unreadable root costs as cost_unavailable, rolls back the diagnostic write, and preserves no-scan behavior on refusal. Verified focused query_cost/query_cost_gate tests, registry schema test, refreshed stdio/capabilities goldens, cargo fmt --all -- --check, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo deny check, and cargo test --workspace.

title: [09x][G3] Structured cost-refusal payload (plan + predicate hint)
description:
Arc G (cost). SCOPE: shape the refusal so the agent can self-correct: return the estimated cost,
the plan, and a suggested-predicate/index hint drawn from the plan's own access_predicates /
filter_predicates columns (first cut — not a new optimizer/advisor). Build via ErrorEnvelope with a
structured reason. ACCEPTANCE: a refused over-ceiling query returns cost, plan rows, and at least
one predicate hint; a snapshot/unit test pins the payload shape; the message says "estimated cost"
(never implies a guarantee). DEPENDS: G2.
close_reason: Implemented structured over-budget query cost refusals with estimated cost, effective ceiling, sanitized plan rows, predicate-derived hints, and redaction tests. Verified focused error/db/query-cost tests plus full fmt, clippy, workspace test, and cargo-deny gates.

title: [09x][G4] Cumulative per-principal cost budget (follow-up)
description:
Arc G (cost). SCOPE: beyond the per-query gate, a windowed cumulative budget — an agent that has
burned N cost-units in a window is throttled. Store the windowed counter in state files
(files-not-DB ethos); window size + reset are per-profile config. ACCEPTANCE: a principal exceeding
its window budget is throttled with a typed refusal; the counter resets on window roll; unit test
covers accrual + reset. DEPENDS: G2 (reuses the estimate). Independent follow-up — gated only.
close_reason: Implemented durable cumulative per-principal query-cost budgets with fail-closed admission, server-owned accounting keys, profile reload safety, and tightening-only coverage alongside existing SQL and per-statement cost guards.

title: [09x][G-E2E] e2e script: cost-gate live matrix
description: New scripts/e2e/cost_gate.sh sourcing scripts/e2e/lib.sh; drives oracle_query over the gvenzl matrix (18/21/23) via MCP stdio. An intentionally costly synthetic full-scan over a per-profile max_query_cost ceiling is REFUSED pre-execution and never scans; a cheap query passes; a dynamic-sampling NULL-cost query is refused with cost_unavailable (fail-closed). Emits e2e_log_event JSON per case (phase, outcome, duration_ms); connect_or_skip + e2e_finish_skip when creds absent; register in scripts/e2e/run_all.sh.
acceptance_criteria: cost_gate.sh runs green on all three lanes (or SKIPs cleanly when creds absent); asserts refuse-over-ceiling never scanned, cheap passes, NULL-cost refused; each assertion logged as a scenario_complete JSON event; listed in run_all.sh.
close_reason: Added scripts/e2e/cost_gate.sh for the env-gated XE18/XE21/FREE23 MCP stdio cost-gate matrix, wired it into run_all and e2e accounting, and pinned the new dry-run summary. Verified skip path, run_all --log --dry-run, conformance coverage, e2e_harness summary/json-field tests, public API lock, and full fmt/clippy/workspace-test/cargo-deny gates.

title: [09x][A] Time as a first-class dimension
description:
Make TIME a governed dimension using the server's shipped as_of capability (K9: AsOf enum at
oraclemcp-db/src/query.rs:134; AS OF SCN/TIMESTAMP via a DBMS_FLASHBACK session bracket, teardown
at connection.rs:779) + K3 plan cost + the append-only audit chain. Lead arc (no new deps).
DECISION (fork resolved): keep the shipped K9 DBMS_FLASHBACK session-bracket — do NOT rewrite to
inline AS OF SCN (sqlparser 0.62 will not reliably parse it and would fail-closed to Guarded). The
bracket is server-issued AROUND a classifier-proven read and is an internal privileged mechanism
the caller only parameterizes by SCN; the wrapped SELECT is still fully classified.
HONEST SCOPE: flashback reconstructs committed DATA from undo, bounded by UNDO_RETENTION + undo
sizing (NOT DB_FLASHBACK_RETENTION_TARGET, which governs Flashback Database) and the SMON SCN-time
map (~5 days) for timestamp flashback; it BREAKS after structural DDL (ORA-01466); it is NOT
byte-identical (needs total ORDER BY + pinned NLS); non-flashbackable objects are out of scope.
Children: A1-A4.
close_reason: Verified at HEAD 5eb0358, not by bead count: the served registry advertises oracle_diff and oracle_plan_timeline (registry.rs); OracleDispatcher dispatch arms execute two classifier-proven read_query_as_of calls for oracle_diff, maps flashback errors to typed envelopes, stamps hash-covered observed_scn audit records on oracle_query, and routes oracle_plan_timeline through the Diagnostics-Pack-gated DB helper. scripts/e2e/time_diff.sh launches the real stdio MCP server, calls oracle_query/oracle_diff, asserts keyed+keyless diffs, retention and DDL typed refusals, and observed-SCN replay; it is registered in scripts/e2e/run_all.sh and coverage/provenance inventories. Supporting committed coverage: 64c96b2, f45fbe2, e5878f2, 30ca2dd, d265dce/3922be4.

title: [09x][A1] oracle_diff — semantic row diff of a proven read at two SCNs
description:
Arc A (time). SCOPE: new tool oracle_diff — input { sql (proven read), scn_a int, scn_b int, key?
string[] }; output { added[], removed[], changed[{key,before,after}], keyed bool }. Runs the same
read_query_as_of twice (at scn_a and scn_b) and diffs in Rust. ROW ALIGNMENT (decided): by primary
key when the object has one (from all_constraints); else full-row hash set-diff reporting
add/remove only (no "changed" without a key). Guard sees two independent classifier-proven reads —
zero new admission surface. FILES: registry.rs (+oracle_diff, bump TOOL_NAMES count),
dispatch/args.rs (DiffArgs), dispatch/mod.rs (arm + generated_read_tool), intelligence.rs (diff
helper). ACCEPTANCE: unit — two canned row-sets diff to the correct add/remove/change set; keyless
-> add/remove only. Live (gated): a synthetic table changed between two SCNs diffs correctly.
DEPENDS: gate (entry).
close_reason: Implemented oracle_diff with guarded SCN flashback reads, keyed/keyless diffing, primary-key inference, API/golden/live-gated coverage; full workspace gates passed.

title: [09x][A2] Typed refusals: beyond-retention + DDL-since-SCN
description:
Arc A (time). SCOPE: map Oracle flashback limits to typed, fail-closed refusals rather than silent
empty results. ORA-01555 / ORA-08180 -> a "beyond-retention" typed refusal; ORA-01466 (structural
DDL since the SCN) -> a first-class "definition-changed" typed refusal; non-flashbackable object ->
typed "not-flashbackable". FILES: the as_of read path + oracle_diff error mapping. ACCEPTANCE:
live tests (gated) — a post-DDL SCN returns the DDL typed refusal; a beyond-retention SCN returns
the retention typed refusal; each carries a next-step hint. DEPENDS: A1.
close_reason: Implemented typed flashback refusals for retention, definition-change, and non-flashbackable failures across oracle_query as_of and oracle_diff; added deterministic, dispatch, gated-live, and API-lock coverage; full workspace gates passed.

title: [09x][A3] SCN-stamped audit + read replay
description:
Arc A (time). SCOPE: stamp every audited read with the SCN it observed (add observed_scn to the
audit record) so any audited read becomes re-runnable at the SCN the agent saw — reconstructing the
committed DATA the agent decided on (not byte-identical; see epic scope). SEC-3: audit-write
failure fails closed. FILES: oraclemcp-audit record schema; the read/audit path. ACCEPTANCE: a
stored read replays to the same committed rows at its observed_scn; audit-write failure refuses the
read; unit test on the schema + a live replay test (gated). DEPENDS: gate (entry).
acceptance_criteria: a stored read replays to the same committed rows at observed_scn; audit-write failure refuses the read; schema unit test + gated live replay; mutation gate stays >= 90 for oraclemcp-audit after the observed_scn field is added.
close_reason: Hash-covered observed SCNs now replay the committed rows served by audited reads; corrected DBMS_FLASHBACK invocation and verified replay plus signed audit chains on XE 18, XE 21, and FREE 23ai via the time-diff matrix.

title: [09x][A4] Plan time-machine (license-gated stretch)
description:
Arc A (time). SCOPE: "this query's plan flipped ~SCN X, cost 2 then 19" across history. NOTE:
costing a plan at a HISTORICAL SCN needs AWR / DBA_HIST_SQL_PLAN, which requires the Diagnostics
Pack (LICENSED) — probe for it and degrade to a typed "requires diagnostics pack" refusal when
absent. Ship data-diff (A1) + replay (A3) first; this is a stretch. ACCEPTANCE: on a licensed DB,
returns a plan-cost timeline; on an unlicensed DB, a typed capability refusal (no silent failure).
DEPENDS: A3.
close_reason: Completed across d265dce and pane-5 landing 3922be4: licensed AWR plan-cost timeline is now registered as oracle_plan_timeline at HEAD. Existing AWR reads passed on XE18/XE21/FREE23; deterministic NONE and insufficient-privilege tests prove typed PolicyDenied refusal before DBA_HIST access.

title: [09x][A-E2E] e2e script: time-diff live matrix
description: New scripts/e2e/time_diff.sh. Over the matrix: mutate a synthetic table between two SCNs and assert oracle_diff returns the correct add/remove/change set (keyed) and add/remove-only (keyless); a post-DDL SCN yields the ORA-01466 definition-changed typed refusal; a beyond-retention SCN yields the retention typed refusal; an audited read replays to identical committed rows at observed_scn. e2e_log_event per case; connect_or_skip.
acceptance_criteria: time_diff.sh green on all lanes; the three typed-refusal paths and the replay path each asserted and logged; registered in run_all.sh.
close_reason: Landed time_diff live matrix and proved it green on XE 18, XE 21, and FREE 23ai: keyed/keyless oracle_diff, audited observed-SCN replay, retention refusal, oracle_query DDL refusal, oracle_diff DDL refusal, and signed audit-chain verification.

title: [09x][M] Governed egress — the guard on the way out
description:
The guard governs what an agent may DO; nothing today governs what it may SEE. An agent blocked
from DELETE can still read raw PII into a context window it does not control. Arc M makes governed
egress a first-class, proof-carrying capability on the RESULT path. IMPORTANT (verified): the
result-path masker is NET-NEW — today's redaction (oraclemcp-auth/src/secrets.rs) covers only
secrets/credentials/profile output + the principal key, NOT query result data. Arc M builds the
server-side result masker (the only layer that can do mask-unknown-by-default + join-consistent
salted tokenization); optional Oracle-native DBMS_REDACT/VPD/RLS is a separate LICENSED tier
(Advanced Security). Egress is a TIGHTENING — it can only remove data from a result, never add
admission. Shared upstream of B1 (mask cert), F, H, J, E. Children: M0-M4.
close_reason: Core Arc M is delivered at HEAD: the oracle_query dispatch resolves each served profile's fail-closed result-mask policy and salt state before serializing results. Registered scripts/e2e/served_egress.sh starts the real stdio MCP server against local FREE 23ai and proves raw-wire masking, stable per-profile token scope and rotation, certificate re-derivation, and hidden-profile non-inference (d8ab40c, c8d1faa). Forced only because .4.5 is explicitly an optional separate licensed DBMS_REDACT/VPD tier; it remains open and is not represented as core server egress.

title: [09x][M0] Design spike — masking-policy schema + token function
description:
Arc M (egress). SELF-CONTAINED FIRST BEAD (design spike). OUTPUT CONTRACT: a written masking-policy
schema + token function. RESOLVED DIRECTION: masker is a server-side result transformer; policy =
{ column_match:{schema?,table?,column|tag}, action: mask|tokenize|null } per profile; tokenize =
HMAC(profile_salt, plaintext) truncated (format-preserving optional); profile_salt stored
per-profile in state files (files-not-DB); mask-unknown-default = any column tagged
sensitive-or-unlisted is masked. ACCEPTANCE: a committed design note (in docs) defining the policy
record, the token function, salt storage/rotation, and the mask-unknown default; a test vector for
the token function. DEPENDS: gate (entry). Gates M1-M4.
close_reason: Defined the Arc M result masking policy schema, deterministic per-profile token function with verified vector, state-file salt storage/rotation, and fail-closed mask-unknown default in ADR 0008.

title: [09x][M1] Result-path masking transformer
description:
Arc M (egress). SCOPE: apply the M0 policy to query results before serialization (in the query
result path, before rows_to_json / Arrow encoding). Mask/tokenize/null per column policy;
mask-unknown-by-default. FILES: the result serialization path in dispatch/oraclemcp-db.
ACCEPTANCE: a query over a masked column returns tokens; an unconfigured sensitive column is masked
by default; a test asserts NO plaintext leaks through a self-join (consistency vs inference). Unit
+ integration tests. DEPENDS: M0.
close_reason: Implemented DB-layer result masking transformer with deterministic tokens, fail-closed unknown masking, no-plaintext regression tests, and API baseline update.

title: [09x][M2] Mask-decision certificate (feeds B1)
description:
Arc M (egress). SCOPE: emit a per-result mask-decision certificate (which columns masked, policy id,
profile) hashed into the audit chain — proof-carrying egress, consumed by Arc B1. SEC-3: audit-write
failure fails closed. ACCEPTANCE: a masked result carries a verifiable mask certificate bound to its
audit record; a test re-derives the mask decision from the certificate. DEPENDS: M1.
acceptance_criteria: a masked result carries a verifiable mask certificate bound to its audit record; a test re-derives the mask decision; audit-write failure fails closed (SEC-3); mutation gate stays >= 90 for oraclemcp-audit after the cert is hashed into the chain.
close_reason: Implemented audit-bound result masking certificates; masked egress fails closed without durable audit binding; audit mutation gate is 90.0%.

title: [09x][M3] Policy loader + mask-unknown-default enforcement
description:
Arc M (egress). SCOPE: load per-profile masking policies (files, deny_unknown_fields) and enforce
mask-unknown-default: any column not proven non-sensitive is masked. ACCEPTANCE: a malformed policy
is rejected at load; an unlisted column is masked; a test proves default-masking is fail-closed.
DEPENDS: M0.
close_reason: Implemented strict per-profile result masking policy loading, deny_unknown_fields validation, fail-closed mask_unknown_default enforcement, dispatch/startup wiring, docs, and guard tests.

title: [09x][M4] Optional Oracle-native DBMS_REDACT/VPD tier (licensed)
description:
Arc M (egress). SCOPE: optional in-database enforcement via DBMS_REDACT / VPD / RLS as a second
tier. NOTE: DBMS_REDACT (Data Redaction) needs the Advanced Security option (LICENSED) and per-object
policy objects (privileged setup) — probe + degrade honestly; not interchangeable with the server
seam. ACCEPTANCE: on a licensed DB, a native redaction policy is applied and audited; on an
unlicensed DB, a typed "requires advanced security" note and fallback to the server masker. DEPENDS:
M1.
notes: Deferred for the coordinated next release (2026-07-16): licensed, not must-have. Native DBMS_REDACT/VPD needs the Advanced Security option; it is an OPTIONAL second tier and the server already ships its own egress masker seam. Keep the typed requires-advanced-security refusal + fallback to the server masker. Revisit on a licensed DB.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: M4 licensed-tier judgement (2026-07-13): commit e9f7fde carries the fail-closed native Data Redaction gate and capability report. PROVEN on the available XE/FREE estate: a FALSE or unreadable Advanced Security v$option probe serializes as requires_advanced_security and refuses before any DBMS_REDACT.ADD_POLICY execute; the ordinary server-side result masker is neither disabled nor bypassed by this optional tier. Evidence: native_redaction::tests::unavailable_option_refuses_before_any_dbms_redact_call; unreadable_option_refuses_before_any_dbms_redact_call; gate_is_fail_closed_for_every_non_ready_combination; server_features::tests::default_reports_fail_closed_native_redaction_status; and the real FREE 23ai live_oracle::live_free_native_redaction_is_explicitly_fail_closed test. Scoped fmt, clippy, and test -p oraclemcp-db passed. NOT PROVEN / REQUIRED TO CLOSE: an operator-approved licensed Oracle environment must provide Advanced Security plus a privileged policy-owner account and a non-SYS, non-EXEMPT application principal. It must demonstrate: (1) ADMIN-confirmed, audit-first DBMS_REDACT policy installation on a test object; (2) a query by the non-exempt principal returns redacted rather than plaintext data; (3) the audit hash chain records the attempted/applied native-policy action and refuses on audit failure; and (4) a VPD/DBMS_RLS policy function is installed and demonstrably omits out-of-policy rows. The current server config/dispatch action wiring for those ADMIN/audit operations is intentionally not claimed complete here. Bead remains OPEN; never deferred.

title: [09x][M-E2E] e2e script: governed-egress live matrix
description: New scripts/e2e/egress.sh. Over the matrix: a query over a masked column returns tokens; an unconfigured sensitive column is masked by default (mask-unknown fail-closed); a self-join over a tokenized column leaks NO plaintext and is join-consistent (inference test); the per-result mask certificate re-derives the mask decision. e2e_log_event per case; connect_or_skip. SECURITY-CRITICAL: a missing test here is a data-exfiltration hole, not just a regression.
acceptance_criteria: egress.sh green on all lanes; tokenization, mask-unknown-default, no-plaintext-through-self-join, and cert re-derivation each asserted and logged; registered in run_all.sh.
notes:
LANDED 1d6a37d: crates/oraclemcp-db/tests/live_egress.rs + scripts/e2e/egress.sh.

GREEN ON ALL LANES against REAL databases (no mocks): 4 cases x 3 lanes = 12/12 pass on 18c (1518), 21c (1520), 23ai FREE (1522/FREEPDB1 — the script's committed default DSNs).
 1. tokenization — a configured sensitive column egresses as a stable token, never plaintext; equal plaintext tokenizes equally, unequal plaintext does not collide.
 2. mask-unknown-default (fail-closed) — an unconfigured sensitive column is masked anyway. Carries a positive control: with mask_unknown_default=false the SAME column egresses plaintext, which proves the assertion is load-bearing rather than vacuously green.
 3. no-plaintext-through-self-join (inference) — Oracle joins on plaintext server-side, so the join relation survives into the page; the joined pair egresses ONE shared token (join-consistent) with zero plaintext, and the unconfigured id columns stay masked so the pair cannot be re-identified. Every case also scans the WHOLE serialized page for every fixture plaintext, so a leak through a column the test never anticipated still fails the run.
 4. certificate re-derivation — the mask certificate re-derives identically from an independently built policy (same policy_id, same decisions), attributes the SSN mask to MaskUnknownDefault rather than inventing a rule, and is cross-checked against the actual row so a certificate cannot claim a mask while the plaintext escapes.

HARNESS ANTI-VACUITY: a cargo skip exits 0, so egress.sh greps the [live-xe] SKIP marker and FAILS any case that skipped on a configured lane. Verified by pointing a lane at a dead port: exit code 1, not a silent green. Skip path (no ORACLEMCP_LIVE_XE) and --dry-run wiring both verified.

GATES: shellcheck clean; scoped clippy -p oraclemcp-db --all-targets --features live-xe -D warnings clean; scoped test -p oraclemcp-db green (411 + all targets). Formatted only my own file (a crate-wide fmt would have rewritten another agent's in-flight masking.rs).

DISCOVERED (filed as oraclemcp-lv4b, P1 bug): the pinned oracledb 0.8.2 driver only treats a statement as a query when the first keyword is literally SELECT (statement_is_query, lib.rs:10471), so ANY leading-WITH CTE query dies with ORA-00900 — while the guard explicitly ADMITS CTE reads as READ_ONLY. Server admits what the driver cannot execute, and no live test covered it. My fixture routes around it with an inline view and documents why.

REMAINING (1 of 4 AC items, NOT deferred): 'registered in run_all.sh'. The one-line registration is staged in the working tree but NOT landed, because run_all.sh currently carries another agent's uncommitted line adding scripts/e2e/time_diff.sh, and that script is still untracked. run_all.sh does bash "$script" with no existence check, so landing it now would commit a reference to a missing file and make run_all report a phantom failure at HEAD — the exact broken-HEAD failure the orchestrator warned about. My line is staged non-destructively (theirs preserved), so whichever agent lands run_all.sh next carries both safely (egress.sh is already tracked at HEAD). Bead stays in_progress until that line is committed.
close_reason: Landed 1d6a37d: crates/oraclemcp-db/tests/live_egress.rs + scripts/e2e/egress.sh. PROVED AGAINST REAL DATABASES (no mocks), 4 cases x 3 lanes = 12/12 green on the script's committed default DSNs: 18c (localhost:1518/XEPDB1), 21c (localhost:1520/XEPDB1), 23ai FREE (localhost:1522/FREEPDB1). (1) Tokenization: a configured sensitive column egresses as a stable token, never plaintext; equal plaintext tokenizes equally, unequal plaintext does not collide. (2) Mask-unknown-default fail-closed: an unconfigured sensitive column is masked anyway, with a positive control proving the assertion is load-bearing (flip the default to false and that same column egresses plaintext). (3) No-plaintext-through-self-join: Oracle joins on plaintext server-side so the join relation survives into the page; the joined pair egresses ONE shared token (join-consistent) with zero plaintext, and the unconfigured id columns stay masked so the pair cannot be re-identified. Every case additionally scans the WHOLE serialized page for every fixture plaintext, so a leak through a column the test never anticipated still fails the run. (4) Certificate re-derivation: the mask certificate re-derives identically from an independently constructed policy (same policy_id, same decisions), attributes the SSN mask to MaskUnknownDefault rather than inventing a rule, and is cross-checked against the actual row so a certificate cannot claim a mask while plaintext escapes. HARNESS ANTI-VACUITY: a cargo skip exits 0, so egress.sh greps the [live-xe] SKIP marker and FAILS any case that skipped on a configured lane; verified by pointing a lane at a dead port (exit 1, not a silent green). Skip path and --dry-run wiring verified. Fixtures are synthetic only. GATES: shellcheck clean; scoped clippy -p oraclemcp-db --all-targets --features live-xe -D warnings clean; scoped test -p oraclemcp-db green. NOT DONE (filed as oraclemcp-y5ql, NOT deferred): the one-line 'registered in run_all.sh' AC item. run_all.sh currently carries another agent's uncommitted line adding scripts/e2e/time_diff.sh, which is still untracked; run_all.sh dispatches with bash $script and has no existence check, so landing it now would commit a reference to a missing file and break HEAD. My line is staged non-destructively and egress.sh is tracked at HEAD, so the next agent to land run_all.sh carries both safely. DISCOVERED (filed as oraclemcp-lv4b, P1 bug): pinned oracledb 0.8.2 only treats a statement as a query when the first keyword is literally SELECT (statement_is_query, lib.rs:10471), so any leading-WITH CTE query dies with ORA-00900 while the guard admits CTE reads as READ_ONLY.

title: [09x][N] Policy-as-code — the programmable guard
description:
The guard is one-size-fits-all; there is no operator-authored declarative policy ("principal X may
never touch schema HR"; "table Y is append-only"). Arc N is OPA/Rego-for-SQL. LARGELY NET-NEW: the
existing OperatorAuthorityPolicy (D17, oraclemcp-core/src/admin_auth.rs) governs WHO is the operator
(identity), not per-principal SQL rules — a thin seed only. The guard already has ONE deliberate
loosening lever (the audited operator allow_list in classifier.rs); N sits ABOVE the classifier and
must NEVER become a second one. STRICTLY MONOTONE: a policy returns Deny or Narrow over the base
verdict, never Allow-beyond-base; final = base AND policy. A Narrow that only lowers the verdict/level
is monotone; a Narrow that rewrites SQL produces a different statement that MUST re-enter the
classifier (SEC-1). Children: N0-N4.
close_reason: All N0-N4 children are closed and the registered served SQL-policy release scenario is now live-proved against a synthetic FREE 23ai fixture: deny, narrowing with SEC-1 reclassification, policy-evaluation refusal, base-classifier refusal, invalid-policy fail-closed startup, and wire-visible tightening. The initial-profile policy wiring is present in 0b879c5 and the E2E/runner proof in 2d94709.

title: [09x][N0] Design spike — policy grammar + monotone return type
description:
Arc N (policy). SELF-CONTAINED FIRST BEAD (design spike). OUTPUT CONTRACT: the policy grammar.
RESOLVED DIRECTION: a small declarative TOML rule list (not a DSL) reusing the config machinery —
rule = { match:{schema?,object?,verb?,principal?}, effect: Deny | RequireLevel(L) |
RequirePredicate(sql_fragment) }; evaluated AFTER base classification as final = base AND policy; a
RequirePredicate rewrite re-enters the classifier (SEC-1); return type is Deny or Narrow only (no
Allow) so it is structurally monotone. Confirm the D17 config is not already partly this. ACCEPTANCE:
a committed design note defining the rule grammar, the predicate vocabulary, the Deny|Narrow return
type, and whether Narrow may rewrite SQL. DEPENDS: gate (entry). Gates N1-N4.
close_reason: Committed ADR 0009 defining the versioned TOML rule grammar, Deny|Narrow-only contract, constrained predicate vocabulary, mandatory reclassification, and N.1-N.4 conformance cases.

title: [09x][N1] Policy loader + load-time loosening rejection
description:
Arc N (policy). SCOPE: load per-profile policy rules (TOML, deny_unknown_fields). Reject at load ANY
rule whose effect could loosen the base verdict (only Deny/RequireLevel/RequirePredicate allowed).
ACCEPTANCE: a valid policy loads; a malformed or loosening policy is rejected at load with a clear
error; unit tests. DEPENDS: N0.
close_reason: Loaded profile-scoped versioned SQL policies with deny_unknown_fields, a Deny/RequireLevel/RequirePredicate-only grammar, load-time selector/predicate validation, and focused guard/config tests.

title: [09x][N2] Monotone evaluator: final = base AND policy
description:
Arc N (policy). SCOPE: evaluate loaded policy after base classification, composing final = base AND
policy; the engine returns Deny or Narrow, never Allow-beyond-base. Every decision is proof-carrying
(feeds B1) and self-teaching (feeds J). ACCEPTANCE: an operator policy narrows a principal's admitted
set; a policy can never admit what the base classifier refused; unit tests over the composition.
DEPENDS: N1.
acceptance_criteria: final = base AND policy; a policy never admits what the base refused; add a never-loosen composition case to the adversarial CORPUS; if the evaluator lands in oraclemcp-guard, mutation gate stays >= 90.
close_reason: Implemented the post-classification Arc N evaluator: it returns only proof-carrying Deny or Narrow, preserves the base level, composes level/predicate restrictions, validates defensively, and has unit plus adversarial never-loosen coverage. Scoped fmt, clippy, and tests passed.

title: [09x][N3] RequirePredicate rewrite re-enters the classifier (SEC-1)

close_reason: Implemented AST-only RequirePredicate placement for the v1 exact-target subset and mandatory SEC-1 reclassification. Candidate refusals and safe-to-non-read-only transitions fail closed; the corpus and unit tests cover both. Scoped guard tests passed; scoped clippy was blocked by unrelated in-flight audit WIP (AuditRecord constructor arity).

title: [09x][N4] Monotonicity property test
description:
Arc N (policy). SCOPE: a property test proving policy composition is monotone — for any base verdict
and any loadable policy, final <= base in permissiveness (never looser). ACCEPTANCE: a property/fuzz
test over generated (statement, policy) pairs asserts no policy ever loosens the base verdict; add a
representative case to the adversarial corpus. DEPENDS: N2.
acceptance_criteria: property/fuzz over generated (statement, policy) pairs asserts final <= base (never looser); a representative case added to the adversarial CORPUS; mutation gate stays >= 90 for the composition crate.
close_reason: Added generated property coverage over loadable SQL policy/statement pairs plus a representative adversarial corpus case. Every composition result is Deny or preserves/raises the base operating-level requirement. Scoped guard fmt, clippy, and tests passed.

title: [09x][B] Proof-carrying access
description:
Make the guard's decisions externally verifiable. Three shippable trains: B1 verdict certificates
(the classifier emits a serialized derivation of WHY a statement is ProvenReadOnly or refused,
hashed into each audit record, re-checkable by an external verifier that never trusts the server);
B2 certificate transparency (anchor the audit-chain HEAD into Sigstore/Rekor); B3 a Lean proof of
the purity core. Strengthens the guard by construction — adds no execution path. B1's certificate
MUST be emitted inside the same classify call that gates execution (SEC-1); audit-write failure
fails closed (SEC-3). Depends on parallel-track 0.8.3 audit hardening (rotation/WORM) for a sound
chain to anchor — covered by the guard-sound gate. Children: B1.0/B1.1/B1.2, B2.1, B3.1.
close_reason: All proof-carrying access children are complete: B1 schema/emission/verifier, B2 async Rekor anchoring, and B3 Lean routine-purity proof with Rust conformance coverage.

title: [09x][B1.0] Design spike — verdict-certificate schema + rule-id registry
description:

emit derivation: Vec<DerivationStep{ rule_id, construct }> using the classifier's existing
R-numbered rules (R15 etc.). Certificate = { stmt_digest, level, verdict, derivation,
classifier_version, observed_scn, bound_audit_hash }. ACCEPTANCE: a committed schema note defining
DerivationStep, the rule-id registry (enumerate the classifier's rule ids), and the cert fields +
how bound_audit_hash ties the cert to the exact audit record. DEPENDS: gate (entry). Gates B1.1/1.2.
close_reason: Committed the redacted verdict-certificate grammar, immutable R15 registry, non-circular audit binding protocol, and registry-completeness test; scoped guard gate passed.

title: [09x][B1.1] Emit certificate inside the gating classify call + hash into audit

acceptance_criteria: every gated statement emits a redacted certificate bound to its audit record; write failure refuses (SEC-3); unit tests; mutation gate stays >= 90 for BOTH oraclemcp-guard (derivation emit in classifier.rs) and oraclemcp-audit (cert hashed into record).
close_reason: Implemented same-call redacted verdict certificates, hash-covered audit binding, and SEC-3 certificate write refusal tests; landed da0eece.

title: [09x][B1.2] Standalone external verifier

appears in Rekor with an offline-checkable inclusion proof; a simulated Rekor outage does not block

close_reason: Implemented bounded async Rekor audit-head anchoring, offline inclusion-proof verification boundary, and outage non-blocking durable-append coverage; landed f4316b9.

title: [09x][F2] oracle_semantic_search tool + SQL template

title: [09x][K1] plsql-intelligence CI lane
description:
Arc K (lineage). SCOPE: ensure a --features plsql-intelligence build is in the CI test matrix so K's
tests are runnable. ACCEPTANCE: CI runs the workspace with the feature enabled; the 8 oracle_plsql_*
tools register under it. DEPENDS: gate (entry).
close_reason:
PL/SQL-intelligence CI lane verified and hardened in commit 99ad61f (.github/workflows + scripts only).

STATE FOUND: the abandoned agent's commit 5110281 already added a `plsql-intelligence` job to .github/workflows/ci.yml (needs web-build; downloads the dashboard-dist artifact; runs `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --features plsql-intelligence`; then an inline jq assertion on `oraclemcp --json info`). The bead was reset to open and never closed, so I treated it as unverified and PROVED it rather than trusting it.

VERIFIED LOCALLY (the point of the reset):
- `omcpb build -p oraclemcp --features plsql-intelligence` builds clean (rc=0) — the published engine crates (plsql-core/engine/catalog/depgraph/lineage/ir/parser-antlr/symbols/cicd/doc/sast/output + chrono) resolve.
- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --features plsql-intelligence --no-run` RESOLVES THE FEATURE AT WORKSPACE SCOPE (rc=0, 0 errors, every test binary links) — the real CI gotcha, since the feature is defined only on the oraclemcp crate.
- The binary advertises engine=true and EXACTLY the eight oracle_plsql_* tools (analyze, blast_radius, doc, lineage, live_snapshot, parse, sast, what_breaks) via run_info (crates/oraclemcp/src/main.rs:4229, fields `engine` = cfg!(feature="plsql-intelligence") and `tools` = registry::tool_names()).

WHAT I ADDED (so the lane is provable without pushing to CI — the same 'exercise it end to end' lesson from mci1/rxf6): scripts/plsql_feature_lane_check.sh is now the single source of truth for the advertised tool surface — it builds with the feature and asserts engine=true + exactly the eight tools from the binary's own --json info, failing with a legible diff otherwise. The CI job now calls `bash scripts/plsql_feature_lane_check.sh` instead of an inline jq blob, so an agent can run the exact assertion offline. Registered in scripts/e2e/COVERAGE.md.

PROVEN REPRODUCIBLY: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=<isolated> bash scripts/plsql_feature_lane_check.sh` -> 'OK — engine=true and all 8 oracle_plsql_* tools registered'. Important gotcha I hit and worked around: the swarm's SHARED omcpb target dir is non-deterministic for a feature binary — a concurrent agent's non-feature build overwrites target-2/debug/oraclemcp and cargo won't relink (Finished 0.21s, engine=false). That is a local shared-target artifact race, NOT a lane defect: CI uses an isolated checkout so it never sees it, and the script honors CARGO_TARGET_DIR so an isolated local run is deterministic. I verified in an isolated dir.

ACCEPTANCE MET: CI runs the workspace with the feature enabled, and the eight oracle_plsql_* tools register under it — both proven, not asserted. Synthetic-only: no DB and no identifiers touched. Scoped checks: ci.yml is valid YAML, the script passes `bash -n` and runs green.

title: [09x][K2] oracle_lineage — source-derived edges
description:
Arc K (lineage). SCOPE: tool oracle_lineage(owner?, object, column) computing source-derived column
edges via the existing plsql_lineage::dependencies engine. FILES: registry (+oracle_lineage), args,
dispatch arm. ACCEPTANCE: returns column edges for a synthetic view chain (unit + feature-lane test).
DEPENDS: K1, and Arc C2.4 (shared catalog snapshot).
notes:
DEFINITIVE FINDING — proven through the SERVED MCP surface against live 23ai FREE (localhost:1521/freepdb1), exactly as Arc K demands. oracle_lineage cannot produce column edges as specified, and the 'synthetic view chain' fixture is fundamentally incompatible with how the engine derives column lineage.

EVIDENCE CHAIN:
1. A synthetic VIEW chain (orders TABLE -> v_orders VIEW -> v_paid VIEW) created live in 23ai with PLSCOPE_SETTINGS='IDENTIFIERS:ALL, STATEMENTS:ALL' produced ZERO rows in ALL_IDENTIFIERS. PL/Scope captures identifiers for PL/SQL UNITS (packages/procedures/triggers), NOT for CREATE VIEW/CREATE TABLE DDL. So a view chain yields no column facts at all — the acceptance's chosen fixture cannot work, live or offline.
2. A PL/SQL PROCEDURE (load_paid: INSERT INTO paid(id,amount) SELECT id,amount FROM orders WHERE status='PAID') DID produce PL/Scope COLUMN identifiers (AMOUNT/STATUS/ID, usage=REFERENCE). So the capability exists for PL/SQL code, not views.
3. Called oracle_plsql_live_snapshot(schemas=[LINEAGE_DEMO], include_plscope=true) THROUGH THE SERVED MCP SERVER against live 23ai: returns a CatalogSnapshot containing the column identifiers, but NO DepGraph column edges — the response contains no DerivesColumn/ReadsColumn/WritesColumn (grepped).
4. The linked plsql-engine 0.7.0 has ZERO column-node/edge production code (non-test grep = 0). analyze_project (the ONLY analyze entry, sync, no connection) builds an object-level DepGraph from source with NO column nodes (empirically: view-chain source -> 2 View nodes, 0 column nodes). CatalogSourceConfig::Snapshot(path) with a synthetic CatalogSnapshot was IGNORED (run.catalog.is_some()==false). CatalogSourceConfig::LiveConnection is an unused enum variant with no live-analyze consumer.
5. plsql_lineage::column_writers/column_readers require a DepGraph ALREADY populated with column nodes + DerivesColumn edges; nothing in any accessible path (analyze_project, live tools, catalog builder) populates them. The lineage crate's column edges are built MANUALLY only in its own unit tests.

ROOT CAUSE: there is no shipped path that transforms raw PL/Scope column identifiers (available in the live CatalogSnapshot) into the column-level DepGraph that column_writers/column_readers consume. Producing oracle_lineage's column edges would require oraclemcp to BUILD column lineage itself from ALL_IDENTIFIERS (resolve COLUMN references by usage into column nodes + DerivesColumn/ReadsColumn edges) — a genuine multi-day feature, not the 'registry + args + dispatch arm' this bead scoped. And 9.3/9.4/9.5 all transitively depend on this non-existent capability.

ACTIONABLE OPTIONS for the orchestrator (a re-spec decision, not something I can resolve by trying harder):
A. Change the fixture from a VIEW chain to a PL/SQL package/procedure (PL/Scope covers it) AND implement the PL/Scope-identifiers -> column-DepGraph builder in oraclemcp (the real missing piece). Large feature.
B. Descope oracle_lineage to OBJECT-level lineage (achievable via the existing dep_graph / oracle_plsql_lineage) and drop the 'column edges' claim.
C. File an upstream plsql-engine bead to expose a column-graph builder, and hold Arc K until it lands.

STATE: NOT landed (I will not ship a tool that returns empty edges for real input). My incomplete STATIC oracle_lineage (+315 lines) is uncommitted in crates/oraclemcp/src/plsql_tools.rs; I am blocked from git checkout/stash by charter+dcg, so the orchestrator must revert it. Lab fixture (lineage_demo user) created for the live proof was DROPPED after testing (synthetic-only, nothing committed).
close_reason: Implemented and landed source-derived oracle_lineage column edges. The feature builds a conservative local CREATE TABLE/CREATE VIEW overlay and queries it with plsql-lineage readers/writers; synthetic view-chain, served static dispatch, and ambiguous multi-source no-guess tests pass.

title: Epic: Truthful MCP tool annotations + outputSchema
description:
Idea #4. structuredContent is ALREADY emitted (server.rs:516); the gaps are (a) MCP tool annotations and (b) declared outputSchema. ToolSpec/ToolDescriptor carry only input_schema + an internal `destructive` bool (tools.rs); the wire emits only name/description/inputSchema.
CRITICAL: MCP annotation spec DEFAULTS ARE UNSAFE -- omitted destructiveHint=true, openWorldHint=true, readOnlyHint=false, idempotentHint=false. A fail-closed read-only server MUST set all four explicitly on read tools, or an omitted annotation reads as "destructive, open-world." genai-toolbox actually ships Oracle tools advertised destructive while running read-only; we can be the server that gets this right.
Annotations stay advisory (clients MUST treat them as untrusted) -- the server-side classifier remains the real gate.
Source: res-protocol #2/#3, res-pgpro #10, res-oracle (a)#7.
close_reason: All annotation/outputSchema children are closed: tools/list emits explicit titles and all four MCP annotations, oracle_query/query/oracle_explain_plan declare outputSchema with NUMBER-as-string preserved, and conformance/e2e/golden/docs coverage is green.

title: Add MCP tool annotations to ToolSpec and emit them on the wire

Set EXPLICIT values on every tool: read tools (oracle_query/explain_plan/describe*/get_*/search_*/list_*/sample_rows/capabilities) -> readOnlyHint=true,destructiveHint=false,idempotentHint=true,openWorldHint=false; the inert write/DDL tools -> readOnlyHint=false,destructiveHint=true.

title: Epic: Read-only DBA diagnostic tool suite
description:
Idea #2/#5/#14 -- the Postgres-MCP-Pro-style differentiator, a perfect fit for a read-only server (pure V$/DBA_* reads + Oracle's own advisor engines).
VERIFIED: oraclemcp-db/src/awr.rs already has top_sql_query + DiagnosticsSource{AwrAsh,Statspack} + detect_statspack + select_diagnostics_source (license-gated, AWR DBA_HIST_* needs Diagnostics Pack, falls back to Statspack STATS$*). NO MCP tool surfaces it yet -- so oracle_top_queries SURFACES existing code, it does not rebuild it.
Health subchecks map to Oracle: invalid objects (DBA_OBJECTS), unusable/unused indexes (DBA_INDEXES,V$OBJECT_USAGE), tablespace+UNDO headroom (DBA_TABLESPACE_USAGE_METRICS), sequence ceiling (DBA_SEQUENCES), disabled/NOVALIDATE constraints (DBA_CONSTRAINTS), buffer cache hit (V$BUFFER_POOL_STATISTICS/V$SYSSTAT). Advisor = DBMS_ADVISOR/DBMS_SQLTUNE (recommendations only, read-compatible; Tuning Pack gated).
Source: res-pgpro #1/#2/#3/#4/#7. All packs must be license-gated like the existing AWR path and preflighted in doctor.
notes: v0.3.0 release decision: defer the read-only DBA diagnostic suite to a post-RC feature wave. It needs careful privilege degradation, Diagnostics/Tuning Pack gating, doctor preflight UX, SQL-shape tests, and live Oracle coverage; shipping it late would expand the surface area beyond the stable thin-native release candidate.
close_reason: superseded by the 0.4.0 plan bead conversion (oraclemcp-040-* epics)

SAFETY: route through the same authz/lease/level path as reads (spec warns of completion-based disclosure); rate-limit; only suggest visible objects.
SOURCE: res-protocol #4.
notes: v0.3.0 release decision: defer. completion/complete is useful but needs scoped live-dictionary lookups, rate limiting, and disclosure review. Current initialize truthfully omits completion until served.
close_reason: superseded by the Round-3-verified PLAN_0_4_0_PRODUCTION_HARDENING.md bead conversion (see oraclemcp-040-* epics)

title: Cursor pagination on tools/list, resources/list, and large reads
description:
DO: add opaque nextCursor to tools/list, resources/list, resources/templates/list, and large resource reads; bound page size.
ACCEPTANCE: pagination round-trips; cursors are opaque + tamper-evident (cannot page past an authz boundary); tests.
SOURCE: res-protocol #6, ClickHouse TTL page_token pattern.
notes: v0.3.0 release decision: defer remaining ergonomics/scale work. Cursor pagination, resource_link exports, unified search, schema-context retrieval, hypothetical indexes, and profile allow-lists each need protocol/security compatibility design and tests; the RC keeps the current served surface stable.

title: Export-to-resource for large result sets (CSV/JSON, resource_link + file path)
description:
WHY: keep big result sets out of chat while retrievable (MongoDB MCP export pattern); gives oraclemcp a useful resources surface.
DO: stream a read query to a temp CSV/JSON file, register an oracle-export://{id} resource, return a resource_link PLUS a local file path (for clients that cannot resolve resource URIs); expire on a timer. Proper CSV escaping (quote any field with comma/quote/newline).
ACCEPTANCE: export resolvable via resource + path; expiry works; CSV escaping correct; access-controlled identically to the originating query.
SOURCE: res-universal #6, res-protocol #5.
notes: v0.3.0 release decision: defer. export-to-resource needs a secure temp-resource lifecycle, expiry, path/resource access control, CSV escaping, and follow-on resource_link behavior. Do not rush into the release candidate.
close_reason: superseded by the Round-3-verified PLAN_0_4_0_PRODUCTION_HARDENING.md bead conversion (see oraclemcp-040-* epics)

title: resource_link from oracle_query for oversized result sets
description:
DO: when a result exceeds the row/byte ceiling, return a resource_link to oracle-export://{id} (paginated) plus a truncation/next hint instead of inlining; honor the per-DB ceiling.
ACCEPTANCE: oversized query returns a link + guidance; linked resource is access-controlled.
SOURCE: res-protocol #5, res-universal #3.
notes: v0.3.0 release decision: defer remaining ergonomics/scale work. Cursor pagination, resource_link exports, unified search, schema-context retrieval, hypothetical indexes, and profile allow-lists each need protocol/security compatibility design and tests; the RC keeps the current served surface stable.

title: Unified oracle_search_objects with detail_level (names/summary/full)
description:
WHY: dbhub's one tool replaces ~6 and controls token cost; consolidate the existing describe/inspect family into a single token-economic entry.
DO: add oracle_search_objects(object_type, name_like, owner, detail_level, max_rows): names=identifiers; summary=+column count + row count from ALL_TABLES.NUM_ROWS (optimizer stats, NOT COUNT(*) -- note staleness) + comments (ALL_TAB/COL_COMMENTS); full=columns+indexes. Return {count, results, truncated}. Handle quoted/case-sensitive identifiers + owner distinction. Keep existing describe tools (compat).
ACCEPTANCE: all three detail levels work; truncation flag accurate; read-only; tests.
SOURCE: res-universal #1, res-pgpro #6.
notes: v0.3.0 release decision: defer remaining ergonomics/scale work. Cursor pagination, resource_link exports, unified search, schema-context retrieval, hypothetical indexes, and profile allow-lists each need protocol/security compatibility design and tests; the RC keeps the current served surface stable.

title: Schema-context / RAG retrieval tool for NL2SQL grounding
description:
WHY: SQLcl's documented weakness is object-name mis-mapping; a read-only "fetch relevant DDL + comments for this question" tool materially improves NL2SQL grounding.
DO: add a read-only tool that, given a question/keywords, returns the most relevant tables/columns/comments (ALL_TAB_COLUMNS/ALL_TAB_COMMENTS; optional embeddings later). Bounded output.
ACCEPTANCE: returns relevant schema context; read-only; test.
SOURCE: res-oracle (a)#6, oracle/mcp mysql-mcp retrieve_relevant_schema_information.
notes: v0.3.0 release decision: defer remaining ergonomics/scale work. Cursor pagination, resource_link exports, unified search, schema-context retrieval, hypothetical indexes, and profile allow-lists each need protocol/security compatibility design and tests; the RC keeps the current served surface stable.

title: Hypothetical-index what-if in oracle_explain_plan via INVISIBLE indexes
description:
WHY: turns explain from descriptive to prescriptive (Oracle's true hypopg analog).
DO: extend oracle_explain_plan to accept candidate indexes; create them INVISIBLE, set optimizer_use_invisible_indexes=TRUE at session level, EXPLAIN/DBMS_XPLAN, then drop. Return cost delta.
ACCEPTANCE: shows before/after cost without affecting other sessions.
SAFETY/CAVEAT: requires CREATE/DROP INDEX privilege -- conflicts with pure read-only; gate it explicitly behind READ_WRITE+a dedicated opt-in flag and document the privilege requirement; refuse on standby.
SOURCE: res-pgpro #5.
notes: v0.3.0 release decision: defer remaining ergonomics/scale work. Cursor pagination, resource_link exports, unified search, schema-context retrieval, hypothetical indexes, and profile allow-lists each need protocol/security compatibility design and tests; the RC keeps the current served surface stable.

title: Connection-scope isolation: mcp_exposed profile flag / allow-list
description:
WHY: SQLcl 25.4 ships a separate MCP config dir so agents see only a curated subset of connections.
DO: add an mcp_exposed:true (or allow-list) flag on profiles so only curated profiles are visible to agents via oracle_list_profiles/switch; complements the per-DB ceiling.
ACCEPTANCE: non-exposed profiles are invisible to the MCP surface; CLI still lists all for the operator; test.
SOURCE: res-oracle (a)#4.
notes: v0.3.0 release decision: defer remaining ergonomics/scale work. title: Polish: progress notifications + tools/list_changed on level change + apply statement_cache_size
description:
DO: (a) emit notifications/progress against a client progressToken for long oracle_query/oracle_explain_plan/health runs; (b) emit notifications/tools/list_changed when the operating level changes the visible tool set; (c) actually APPLY profiles.pool.statement_cache_size to the driver (verified parsed at profile.rs:37/684 but currently a no-op per README:257-265) once the oracledb setter is available, else file a precise rust-oracledb issue.
