title: Epic: Wire OAuth/TLS/host-origin into the binary HTTP transport (S1)
description:
VERIFIED gap (S1): the shipped binary builds HttpTransportConfig::default() at crates/oraclemcp/src/main.rs:707 -> OAuth None, no TLS/mTLS, JSON-only (no SSE), empty host/origin allowlists, loopback gated behind mandatory --allow-no-auth. Code comment main.rs:753-755 verbatim: "the HTTP transport has no OAuth wired from config yet." The W10 ScopeGrant scope-enforcement is real but reachable only at the dispatch layer / by an embedding caller, NOT through the served HTTP transport -- yet README:121-128 and docs/behavior-inventory.md describe OAuth scope enforcement + TLS as live. This is a truth-in-advertising + security gap.
Operator decision (2026-06-18): WIRE it into the binary (not doc-patch). The auth machinery already exists in oraclemcp-auth (oauth_rs.rs OAuth2.1 RS, scope.rs RBAC, tls.rs rustls); this is config-plumbing, not new crypto.
Children: config plumbing, OAuth validation, TLS/mTLS, session-id validation, protected-resource metadata, e2e tests, doc reconcile.
close_reason: All HTTP auth/TLS child beads are implemented, tested, documented, and closed: config plumbing, OAuth scope enforcement, protected-resource metadata, TLS/mTLS listener, stateful session validation, transport golden/conformance tests, and docs reconciliation.

title: Plumb HttpTransportConfig from CLI/config instead of ::default()
description:
WHY/STATE: main.rs:707 hardcodes HttpTransportConfig::default() (auth off, JSON-only, empty allowlists).
DO: derive HttpTransportConfig from CLI flags + profile/config: OAuth issuer allowlist + audience (RFC8707) + required scopes, host/origin allowlists, JSON-vs-Streamable-SSE mode, stateful session toggle, TLS material paths. Add flags (e.g. --oauth-issuer/--oauth-audience/--require-scope/--allowed-origin/--tls-cert/--tls-key/--http-stateful) with safe fail-closed defaults.
ACCEPTANCE: serve --listen consumes a real config; unit tests cover flag->config mapping; default with no auth flags still refuses routable bind without explicit opt-in.
SOURCE: self-map S1.
close_reason: Implemented binary HTTP transport config from profiles.toml and CLI: host/origin allowlists, JSON/stateful toggles, OAuth resource/issuer/authorization-server/scope/HS256 secret references, fail-closed startup, protected-profile secret policy, and TLS/mTLS config rejection until the native listener is wired. Added unit coverage for flag-to-config mapping and updated README/behavior inventory/robot docs.

title: Wire OAuth 2.1 resource-server validation + scope->level into served HTTP
description:
WHY: W10 ScopeGrant ceilings are tested at the dispatch layer but never supplied by the served HTTP transport.
DO: in serve_http, validate bearer via oraclemcp-auth (oauth_rs.rs: alg=none rejected, issuer allowlist, audience binding, exp/nbf, HS256+injectable RS256/ES256), build ScopeGrant from scopes, carry it into ToolDispatch so apply_oauth_scopes lowers the effective ceiling (min(profile max_level, scope ceiling); protected profiles stay READ_ONLY).
ACCEPTANCE: e2e tests prove narrow token < broad token < profile ceiling, protected profile pinned READ_ONLY, missing/invalid token -> 401 + WWW-Authenticate, insufficient scope -> 403. All through the binary HTTP server, not just DispatchContext.
SOURCE: self-map S1, res-protocol #10.
close_reason: Implemented OAuth 2.1 bearer enforcement through served HTTP: valid tokens carry scopes into ToolDispatch, scope ceilings are applied end-to-end, missing/invalid tokens return 401 WWW-Authenticate, insufficient scope returns 403, metadata remains public, and narrow/broad/profile ceilings are covered by binary HTTP e2e tests. Verified: cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace; cargo deny check; scripts/oraclemcp_boundary_lint.sh; scripts/sensitive_data_lint.sh.

title: Wire rustls TLS/mTLS into the binary HTTP listener
description:
WHY: oraclemcp-auth/src/tls.rs build_server_config (rustls, optional client-cert verify) is never invoked by the binary.
DO: when TLS material is configured, wrap the TcpListener with rustls; support optional mTLS client-cert verification; keep plaintext path behind --allow-no-auth only.
ACCEPTANCE: TLS handshake test; mTLS required-client-cert test; plaintext still requires explicit opt-in.
SOURCE: self-map S1.
close_reason: Wired native rustls TLS/mTLS into the binary HTTP listener. Added serve_https/serve_https_until using the existing HTTP parser after TLS termination, proper close_notify teardown, CLI/config PEM loading, mTLS as an auth mode, accurate HTTP/HTTPS startup warnings, docs updates, server-only TLS handshake test, mTLS rejects-no-client-cert/accepts-client-cert test, and binary PEM-path config test. Verified: cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo test -p oraclemcp-core http::tests::serve_https -- --nocapture; cargo test -p oraclemcp http_tls_material_builds_native_tls_config -- --nocapture; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace; cargo deny check; scripts/oraclemcp_boundary_lint.sh; scripts/sensitive_data_lint.sh.

title: Validate mcp-session-id on subsequent stateful HTTP requests
description:
WHY/STATE: self-map: mcp-session-id is emitted on stateful init but NEVER validated on later requests.
DO: persist issued session ids; reject/relabel requests bearing unknown/expired session ids per Streamable HTTP semantics; keep stateless mode working.
ACCEPTANCE: test that a forged/stale session id is rejected; valid session id round-trips.
SOURCE: self-map protocol gaps.
close_reason: Implemented stateful Streamable HTTP mcp-session-id validation. Initialize registers issued ids, subsequent stateful POST/DELETE requests require a known id, forged/missing ids are rejected before dispatch, DELETE invalidates ids so stale ids fail, and listener wrappers install a session store automatically. Updated golden harness and behavior inventory. Verified: cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo test -p oraclemcp-core http::tests::stateful -- --nocapture; cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --test golden_behavior golden_http_stateful_streamable_session -- --nocapture; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace; cargo deny check; scripts/oraclemcp_boundary_lint.sh; scripts/sensitive_data_lint.sh.

title: Serve protected-resource metadata + WWW-Authenticate from the binary
description:
WHY: RFC 9728 protected-resource metadata route and WWW-Authenticate are part of the OAuth RS contract but only reachable via non-default config.
DO: when OAuth is enabled, serve /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource (public) and emit WWW-Authenticate pointing at it on 401.
ACCEPTANCE: metadata document validates against RFC 9728; 401 carries correct WWW-Authenticate.
SOURCE: res-protocol #10, self-map.
close_reason: Verified and tightened binary HTTP OAuth metadata/challenge contract: /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource is public and returns resource, authorization_servers, bearer_methods_supported, and scopes_supported; 401 challenges point at the metadata URL exactly; invalid tokens include invalid_token; insufficient scope returns 403 with insufficient_scope. Verified through crates/oraclemcp/tests/e2e_http_oauth.rs plus cargo fmt --all -- --check, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test --workspace, cargo deny check, scripts/oraclemcp_boundary_lint.sh, scripts/sensitive_data_lint.sh.

title: End-to-end HTTP auth/scope/TLS golden + conformance tests through the binary
description:
WHY: the security claims must be proven at the transport boundary, with logging, before docs assert them.
DO: extend golden_behavior.rs + conformance to drive the actual served HTTP transport with: anonymous->refused, valid token+scope->allowed, narrow/broad/protected scope matrix, bad Origin->403, forged session-id->refused, TLS/mTLS handshake. Structured test logs.
ACCEPTANCE: all pass in CI (non-live); coverage matrix updated in tests/conformance/COVERAGE.md.
SOURCE: self-map quality posture.
close_reason: Completed served HTTP OAuth/Origin/session golden proof, binary HTTP OAuth scope/session e2e coverage, TLS/mTLS proof mapping, and conformance coverage matrix; gates passed.

title: Reconcile README + behavior-inventory with the now-wired HTTP security
description:
WHY: README:121-128 / behavior-inventory currently describe HTTP OAuth+TLS as live while the binary ships them off. After wiring, make docs exactly match reality (flags, defaults, fail-closed behavior).
DO: update README + docs/behavior-inventory to document the real flags/defaults; remove/replace the "no OAuth wired from config yet" note (main.rs:753-755) once true.
ACCEPTANCE: docs match the shipped binary; release_preflight doc checks pass.
SOURCE: self-map S1.
close_reason: Reconciled README, CHANGELOG, robot docs, CLI help, and behavior inventory with wired HTTP OAuth/TLS/mTLS behavior; removed stale TLS-rejected/proxy-only wording; release_preflight and full gates passed.

title: Epic: Positioning and documented guarantees
description:
Convert the research's converged positioning into documented, test-backed guarantees: "the safe-by-construction, precision-correct, single-binary read-only Oracle MCP." Neither SQLcl (privilege-delegated, write-capable, JVM) nor genai-toolbox (Query-vs-Exec, no parser, flag-flippable, NUMBER->lossy float) statically guarantees no mutation, and genai-toolbox loses NUMBER precision (int64/float64).
Source: res-oracle (b) differentiators.
notes: v0.3.0 release decision: defer external comparison copy. The RC now has test-backed NUMBER string fidelity and direct safety claims; SQLcl/genai-toolbox comparison text needs current source verification and citations to avoid stale or overbroad release claims.
close_reason: superseded by the 0.4.0 plan bead conversion (oraclemcp-040-* epics)

title: Document NUMBER->string as a guarantee with a differential test vs lossy float
description:
WHY: genai-toolbox maps NUMBER to int64/float64 (lossy); oraclemcp's NUMBER->string is a concrete correctness win.
DO: add a differential test proving high-scale/large NUMBERs survive losslessly as strings (and would lose precision as float); document NUMBER->string as an explicit guarantee in README.
ACCEPTANCE: test passes; README states the guarantee.
SOURCE: res-oracle (b)#2.
close_reason: README documents Oracle NUMBER cells as strings by default, and crates/oraclemcp-db/tests/type_fidelity.rs now proves a 38-digit NUMBER round-trips exactly by default while numbers_as_float=true emits a lossy JSON number. Verified: cargo test -p oraclemcp-db --test type_fidelity.

title: README positioning + comparison vs SQLcl / genai-toolbox
description:
DO: add a positioning section: "safe-by-construction, precision-correct, single-binary read-only Oracle MCP." Contrast with SQLcl MCP (privilege-delegated, write-capable, JVM, stdio-only) and genai-toolbox (Query-vs-Exec, no classifier, flag-flippable, NUMBER-lossy). Lead with the fail-closed classifier; note zero-runtime (no JVM/Python/Instant-Client) deploy.
ACCEPTANCE: README section accurate + cited; no overclaiming.
SOURCE: res-oracle (b).
notes: v0.3.0 release decision: defer external comparison copy. The RC now has test-backed NUMBER string fidelity and direct safety claims; SQLcl/genai-toolbox comparison text needs current source verification and citations to avoid stale or overbroad release claims.
close_reason: superseded by the Round-3-verified PLAN_0_4_0_PRODUCTION_HARDENING.md bead conversion (see oraclemcp-040-* epics)

title: Epic: Serve and advertise the built-but-unserved MCP protocol surface
description:
VERIFIED gap (S2): crates/oraclemcp-core/src/server.rs:193-195 advertises only {"capabilities":{"tools":{}}}; the dispatcher (server.rs:369-373) handles only initialize / notifications/initialized / tools/list / tools/call. But resources.rs (resource templates like oracle://object/{owner}/{type}/{name} + a 5-playbook prompt catalog) and subscriptions.rs (resources/subscribe + resources/updated) are fully built, exported, and tested -- just never served. Spec-compliant clients gate resources/list, prompts/list, resources/subscribe, completion/complete on capability negotiation, so this shipped code is DEAD to strict clients.
Idea #1 (highest ROI, additive, no new safety surface): advertise + wire the dispatcher arms.
Source: res-protocol #1, self-map S2. Spec revision in tree: protocolVersion 2025-11-25 (server.rs:193).
notes: v0.3.0 release decision: defer the remaining unserved subscription/update surface. The stable RC serves and advertises resources/list, resources/read, resources/templates/list, prompts/list, and prompts/get with conformance coverage; resources/subscribe remains intentionally unadvertised until durable DBMS_CHANGE_NOTIFICATION or polling semantics are designed and tested.
close_reason: superseded by the 0.4.0 plan bead conversion (oraclemcp-040-* epics)

title: Advertise resources/prompts/completions/listChanged in initialize
description:
WHY/STATE: server.rs:194 emits only {"tools":{}} so built resources/prompts/subscriptions are invisible to compliant clients.
DO: emit capabilities reflecting what is actually served: tools{listChanged}, resources{subscribe,listChanged}, prompts{listChanged}, completions{} -- only flip each on as its dispatcher arm lands (do not advertise unserved features).
ACCEPTANCE: initialize result advertises served capabilities; conformance asserts negotiation; nothing advertised that 404s.
SOURCE: res-protocol #1, self-map S2. Additive, no new safety surface.
close_reason: Advertised only served MCP capabilities: tools/listChanged=false; added conformance coverage proving resources, prompts, and completion remain unadvertised until dispatcher arms exist; refreshed golden transcripts.

title: Wire resources/list + resources/read + resources/templates/list arms
description:
WHY: resources.rs (16KB, templates incl. oracle://object/{owner}/{type}/{name}) is built but not dispatched.
DO: add server.rs dispatcher arms for resources/list, resources/read, resources/templates/list backed by resources.rs; enforce that resource reads honor the active session lease + operating level + per-DB ceiling (only expose what the current grant can see).
ACCEPTANCE: resources resolve; authz parity with reads; golden+conformance tests.
SAFETY: resource reads route through the same fail-closed/authz path as oracle_query.
SOURCE: res-protocol #1, res-universal (schema-as-resources).
close_reason: Wired served MCP resource methods: initialize advertises resources with subscribe/listChanged false; resources/list and resources/templates/list respond; resources/read serves capabilities/tools directly and schema/object resources through the existing guarded tool dispatch path; prompts/completion/session resources remain unadvertised. Covered by conformance, golden fixtures, full workspace tests, and release gates.

title: Wire prompts/list + prompts/get dispatcher arms
description:
WHY: a 5-playbook prompt catalog exists (resources.rs:239-281) but is not served.
DO: add prompts/list + prompts/get arms backed by the catalog; parameterized prompts validate args.
ACCEPTANCE: prompts resolve; conformance tests; advertised only after this lands.
SOURCE: res-protocol #1.
close_reason: Served the built-in prompt catalog over prompts/list and prompts/get, advertised prompts only after handlers existed, validated prompt arguments with structured JSON-RPC error data, and kept completion unadvertised. Covered by conformance, golden fixtures, full workspace tests, and release gates.

title: Wire resources/subscribe + resources/updated notifications
description:
WHY: subscriptions.rs (resources/subscribe + resources/updated registry) is built but not served.
DO: add subscribe/unsubscribe arms + emit resources/updated; back DDL-change notifications with Oracle DBMS_CHANGE_NOTIFICATION where available, else document polling.
ACCEPTANCE: subscribe -> receive updated on change; capability resources{subscribe:true} only after this lands.
SOURCE: res-protocol #1.
notes: v0.3.0 release decision: defer. resources/subscribe needs durable event semantics and Oracle DBMS_CHANGE_NOTIFICATION or documented polling behavior. Shipping this late would expand the protocol surface and risk unstable promises; current initialize truthfully advertises resources.subscribe=false.
close_reason: superseded by the Round-3-verified PLAN_0_4_0_PRODUCTION_HARDENING.md bead conversion (see oraclemcp-040-* epics)

title: Conformance + golden tests for served resources/prompts
description:
DO: extend mcp_conformance.rs + golden transcripts to cover capability negotiation and the served v0.3.0 arms: resources/list, resources/read, resources/templates/list, prompts/list, and prompts/get. Assert unserved arms such as completion and subscriptions remain unadvertised until implemented; subscription behavior is tracked separately in oraclemcp-epic-proto-surface-57t.4.
ACCEPTANCE: 100% of newly-served resources/prompts MUST/SHOULD items tracked and green in tests/conformance/COVERAGE.md.
close_reason: Conformance coverage now tracks initialize negotiation, served resources/list/read/templates, served prompts/list/get, and negative unadvertised completion/subscription behavior; subscription implementation remains deferred in oraclemcp-epic-proto-surface-57t.4. Verified: cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --test mcp_conformance.

title: Decision: keep oracle_session (guarded-write router) UNWIRED in the read-only binary
description:
WHY/STATE: session_tool.rs:194-336 implements a full lease/escalation/transaction router with step-up, but it is deliberately NOT in TOOL_NAMES (verified absent from registry.rs). AGENTS.md:46-48: the guarded-write machinery is built but deliberately not surfaced by THIS binary (writes are the sibling plsql-mcp's job).
DO: record this as an explicit, durable decision (code comment + behavior-inventory line) so a future agent does not "fix" the unserved tool by surfacing writes. If writes are ever desired they belong behind a separate, opt-in build, not the read-only release.
ACCEPTANCE: decision documented next to session_tool.rs and in behavior-inventory.
close_reason: Documented the durable decision next to session_tool.rs and in behavior-inventory: oracle_session remains unadvertised in the read-only oraclemcp binary unless a future explicit opt-in surface adds separate gates. Verified fmt check, registry surface test, release preflight, and diff check.

title: Epic: Ship v0.3.0 thin-native release honestly
description:
The v0.3.0 thin-native release candidate is implemented locally on oracledb 0.2.2. The branch contains committed work for thin-native config/profile handling, live-XE evidence, served HTTP OAuth/TLS/mTLS, MCP resources/prompts, tool annotations, output schemas, NUMBER string-fidelity proof, and explicit post-RC deferrals for larger feature waves.

Remaining release gates are outward-facing and operator-gated: push the local commits to origin, then cut/tag/publish v0.3.0 across crates.io / GitHub / GHCR / MCP registry only after explicit authorization. AGENTS.md requires pushed work to be up to date with origin, but also forbids push/publish without operator go-ahead.

Children: release verification, live-xe evidence, push committed RC body, cut/tag/publish.
close_reason: v0.3.0 is shipped and verified on oracledb 0.2.2. Main was pushed to origin; tag v0.3.0 triggered successful release workflow 27776856551; GitHub release assets and SHA256 files exist; all 9 crates are on crates.io at 0.3.0; GHCR manifest ghcr.io/muhdur/oraclemcp:0.3.0 is inspectable; MCP registry lists io.github.MuhDur/oraclemcp 0.3.0. Release-critical child beads are closed truthfully; larger post-RC DBA, safety-depth, ergonomics, protocol-subscription, positioning, and upstream-blocked items remain deferred for stability rather than fake-closed.

title: Verify quality gates green on the uncommitted thin-native working tree
description:
WHY: ~4848 insertions are uncommitted (oracledb 0.1.1->0.2.1, edition wiring, profile/config capabilities). Prove they pass before committing/shipping.
DO: on pinned nightly-2026-05-11 run: cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace; cargo deny check. Also run scripts/oraclemcp_boundary_lint.sh (engine-free + forbidden-dep gate) and the sensitive-data lint.
ACCEPTANCE: all gates green; capture each command's output. If anything fails, fix in-tree (no scope reduction) before closing.
SOURCE: AGENTS.md gates; self-map quality posture.
close_reason: Verified the uncommitted thin-native working tree with pinned nightly gates: cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace; cargo test --workspace --all-targets; cargo deny check; bash scripts/oraclemcp_boundary_lint.sh; bash scripts/sensitive_data_lint.sh. All passed; cargo deny emitted only existing unmatched allowance warnings.

title: Run the live-xe suite against local Oracle Free 23ai and record evidence
description:
WHY: serialization/lease/DBMS_OUTPUT/edition/profile changes need live proof; 7 gvenzl/oracle-free:23-slim containers are running locally (rust-oracledb-free, plsql-intelligence-xe healthy).

title: Epic: Read-only defense-in-depth + auditability
description:
Ideas #3 + #6. Double down on the core identity (safe-by-construction read-only).
(1) Session-level read-only backstop so the guarantee is two-layer, not classifier-only -- even a hypothetical classifier bug cannot mutate. (2) SQLcl-style auditability: per-statement marker comment + dynamic V$SESSION tagging so a DBA can see/trust MCP traffic WITHOUT trusting the client. (3) prompt-injection output fencing for returned rows.
VERIFIED: connection.rs:504-537 already applies DBMS_APPLICATION_INFO.SET_MODULE/SET_ACTION/SET_CLIENT_INFO + client_identifier from profile session_identity (static) -- so dynamic LLM/agent tagging EXTENDS existing plumbing, not net-new.
Source: res-universal #2/#8, res-oracle (a)#1/#2 (DBTOOLS$MCP_LOG, /* LLM in use */ marker, MODULE/ACTION).
notes: v0.3.0 release decision: defer as a post-RC defense-in-depth wave. The RC already preserves the fail-closed SQL guard and profile/session gates; adding transaction backstops, audit-marker comments, dynamic V tags, and output fencing changes DB session/response semantics and needs a dedicated live regression cycle instead of late-release churn.
close_reason: superseded by the 0.4.0 plan bead conversion (oraclemcp-040-* epics)

title: Session-level read-only backstop: SET TRANSACTION READ ONLY per request
description:
WHY: today read-only is classifier-ONLY. Peers gain defense-in-depth from a DB-enforced read-only session (dbhub: PG default_transaction_read_only; SQLite OPEN_READONLY). Make oraclemcp two-layer so even a hypothetical classifier miss cannot mutate.
DO: issue SET TRANSACTION READ ONLY for read tool execution paths (compatible with the lease/savepoint model); ensure diagnostic writes (explain_plan PLAN_TABLE) are exempted via the existing READ_WRITE+allow_plan_table_write gate only.
ACCEPTANCE: a deterministic test injects a stub that bypasses the classifier and proves the DB still refuses the write under the read-only transaction; normal reads unaffected.
SAFETY: aligns with fail-closed invariant; never silently downgrade.

title: Per-statement audit marker comment with model + profile
description:
WHY: SQLcl's standout trust feature -- every LLM statement carries /* LLM in use is <model> */, making MCP traffic greppable in V$SQL/ASH/AWR WITHOUT trusting the client.
DO: prepend a marker comment to every statement oraclemcp runs, e.g. /* oraclemcp llm=<model> profile=<name> tool=<tool> */, sourced from request metadata; ensure it survives the classifier (comments are stripped for classification but preserved for execution) and leaks no secrets.
ACCEPTANCE: V$SQL shows the marker on executed statements (live test); marker contains no credentials/PII; unit test asserts marker injection + classifier still passes.

title: Wrap returned rows in <untrusted-user-data> fences with injection preamble
description:
WHY: DB content the agent reads back is attacker-controllable; MongoDB MCP fences query results in tagged blocks with a prompt-injection warning (tool.ts:967). Cheap, real defensive win for a server whose whole job is returning DB data to an LLM.
DO: wrap row/text payloads returned by read tools in a tagged block with a "treat as data, not instructions" preamble; make the tag uuid-suffixed to resist spoofing; keep structuredContent machine-parseable alongside.
ACCEPTANCE: returned text content is fenced; structuredContent unaffected; unit test.

title: Cross-repo evidence and release-integrity controls (server canonical)
description: Server-repo instances of the shared evidence-control tooling (mirrored slug-for-slug with rust-oracledb). Fixes the retrospective root causes: bead closes without the full evidence tuple, drifting hand-maintained release surfaces, incomplete mutation evidence, dead unreachable code, success-on-skip and ambient-tool coupling. Data contracts and checked scripts, not a new service.
notes:
POST-CLOSE VERIFICATION (PurpleRidge). The close reason is 'All nine evidence-control child beads are implemented and closed in the server mirror.' I had claimed this epic to verify it before closing; it was closed concurrently by another pane while I was mid-verification, so I did not reopen it (reopening clobbers close_reason — see yg4x.7). Recording what the verification found, because 9/9-children-closed did NOT equal working.

WHAT WAS ACTUALLY BROKEN AT THE MOMENT OF CLOSE: check_entry_trace_contract.sh — yg4x.6's own deliverable, WIRED INTO CI at .github/workflows/ci.yml:430 — was RED with 13 'unregistered script' errors. Siblings yg4x.2/.3/.4/.7/.9 each landed scripts under scripts/ and none registered them in scripts/entry_trace_contract.json. Every one of those beads was closed. So this epic closed green-on-paper while shipping a red required CI gate — precisely the retrospective root cause it exists to fix ('bead closes without the full evidence tuple'). FIXED: 13 scripts registered as entry_point/required-quality (landed in 6e6de44, see provenance caveat below); a 14th, migrate_mutation_result.py, appeared minutes later and was likewise unregistered — fixed in 3976ef8. That is THREE recurrences in one session, which says the contract is only as strong as a habit that is not holding across panes; nothing catches an unregistered script until CI runs. Worth considering a pre-commit or a check inside the scripts that add scripts.

WHAT I VERIFIED GREEN (ran, not read): yg4x.1 all four schemas BYTE-IDENTICAL to rust-oracledb via cmp (required-proof/release-candidate-proof/mutation-result/bead-close-evidence) + provenance_check.sh exit 0; .2 verify_required_local.sh + test_verify_required_local.sh exit 0; .3 verify_release_exact_sha.sh + test exit 0; .4 release_surface_manifest.py + check_release_surface_versions.sh exit 0; .5 check_bead_close_evidence.sh exit 0; .6 now 'entry-trace: OK' exit 0 after the fix; .7 check_mutation_result + test exit 0; .9 check_resource_budget.sh exit 0.

WHAT REMAINS OPEN: yg4x.8's ci_taxonomy.py --check exits 1 on .github/workflows/_quality.yml (missing timeout-minutes/permissions). NOT fixed here and NOT a defect in either tool: yg4x.2 parks a non-workflow data file (no 'on:' trigger) inside .github/workflows/, and yg4x.8's checker correctly treats that directory as workflows. Two closed siblings' designs collide; it is a design call for their owners, and adding timeout-minutes to a file GitHub never runs would green the gate while asserting nothing true. Filed as oraclemcp-bqna with the three options and a recommendation. ci_taxonomy is not invoked by any workflow, so this is a red LOCAL gate, not a red pipeline — which is also why .8's close did not catch it.

PROVENANCE CAVEAT on the 13-script fix: I staged scripts/entry_trace_contract.json but another pane's 'git add -A' swept it into their commit 6e6de44 'feat(console): make the Workbench Commit a deliberate act too (oraclemcp-2ekf)', alongside web/src/app/App.tsx. The content is committed and the gate is green; I did not rewrite shared history to re-attribute it. It is a live example of why panes must stage only paths they own.

title: entry-trace-tristate-e2e: entry-point trace contracts + tiered PASS/SKIP-reason/FAIL
description: MIRROR (both). run_all.sh already records pass/skipped/fail; make it declarative and enforce by tier. Every production MCP tool/HTTP route/CLI/config-gate has a tested trace to dispatch/guard/result and its canonical runner; every e2e script is registered or explicitly excluded with a reason. Required-tier skips FAIL; declare/provision omcpb rather than ambient PATH; no live test fabricates a PASS when its DB is absent. Start with l6xn, 5c7i, K3, C3.3. Depends on evidence-contract-v1.
design:
CI ARCHITECTURE (decided 2026-07-16 — fast critical path + correct coverage + nothing runs forever). Supersedes earlier notes.
FOUR TIERS BY TRIGGER:
1. Every push/PR (hosted, timeout ~15m): fmt, clippy, deny, lint scripts, ALL DB-free unit/property/metamorphic/guard tests. No live DB. Only tier developers wait on.
2. Live-on-demand (hosted, ~25m): a `live` label OR a DB/driver path-filter fires ONE 23ai lane, only for PRs touching DB code.
3. Nightly scheduled (parallel matrix, each lane ~30m cap): full 18c/21c/23ai + OCI Always-Free-ADB + soak/canary. Off the critical path; where correctness lives.
4. Release tag (exact-SHA, blocks publish): full matrix + OCI producing release-candidate-proof/v1. Rare; correctness paramount.
NOTHING-RUNS-FOREVER CONTROLS: every job sets timeout-minutes (fast 15 / live 25-30 / soak capped); version matrix runs PARALLEL (wall-clock = slowest lane, not sum); concurrency cancel-in-progress on PR workflows. On hosted, each live lane cold-starts its gvenzl DB per run (acceptable: nightly/release are off the critical path and hard-capped); LOCAL runs on the operator box reuse warm long-lived containers to skip startup.
RUNNER PLACEMENT (both repos PUBLIC; operator decision 2026-07-16: NO GitHub self-hosted runners): HOSTED-ONLY. Fast tier + 23ai + OCI on GitHub-hosted — 23ai/oracle-free (the LARGEST image) already runs hosted, so the smaller XE 18c/21c fit too. Nightly matrix runs ONE Oracle version PER PARALLEL hosted runner (never stack all three on one runner) + the existing disk-freeing step + faststart/slim images. If a version genuinely cannot fit a hosted runner even then, run THAT lane as a LOCAL gate on the operator box emitting the same required-proof/release-candidate-proof artifact for the exact SHA (attributable) — do NOT add a self-hosted GitHub runner. This dev box = fast-iteration + the only local-gate fallback. Live PROOF OF RECORD = exact-SHA CI (or attributable local) artifact, never an oral pass; required-tier live SKIP = FAIL; no mocks (real gvenzl/ADB).
close_reason: Mirrored entry-trace/v1 contract with complete top-level script inventory, required PASS/SKIP/FAIL semantics, self-tests, and Required CI wiring; checker passes offline and required SKIP fails closed.

Replace with mutation-result/v1: raw counts, declared denominator/formula, recomputed rate, per-survivor taxonomy, and mutant-fails-test plus HEAD-passes-test witnesses per kill. Gate rejects null end-time, incomplete shards, wrong SHA, arithmetic mismatch, non-witnessed kills. Migrate the committed 92.6/96.9 certification with provenance; keep the 90 threshold. acceptance_criteria: Recompute rate from raw counts; reject null end-time, incomplete shards, wrong SHA, arithmetic mismatch, non-witnessed kills. Migrate the committed guard 92.6 / audit 96.9 GATE-SEAL certification into mutation-result/v1 WITH its provenance (kill commits); distinguish pre-kill vs final; keep the 90 threshold. Do NOT launch a new broad mutation run (skills-plan de-scopes it) — freeze/back-fill the existing artifacts.
notes: PROVENANCE NOTE (PurpleRidge, not the implementer): this bead was implemented and closed by ANOTHER pane (commit 36c7085 'ci: add mutation result self-check gate', scripts/check_mutation_result.py|.sh + test_check_mutation_result.sh). I briefly claimed it at 14:58Z because br still reported it open/unclaimed, saw their commit land 17 seconds old, and backed off — but my 'br update --status open' to release the claim landed AFTER their close and reverted it (transition reported 'closed -> open'), destroying the close_reason in the DB. Restored at 15:0xZ by re-closing with their EXACT original reason, recovered verbatim from .beads/.br_history/issues.20260716_145915_772291769.jsonl — I did not author, paraphrase, or invent any of their close evidence, and I did not verify their gate. The close is theirs; only this note is mine. LESSON for the swarm: 'br update --status open' to release a claim is NOT safe on a shared DB — it silently overwrites a concurrent close and drops close_reason. Re-check status immediately before any status write, and prefer leaving a claim alone over 'releasing' it. Recovery path if it happens again: the previous state is in .beads/.br_history/*.jsonl (timestamped snapshots), so a clobbered close_reason is recoverable verbatim rather than lost.

title: Resource-budget harness: isolated target dirs + RAM + PID/task caps for heavy build/test/mutation runs
description: MIRROR (both repos). Retrospective flagged this [x3] as the ACTUAL missing control: agent-killing incidents (~40GB RSS global OOM; cargo-mutants fanning to ~9,700 threads exhausting the cgroup 512-task limit so even fork() failed; a "safe" 4-shard rerun re-created the lockout). RAM caps alone were insufficient — the PID/task budget was the scarce resource. CHANGE: a reusable wrapper/convention every heavy run uses — isolated per-run CARGO_TARGET_DIR (never the global tmpfs one), explicit memory cap, explicit PID/task cap, safe shard serialization. ACCEPTANCE: a bounded-fanout test proves a heavy run cannot exceed the declared task/PID budget; each run uses an isolated target dir; required-proof and mutation-result records embed resource_budget. Matters even for a single owner running local builds/mutation.
close_reason: Mirrored enforced resource_budget harness with disk-backed isolated targets, MemoryMax/TasksMax cgroup limits, tmpfs refusal, bounded-fanout proof, and contract-shaped budget emission; all 7 checks pass.

title: Wire explicit thin-driver client identity fields from profiles
description:
## Current gap

`rust-oracledb` already accepts a full `ClientIdentity` with `program`, `machine`, `osuser`, `terminal`, and `driver_name`. oraclemcp only exposes `session_identity.module`, `client_identifier`, and `driver_name`, then currently maps `module -> program`, process `$HOSTNAME -> machine`, process `$USER -> osuser`, and `client_identifier -> terminal` in `crates/oraclemcp-db/src/connection.rs`.

That means operators cannot deliberately set the Oracle-visible client identity fields that the thin driver already supports. It also makes the README stale because it says the driver lacks setters, while the driver now has them.

## Required change

Extend profile/session identity config and DB connect options so operators can set driver-level identity fields explicitly:

- `session_identity.program`
- `session_identity.machine`
- `session_identity.os_user` or `osuser` with one documented spelling
- `session_identity.terminal`

Preserve existing `module`, `action`, `client_identifier`, and `client_info` behavior as Oracle session attributes set after connect via `DBMS_APPLICATION_INFO` / `DBMS_SESSION`.

## Implementation notes

- Keep backwards compatibility: if the new driver-level fields are absent, retain current defaults/mapping so existing profiles still work.
- Prefer explicit driver fields over overloaded values when present.
- Keep `driver_name` in the connect-time identity.
- Do not expose configured identity values in `oracle_list_profiles`; these can identify operators, hosts, tenants, or workflows.

## Acceptance criteria

- Config schema accepts and inherits the new identity fields.
- `profile_to_options` carries them into `OracleConnectOptions`.
- `RustOracleConnection` maps them into `oracledb::protocol::ClientIdentity`.
- Unit tests prove defaults, explicit overrides, inheritance, and metadata redaction.
- README/setup templates accurately distinguish driver-level identity from DBMS session attributes.
- A live 23ai/FREE verification note or test checks `SYS_CONTEXT`/`V$SESSION` fields where privileges allow it.
close_reason: Implemented explicit thin driver identity fields with profile/core/db mapping, redaction, README/setup updates, unit tests, workspace gates, and gated live-test path

title: E11: never infer a gate pass from a successful push (charter rule 15 + verified push)
description: Live incident 2026-07-21: full_gate.sh printed '!!! GATE FAILED: cargo test --workspace --features plsql-intelligence' and 25 commits were pushed on it anyway, turning CI red. Encode the rule: read the gate verdict yourself; never infer a pass from a successful push. Deliverable: AGENTS.md constitution rule + a push path that runs the gate, captures its exit status, and refuses to push on a non-zero verdict, so the verdict cannot be skipped by not reading it.
close_reason: Rule 15 + verified-push: the gate runs inside the push path, so a failing verdict never reaches git push [closing=5b9b65bfc54ed46652b943dc5d21ca8a8512d9f2 source=32122cb9857684ace414e03ef4a3ca6771dc0183 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-fbqr0.json]

title: Field-test 2026-07 findings: driver adoption + install/onboarding polish
description:
Umbrella for the 2026-07-03 fresh-install field test of v0.6.6 (WSL2/Ubuntu, real one-liner install, onboarding, Claude Code MCP wiring, live-connect attempt against a customer fleet). Headline results: install + onboarding + integration are solid - prior issues 7/9/10/11/14 confirmed fixed live; serve answered a real MCP handshake with 52 governed tools on the setup --write starter profile.

The one blocker is upstream: the oracledb 0.5.1 thin driver cannot connect to any pre-23ai Oracle server (fleet is 19c). Root-caused in the driver repo (epic rust-oracledb-pre23ai-connect-z47u): unhandled TNS RESEND packet + fast-auth-only connect flow; reproduced locally on gvenzl XE 18/21 while python-oracledb thin connects. Driver fix ships as oracledb 0.5.2; this epic carries the oraclemcp-side adoption, error-UX, and the install/onboarding polish items the test surfaced.

Sanitization rule for all children: no customer hostnames, service names, machine paths, or employer identifiers in beads/commits/docs - describe the environment generically (customer fleet, Oracle 19c, plain TCP).
close_reason: All 9 children closed: driver 0.6.0->0.7.1 adoption, actionable connect-error envelopes, setup snippet fix, install hint, XDG config discovery, installer gnu-triple + cosign dry-run, doctor/protocolVersion nits, pre-production version matrix. Field-test blocker fully resolved and released; standing ladder e2e + version matrix guard it.

title: Adopt oracledb 0.6.0 (pre-23ai connect+query support): deliberate exact-pin bump + full gates
description:
Bump the exact pins oracledb = "=0.5.1" and oracledb-protocol = "=0.5.1" to 0.5.2 once the driver ships pre-23ai connect support (TNS RESEND handling + classic non-fast-auth session establishment; driver epic rust-oracledb-pre23ai-connect-z47u). This is what makes oraclemcp usable against Oracle 18c/19c/21c fleets - today doctor --online fails the connectivity check on any pre-23ai server ("unknown TTC message type 11 at position 4"), reproduced locally on gvenzl/oracle-xe:18-slim and :21-slim.

Per the driver-seam policy (workspace Cargo.toml comments + scripts/oraclemcp_driver_seam_lint.sh): the whole driver surface is confined to crates/oraclemcp-db/src/connection.rs; bump both pins together and deliberately; re-run the adapter contract suite and the full gate battery (fmt, clippy -D warnings, workspace tests, cargo deny, agent-surface/driver-seam/honesty/api-lock lints) plus the live matrix (XE 18, XE 21, FREE 23ai) before merging. Watch for new driver error variants (e.g. an unexpected-packet-type error) that connection.rs should map.
notes: VERSION CORRECTION 2026-07-04: the driver release carrying pre-23ai support is 0.6.0, not 0.5.2 - the protocol crate's builder signatures changed (ttc_field_version params), and the driver repo's ADR-0002 semver contract requires a minor bump for breaking changes on 0.x. Everything else in this bead applies unchanged; bump BOTH exact pins (oracledb, oracledb-protocol) to =0.6.0. Driver release scope: RESEND handling, classic (non-fast-auth) session establishment, ub8 token gating, classic response framing for core query paths, duplicate-row fetch-continuation fix. Live-verified on XE 18/21 + 23ai FREE in the driver repo.
close_reason: Adopted oracledb 0.6.0 (was titled 0.5.2; semver truth is 0.6.0 per driver ADR-0002). Both exact pins bumped (=0.6.0 oracledb + oracledb-protocol), driver-seam pin-contract test updated, stale doc references refreshed. Full gate battery green: build, fmt, clippy -D warnings, complete workspace tests, cargo deny, agent-surface/driver-seam/honesty/api-lock/boundary lints. LIVE end-to-end verification on the three-lane lab matrix: doctor --online connectivity PASS on XE 18 + XE 21 (the exact check that failed in the field) and 23ai; real MCP oracle_query returned correct v$version banners through the full stack on all three lanes.

title: Actionable connect-error envelopes + doctor guidance for driver handshake failures
description:
Field UX gap: doctor --online and MCP error envelopes surfaced the raw driver string "unknown TTC message type 11 at position 4" with no interpretation and no next steps. It was opaque to the user AND actively misleading (it names the TTC application layer while the failing byte was a network-layer TNS packet type; triage chased wrong hypotheses for a session).

Scope: in the driver-seam adapter (crates/oraclemcp-db/src/connection.rs), map driver connect/handshake failures into structured ErrorEnvelope classes with plain-language messages and next_actions - e.g. server-generation-not-supported (pre-23ai until the 0.5.2 adoption lands), listener-redirect-unsupported, wallet-format-unsupported (already good), and a generic handshake-protocol-error that names the phase and points at the driver handshake-trace instructions. doctor --online should print the same guidance (fix: lines with a concrete command or doc link, matching its existing remediation style). Include the negotiated/attempted endpoint metadata only in redacted form, consistent with existing doctor redaction guarantees.

Test: unit tests over the adapter mapping; doctor snapshot test with a simulated pre-23ai handshake failure. Depends on the 0.5.2 pin bump for the new variants, but the envelope/class scaffolding and the current-string mapping can land first.
close_reason: Connect-error UX landed: driver-seam adapter maps oracledb 0.6.0 handshake failures (UnexpectedPacket, ConnectResendLoop, FastAuthRequired, UnsupportedFeature, ListenerRefused ERR= extraction, below-floor server refusal, wallet diagnostics) to structured envelopes with plain-language messages + next_actions incl. ORACLEDB_TRACE_CONNECT triage guidance; doctor --online surfaces the same fix: lines; new public ConnectFailureKind surface baselined via api-lock procedure; unit tests over the mapping.

title: setup MCP snippet defaults to a wrapper path nothing creates - unify on real binary
description:
Field friction (paste-broken UX): oraclemcp setup --profile

prints MCP client snippets whose command is the --wrapper-path default ~/.local/bin/oraclemcp-local - but nothing ever creates that wrapper: setup only prints a wrapper template, and the installer never writes one. A fresh user who pastes the setup snippet wires their MCP client to a nonexistent path and gets a dead server entry. Meanwhile install.sh print_client_snippet correctly uses the resolved real binary, so our two snippet sources disagree. Fix: default the setup snippet command to the resolved real binary path (or the om alias) exactly like the installer does; print the wrapper-path variant only when the operator explicitly passes --wrapper-path, and in that case state clearly that the wrapper must exist (or offer to write it through the same governed config-ops path). Keep Claude JSON and Codex TOML variants consistent. Test: CLI test asserting the default snippet command equals the resolved current-exe (or om) and never the uncreated wrapper path; a second test for the explicit --wrapper-path flow.
close_reason: setup snippets now default to the resolved real binary (current_exe, matching install.sh print_client_snippet); --wrapper-path is opt-in (no default) and both JSON payload + human output state the wrapper must be created first; Claude JSON and Codex TOML stay consistent; unit test setup_payload_default_snippets_use_the_real_binary_not_a_wrapper + CLI tests setup_default_snippet_command_is_the_resolved_binary / setup_explicit_wrapper_path_snippets_state_wrapper_must_exist

title: Align setup Install: hint with supported channels (cargo install needs the nightly pin)
description: setup output includes an "Install:" hint recommending cargo install oraclemcp. On stable Rust that fails: the workspace needs the pinned nightly (asupersync try_trait_v2 features), and source install is deliberately not the primary channel. Align the hint with reality: the one-line install.sh command (with cache-buster + version), oraclemcp self-update for existing installs, and cargo binstall as the cargo-ecosystem path; mention cargo +nightly-<pin> install only as the deliberate source-build escape hatch, matching README "Source builds" wording. One-line change + snapshot test update.
close_reason: setup Install hint now lists real channels: one-line install.sh (cache-buster + pinned version), oraclemcp self-update, cargo binstall, and cargo +nightly-2026-05-11 install as the explicit source-build escape hatch (plain cargo install fails on stable); JSON payload install{} restructured accordingly; asserted in setup_payload_default_snippets_use_the_real_binary_not_a_wrapper

title: Config discovery: honor XDG_CONFIG_HOME (or document the asymmetry)
description:
Field surprise: exporting XDG_CONFIG_HOME does NOT relocate config discovery - the search order is ORACLEMCP_CONFIG, then literal ~/.config/oraclemcp/profiles.toml, then config.toml - while STATE does follow XDG_STATE_HOME (write-intents, audit). XDG-native users expect config to follow XDG_CONFIG_HOME.

Decide and implement ONE of: (a) preferred - honor XDG_CONFIG_HOME in oraclemcp-config discovery (insert $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/oraclemcp/... ahead of the ~/.config fallback; keep ORACLEMCP_CONFIG highest), with tests for precedence and doctor/config-ops path reporting; or (b) document the asymmetry prominently in README config-discovery section + docs/configuration.md. Option (a) is a small, backwards-compatible superset (on most boxes XDG_CONFIG_HOME is unset or already ~/.config). Either way add a regression test pinning the chosen precedence.
close_reason: option (a) implemented: OracleMcpConfig discovery now honors $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/oraclemcp/{profiles,config}.toml ahead of the ~/.config fallback (absolute paths only, per XDG spec; ORACLEMCP_CONFIG stays highest) via new config_search_dirs(); operator_config_target_path (setup --write/doctor fresh-target) follows the same order; precedence regression tests xdg_config_home_precedes_home_config_and_explicit_env_stays_highest (incl relative-XDG-ignored) + CLI test setup_write_default_target_honors_xdg_config_home; README + docs/configuration.md discovery order updated

title: install.sh: allow explicit --target linux-gnu (published tarball is unreachable)
description:
install.sh detect_target maps Linux:x86_64 to x86_64-unknown-linux-musl and validate_target accepts only musl/darwin triples - so the published x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu release tarball is unreachable through the installer even with an explicit --target (it is rejected). Field impact minor (static musl runs everywhere incl. WSL2) but a user who wants the gnu build must download + extract by hand.

Fix: accept gnu triples when passed explicitly via --target (keep musl the auto-detected default), or document musl-only clearly in README install section. Prefer the former; it is a validate_target allow-list widening plus an installer-lint/e2e case (scripts/e2e installer suite has dry-run coverage to extend).
close_reason: validate_target now accepts x86_64/aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu when TARGET_EXPLICIT=1 (--target); musl stays the auto-detected Linux default and non-matrix triples still fail; usage text + README install section document the explicit gnu path; installer smoke suite gained a gnu dry-run plan assertion (gnu tarball URL) and a still-rejected non-Unix triple case; install.ps1 checked - no equivalent restriction (Windows zip only), no mirror needed

title: install.sh --dry-run: surface the cosign soft-skip in the plan output
description: install.sh --dry-run prints verification_posture: prefer and lists the cosign .sig/.crt/.attestation URLs as if they will be fetched, but on a cosign-less box the real run soft-skips them (should_download_cosign_metadata short-circuits; SHA-256 stays enforced). Expectation mismatch between plan and run. Add a dry-run plan line when cosign is absent, e.g. "cosign: not installed - authenticity check will be skipped (SHA-256 still enforced); install cosign or use --verify require to change this". Cosmetic; extend the installer lint/e2e dry-run assertions.
close_reason: print_plan now emits an honest cosign line when cosign is absent: posture prefer gets 'cosign: not installed - authenticity check will be skipped (SHA-256 still enforced); install cosign or use --verify require to change this'; posture require gets a fail-closed warning (ORACLEMCP_INSTALL_COSIGN_REQUIRED); checksum-only unchanged (already explicit). Smoke suite gained 3 dry-run assertions (prefer soft-skip line, require fail-closed line, quiet when cosign present) - all pass; shellcheck clean

title: Cosmetic: doctor check print order; initialize protocolVersion echo per MCP spec
description: Two cosmetic items from the field test, bundled: (a) doctor human-readable output prints check 13 (State layout) before check 12 (Call timeout); order the printed checks numerically. (b) MCP initialize echoes protocolVersion 2025-11-25 even when the client offered 2024-11-05; per the MCP spec the server SHOULD respond with the requested version when it supports it, otherwise its latest supported. Verify against the current spec text and reply with the client-offered version when supported (lenient clients cope today, strict clients may not). Both are output-only changes; update the relevant snapshot/protocol tests.
close_reason: (a) run_doctor now assembles check 12 (Call timeout) before 13 (State layout) so human output prints numerically; regression test checks_are_ordered_by_id. (b) verified MCP lifecycle spec (modelcontextprotocol.io: server MUST respond with the requested version when supported, else its latest): added SUPPORTED_PROTOCOL_VERSIONS (2024-11-05/2025-03-26/2025-06-18/2025-11-25), initialize now echoes a supported client-offered protocolVersion and negotiates unknown ones up to 2025-11-25; HTTP mcp-protocol-version header gate widened to the same supported set so a session negotiated at an older revision is not 400-ed afterwards; tests initialize_echoes_a_supported_client_protocol_version, initialize_frame_negotiates_older_client_version, updated header-gate test

title: Pre-production Oracle version matrix (XE 18/21 + 23ai) for live tests + release checklist
description:
Systemic gap the field test exposed: oraclemcp live verification (live-xe suite, doctor --online, e2e) has only ever run against gvenzl/oracle-free:23 - so a driver connect path that cannot reach ANY pre-23ai server shipped through every gate and was first caught by a customer field install against 19c.

Scope: add a scripted, documented pre-production matrix - gvenzl/oracle-xe:18-slim, gvenzl/oracle-xe:21-slim, gvenzl/oracle-free:23-slim on distinct host ports (compose file or a scripts/ helper mirroring the driver repo matrix) - and run the live profile/config smoke subset plus doctor --online --profile against each version. Document in docs/operations.md testing section and add to docs/release-checklist.md so a release cannot ship without pre-23ai connect coverage. Optionally document the genuine-19c lane (Oracle Container Registry EE image, free OTN login for dev/test) as an extra, not a gate requirement. Goes fully green only after the 0.5.2 pin bump; land the harness first with the 18/21 lanes marked expected-fail until then so the flip to green is visible.
notes: SCOPE RAISED (operator directive 2026-07-04): not just doctor+query smoke. Required per-lane e2e across the FULL operating-level ladder, end to end through the MCP surface: (1) doctor --online green; (2) READ_ONLY: oracle_query with row-value assertions; (3) READ_WRITE: oracle_preview_sql -> oracle_set_session_level elevation (preview + grant + execute) -> oracle_execute DML with default rollback verified, then commit via single-use execution grant, row counts checked; (4) DDL: create/drop through the preview->grant->execute gate; (5) audit chain entries verified for each privileged step; (6) drop back to READ_ONLY and verify refusal of writes. Lab profiles per lane (XE18/XE21/FREE23) with a writable max_level=DDL lab profile; wire into docs/release-checklist.md as a REQUIRED release gate so no oraclemcp release ships without pre-23ai coverage; genuine-19c lane documented as optional operator-run step. Rationale: the thin driver was only ever validated against 23ai and the envelope gap shipped to the field.
close_reason: Ladder e2e landed: scripts/e2e/oracle_version_matrix.sh + oracle_ladder_session.py drive the real binary over MCP stdio per lane (XE18/XE21/FREE23): doctor --online, READ_ONLY query with value assertions + write refusal, preview->grant->elevate->DML rollback-then-commit, governed DDL create/drop, drop-back-to-RO refusal, audit evidence; wired into run_all.sh, docs/release-checklist.md (required gate) and docs/operations.md; genuine-19c lane documented as operator-run extra. Live run: all three lanes pass, 0 failures.

title: H14: CC1 lease-strand-on-panic regression (plan 30.4 item 7)
description: Plan section 30.4 item 7: inject a framework panic and assert the retry is ADMITTED, not refused 409. Grounding: no bead anywhere in the tracker mentions lease-strand. The two nearest existing tests are NOT this regression: crates/oraclemcp/src/dispatch/tests.rs:2736 poisons the quarantine mutex and asserts a profile switch fails cleanly, and crates/oraclemcp-core/tests/lane_state_machine.rs:88 asserts a panic terminal path releases capacity without touching a sibling lane. Neither drives the 409 path. Start by locating where a stranded lease produces 409 (crates/oraclemcp-core/src/http/ and the idempotency/write-intent path), then add the regression with a panic hook. Tier 1.

(b) parent-directory fsync on audit-log create so the file's directory entry is itself durable across a crash. 68 audit tests incl forged-interior-MAC + dir-fsync observability; fmt/clippy(0)/workspace-tests/5-lints/deny green; live ladder 21/21 across xe18/xe21/free23.

title: [infra][P1] Swarm build storm filled /tmp tmpfs (CARGO_TARGET_DIR) -> EDQUOT froze the box
description:
INCIDENT 2026-07-16 ~14:16: the 8-agent release swarm froze the whole box; the orchestrator's shell could not fork any external process for ~20 min.

TRUE ROOT CAUSE: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/cargo-target (set in ~/.zshrc) points into /tmp, which is a 124 GB tmpfs (RAM-backed). With 8 agents running concurrent full `cargo build/test --workspace` against that shared target, build artifacts grew to 73 GB and total tmpfs/shared hit ~130 GB > the 124 GB limit. Every write to /tmp then failed with EDQUOT (disk quota / no space). Because the Bash tool captures command output via temp files in /tmp, the orchestrator's commands returned exit 1 with empty output (no-output builtins like `true`/`cd` still worked); the operator's pty-backed shell was unaffected. Misleading secondary symptom: fork appeared to fail only from large processes — but overcommit_memory=0 (heuristic) ruled out strict-overcommit; the binding limit was tmpfs space, not PID/thread count (ps -eL=7856 vs ulimit -u=32768) nor cgroup pids (5607/598511).

EVIDENCE: df -h /tmp -> tmpfs 124G, was full; du /tmp/cargo-target=73G; free -h shared=130Gi, free=5Gi. After `rm -rf /tmp/cargo-target`: /tmp 22% used, 98G free, shell recovered.

FIXES:
1. DONE: symlinked /tmp/cargo-target -> /home/durakovic/.cache/cargo-target (on 4.5TB root) so live agents' builds transparently land off tmpfs without relaunch.
2. DONE: ~/.cargo/config.toml [build] jobs=4 (cap per-build rustc parallelism).
3. TODO: update ~/.zshrc CARGO_TARGET_DIR to the root path for future/relaunched shells.
4. ENFORCE: agents must acquire an Agent Mail build slot (cap 2 concurrent full builds/repo) before any full `cargo --workspace`; use scoped `cargo check/test -p` for iteration.
5. CONSIDER: cc agents launch with NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=32768 (32GB heap reservation each) — oversized; drop to ~4096 on next respawn to cut memory pressure.

DELIVERABLE: a documented multi-agent build-concurrency + scratch-space policy for this box (never put a shared CARGO_TARGET_DIR on tmpfs; bound concurrent full builds via slots), so a single agent cannot exhaust a shared resource and wedge the swarm.
close_reason: DELIVERABLE SHIPPED: docs/multi-agent-build-policy.md (commit 07f7c98) — the documented multi-agent build-concurrency + scratch-space policy this bead asked for. Runtime fixes 1/2/3 were already applied by the operator; I VERIFIED each rather than trusting the bead text: (1) df -h $(readlink -f /tmp/cargo-target) reports /dev/mapper/ubuntu--vg-ubuntu--lv 4.5T (bind-mounted onto root, NOT tmpfs) — note it is a BIND MOUNT, not the symlink the bead describes, so 'ls -ld' still shows a plain dir and readlink resolves to itself; the honest check is df, which the doc now prescribes. (2) ~/.cargo/config.toml [build] jobs = 4 present. (3) item 3 is NO LONGER TODO — ~/.zshrc line 59 already exports CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/home/durakovic/.cache/cargo-target. /tmp now 24% used. POLICY CONTENT: 5 rules (shared CARGO_TARGET_DIR never on tmpfs; bound concurrent full builds behind Agent Mail slots cap 2/repo; scoped -p builds for iteration; jobs cap; never 'fix' shared-resource exhaustion by deleting shared state since it forces a cold rebuild on every pane and is an operator call per RULE 1) + a diagnosis fingerprint + shared-checkout hazards. The highest-value section is recognising the failure, because it is DECEPTIVE: df reported 25G free and inodes 19% throughout (binding limit was per-user usrquota, not disk) — the tell is EDQUOT/os error 122, never ENOSPC; it surfaces first as 'ld terminated with signal 7 [Bus error]', and once /tmp is unwritable the harness wedges so that 'echo hello' exits 1 with no output while 'true' works (output is buffered through /tmp). HONEST GAP RECORDED, NOT PAPERED OVER: rule 4/ENFORCE is currently UNENFORCEABLE — acquire_build_slot returns 'Build slots are disabled. Enable WORKTREES_ENABLED to use this tool', verified from this pane, independently of BoldCreek's identical report. Until enabled, an agent told to take a slot before a full build can only block or bypass; blocking is correct but is already stalling BoldCreek on 3 beads (yg4x.1, yg4x.8, y1x7). ACTION FOR THE OPERATOR: enable WORKTREES_ENABLED or name an explicit alternative in the swarm brief, else the policy's own enforcement mechanism does not exist. Item 5 (NODE_OPTIONS 32GB->4096 on respawn) is a launcher concern outside this repo and is left to the operator. NOT DONE: the AGENTS.md pointer line — BoldCreek holds an exclusive lease on AGENTS.md (x1hr.2); routing around a live reservation is precisely what this doc argues against, so I asked them to add it instead.

title: CLI serve tests inherit stdin, so cargo test hangs 30s+ for any dev on a terminal
notes:
FOUND + PROVEN during the phase-2 review (PurpleRidge). NOT a product defect and NOT red in CI — a latent test-hygiene trap that costs a developer/agent 30s per test and looks like a hang.

SYMPTOM: 'cargo test -p oraclemcp --test cli' from an interactive terminal (or an agent harness) fails 3 tests with 'oraclemcp did not exit within 30s': discovery_annotated_config_boots, setup_generated_client_snippet_launches_serve_as_written, setup_write_round_trips_profiles_through_config_ops.

CAUSE: those tests spawn the serve binary with .stdout(Stdio::piped()).stderr(Stdio::piped()) but NEVER set .stdin(...), so the child INHERITS the parent's stdin. serve over stdio exits on stdin EOF. Under CI stdin is /dev/null -> immediate EOF -> serve exits 0 -> green. Under a terminal/agent shell stdin stays open -> serve correctly waits forever -> the test's own 30s timeout fires. The server is behaving correctly; the test is non-hermetic w.r.t. inherited stdin.

PROOF (both directions, same commit): 'cargo test -p oraclemcp --test cli -- <the 3 tests>' FAILS with the 30s timeout; the identical command with '< /dev/null' passes all 3 in 0.11s. That 0.11s-vs-30s gap is the whole bug.

WHY IT MATTERS beyond annoyance: it burns 90s and reads exactly like a product hang, so the natural reaction is to go hunting in serve's shutdown path — I nearly did. It also means these tests silently depend on the invoker's stdin, which is ambient coupling of the kind yg4x.6 exists to remove.

FIX: add .stdin(Stdio::null()) to the serve spawns in crates/oraclemcp/tests/cli.rs (the same 3 sites; grep for wait_with_timeout callers that spawn serve). Do NOT 'fix' it by extending the timeout — that hides it and doubles the wait.

FALSE LEAD worth recording so nobody re-runs it: the failures report "live_db":true and this box has a live Oracle on localhost:1521 (the plsql-intelligence-xe container), so 'the tests are accidentally hitting a real DB' is an extremely plausible and WRONG theory. live_db is a build-capability flag, not a live connection, and the fixtures use TNS aliases / port 1. It is stdin, nothing to do with Oracle.
close_reason: Serve CLI subprocesses now receive closed stdin, so tests terminate on EOF rather than inheriting the invoker terminal.

title: [CI][Kani] Bound Kani workflow build parallelism to avoid runner SIGTERM
description: GitHub Kani Safety on 7101873 fails red because the guard + audit BMC job is SIGTERM'd with exit 143 during the session_gate_never_allows_below_required_level harness. The same harness passes locally with TMPDIR=/var/tmp CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/var/tmp/oraclemcp-kani-target cargo kani -p oraclemcp-guard --harness session_gate_never_allows_below_required_level --default-unwind 16. Evidence points to runner/resource cancellation, not a proof failure. Proposed fix: lower Kani workflow Cargo build parallelism from 16 to a conservative value and rerun Kani/CI.
close_reason: Bound Kani Cargo build parallelism to 2 after GitHub Kani Safety repeatedly SIGTERM'd the guard session gate proof with exit 143 while the same harness and the workflow-relevant guard proofs passed locally; cargo test --workspace --all-targets also passed.

title: harness: OCI IAM user-mapping ORA-01017 (Identity Domains subject)
description: OCI-live harness follow-up, not a driver defect. The committed driver fixes r2t0 and bvyt make wallet/TCPS live-green and permit classic IAM token authentication to reach the ADB. On free-tier OCI ADB run 20260717T035136Z-3426382, the JWT claims were sub=<redacted OCI IAM user OCID> and userName=dbUserName=omcp-ci. The direct ADMIN-wallet bootstrap successfully ran BEGIN DBMS_CLOUD_ADMIN.ENABLE_EXTERNAL_AUTHENTICATION(type => OCI_IAM); END;, verified identity_provider_type=OCI_IAM, created OMCP_IAM_ACCEPT IDENTIFIED GLOBALLY AS IAM_PRINCIPAL_NAME=<token sub OCID>, and granted CREATE SESSION. The IAM profile used username=OMCP_IAM_ACCEPT, use_iam_token=true, strict DN matching, and SNI=true. Wallet doctor plus governed SELECT USER FROM DUAL passed; the IAM token validity check passed; IAM Connectivity still returned ORA-01017 before the guarded query. Determine the exact OCI Identity Domains external-name form ADB expects for this token (for example domain-qualified principal versus OCID) and make the harness map that form. Do not change the fail-closed guard or driver. Every run must remain Always Free and destroy the ADB.
notes:
Latest evidence: enablement succeeded; sub is an OCI user OCID; generated connection username was the mapped schema OMCP_IAM_ACCEPT; IAM Connectivity was ORA-01017 (auth_mode=bad_credentials, failure_class=INSUFFICIENT_PRIVILEGE). Wallet/TCPS was fully green. All ADBs were destroyed. No further live run until the operator chooses the mapping hypothesis.

title: [P0][policy] unsupported SQL shape bypasses global principal/verb deny
description:
Confirmed Arc N policy fail-open.

Policy grammar explicitly permits omitted selectors to match every dimension. Configure:
version = 1
[[rules]]
id = "deny-principal-reads"
match = { principal = "oauth:subject-1", verb = "select" }
effect = { kind = "deny" }

Exact request (base classifier is Safe / READ_ONLY):
WITH x AS (SELECT 1 FROM dual) SELECT * FROM x

Expected: MatchingDenyRule. Actual: admitted identity narrowing. StatementPolicyFacts::derive returns None for every WITH/query shape outside one unaliased single-table SELECT. enforce_sql_policy only denies that failure when policy_selects_a_target() sees schema/object selectors. For principal- or verb-only rules it returns PolicyGate::Admitted(identity) before SqlPolicyConfig::evaluate is called, so the matching Deny is silently skipped and dispatch executes the query.

This directly violates the grammar's contract that omitted selectors match every value and that an empty match is a global tightening rule. It is a policy classification widening, not a cosmetic unsupported-shape limitation.

Acceptance: all valid configured deny/level rules must be evaluated or refused for every classifier-admitted statement; unsupported target resolution may be represented as absent schema/object, but must not bypass global, verb-only, or principal-only rules. Add regressions for principal+verb Deny over WITH, JOIN and a global Deny.
close_reason: Fixed the CTE/unsupported-read policy bypass: parsed query shapes retain a proven SELECT verb, target-free principal/verb/global deny and level rules evaluate, unknown policy facts refuse, and target-specific rules remain fail-closed. Reproduced the former CTE evade; policy-gate regression corpus passed; omcpb test -p oraclemcp-guard passed (199 unit and 102 integration/property tests). Scoped clippy is blocked only by unowned classifier.rs WIP at line 2694 and was reported.
