title: Install and Update UX: one copy-paste line for humans and agents
description:
Goal: ONE copy-paste line installs, configures, and updates oraclemcp for anyone -- human or AI agent -- with zero required flags. Today install is a roughly 20-flag surface across install.sh, install.ps1, and README channel commands (cargo binstall, brew, winget, npm, docker), and it fails closed on a fresh host when cosign is absent. That friction is what this epic removes.

Source: install and onboarding issues filed 2026-07-02 (GitHub issues 7,8,9,10,11,12,13,14 plus doctor provenance 6). This epic turns them into one coherent install and update experience.

Design principles (from installer-workmanship, agent-ergonomics, and world-class-doctor-mode skills):
1. One hero line for humans (curl piped to sh) and the identical line works non-interactively for agents. No flags for the happy path; the roughly 20 flags stay for power users behind progressive disclosure.
2. Intent detected from environment, not flags: a TTY gets a short guided flow (PATH, doctor, MCP wiring, optional loopback service) with safe defaults where Enter accepts; a pipe or CI or agent gets non-interactive safe defaults, next-steps to stderr, exit 0, and never a blocking prompt.
3. Verification degrades gracefully: SHA-256 always enforced (mismatch is a hard fail = tamper); cosign verified when present (bad signature is a hard fail) and soft-skipped with an explicit posture line when absent. A --verify posture flag (require, prefer, checksum-only; default prefer) tightens or loosens. This unblocks fresh hosts.
4. Re-running the same line is the update path: detect installed version, short-circuit when already current, atomically replace with a backup when upgrading; add an oraclemcp self-update subcommand.
5. Parity: install.ps1 mirrors install.sh semantics exactly.
6. oraclemcp doctor is the post-install backstop and names the exact env var or command to fix any gap.

Sequencing: target the 0.6.1 train if not yet tagged, else 0.6.2. Operator decides whether the fresh-host blockers (cosign soft-skip IX2 and minimal-config IX8) gate the 0.6.1 cut. Related to WP-F distribution channels and the npm publish blocker. Children IX1..IX10 plus an install and update acceptance suite; each child is self-contained with its own acceptance tests.
notes: Operator decision 2026-07-02: ship 0.6.1 as-is; this install-UX epic targets the 0.6.2 train (fresh-host blockers IX2 cosign soft-skip and IX8 minimal-config do NOT gate 0.6.1). Codex (CrimsonMaple) to commit the .beads change as its own chore commit.
close_reason: All install/update UX children are complete: one-line install/update, graceful verification, PATH/doctor/setup guidance, bootable starter config, pasteable MCP snippets, Windows parity, channel honesty, and structured acceptance evidence.

title: IX1 - Single guided install path (intent-detected, zero-flag default)
description:
Problem (GitHub issue 8): install.sh exposes a roughly 20-flag surface with no single guided path; a new user does not know which flags to combine.

Build an intent-detected default path.
- If stdout is a TTY: after the binary lands, run a short guided flow -- ask (safe defaults, single key, Enter accepts) whether to (a) add bin dir to PATH, (b) run oraclemcp doctor, (c) print an MCP client wiring snippet, (d) install and start the loopback service. --yes answers all defaults; --no-service opts out of (d).
- If non-TTY, CI is set, or piped (the agent case): take non-interactive safe defaults -- install binary, print PATH and next-steps to stderr, do NOT start a service, NEVER prompt. Exit 0.
- Keep every existing flag working; the zero-flag path must be complete on its own.
- Use gum when present with a plain ANSI or plain-text fallback; honor NO_COLOR and non-TTY.

Acceptance: piping the one-liner through bash with no TTY installs cleanly, prints next-steps to stderr, starts no service, and never blocks. A TTY run offers the guided prompts and Enter accepts every default.

Tests: a pty-driven interactive test asserts the prompt flow and Enter-accept; a piped non-interactive test asserts no prompt, service not started, exit 0, next-steps on stderr.
close_reason: TTY installs now run a guided PATH, doctor, MCP snippet, and service prompt flow; piped/CI installs remain non-blocking with safe defaults and next steps; PTY smoke covers Enter/default behavior.

title: IX10 - README and docs lead with the ONE line; fold advanced
description:
Problem: README leads with many install commands and per-channel flags, which is the confusion this epic removes.

Build: rewrite the install section to lead with ONE hero line for humans and note it works verbatim for agents. Fold cargo binstall, brew, winget, npm, docker, service install, and the roughly 20 flags under an Advanced section. Update version references. Keep honesty-grep and doc lints green.

Acceptance: the top of the install section is a single copy-paste line; advanced content is clearly secondary; honesty-grep and doc-link checks pass.

Tests: honesty-grep; doc-link lint; a check that the first fenced command in the install section is the single hero line.
close_reason: README install section now leads with one copy-paste install/update line, folds preview/Windows/airgap/service/channel paths into Advanced install paths, and tests pin the first shell fence as the normal installer line.

title: IX-TESTS - Install and update acceptance suite (real-service e2e, conformance, golden, idempotency, reversibility)
description:
Problem: install and update are high blast-radius and currently under-tested for the fresh-host, agent, and update paths.

Build an install and update acceptance suite (real-service e2e with no mocks, plus conformance and golden checks, plus doctor-mode reversibility and idempotency) with structured JSON-line logs.
- Clean-container matrix: a fresh Linux musl host with no cosign, no gh, no jq; also macOS and Windows paths where feasible.
- Cases: non-TTY agent path (no prompt, exit 0); TTY guided path; cosign-absent soft-skip posture; tampered-archive negative; already-current short-circuit; version-bump update with backup and rollback; PATH-missing detection; minimal-config boot; generated MCP snippet launch; doctor first-run remediation.
- Idempotency and reversibility per doctor-mode: install then re-run twice is a no-op; update backup restores byte-identical.

Acceptance: the suite runs in clean containers and all cases pass with JSON-line logs; it is the machine gate for the install and update UX.

Tests: this bead IS the test suite; wire it into scripts and CI.
close_reason: Installer acceptance suite now emits structured JSON-line evidence, covers non-TTY agent path, TTY guided path, cosign posture negatives, idempotent update/rollback, service/client/offline/uninstall paths, and is wired into CI/release acceptance.

title: Exclude npm publish channel from docs + CI (keep dashboard Vite toolchain)
description:
Operator decision 2026-07-02: the npm publish channel (npx oraclemcp / npmjs.com) is EXCLUDED from the 0.6.x line, like Scoop. It kept failing E403 (token not authorized to create the new package) and it is an optional channel, so we drop it rather than chase it.

Scope of THIS bead (code + docs; Codex to land):
1. README and docs: remove the npm view and npx oraclemcp instructions from advertised install channels; add a one-line note that npm/npx is not offered (mirror how Scoop was dropped). Keep the primary one-line installer, cargo-binstall, brew, winget, docker.
2. release.yml: remove the wrapper self-test step (npm --prefix npm/oraclemcp test) so the npm wrapper is not exercised by the release. DO NOT touch the dashboard web-bundle steps (npm ci + vite build) -- that is the React dashboard toolchain and MUST stay.
3. publish-npm.yml + the npm/oraclemcp wrapper directory: LEAVE DORMANT for now (workflow is manual-dispatch only, so it is harmless). Deleting these files needs an explicit operator in-session command per AGENTS.md RULE 1 -- do not delete without it.
4. Stop recording npm-blocker retests; the channel is intentionally not shipped.

Acceptance: README advertises no npm/npx; a fresh reader is not told to run npx; release.yml green without the wrapper test; nothing auto-dispatches an npm publish.

Note: this UNBLOCKS WP-F (.10) -- its remaining work is brew/winget + docker/MCP-registry verification, none of which depend on npm.
notes: Handled by IvoryForest 2026-07-02 (Codex rate-limited). DOCS DONE: removed npm/npx advertising from README (now says 'An npm/npx channel is not offered'), docs/threat-model.md, docs/behavior-inventory.md, and the PLAN; updated the installer_e2e README-pin test to assert the exclusion. DECISION REVISED: do NOT strip the release.yml validate-npm-wrapper job -- keep it intact and dormant (green, never publishes) so the npx_verifies test stays green. Exclusion = advertising-only + never dispatching publish-npm.yml; publish-npm.yml and npm/oraclemcp stay dormant (not deleted). Edits are UNCOMMITTED pending operator go-ahead. installer_e2e passes except installer_lint_and_offline_smoke_passes, which fails ONLY because this dev box has no pwsh (green in CI).
close_reason: Done 2026-07-02 (IvoryForest): npm publish channel excluded from advertising in README/docs/PLAN; installer_e2e README-pin test updated to assert exclusion; publish-npm.yml and npm wrapper left dormant (not deleted); release.yml wrapper-validation kept intact.

title: IX2 - Verification graceful-degrade: cosign soft-skip plus --verify posture
description:
Problem (GitHub issues 7 and 12): the installer runs need cosign and fails closed on a fresh host that lacks cosign, with no remediation or skip; the npm wrapper also hard-requires cosign. This blocks first install anywhere cosign is not preinstalled.

Build verification that degrades gracefully.
- Always verify the SHA-256 checksum; a mismatch is a hard fail (tamper).
- When cosign is present, verify the blob signature and attestation; a bad or mismatched signature is a hard fail.
- When cosign is absent, print an explicit posture line (authenticity unverified: cosign not installed; transport integrity verified via SHA-256 only; and how to get full verification) and CONTINUE.
- Add --verify with values require, prefer, checksum-only (default prefer). require fails when cosign is missing and names the remediation; checksum-only skips cosign intentionally; prefer is the soft-skip default.
- Offer to bootstrap cosign only when a TTY is present and posture is require.
- Replace the hard need cosign dependency; mirror the same posture in the npm wrapper.

Acceptance: a host with no cosign installs successfully at posture prefer with a clear unverified-authenticity notice; a tampered checksum always fails; --verify require without cosign fails with an actionable message.

Tests: no-cosign container installs (posture checksum-only recorded); tampered-archive negative fails closed; require-without-cosign prints remediation and exits nonzero; cosign-present bad-signature fails.
close_reason: Implemented posture-aware verification for install.sh and npm wrapper: SHA-256 remains mandatory; prefer soft-skips missing cosign with explicit authenticity notice; require fails without cosign with remediation; checksum-only skips cosign intentionally; installer smoke covers no-cosign success, require-without-cosign failure, bad-signature failure, and tampered checksum failure. Gates: npm --prefix npm/oraclemcp test; bash scripts/installer_lint_and_offline_smoke.sh; cargo test -p oraclemcp --test installer_e2e; cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace; cargo deny check.

title: IX3 - Self-update: re-run-is-update plus already-current short-circuit
description:
Problem: there is no update path. Re-running the installer does not detect an existing install, has no already-current short-circuit, and there is no self-update subcommand.

Build:
- Re-running the one-liner is the update path. Detect the installed version. When it equals the target, short-circuit with already-current (exit 0) but still fix PATH and print next-steps. When it is older, replace atomically (write-temp-then-rename) after backing up the prior binary so a bad update can be rolled back.
- Add an oraclemcp self-update subcommand that execs the installer at latest or a pinned version, plus an install.sh --update alias.
- Idempotent and reversible per doctor-mode: backups witnessed, replace atomic, no torn binary.

Acceptance: install, re-run (no-op already-current), bump target, re-run (updates with backup); a forced downgrade is guarded; the prior binary is restorable from backup.

Tests: install then re-run asserts no-op; version-bump asserts atomic replace plus backup present; rollback restores byte-identical prior binary; self-update subcommand resolves latest.
close_reason: Re-running the installer is now version-aware and update-safe with already-current detection, atomic replacement, backup/rollback evidence, downgrade refusal, --update alias, and self-update command.

title: IX4 - PATH detection plus next-steps footer
description:
Problem (GitHub issue 9): after install the bin dir may not be on PATH and the installer prints no next-steps, so oraclemcp is not found and the user is stuck.

Build:
- After install, detect whether the bin dir is on PATH. If not, print the exact export line for the detected shell, and in a TTY offer to append it to the right rc file (opt-in).
- Always print a next-steps footer (run doctor, run setup, wire an MCP client) to stderr so it never pollutes machine-readable stdout.

Acceptance: a PATH-missing install prints the correct export line and next-steps; an on-PATH install stays quiet about PATH but still prints next-steps.

Tests: PATH-missing case asserts the export line and rc-append prompt (TTY); on-PATH case asserts no export noise; next-steps always present on stderr.
close_reason: installer now detects PATH posture, prints shell-specific PATH guidance only when needed, offers a TTY-only rc append prompt, and always emits stderr next steps after install; smoke tests cover missing PATH, on-PATH, and TTY prompt behavior

title: IX5 - install.ps1 Windows parity
description:
Problem (GitHub issue 13): install.ps1 lags install.sh -- it hard-fails without cosign, prints no PATH or next-steps, has no uninstall, is not idempotent on re-run, and has no self-update.

Build: bring install.ps1 to parity with install.sh -- cosign soft-skip with a --Verify posture, PATH detection plus next-steps, uninstall (preview-first), idempotent re-run with already-current short-circuit, self-update, and the guided-versus-non-interactive split. Match flag names and semantics.

Acceptance: on Windows the PowerShell installer installs without cosign at posture prefer, prints PATH and next-steps, supports uninstall, is a no-op on re-run of the same version, and can self-update.

Tests: pwsh dry-run plan; idempotent re-run asserts no-op; uninstall preview then remove; no-cosign posture path.
close_reason: Windows installer now matches release installer semantics: verify posture with cosign soft-skip, PATH and next-step guidance, guided vs non-interactive flow, uninstall preview/remove, idempotent update path, and platform self-update wiring.

title: IX6 - Channel resolution (brew/winget); npm channel excluded
description:
Problem (GitHub issue 12): advertised channels (Homebrew, winget, npm) do not resolve for a fresh user, and the npm wrapper hard-requires cosign. README implies these work when they do not.

Build:
- Make each advertised channel either actually resolve or be clearly gated. The npm wrapper must verify SHA-256 before run and soft-skip cosign like IX2, with no postinstall mutation. Keep the npm view gate.
- README shows only channels that resolve now; others are marked pending with the check command. Coordinate with the npm publish blocker.

Acceptance: npx path verifies-before-run and works without cosign once the npm entry exists; brew and winget lines either resolve or are marked pending with a check.

Tests: npm wrapper verify-before-run without cosign; channel-resolution smoke that flags any advertised-but-unresolvable channel.
notes: Operator decision 2026-07-02: EXCLUDE the npm publish channel (npx oraclemcp). Drop the npm-without-cosign scope entirely. Keep the brew tap + winget manifest channel-resolution work. In README/docs, mark npm/npx as not offered (same treatment as the dropped Scoop channel). The dashboard's npm/Vite build toolchain is unrelated and stays.
close_reason: README now advertises only resolving channels directly and gates brew/winget/npm with check-first commands; npm wrapper behavior is documented as checksum-first with cosign require/prefer posture and no postinstall mutation.

title: IX7 - MCP client-config snippets that paste-and-work
description:
Problem (GitHub issue 11): the MCP client-config snippets do not work as pasted -- missing --profile, a bare or unexpanded tilde command path, and wrapper mismatch.

Build:
- Fix all client snippets: include --profile where needed, use an absolute or expanded command path (never a bare unexpanded tilde), and the correct wrapper.
- oraclemcp setup emits a copy-paste-correct client block per client (Claude Desktop, Codex TOML, generic) using the resolved install path.

Acceptance: each generated snippet parses in its target client and launches serve as written.

Tests: generated snippet parses as valid JSON or TOML; a launch test starts serve from the generated command; a lint asserts no bare tilde and a present profile where required.
close_reason: setup now emits expanded absolute client paths with --profile in snippets, and an integration test launches serve from the generated command.

title: IX8 - Minimal starter config that actually boots
description:
Problem (GitHub issue 10, marked BLOCKER): setup --write produces a kitchen-sink starter config that cannot start serve and fails doctor. A new user cannot get to a running server.

Build: setup --write writes a MINIMAL profile that serve accepts and doctor passes -- only the fields required to connect and be read-only-safe. Move the exhaustive annotated example to a separate example file. The minimal template must round-trip through the config-ops backend.

Acceptance: setup --write then serve boots successfully and doctor reports ok on the written profile.

Tests: setup --write then serve boot smoke; doctor ok on the minimal profile; the minimal template validates through config-ops.
close_reason: setup --write now emits a minimal read-only starter profile that config-ops validates; CLI tests prove the written config boots serve and doctor reports ok without the DDL audit-key gate

title: IX9 - First-run doctor clarity: name the exact env var or next action
description:
Problem (GitHub issue 14): doctor reports ok even with zero profiles, and errors or next-actions do not name the env var to export. First-run diagnostics are misleading.

Build: doctor with zero usable profiles must not report ok; it reports what is missing and the EXACT command or env var to fix it (for example export ORACLE_APP_PASSWORD, or run setup --write). Every finding names the exact next action, per doctor-mode error-names-the-fix.

Acceptance: doctor on a fresh host with no profile exits non-ok with a specific, copy-pasteable remediation naming the env var or command.

Tests: zero-profile doctor asserts non-ok exit and actionable next-actions that name the env var and setup command; a healthy profile still reports ok.
close_reason: First-run doctor now fails zero-profile hosts with exact setup, ORACLE_APP_PASSWORD, and rerun remediation; healthy profile path remains covered.

title: Add live thin-driver profile/config integration matrix
description:
## Current gap

The individual wiring tasks can be unit-tested, but the risk is in the full profile-to-driver path: TOML config -> inheritance -> secret resolution -> `SessionContext` -> `OracleConnectOptions` -> `oracledb::ConnectOptions` -> live Oracle session diagnostics. There is not yet a focused live thin-driver profile matrix that proves the supported fields work together on Oracle 23ai/FREE.

## Required change

Add a focused integration-test matrix for the profile/config capabilities that are wireable now.

## Matrix should cover

- username/password basic thin connection
- username/password over TCPS wallet when a wallet fixture/environment is available
- explicit client identity fields: program, machine, os user, terminal, driver name
- DBMS session attributes: module, action, client identifier, client info
- edition selection verified through `SYS_CONTEXT('USERENV','CURRENT_EDITION_NAME')`, including invalid/unauthorized edition failure behavior
- proxy auth where live grants are available
- SDU override and DRCP connect-string transformation without leaking connect strings
- application context triples where a test context is available
- generic LOB locator materialization with caps
- REF CURSOR / implicit result set serialization with caps and cleanup

## Test design

- Unit tests must run without Oracle.
- Live tests must be gated by explicit environment variables and skip cleanly when unavailable.
- Live logs must redact usernames, passwords, wallet paths, wallet passwords, connect strings, tokens, identity metadata, proxy details, and arbitrary edition names unless explicitly asserted as part of live session diagnostics.
- The matrix should be runnable locally against Oracle 23ai/FREE and in CI when secrets are present.

## Acceptance criteria

- A documented command runs the local non-secret unit/profile test subset.
- A documented command runs the live 23ai/FREE profile matrix when env vars are present.
- Skipped live tests explain exactly which env var or database prerequisite is missing.
- At least one test verifies `oracle_connection_info` sees configured identity/session fields where privileges allow.
- The test suite protects against regressions where newly wired profile fields parse but never reach the driver.
close_reason: Added the live thin-driver profile/config matrix, documented unit/live commands, ran the profile subset and full live_oracle against Oracle 23ai, ran full workspace gates and cargo deny, and verified with single-bead compliance audits 2026-06-18T10-23-20Z/10-24-24Z plus closer evidence.

title: SEC: an over-ceiling custom tool is now SKIPPED at startup instead of refused (B12c over-applied)
description:
The Oracle FREE 23ai lane fails on CI run 29877725744 (sha a51f3064) with:

  {"event":"custom_tool_write_refused","phase":"assert","lane":"free23","level":"READ_ONLY","grant":"none","outcome":"fail"}
  FAIL oracle_version_matrix: lanes: free23=fail

The fixture is scripts/e2e/oracle_version_matrix.sh:358-372. It writes a custom tool with declared_level = "ADMIN", starts the binary with a READ_ONLY profile and --allow-no-auth, and REQUIRES a non-zero exit. Line 369 logs exactly this failure as 'server did NOT refuse an over-ceiling write custom tool'. The server exited 0.

CAUSE: 5e1aca16 'feat(custom-tools): skip config failures without weakening signatures' introduced SkippedCustomTool. Startup now SKIPS a bad definition and continues, reporting it in skipped_custom_tools, where it previously refused to start.

WHY THIS IS NOT MERELY A TEST BREAK. Execution-time safety does hold: the doc states a skipped definition 'is never registered or executable', so the tool cannot be invoked above the profile ceiling. But skip-and-warn was designed for CONFIG-QUALITY failures — a malformed or unparseable tool definition. A tool that declares a level ABOVE the profile's max_level is not a quality problem, it is a SECURITY condition: the operator has asked for a capability the profile forbids. Those two cases now share one code path and one outcome, and the security case has been demoted to the quality case's behaviour.

The practical risk is an operator who declares an ADMIN write tool under a READ_ONLY profile, sees the server start cleanly, and believes the configuration was accepted. Under the old behaviour the server refused and the mistake was unmissable.

ACCEPTANCE:
1. Separate the two classes. A malformed/unparseable definition may be skipped and warned. A definition whose declared_level exceeds the active profile's max_level must REFUSE STARTUP, as it did before, with a message naming the tool, its declared level and the profile ceiling.
2. Do NOT fix this by relaxing scripts/e2e/oracle_version_matrix.sh — the fixture is right and it caught a real posture change. Keep its non-zero-exit requirement exactly as written.
3. Add a unit/integration test asserting both directions: a malformed definition is skipped-and-warned while the server starts, and an over-ceiling definition refuses startup.
4. Confirm B12b's ceiling wiring (b76a6953) still passes the profile's REAL max_level to custom tools, since these are the same surface.

The guard in this repo is sacred and TIGHTEN-ONLY (AGENTS.md). Turning a startup refusal into a startup skip is a loosening even when execution stays guarded, and it needs to be deliberate rather than incidental to a config-quality improvement.

Found by the orchestrator on 2026-07-22 while diagnosing why the free23 lane failed after the twenty-one-commit push.
close_reason: Over-ceiling and forbidden custom tools are startup security refusals again; malformed config-quality files still skip-and-warn. [closing=5c6ae17707dc093fbea4ae321d818b25e066495c source=b514ee3ccbeedbcbb3d2ed07a6e6a4f0822ca0c4 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-jjtrc.json]

title: plsql live_snapshot/blast_radius bypass GuardedGeneratedReadConn: bulk catalog reads leave no audit record
notes:
CONFIRMED by code trace in the phase-4 audit (PurpleRidge). Adversarially verified — and DELIBERATELY SCOPED DOWN, see "WHAT THIS IS NOT".

THE ASYMMETRY (dispatch/mod.rs ~12333-12356). Two sibling arms of one match hand the plsql feature surface DIFFERENT connections:
  "oracle_lineage"                                  => dispatch_live(cx, &guarded_metadata_conn, ...)
  "oracle_plsql_live_snapshot"|"oracle_plsql_blast_radius" => dispatch_live(cx, &observed_metadata_conn, ...)
guarded_metadata_conn is a GuardedGeneratedReadConn wrapping observed_metadata_conn. Its before_query (mod.rs:7634) does two things: ensure_generated_read_sql_allowed(sql) AND append_audit_with_observed_scn(...). observed_metadata_conn is a bare ReadUncertaintyConn (mod.rs:12307) whose query_rows just forwards to inner with an observe() wrapper — no classifier, no audit.

CONSEQUENCE: oracle_plsql_live_snapshot and oracle_plsql_blast_radius perform LIVE BULK CATALOG EXTRACTION over caller-chosen schemas (objects, columns, constraints, indexes, triggers, synonyms, source, PL/Scope — see catalog_query_specs in oraclemcp-db/src/catalog_extract.rs:363) and leave NO record in the audit hash-chain. oracle_lineage, doing the same class of dictionary read through the same conn family, DOES record. Verified there is no compensating per-tool-call audit at dispatch entry: the generated-read audit fires per-QUERY inside before_query only.

Why it matters: the server's documented property is that the audit hash-chain records every privileged action. An agent can bulk-extract the full schema shape of every visible schema of a production DB via live_snapshot and the chain shows nothing. That is a FORENSICS/completeness gap — after an incident you cannot answer "who extracted what, when".

WHAT THIS IS NOT — I chased these and they are all clean, do not re-derive them:
- NOT SQL injection. Caller schema names (LiveSnapshotArgs.schemas) reach the DB as BIND PARAMETERS: oracle_bind_placeholders(schema_count, 1) builds ":1, :2, ..." from the COUNT ONLY (catalog_extract.rs:514 interpolates integers), and schema_filter_binds maps each name to OracleBind::String (catalog_extract.rs:519). The SQL text is fixed and server-owned. No caller SQL text ever reaches Oracle on this path.
- NOT a classifier bypass in any live sense. The classifier gates CALLER SQL; there is none here, so ensure_generated_read_sql_allowed has nothing to prove. Its absence costs a defense-in-depth BACKSTOP, not a live control.
- NOT a privilege/level bypass. These are read-only dictionary reads bounded by the profile's own DB grants; nothing writes, nothing elevates.
- NOT data egress beyond metadata the profile's DB user can already select.

SEVERITY: filed LOW (p3) on that basis. Re-rate to MEDIUM if the team treats audit completeness as a hard property rather than best-effort — the argument for MEDIUM is that this is the ONE tool that bulk-reads an entire catalog and it is precisely the one with no trace. I am not making that call unilaterally.

PLAUSIBLY DELIBERATE, so confirm intent before "fixing": a full live_snapshot issues ~10+ rowset queries per call, so auditing each would add a burst of chain records per invocation. If that was the reason, the right fix is probably ONE tool-level audit record per live_snapshot/blast_radius call (subject, tool, schemas requested, rowset+row counts), NOT per-query records — that gives forensics the answer without flooding the chain. Whoever owns the plsql surface should decide; do not just swap in guarded_metadata_conn without checking the record volume.

CONTEXT: this whole surface only compiles under --features plsql-intelligence, which is non-default, and per oraclemcp-6fyp's sibling problem the feature build gets far less gate coverage than the default one. Axiom 8 (attack surfaces expand faster than defenses) applies: this is exactly where a control silently fails to get applied.
close_reason:
FIXED in 3f7f172. TRIAGED FIRST at the operator's request, because the title said "bypass Guard" while it was filed P3 and those cannot both be true.

VERDICT: (b) audit gap, NOT (a) guard bypass. P3/LOW was the correct rating; NOT a publish blocker. Evidence:
- NO CALLER SQL REACHES ORACLE on this path. SQL text is built from INTEGERS ONLY: oracle_bind_placeholders(count, start) emits ":1, :2, …" via format!(":{}", int) (catalog_extract.rs:507-517); caller schema names become OracleBind::String via schema_filter_binds (519-525); every *_sql() builder takes only that placeholder string. A bind cannot alter SQL structure, so there is nothing for a caller to smuggle past a classifier.
- THE SKIPPED CHECK IS A SELF-CHECK, NOT A CALLER GATE. ensure_generated_read_sql_allowed (dispatch/mod.rs:7383) classifies the SERVER'S OWN generated SQL, checks it against a built-in metadata/monitor allowlist, and proves it read-only. It takes no session and enforces NO operating level. Skipping it cost a defense-in-depth backstop, not authorization.
- Nothing on this path writes or elevates: fixed read-only SELECTs against ALL_* dictionary views, bounded by the profile's own DB grants.

THE REAL DEFECT (fixed): oracle_lineage ran on guarded_metadata_conn (classify + audit) while oracle_plsql_live_snapshot/oracle_plsql_blast_radius ran on the bare observed_metadata_conn — a ReadUncertaintyConn that only forwards. Identical class of dictionary read, neither classified nor recorded. Confirmed there is no compensating per-tool-call audit: the generated-read record is written per-QUERY inside GuardedGeneratedReadConn::before_query (7634), and `auditor` is populated unconditionally (12323) regardless of generated_read_tool() — which does NOT list any plsql tool (7408-7430) — so lineage recorded and these two did not. Consequence: an agent could bulk-extract the full catalog of every visible schema and the hash-chain showed nothing.

FIX: one-word — both arms now take &guarded_metadata_conn, exactly like oracle_lineage.

CHECKED BEFORE CHANGING that the guard would not BREAK these tools (the real risk of this fix): the catalog SQL normalizes to "SELECT OWNER, …" and contains " FROM ALL_", satisfying generated_read_sql_is_allowlisted (7344). Then proved it rather than trusting the reasoning.

VERIFIED LIVE against 23ai with --features plsql-intelligence, driving the REAL served binary over MCP stdio (not a unit test):
  before: no audit file for this path at all
  after : isError=false — the tool still works, so all 42 queries passed the allowlist AND the classifier
          43 records => {"oracle_plsql_live_snapshot": 42, "lane_lifecycle": 1}
42 = one per catalog rowset query, each carrying SQL + observed_scn. That is the forensics answer the chain previously could not give.

TRADEOFF, stated not hidden: 42 chain records + 42 SCN round-trips per snapshot is a real volume increase. It is exactly what oracle_lineage and every other generated-read tool already do, so it is CONSISTENT rather than a new anomaly. If the team prefers ONE tool-level record per call, that is a deliberate design change to make on top — not a reason to leave these reads untraced. My original bead note floated the one-record option; having measured it, per-query matches the existing contract and I did not invent a second pattern unilaterally.

SEC-3 note: this inherits the existing write-fail=fail-closed path — append_audit_with_observed_scn propagates via `?`, so an audit write failure now refuses these tools rather than letting them read untraced.

NOT WEAKENED: no cfg change, no classifier change, no level change; the fix only ADDS a classifier + audit in front of reads that had neither. Gates: fmt clean; clippy -p oraclemcp --features plsql-intelligence --all-targets -D warnings clean; clippy default clean; dispatch 319/319 green.

title: Wire thin TCPS wallet password and TLS options from profiles
description:
## Current gap

The current `rust-oracledb` driver exposes TCPS/wallet and TLS controls that oraclemcp does not fully surface:

- `ConnectOptions::with_wallet_password(...)`
- `with_ssl_server_dn_match(...)`
- `with_ssl_server_cert_dn(...)`
- `with_use_sni(...)`
- `with_wallet_location(...)`

oraclemcp currently only passes `oci.wallet_location` and then unconditionally sets `use_sni(true)` when a wallet location exists. There is no profile-safe wallet password reference, no explicit server DN controls, and no way for an operator to choose SNI behavior except by embedding some options in the connect string.

## Required change

Add explicit profile config for currently supported TCPS/wallet options, then map them into `OracleConnectOptions` and `oracledb::ConnectOptions`:

- wallet location, preserving existing `[profiles.oci].wallet_location`
- wallet password through a secret reference, not a literal TOML value
- `ssl_server_dn_match`
- `ssl_server_cert_dn`
- `use_sni`

## Security requirements

- Wallet passwords must be resolved through the same safe credential path style as `credential_ref` or an equally explicit `wallet_password_ref` field.
- Wallet password values, wallet paths, connect strings, usernames, and DN values that could reveal topology must not leak through `Debug`, doctor output, profile metadata, or error envelopes.
- Redaction tests must include driver errors containing wallet path + wallet password + connect string.

## Acceptance criteria

- Strict config parsing accepts the new fields and rejects misspellings.
- Inheritance works for the new TCPS fields.
- `profile_to_options` carries all fields to `OracleConnectOptions`.
- `to_connect_options` applies the values with the driver's `with_*` methods.
- Existing wallet profiles keep working with the previous default behavior unless a new explicit value overrides it.
- Unit tests cover mapping, inheritance, default behavior, and redaction.
- README/setup templates explain when to use `connect_string` query parameters vs named profile fields.
close_reason: Implemented thin TCPS wallet password and TLS profile wiring with safe wallet_password_ref resolution, driver builder mapping, redaction hardening, docs, setup templates, unit tests, workspace gates, and gated live-test hooks

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Compliance calibration: deterministic single-bead audit 2026-06-16T07-31-11Z scored 690 because the fallback extractor treated prose field oci.wallet_location as a file path and missed unit-test evidence. Ground truth: config fields are in crates/oraclemcp-config/src/profile.rs; core mapping is in crates/oraclemcp-core/src/connect.rs; driver with_* mapping and redaction tests are in crates/oraclemcp-db/src/connection.rs; DB connect option redacted Debug is in crates/oraclemcp-db/src/types.rs; wallet_password_ref resolution and doctor redaction are in crates/oraclemcp/src/main.rs and crates/oraclemcp-core/src/doctor.rs; docs/setup are in README.md and crates/oraclemcp/src/robot_docs.rs. Gates passed: fmt, clippy -D warnings, cargo test --workspace, cargo deny check. Live test harness exists with TCPS envs; current local DB credentials return ORA-01017, so live TCPS is not proven yet.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: Post-close fresh-eyes hardening: resolve_profile_secret now summarizes SecretError without echoing env var names, malformed secret refs, or backend locators; added profile_secret_resolution_errors_do_not_echo_secret_locators. Reverified cargo fmt --all -- --check, clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test --workspace, and cargo deny check.

title: [P0][guard] expression subqueries bypass statement SideEffectOracle
description:
Confirmed fail-open classification widening in the engine-bound strict guard.

Exact reproducer (also scalar/IN expression variants):
SELECT 1 FROM dual WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sensitive_orders)

Give the SideEffectOracle ProvenSideEffecting when its input contains sensitive_orders and ProvenReadOnly otherwise. Expected: Guarded / READ_WRITE, with sensitive_orders surfaced. Actual: Safe / READ_ONLY: query_base_objects only traverses FROM/JOIN factors, CTEs and derived table subqueries, never Select projection/selection expression subqueries. It returns only dual; classify_statement consults statement_purity([dual]) and treats the statement as pure. Thus a VPD policy / trigger-like side effect attached to sensitive_orders is never inspected.

This is a classification widening, not cosmetic: served strict mode has statement_unknown_guarded enabled, but it cannot fail closed because the omitted object is never supplied to the oracle. Existing b62e894 fixed the same bypass for NestedJoin/PIVOT/UNPIVOT and the metamorphic reachability suite does not cover expression-subquery positions.

Acceptance: structurally collect all Query nodes reachable from SELECT expressions and predicates (at least Expr::Subquery, Expr::Exists, and Expr::InSubquery), preserve CTE alias filtering/deduplication, and add strict-oracle regressions for EXISTS, IN (SELECT), and scalar subquery forms.
close_reason: Reproduced Safe/READ_ONLY for EXISTS over sensitive_orders, then replaced manual base-object traversal with scoped SQLParser visitor collection. Strict-oracle regressions now pass for EXISTS, IN, scalar, ANY, ALL, and comparison-RHS subqueries.

title: [bughunt][installer] Smoke negative verification cases can be bypassed by stale already-current prefix
description: Verified during full cargo test: scripts/installer_lint_and_offline_smoke.sh reused target/installer-smoke across runs, while install.sh intentionally exits early when the requested version is already installed. A stale checksum-reject-digest-only prefix could therefore make invalid checksum sidecar cases succeed without exercising verification. Fix: force negative installer verification scenarios so already-current cannot short-circuit them. Guard: cargo test -p oraclemcp --test installer_e2e installer_lint_and_offline_smoke_passes reproduces the digest-only failure before the patch and passes after.
close_reason: Duplicate of oraclemcp-n3l2, which already records and commits the installer smoke stale-root fix; no separate code change needed.

Split or otherwise facade the test monolith without weakening concurrency_contract.rs include_str! or dashboard_e2e's non-recursive http/*.rs security scan; preserve all route/auth/CSRF/SSE contracts. title: [CI-RECOVERY-2026-07][SBOM] Normalize duplicate npm component identities
description: Fresh Required CI run 29208024244 fails scripts/release_sbom_check.sh --source because npm 10.9.4 emits duplicate vite@7.3.6 components/dependency records for two lockfile installation paths. Verified no dangling refs; duplicate component metadata and dependency edges are identical except cdx:npm:package:path. Proposed fix: normalize raw npm CycloneDX deterministically, collapsing only compatible duplicate bom-ref identities, retaining all unique properties and dependency targets, and fail closed if duplicate security/legal metadata conflicts. Guard tests: real dashboard build/source gate plus synthetic compatible-collapse and conflicting-duplicate refusal cases.

Added deterministic fail-closed normalization that collapses only metadata-compatible duplicates, preserves both paths and all dependency targets, refuses conflicting security/legal metadata, and captures raw npm output to a file to avoid observed 64 KiB pipe truncation. title: [09x][B1] Verdict certificate is never projected onto the operator wire — the console can prove nothing
description: SYMPTOM: no verdict certificate ever reaches a client. Arc B1 computes a VerdictCertificate in the guard (crates/oraclemcp-guard/src/classifier.rs, VerdictCertificate::from_decision), binds its domain-separated core hash to the audit record (AuditRecord::verdict_certificate_core_hash, crates/oraclemcp-audit/src/record.rs:416, covered by the schema-v10 chain hash and HMAC) and can verify it standalone (crates/oraclemcp-verifier). But redacted_audit_record() (now crates/oraclemcp-core/src/http/operator.rs, formerly http/mod.rs) projects only seq/timestamp/subject_id_hash/tool/danger_level/decision/outcome/correlation/rows_affected/sql_sha256/db_evidence/proof — it omits BOTH verdict_certificate_core_hash and the certificate itself, and no other operator route carries them. THE DASHBOARD IS SHOWING LESS PROOF THAN THE BACKEND ACTUALLY HAS: the guard proves why every statement was admitted or refused and the audit chain covers that proof, yet the verdict-proof inspector (bead …15.7, commit 08898ce) can only ever report 'no proof-carrying records'. Its client-side re-checks (audit_binding, statement_digest, rule_registry, chain_hash) are implemented and tested and are waiting for the two fields. WHY IT MATTERS: an operator cannot see, and cannot independently verify, the classifier decision that governed a statement — the proof exists but is invisible outside the process. A FIX MUST PROVE: (1) the certificate is persisted alongside the record on the certificate-aware audit append path; (2) redacted_audit_record projects verdict_certificate + verdict_certificate_core_hash and stays redaction-clean by construction (registry construct labels only — no SQL, no bind values, no schema identifiers), with a test asserting no SQL text or bind material can appear; (3) an http test round-trips a proof-carrying record over GET /operator/v1/audit-tail and asserts bound_audit_hash == record.entry_hash and that the recomputed core hash matches; (4) the four checks the inspector makes all pass against that response.
close_reason: Completed B1 in 5a9c361: certificate-bearing audit records now persist a closed-grammar verdict certificate beside the signed record, and audit-tail projects it only after bound audit hash, statement digest, and signed core-hash checks. Tests cover redacted wire projection, post-append forgery omission, forged client input rejection, and apply-time reclassification.

title: Wire supported thin proxy authentication from profiles
description:
## Current gap

oraclemcp already has `AuthAdapter::Proxy { proxy_user, target_schema }` in `crates/oraclemcp-db/src/auth_adapter.rs`, and the current thin driver exposes `ConnectOptions::with_proxy_user(...)`. The profile schema and `profile_to_options` path do not expose or apply this capability, so proxy authentication remains stranded code.

## Required change

Add a strict profile configuration shape for supported proxy authentication and wire it into the thin connect path. The profile should make the two identities unambiguous:

- the authenticating proxy account that owns the password/credential reference
- the target schema/client identity that Oracle should proxy into

Then map that to the driver's `with_proxy_user(...)` in the form the driver expects, using the existing `AuthAdapter::Proxy` validation helpers where appropriate.

## Explicit non-scope

Do not implement Kerberos, RADIUS/native MFA, OCI IAM database-token auth, or passwordless external/wallet auth in this bead. Those are not supported by the current driver surface and should continue to fail explicitly.

## Security and safety requirements

- Proxy config must not weaken profile `max_level`, `default_level`, `protected`, or signed-tool requirements.
- Proxy account names and target schemas should not leak through `oracle_list_profiles` unless there is an intentional, documented non-secret metadata field.
- Errors must distinguish incomplete proxy config from unsupported enterprise auth.

## Acceptance criteria

- Config accepts a documented proxy auth shape and rejects incomplete proxy settings.
- `profile_to_options` carries proxy intent into `OracleConnectOptions`.
- `to_connect_options` calls the driver's `with_proxy_user(...)` correctly.
- Unit tests cover valid proxy config, missing proxy user, missing target schema, inheritance, redaction, and preservation of profile operating-level gates.
- A live test plan documents the required Oracle grants, for example `ALTER USER target GRANT CONNECT THROUGH proxy`, and verifies session identity where privileges allow.
close_reason: Implemented strict thin proxy_auth profile shape, inheritance/validation, AuthAdapter and OracleConnectOptions wiring, driver with_proxy_user mapping, redaction hardening, docs/setup/live-test hooks, and workspace gates; live smoke currently skips because configured DB credentials return ORA-01017

title: Pairing bootstrap secret rides in the dashboard URL query string (recoverable from browser history/extensions)
description:
Browser-security LOW residual from the dashboard review pass (independently confirmed by a second reviewer; deliberate design tradeoff, well-mitigated — filed for tracking, not urgent).

crates/oraclemcp-core/src/dashboard_auth.rs:557-562 builds the pairing URL as http://127.0.0.1:PORT/dashboard/pair?ticket=<64-hex>. The one-time bootstrap secret rides in the URL query string. When the operator opens that URL, the ticket string can be recovered from browser history, or by a browser extension with tabs/webNavigation permissions.

Why LOW (mitigations already present):
- Single-use: consumed atomically on the first navigation (fs::remove_file is the consume gate before session mint, dashboard_auth.rs:280).
- 60s TTL (DASHBOARD_PAIRING_TTL_SECONDS), listener-instance + audience bound (HMAC), loopback-only.
- The /dashboard/pair response is 303 -> / with cache-control: no-store (mod.rs:1177-1181) and Referrer-Policy: no-referrer (mod.rs:1337), so a later history replay fails closed (InvalidTicket).
- This is the only channel a top-level browser navigation offers for a bootstrap secret (headers are not settable on navigation), so secret-in-URL is a standard local-pairing tradeoff.

Trigger: run `oraclemcp dashboard`, open the printed URL; a browser extension or a later reader of history recovers the (already-consumed, expired) ticket string.

Severity: LOW. Fix direction (optional): deliver the bootstrap secret via a POST body form or a fragment (#) that never reaches the server log / history the same way, or a copy-paste code exchanged out of the URL. Given the layered mitigations this is acceptable as-is.
design:
TRANSPORT: the implementer takes the BEST engineering decision for our values — fail-closed, security-first, clean agent+operator UX — and documents the rationale in the close evidence. The acceptance below is BINDING regardless of the transport chosen. No transport is pre-selected.

Options as INPUT (not a decision):
(a) URL fragment — a SERVER+client protocol change (rebuts "fragment is only client-side"): the server STOPS consuming the ticket from the GET query and adds a narrow same-origin POST exchange; the bootstrap page reads location.hash, history.replaceState-scrubs it, then POSTs the ticket in the request BODY. Keeps the secret out of the request-target, access logs and Referer. Residual: brief address-bar/history exposure before scrub; JS joins the bootstrap security boundary.
(b) Copy/paste one-time code — secret never in ANY URL. Residual: clipboard/terminal scrollback; costs the click-to-open UX.
(c) POST form — must reject Origin: null (file:// forms) and never weaken origin validation for convenience.
A frontend-only change is INSUFFICIENT: the server currently consumes the ticket from the GET query before JS runs, so the server protocol must change.

ACCEPTANCE (transport-independent, ALL required): a real loopback served test proves the bootstrap secret is absent from URL/query, redirect Location, Referer, access-log fixture, error text and telemetry; exactly one session mint; replay + expiry + cross-listener fail closed; preserve loopback peer check + listener/audience HMAC binding + 60s TTL + single-use verify-before-atomic-consume + no-store + no-referrer + CSP + strict/HttpOnly cookie + no automatic browser launch. Cite the served-path test (not only a unit test) in the close evidence.
close_reason:
TRANSPORT CHOSEN: copy/paste one-time code, submitted through a script-free same-origin HTML form POST (options b+c converged). Rationale: (a) URL fragment was REJECTED — it does not meet the binding acceptance. An extension holding tabs/webNavigation permission receives the full URL INCLUDING the fragment on navigation, before page JS can history.replaceState-scrub it, so the secret stays recoverable from extensions and (racily) history; the bead itself concedes 'brief address-bar/history exposure before scrub'. (c) POST form cannot originate from nowhere — the form must be served by us at a secret-free URL, and a file:// form would carry Origin: null — so (c) collapses into (b). (b) additionally keeps JS OUT of the bootstrap security boundary entirely (a plain form needs no script), a strictly smaller attack surface than (a), which the bead notes makes 'JS join the bootstrap security boundary'. UX cost is small: run_dashboard_cmd already refuses to auto-launch a browser (an acceptance to PRESERVE), so the operator already copy-pastes a URL by hand; this adds one paste into an autofocused field.

PROTOCOL: server change as the bead required. GET /dashboard/pair serves the script-free form (no secret in it). POST /dashboard/pair reads the code ONLY from an application/x-www-form-urlencoded body. A legacy ?ticket= URL is REFUSED (400 dashboard_pairing_query_secret_refused) WITHOUT consuming the ticket it names, so a replayed history entry neither pairs nor burns a live ticket. CLI prints a secret-free URL plus the code (--json adds pairing_code).

SERVED-PATH TEST (cited per acceptance, real sockets + native parser via serve_http_until, NOT only a unit test): crates/oraclemcp-core/src/http/tests_dashboard.rs — served_dashboard_pairing_keeps_the_bootstrap_secret_out_of_the_request_target (proves: secret absent from URL/query/fragment, from the served form body, from the 303 redirect Location, and from every request target collected as an access-log fixture; no-store; no-referrer; frame-ancestors none; HttpOnly+SameSite=Strict; replay is 401 with NO set-cookie = exactly one session mint; error text never echoes the code) and served_dashboard_pairing_refuses_a_secret_in_the_query_without_consuming_it (legacy URL refused, then the body exchange still pairs = the refusal did not consume). Expiry: dashboard_auth.rs expired_pairing_ticket_fails_closed_and_is_swept (new). Cross-listener: pairing_ticket_is_bound_to_exact_listener_instance_and_audience (existing, still green). Referer/telemetry: secret is no longer in the request target at all, and nothing logs query_string.

PRESERVED: loopback peer check, listener/audience HMAC binding, 60s TTL, single-use verify-before-atomic-consume, no-store, no-referrer, CSP (form-action self), Secure/HttpOnly/SameSite=Strict cookie policy, no automatic browser launch. Origin enforcement reused as-is (enforce_dashboard_post_headers rejects absent/null/cross origins). POST carries its own 1 KiB body cap since the route returns before the MCP cap.

GATE: cargo fmt clean; clippy all 3 CI invocations (workspace + oraclemcp-core/oraclemcp with dashboard-bundle) 0 warnings; cargo test -p oraclemcp-core 643 lib tests + all integration bins green; dashboard-bundle lib tests green; cargo deny ok; api-lock/agent-surface/driver-seam/honesty-grep all exit 0; web tsc --noEmit clean. Docs updated (README, docs/configuration.md, docs/threat-model.md T12). NOT caused by me and PRE-EXISTING: cargo test --workspace has 2 failures in oraclemcp/tests/e2e_harness.rs — every failing scenario is a missing live-lab credential (ORACLE_MATRIX_XE18/_USER/_PASSWORD/DSN), committed+unmodified scripts, bead oraclemcp-5c7i territory.

title: [HIGH] Add live operator OAuth scope e2e that proves read scope blocks writes
description: Coverage gap found in phase-3 server verification: d94040a preserves ScopeGrant through /operator/v1/actions/execute, but its only regression proof is crates/oraclemcp-core/src/http/tests_auth.rs::oauth_scope_is_forwarded_to_operator_action_dispatch using ScopeEchoDispatch via handle_http_request. The binary HTTP OAuth tests use NoExecMock and do not cover the operator action route.\n\nAdd a real 23ai (and matrix-ready) served-oraclemcp acceptance test, with no database mock, that: (1) configures a non-protected profile whose default/max level is READ_WRITE; (2) authenticates an allowlisted OAuth bearer with oracle:read; (3) sends an operator action oracle_execute for a disposable fixture UPDATE through the actual HTTP socket; (4) proves a typed READ_ONLY scope refusal before confirmation/execution, audit records the refusal, and the Oracle fixture value is unchanged; (5) includes an admin-scope control only when it can remain rollback-by-default. Wire it into the live matrix with true pass/fail/typed skip output. This protects the fail-closed scope ceiling against future operator-route propagation regressions.
close_reason: Added live served operator OAuth scope-ceiling E2E: an oracle:read token clamps an ADMIN profile to READ_ONLY, allows only a real XE read, blocks preview/execute DML, and verifies the blocked audit chain.

title: Rule 15: verified-push must reject a stale verdict from another SHA
description: scripts/swarm_discipline.sh verified-push / gate-verdict must bind a verdict to the exact candidate HEAD. Today a bounded verified-push at a67a8700 timed out before writing a verdict; gate-verdict then surfaced an unrelated self-test record for 2106de73 as a generic failure instead of saying no verdict exists for a67a8700. A mismatched verdict that merely looks present risks either a false push decision or false diagnosis. Acceptance: (1) candidate SHA mismatch is an explicit refusal, never a usable verdict; (2) no record and a record for another SHA report "no verdict recorded for <candidate SHA>"; (3) verified-push never pushes unless recorded verdict SHA exactly equals candidate HEAD and status=pass; (4) self-tests cover absent, stale/mismatched, matching-pass, and matching-fail verdicts; (5) output identifies both candidate and record SHA when mismatched.

title: Driver: a leading WITH (CTE) query is not recognized as a query -> ORA-00900
description:
Found while building the governed-egress live matrix (bead .4.6), reproduced live on XE 21c.

SYMPTOM: any query whose first keyword is WITH (an ordinary Oracle CTE) fails at runtime with
'ORA-00900: invalid SQL statement'. The identical statement runs fine in sqlplus against the same DB.

ROOT CAUSE: the pinned driver oracledb 0.8.2 decides whether a statement is a query by looking for a
literal leading SELECT keyword:

    fn statement_is_query(sql: &str) -> bool {
        sql.trim_start()
            .split(|ch: char| !ch.is_ascii_alphabetic())
            .next()
            .is_some_and(|keyword| keyword.eq_ignore_ascii_case("select"))
    }

(oracledb-0.8.2/src/lib.rs:10471, called at 4655/4743/4785/4871/7432/7541). A CTE-led statement is
therefore sent down the non-query path and the server rejects it.

WHY THIS MATTERS (not cosmetic): the fail-closed guard EXPLICITLY admits CTE reads -- WITH-wrapped
reads are a documented vector in the classifier adversarial corpus and classify as READ_ONLY. So the
server admits a statement the driver then cannot execute. Every CTE query a user or agent writes --
an extremely common Oracle read shape -- is currently broken end to end, and no live test covers it
(grep: no live test in oraclemcp-db issues a leading-WITH query today).

FIX: recognize the query shapes Oracle accepts on the query path -- at minimum WITH (and consider
'(' -led parenthesized SELECT). Belongs in the rust-oracledb driver; oraclemcp pins =0.8.2, so it
needs a driver patch + version bump, or an interim server-side guard that rejects with a clear error
instead of a raw ORA-00900.

TESTS: add a live case issuing 'WITH t AS (SELECT ... FROM dual) SELECT ... FROM t' and assert rows
come back; add the same shape to the driver's own live suite. crates/oraclemcp-db/tests/live_egress.rs
currently routes AROUND this with an inline view and documents why (see the staff() fixture comment).
close_reason: Completed the driver-side CTE query-path fix in rust-oracledb f800a35340bd0a9ce46618151651396a9e855835, with conservative WITH+SELECT/DML/PLSQL/comment/nested-CTE unit coverage and green driver fmt, clippy, tests, cassette tests, and deny. Added ignored live MCP regression 0a410092a0a2a574d1ed89d003b51532d9bd6bf7. Server consumption remains blocked: oraclemcp deliberately pins =0.8.2 until an operator-approved crates.io driver release.

title: E15: mirror charter rules 13-18 into rust-oracledb AGENTS.md
description: Cluster E's charter is 'both repos'. Rules 1-12 landed in both AGENTS.md files (bead oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.6.2); rules 13-18 and scripts/swarm_discipline.sh landed in oraclemcp only, because that half is the sibling checkout /home/durakovic/projects/rust-oracledb and belongs to the driver panes. Port the six rules and the script (its subcommands are repo-agnostic; only the default unbounded-wait-lint file set and the entry-trace registration are repo-specific). Source: oraclemcp commits 32122cb and a08f3d5.

title: Guard test: release.yml publishing jobs must stay tag-gated
description:
The pre-tag release rehearsal (workflow_dispatch, plan sec.29.3) is safe only because every publishing job refuses to run off a tag. That guarantee is currently four 'if: startsWith(github.ref, refs/tags/)' expressions plus one transitive skip -- convention, not an enforced contract. A rehearsal that published would be unrecoverable: crates.io versions are immutable and registry names are permanent.

I attempted this test and DELETED IT rather than ship it: my draft asserted reachability over the parsed job blocks and did NOT fail when I neutralised publish-crates' tag guard as a mutation check. A guard test that cannot fail is worse than no test, because it advertises a guarantee nobody re-verifies. The bug was in the block-extraction/fixed-point logic, not in the idea.

WHAT IS NEEDED: parse release.yml as YAML (pyyaml in a script, or serde_yaml in a test) rather than slicing raw text, compute reachability from a workflow_dispatch trigger over the needs graph, and assert every job that touches an outward-facing surface (release, publish-crates, docker, publish-mcp-registry, verify-mcp-registry) is unreachable. ACCEPTANCE: the test must FAIL when any one of those guards is removed -- demonstrate that with a recorded mutation run, not by assertion.

Related and cheaper: plan sec.25.4 already proposes adding actionlint to the cheap contracts job; actionlint is not installed on the dev host today.

title: connect-error: account-locked/password-expired ORA codes misclassified as retryable ConnectionFailed
description:
MULTI-PASS BUG HUNT 2026-07-04, Part A connect-error mapping (fresh-eyes sub-audit).

FINDING P1 (auth classification completeness — the 'auth miss testing didn't catch' shape): classify_ora_code (oraclemcp-error/src/lib.rs) did not recognize the account/password lifecycle ORA codes 28000 (account locked), 28001 (password expired), 28002 (grace period), 28011 (password will expire), 1005 (null password). They fell to ErrorClass::Internal, which DbError::Connect::into_envelope (oraclemcp-db/src/error.rs:276) rewrites to ConnectionFailed with next-steps 'verify the connect string ... reconnect'. That is confidently WRONG guidance: a reconnect never clears a locked account or expired password (needs DBA unlock / password change). NOT a lockout amplifier — none of these are in resilience.rs TRANSIENT_ORA_CODES, and the pool does not retry failed connects (verified), so no auto-retry hammers the account. Correctness/guidance miss on the auth axis.

FIX: classify 28000/28001/28002/28011/1005 (alongside 1031/1017/1045/28009) as ErrorClass::InsufficientPrivilege — terminal, non-retryable, default_suggested_tool=None (no misleading reconnect). Raw ORA-2800x message still surfaced verbatim so the real cause is visible. Chose an existing terminal auth class over a new ErrorClass variant to avoid public-API / api-lock churn in a stability-focused session.

FUTURE (noted, not done): a dedicated ErrorClass::AuthenticationFailed / AccountLocked variant would give the agent a sharper signal (locked vs expired vs wrong-password) than the InsufficientPrivilege authn/authz conflation; additive per the enum's non_exhaustive design but a broader change.

Also surfaced by the same sub-audit (NOT fixed, logged as follow-ups): P2 redaction is exact-substring only (decomposed host/port/service + Oracle-uppercased identifiers can survive sanitize_driver_error); P2 mid-call-cancellation session reuse-safety depends on hard-coded driver string markers rather than the cx-cancelled checkpoint.

TEST: account_lifecycle_codes_are_terminal_auth_not_retryable_connection_failures (oraclemcp-error) asserts class=InsufficientPrivilege, !is_retryable, suggested_tool None, and end-to-end envelope for 'ORA-28000: the account is locked'. error crate suite green (14).
close_reason: FIXED + tested (classify_ora_code maps 28000/28001/28002/28011/1005 to terminal non-retryable InsufficientPrivilege; account_lifecycle_codes_are_terminal_auth_not_retryable_connection_failures). error suite (14) + workspace green. Dedicated auth ErrorClass variant noted as future.

title: audit sink + config have no advisory file lock: two server instances on the same audit log fork the hash chain
description: FileAuditSink::open (crates/oraclemcp-audit/src/sink.rs) and the config loader take no advisory OS file lock (no flock/fd-lock). Two oraclemcp server instances pointed at the same audit log path each resume from the same tail (seq=N) and then both issue seq=N+1 in append mode, forking the tamper-evident hash chain. It is detectable later (verify_records flags seq-not-monotonic / prev-hash-mismatch) but the running servers do not prevent or notice it, and the fsync-before-execute / poisoning invariants are per-process only. Same exposure for concurrent writers to the config/snapshot files. RECOMMENDATION: take an exclusive advisory lock (e.g. pure-Rust fd-lock / rustix flock, no C toolchain) on the audit log (and a single-writer lock on the state dir) at startup; a second instance must fail closed with an actionable 'another oraclemcp is writing this audit log' error. Left as a bead (architectural: new dependency + cross-platform + startup-sequencing) rather than fixed in this pass. Evidence: grep for flock/FileExt/lock_exclusive across oraclemcp-audit + oraclemcp-config returns nothing.
close_reason: Landed + verified: AuditLogLock takes an exclusive advisory OS lock (std File::try_lock, no external dep, forbid-unsafe holds) on an audit .lock sidecar recording the holder pid; a second instance fails closed with AuditError::Locked{path,holder_pid} instead of forking the hash-chain. Advisory flock releases on process exit so a clean restart after a crash re-acquires. Two-writer test passes; audit suite 65/65 green; api-lock/boundary/deny clean; live ladder 21/21 across xe18/xe21/free23.

title: [QA100][P1][release-test] Build real archive fixtures for verified distribution metadata
description:
Finding

QA41 correctly made render_distribution_manifests.sh hash each release archive, but the pre-existing binstall_brew_winget_metadata_valid integration fixture still created only fake .sha256 sidecars with chosen digests and no archives. The broad workspace suite and isolated test therefore failed at the release gate (missing archive, or checksum mismatch if stale files existed).

Fresh verification

The test helper wrote only sidecars. The renderer now unconditionally calls sha256_of_archive before rendering. QA20 independently reproduced the isolated failure; static control flow proves it on a clean target. This is not a production design choice: the release renderer is right to fail closed, while the acceptance test must exercise real bytes.

Impact

The mandatory workspace test gate was red after the QA41 security fix, hiding later failures and encouraging bypass of the exact artifact-authentication invariant QA41 introduced.

Proposed fix

Make installer_e2e create deterministic archive byte fixtures, compute their real SHA-256, write basename-bound sidecars, assert rendered metadata contains those computed digests, and use a per-process nanosecond-qualified directory so concurrent test binaries cannot contaminate one another.

Guard tests

The exact integration test must pass from an empty unique fixture root; tampering and missing-archive coverage remains in e2e_harness; the full installer integration suite must no longer depend on stale target contents.
close_reason: Confirmed QA41 left a stale fake-sidecar fixture incompatible with mandatory byte rehashing. The test now writes deterministic real archives, derives exact SHA-256 sidecars from those bytes, asserts the computed digests, and uses a process/time-unique root. Exact regression, all 12 installer integration tests, strict test-target clippy, rustfmt, and diff checks pass.

title: installer smoke TTY guided test hangs (exit 124) when gum is installed - PTY answer feed mismatch
description:
Discovered during field-test bead .7 verification: bash scripts/installer_lint_and_offline_smoke.sh exits 124 on a box where gum is installed at /usr/bin/gum. The 'TTY guided install path' PTY test (timeout 20s script -qefc ...) feeds printf 'y
n
n
n
' line answers, but with gum on PATH the installer's prompt_yes_no switches to gum confirm, which consumes single keypresses - the feed desyncs, the 'Run oraclemcp doctor now?' gum prompt waits for input, and timeout kills the session (exit 124). Reproduced identically on HEAD (pre-existing, not a regression). Fix ideas: run the PTY smoke with gum forced off (e.g. NO_COLOR=1 in the smoke env, since prompt_yes_no already falls back on NO_COLOR), or feed gum-compatible keypresses, or stub gum out of PATH for the PTY blocks.
close_reason: fixed: both PTY guided-install invocations in scripts/installer_lint_and_offline_smoke.sh now set NO_COLOR=1, pinning install.sh prompt_yes_no to its plain read-based prompts (the piped line answers and the [y/N]/[Y/n] prompt-text assertions are written for that path); a host-installed gum no longer swallows keypresses and hangs the 20s timeout; suite exits 0 with 21 pass gates on a gum-equipped box

title: [09x][J][P1] Exercise refusal corpus through the served MCP surface
description: Arc J's corpus writer is reachable from OracleDispatcher::dispatch/dispatch_stream and served construction, but HEAD has no end-to-end scenario for it. scripts/e2e contains no refusal-corpus script, scripts/e2e/run_all.sh registers no such scenario, and COVERAGE.md/PROVENANCE.md contain no Arc J coverage. Add a real MCP stdio/HTTP scenario that submits a refusal carrying synthetic identifiers/binds/secrets, proves the default served dispatcher appends exactly a validated redacted JSONL record (and no raw identifier/bind/secret), proves an unsafe suggested rewrite is not persisted, and register it in run_all.sh plus the coverage/provenance inventories. Do not close Arc J until this run is registered and exercised.
close_reason: Landed ff873e5: real served stdio E2E submits a synthetic forbidden request, validates exactly one redacted refusal-corpus JSONL record, proves raw identifiers/binds/secrets are absent at byte level, and proves an unsafe rewrite is not persisted; registered in the E2E runner with coverage and provenance.

title: Windows: shipping_spool concurrency tests fail in CI (not caused by 091 push)
description:
Two oraclemcp-audit tests panic on the Windows workspace lane of CI run 29831595915 (SHA 7dce026):
  shipping_spool::tests::concurrent_local_chain_stays_gap_free_while_shipping_is_stalled
  shipping_spool::tests::slow_destination_does_not_delay_durable_local_append
Result: 222 passed, 2 failed, -p oraclemcp-audit --lib.

ATTRIBUTION: git log a8f31b0..7dce026 -- crates/oraclemcp-audit is EMPTY, so the 091 swarm push did not touch this crate. This is either a pre-existing Windows-only defect that the previous green run did not trip, or timing flake under Windows CI load. The Windows lane was made green on 2026-07-19 and shipping_spool has prior history of a shutdown lost-wakeup deadlock, so treat a real ordering bug as the leading hypothesis and flake as the fallback - do not assume flake and retry.

ACCEPTANCE: reproduce on Windows (or under an equivalent scheduler-stress harness), root-cause as ordering/lost-wakeup vs flake, fix the defect or make the test deterministic without weakening what it asserts, and cite the CI run that goes green.
close_reason: Windows shipping_spool concurrency tests fixed at 731b083a (GateForwarder replaces Linux-calibrated wall-clock budgets). Windows CI acceptance: Rust workspace (Windows) job on run 29899848208 (head 40b1f032, containing 731b083a) completed success. Both previously-failing tests pass on Windows. [closing=1a6ba044a113243bc1a11b75349010b573ec9f39 source=2746b6c12347fd1a7bd8e899d2f0b7c9a1b0f1bf evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-mqmo9.json]

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: ROOT-CAUSED and fixed at af5b6884 (commit 731b083a). NOT flake, and NOT the lost wakeup this file has history of. Ruled out by reading the code: run_worker scopes its queue lock in a 'let next = { ... }' block and releases it BEFORE destination.forward(), so a stalled destination cannot block appenders; stop() already carries the lock-then-notify fix for the historical shutdown lost wakeup. REAL CAUSE: both tests asserted a wall-clock budget (150ms for one append, 1000ms for eight) while a sleeping destination ran, but enqueue() performs create + write + sync_all() + rename while holding the spool mutex. sync_directory is already a no-op on Windows, but the per-file sync_all() is not - FlushFileBuffers on a virtualised runner costs orders of magnitude more than an ext4 fsync, and eight serialise through the mutex. The budgets were calibrated to Linux fsync latency; the property under test held perfectly. FIX: assert the property instead of timing it. GateForwarder (already present in this file) blocks the destination until the test opens the gate, so an append returning while delivered_records is still 0 provably did not wait for it - true at any machine speed, and strictly stronger than a threshold a fast machine could pass by luck. The gap-free 1..=8 assertion is unchanged. A 30s liveness guard remains because a gate turns 'appends blocked' into a hang rather than a fast failure; it separates 'returned' from 'blocked forever', not '1ms' from '125ms'. Gotcha worth knowing: the gate MUST be opened before the auditor drops - locals drop in reverse declaration order so auditor precedes _release, and stop() then waits forever on a gated destination. I hit that deadlock while writing it. 244 audit tests pass, clippy clean with -D warnings, pair runtime 2.3s -> 0.01s. STILL OPEN: the acceptance requires citing a Windows CI run that goes green on this commit. That cannot be asserted from here - watch the Windows workspace lane for af5b6884.

comments.author: SageRaven
comments.text: Independent revalidation 2026-07-21: inspected 731b083a and current spool code. `run_worker` drops the queue lock before `destination.forward`, while `enqueue` serializes local write+sync_all under the mutex; the pre-fix 150ms/1000ms assertions measured Windows FlushFileBuffers latency, not destination blocking. The replacement gate proves delivery remains at zero while appends return, preserves gap-free 1..=8 local chain, and retains only a 30s hang detector. Linux stress: each named regression test passed 50 consecutive exact runs (100 total). `cargo test -p oraclemcp-audit --lib`: 244 passed; `cargo clippy -p oraclemcp-audit --lib -- -D warnings`: passed. Required Windows CI evidence is not available yet: `gh run list --commit 731b083a...` returned no runs and the commit is not in origin/main, consistent with the current merge/push hold. No further local code change is justified; retain the bead open for the first green Windows workspace run.

comments.author: SageRaven
comments.text: CORRECTION to the prior revalidation comment: `git branch -r --contains 731b083a` confirms the fix IS already an ancestor of origin/main. The exact-SHA Actions query was empty because CI ran on later heads. Windows workspace job on run 29856608420 (head af5b6884, containing 731b083a) failed overall; newest main run 29869012573 (head a6944a7e) also has Rust workspace (Windows) failed. Neither is a green Windows acceptance citation, and overall-red jobs cannot be reported as a spool pass. The required remaining evidence is therefore a green Windows workspace run after the unrelated red-main failures clear.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: 2026-07-21 revalidation: committed 731b083a replaces Linux-calibrated 150ms/1000ms wall-clock budgets with GateForwarder. The gate proves delivery cannot progress while local append returns, preserving the gap-free 1..=8 assertion; the remaining 30s bound is a hang detector, not a performance threshold. Personally ran each targeted test 50 exact times on Linux (100 runs total): all passed. Also passed cargo test -p oraclemcp-audit --lib (244 tests) and cargo clippy -p oraclemcp-audit --lib -- -D warnings. Hosted acceptance is pending: Rust workspace (Windows) for CI run 29874536415 at head 4ed59e561182eda7e0559e526c45ebca8a6615ea was actively running cargo test workspace on Windows at 22:43Z; do not treat it as green until that job concludes success.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: 2026-07-22 Windows CI acceptance: Rust workspace (Windows) job on run 29899848208 (head 40b1f032, containing fix 731b083a) completed with conclusion=success. The two shipping_spool tests that failed on run 29831595915 pass on Windows. The overall run has an unrelated Oracle FREE 23ai matrix failure; the Windows workspace lane itself is green. This is the required Windows CI citation.

title: Multi-agent orchestration controls: capacity admission, durable worker contract, clean-HEAD landing gate, hot-file ledger, watchdog
description: DEFERRED (process; filed so it is not lost — retro recommendation). Retrospective P3 items 13-15 (STATUS_REPORT.md:513-522,468,471): the largest PROCESS failures came from missing swarm controls — capacity-aware admission (probe model/quota/health before fan-out; do not mark in_progress until a worker acks), a durable worker contract (order file + ack hash + first-deliverable deadline + captured logs/PID/result), a clean-committed-HEAD landing gate after every landing, a hot-file ownership ledger / partition-by-gate-unit, and a slow health watchdog. Caused a shared-tree landing deadlock, half-landings missing files, and stale-pane false-success delivery. skills-plan.md:278 de-scoped multi-agent fan-out for the single-owner campaign, so this is DEFERRED. NOTE: MCP Agent Mail (file reservations, build slots, inboxes) already provides partial coverage; un-defer + scope against it before the next real swarm.
design: Reactivate the existing orchestration-control task and close its full durable contract: capacity admission, acknowledgement hash, first-deliverable deadline, PID/log/result capture, clean-HEAD landing gate, hot-file ownership ledger, watchdog, and durable orders.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Every original 3748 requirement has executable proof and no parallel orchestration umbrella duplicates or weakens it.

title: Installer smoke uses stale fixed target root
description: The installer offline smoke script reuses target/installer-smoke across runs. Invalid checksum-sidecar cases assert that prefix/bin/oraclemcp does not exist after a rejected install, but stale prefixes from earlier runs can make a correct rejection fail as 'reached archive installation'. Use a unique smoke root per script run or otherwise isolate case prefixes so the test is deterministic.
close_reason: Fixed installer offline smoke flake by isolating SMOKE_ROOT per run so stale prefixes cannot make rejected checksum sidecars appear installed. title: Epic: native Asupersync runtime adoption
description: Tracks replacement of Tokio runtime assumptions with Asupersync context, scopes, cancellation, budgets, admission, shutdown, deterministic tests, and runtime-native primitives. Asupersync is not an executor swap: controlled APIs must take &Cx, spawned work must be region-owned, and tests must prove cancellation/quiescence behavior.
design: This epic owns the runtime semantic migration. Asupersync is adopted for structured concurrency, Cx propagation, cancellation, budgets, region-owned tasks, deterministic testing, and capability narrowing. Do not treat it as an executor swap. Controlled async APIs should accept &Cx, long loops should checkpoint, spawned work should belong to a request/tool region, and cancellation must leave resources clean.

notes: Child scope: W2 nightly pin, W6a pure dispatch, W6b DB dispatch, W7a runtime primitives, W7b DB cancellation cleanup, and W11 deterministic tests. Closure requires no production Tokio runtime dependency, no ambient runtime-handle assumptions, and tests for shutdown, admission, timeout, cancellation cleanup, quiescence, and panic/redaction safety.
close_reason: All runtime migration children are closed: nightly CI, Cx propagation through pure and DB dispatch, Asupersync runtime primitives, DB cancellation cleanup, and deterministic Asupersync tests. Production Tokio/runtime-handle dependencies are absent; shutdown, timeout, admission, cancellation cleanup, quiescence, preview-token races, and redaction behavior are covered by local and CI gates.

Native stdio is deliberately first because it proves the MCP core before HTTP session complexity. HTTP must preserve Streamable HTTP and protected-resource metadata; dropping HTTP behavior is not an acceptable simplification.
