TEST. Prove the annotated writer output is valid, bootable, and cannot silently drift from the ConnectionProfile schema, the OracleMcpConfig top-level schema, or from oraclemcp.example.toml.

BACKGROUND. The example file is anti-rotted by crates/oraclemcp-config/tests/example_config_parses.rs; the writer needs the same guard so a new profile or top-level field cannot be added without appearing in the generated annotated config.

ACCEPTANCE and CHECKS.
- The writer output for a synthetic multi-net-service input parses through OracleMcpConfig::from_toml_str (the same loader config-ops uses at config_ops.rs:280) and carries no unknown top-level keys (deny_unknown_fields at lib.rs:83).
- Every serde field name of ConnectionProfile (profile.rs:457 to profile.rs:550) appears in the rendered output either SET or as a commented help line; a missing field FAILS the test (anti-rot). Likewise every top-level OracleMcpConfig field (schema_version, default_profile, monitor_profile, http, audit, profiles) appears SET, commented, or represented by the example.toml pointer note.
- max_level and default_level are SET to READ_ONLY in the rendered output (safety-legibility regression guard).
- Help lines use exact serde field names: uncommenting a sampled optional (for example call_timeout_seconds and one [profiles.*] section) still parses through the strict loader.
- The one-line help strings are consistent in meaning with oraclemcp.example.toml for the overlapping fields (assert a sampled set matches, acknowledging the discovery writer comments fields example.toml may SET).
- A rendered config boots: spawning serve against it does not error at config load, and doctor (offline) reports no config blocker.

TEST PLAN. A unit or integration test in the config or binary crate using a fixture; reuse the strict loader and, for the boot check, the CLI harness in crates/oraclemcp/tests/cli.rs. No live DB.
close_reason: Annotated-writer parity + anti-rot test bead. Added 3 test targets: (1) crates/oraclemcp-config/tests/discovery_writer_parity.rs — rendered output parses via from_toml_str (deny_unknown_fields honored), every ConnectionProfile + OracleMcpConfig serde field (from serializing a fully-populated instance, independent of the disposition table) appears SET/commented/pointer in the output (complements .1's table↔struct guard by pinning rendered↔struct), both levels SET READ_ONLY, uncommenting a sampled scalar+section still parses, and help wording shares distinctive terms with oraclemcp.example.toml for 5 overlapping fields. (2) crates/oraclemcp-core/tests/discovery_writer_config_ops.rs — rendered output stages through ConfigOpsBackend::stage_config_draft (the config_ops.rs:280/:281 re-parse path) with byte-identical draft hash. (3) crates/oraclemcp/tests/cli.rs discovery_annotated_config_boots — the rendered config boots: serve loads it (exit 0, status, no CONFIG_INVALID, no audit-key gate) and offline doctor --profile reports ok + READ_ONLY. Full workspace test green except the pre-existing pwsh installer test.

title: audit chain does not resume across server restarts: second run re-issues seq=1/genesis into the same audit.jsonl, so 'audit verify' reports BROKEN (PrevHashMismatch) on any multi-run file
description:
GROUND TRUTH (verified 2026-07 while implementing the head anchor, bead oraclemcp-xb51): crates/oraclemcp-audit/src/sink.rs Auditor::new always starts ChainState { seq: 0, last_hash: GENESIS_HASH } and crates/oraclemcp/src/main.rs build_auditor opens FileAuditSink in append mode with NO chain resume. A server restart therefore appends a fresh seq=1/prev_hash=genesis record after the previous run's records in the SAME default audit.jsonl, and 'oraclemcp audit verify' reports BROKEN (PrevHashMismatch or SeqNotMonotonic) at the run boundary. Loud fail — not a silent hole — but it means the documented 'verify periodically' workflow only works on single-run files.

ANCHOR INTERACTION: the new head-anchor sidecar (audit.jsonl.anchor) is overwritten by the new run's first durable append (seq=1), regressing below the previous run's head. Harmless today only because verify already fails at the restart boundary before the anchor is consulted; once resume lands, the anchor logic needs no change (it tracks the resumed monotonic seq).

SUGGESTED FIX: on startup, if the audit file is non-empty, read the LAST well-formed record and seed ChainState { seq: last.seq, last_hash: last.entry_hash } (fail closed if the tail record is malformed or, with the sidecar present, if it contradicts the anchor). Alternatively rotate the file per run (audit.<ts>.jsonl) and teach verify to walk segments — bigger surface, less appealing under files-first. Add tests: restart-resume keeps one verifiable chain; anchor advances across restarts; malformed tail refuses startup (operator repair path).
close_reason: Fixed: Auditor::resume_from(audit_path) seeds ChainState from the last well-formed record on startup so a restart continues ONE verifiable chain instead of re-issuing seq=1/genesis; wired into build_auditor after with_head_anchor. Fails closed (AuditError::ResumeRefused, operator-repair message) on a malformed tail or a tail that contradicts the head anchor (truncation/divergence); anchor advances across restarts, never regresses; record-before-anchor fsync ordering untouched; append-only record format unchanged. Tests: 4 new sink tests (restart-resume verifies as one chain + anchor advances; malformed-tail refusal; tail-behind-anchor truncation refusal; empty-log fresh genesis). Live ladder e2e extended with a restart-resume step: all 3 lanes xe18/xe21/free23 grow run1=15 to run2=30 records as one chain-from-genesis. Gates green: fmt, clippy -D warnings, cargo test --workspace (0 failed), 6 oraclemcp lint scripts, live matrix exit 0.

title: oracle_create_or_replace: PL/SQL stored-code replacement gated at READ_WRITE (not DDL) + audited as oracle_execute
description: Observed live during e2e hardening (2026-07 field-test surfaces). Two related ground-truth observations about oracle_create_or_replace: (1) LEVELING: a CREATE OR REPLACE of a PL/SQL stored object (PROCEDURE/FUNCTION/PACKAGE/BODY/TRIGGER) classifies GUARDED with required_level=READ_WRITE (reason: 'PL/SQL block (cannot be proven side-effect-free here)'), because the classifier levels it by its PL/SQL body rather than as DDL. A CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW, by contrast, classifies as DDL. Consequence: replacing stored PL/SQL CODE is gated one tier LOWER (READ_WRITE) than a view (DDL); an operator elevated to READ_WRITE for DML can also replace stored procedures. The oracle_create_or_replace tool itself mints its own single-use confirmation grant ONLY when required_level >= Ddl (dispatch/mod.rs create_or_replace_inner), so for a PL/SQL object it returns confirmation=None and its next_actions delegate to the general oracle_preview_sql -> oracle_execute path. Note oracle_patch_source, by contrast, FORCES patch_required_level=Some(Ddl) always. So code replacement via create_or_replace is READ_WRITE while the same via patch_source is DDL — an inconsistency. (2) AUDIT ATTRIBUTION: because create_or_replace delegates to execute_sql, the persisted audit record carries tool='oracle_execute', not 'oracle_create_or_replace' (verified: audit.jsonl from live matrix shows compile_object/patch_source under their own tool names but create_or_replace only as oracle_execute). Not a fail-closed hole (the SQL text + decision are recorded; the classifier still gates at the proven level), but worth a design decision: should stored-code CREATE OR REPLACE require DDL for consistency with patch_source, and should the audit attribute the create_or_replace tool? Left as a task bead (touches the fail-closed classifier tiering / tool gate — needs operator design call, not an in-pass fix). Evidence: scripts/e2e/oracle_ladder_session.py source_create_or_replace_view vs source_create_procedure_via_execute; live run 20260704T195124Z all three lanes green.
notes: DECISION 2026-07-06 (operator delegated: best-fit + low-effort): TIGHTEN — a PL/SQL-bearing CREATE [OR REPLACE] {PROCEDURE|FUNCTION|PACKAGE|PACKAGE BODY|TRIGGER|TYPE BODY} floors at OperatingLevel::Ddl (Stage A body-danger analysis STILL escalates above Ddl for dangerous bodies; the fix only raises the FLOOR). Rationale: (a) levels.rs ladder doc already states 'CREATE OR REPLACE -> Ddl'; (b) same fail-open-tier defect class as oracle-y54x.1 (which floored VIEW/SYNONYM/TYPE object-clobbering replaces at Ddl but left PL/SQL forms at ReadWrite via Stage A); (c) consistency with oracle_patch_source (already forces Ddl) and CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW (Ddl); (d) security: a ReadWrite/DML principal must NOT be able to replace stored code (definer-rights escalation). PURE TIGHTENING (ReadWrite->Ddl for benign bodies; never loosens — max(Ddl, body_level)>=body_level), respects the fail-closed 'only tighten' invariant. AUDIT ATTRIBUTION auto-fixes: create_or_replace_inner delegates to oracle_execute ONLY when required_level < Ddl (confirmation=None path); once PL/SQL CoR is Ddl it mints its own single-use grant and the audit records tool='oracle_create_or_replace'. So ONE leveling change fixes both the tiering inconsistency AND the audit attribution. Deferred/out-of-scope: a dedicated finer 'stored-code' capability tier below Ddl (a larger future capability design; not needed now — Ddl is the correct safe baseline).
close_reason: PL/SQL-bearing CREATE [OR REPLACE] {PROCEDURE|FUNCTION|PACKAGE|TRIGGER} now floors at OperatingLevel::Ddl via max(Ddl, body_level) in classifier stage-A consumer (pure tightening: benign ReadWrite->Ddl, dangerous bodies still escalate ABOVE Ddl to Forbidden, anonymous blocks unaffected at ReadWrite). Audit attribution auto-fixed: execute_sql threads canonical audit tool name so create_or_replace applies audit under tool=oracle_create_or_replace (verified live audit.jsonl DESTRUCTIVE/ALLOWED/SUCCEEDED). Gates green: fmt, clippy -D warnings, cargo test --workspace (62 suites), api-lock (baseline refreshed for new StageA::PlSqlBlock::plsql_create field), agent_surface/driver_seam/honesty/boundary lints, cargo deny. Live 3-lane ladder pass: xe18 xe21 free23 (CREATE OR REPLACE PROCEDURE requires Ddl + audited as oracle_create_or_replace).

title: connect-error: redaction is exact-substring only (decomposed host/port/service + uppercased identifiers can leak); mid-cancel session reuse marker-dependent (P2)
close_reason: Redaction hardened + structural session-reuse fix. (1) sanitize_driver_error (crates/oraclemcp-db/src/connection.rs) now runs two fail-closed passes: exact/longest-first verbatim removal of high-entropy secrets (password/token/wallet/DN/connect-string/app-context/identity), then case-insensitive token-boundary removal of DECOMPOSED topology identifiers (host/port/service via crate::tns::extract_hints, plus username/schema) so an ORA message naming only the host/service, or an Oracle-UPPERCASED identifier, is scrubbed; boundary rule prevents over-redaction (SYS!=SYSDATE, 1599!=15990). Feeds ErrorEnvelope/doctor(into_envelope)/structured-logs via the single DB chokepoint (driver-seam lint keeps raw oracledb::Error inside the adapter). (2) Marker-fragility: fetch_batch_call_timeout + fetch recovery-cancel-failed now return the STRUCTURAL DbError::Cancelled (is_uncertain_session_state flags from kind, not message text); MARKERS list documented as driver-string fallback only. Tests: 8 new redaction tests (decomposed host/port/service, uppercased service+schema, full connect string, TNS descriptor, benign unchanged, boundary lookalikes) + fetch_call_timeout structural test; all pass. Gates green: fmt/clippy-Dwarnings/test(0 failed)/5 lints+api-lock(no API change)+boundary+sensitive_data+cargo deny. Honest gap: doctor's own sanitized_detail remains exact-substring but only ever receives already-sanitized DbError text, so no decomposed/uppercased leak in practice.

title: [CI-RECOVERY-2026-07][api-lock] Refresh intentional guard public API
description: Verified GitHub job 86689063474 reports public API drift in oraclemcp-guard after intentional placeholder-analysis and grant-cap additions.
acceptance_criteria: Review every delta for intentionality; refresh only the guard API snapshot; scripts/oraclemcp_api_lock.sh passes and snapshot captures the new symbols.
close_reason: Reviewed the complete cargo-public-api diff: the only additions are named_bind_placeholders at module/root and MAX_LIVE_GRANTS, all intentional APIs with implemented callers/tests. Refreshed only the oraclemcp-guard snapshot; the exact three-crate API lock passes.

title: P6: lint normalized plan-to-Beads graphs before promotion
description: Bootstrap precondition authorized by the operator's 2026-07-18 GO. Implement a deterministic stdlib-only validator for normalized plan-to-Beads import graphs before any remaining PLAN_ENGINEERING_PROGRAM.md program bead is created. It must make the plan's confidence claims mechanical: unique task slugs, one unique tracking label per task, resolvable local and cross-repository handoff references, acyclic dependency and handoff order, repository-local native Beads dependencies only, required self-contained task fields, and declared SemVer target assertions. The validator is an import-time check, not a second status tracker. Source: docs/plan/PLAN_ENGINEERING_PROGRAM.md §§27.6, 32.4, 33 and docs/plan/RETRO_SWARM_CAMPAIGN_2026-07.md P6.
design: Input contract: required JSON object with schema, program, source_document {path, sha256}, repositories, trackers, release_targets, and tasks. Trackers bind each repository slug to a JSONL path and source_repo identity. Tasks carry unique plan provenance, self-contained scope/acceptance/evidence, same-repo blockers and parent, checksum-bound cross-repo handoffs, operator gate, promotion disposition, validated tracker lineage, and an explicit reuse action. Deferred existing-Bead mappings resolve without mutating their tracker. Validation is stdlib-only, fail-closed, read-only, and iterative for deep mixed parent/dependency/handoff cycles. The bound source checksum prevents silent plan drift. Promotion creates every new issue deferred, wires and checks the live-plus-plan graph including closed nodes, then activates only tasks without operator or checksum holds; cluster J is excluded without an explicit flag.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Valid normalized graphs exit 0 with a deterministic summary. 2. Duplicate slugs, task labels, plan labels, reused IDs, or lineage mappings fail. 3. Unknown or cross-repo-native dependency, parent, handoff, lineage, tracker, or source references fail. 4. Cross-repo handoffs require an artifact and required checksum and remain deferred at promotion. 5. Mixed parent/dependency/handoff cycles and live-plus-plan cycles fail before actionable mutation. 6. Malformed fields, paths, JSONL, UTF-8, source checksums, SemVer, types, priorities, tiers, gates, dispositions, and reuse actions fail without crashing. 7. Promoted tasks cannot depend on non-promoted native tasks. 8. New issues stage deferred; graph and lineage edges land before activation; GCP is held by default. 9. Unit and CLI tests cover validation and staging/activation order. 10. The complete live engineering-program manifest passes before bulk Bead creation.
notes: v2 core landed in 19555305: stdlib-only checksum-bound plan-bead-graph/v2 manifest validation; strict repository/tracker/lineage/reuse/SemVer/native-edge/handoff/cycle checks; deterministic promotion plan and opt-in stage→wire→post-stage-live-graph→activate flow; Cluster J excluded without --include-gcp. Eight DB-free unit/CLI/promotion-order tests pass and are registered in entry-trace. The retained live train lint is honestly red on three non-sink train-091 beads (oraclemcp-dapll, oraclemcp-m4kua, oraclemcp-r3sti). Criterion 10 remains open as child oraclemcp-n4rnp: the complete normalized engineering-program manifest is not yet present (only 5 of 173 current train-091 beads carry plan provenance labels). Do not close P6 until that child provides a committed manifest and PASS.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text:
SELFTEST LANDED as ad8c11dd (on origin/main), wired into the CI lint job. NOT yet guard-closed: the main checkout is diverged from origin (ahead 4, behind 3) and its scripts/plan_bead_graph_lint.py carries my same edit uncommitted, so the guarded close hits SCOPE_DIRTY_AT_CLOSE / SCOPE_CHANGED_AFTER_SOURCE. Close once the checkout reconciles with origin.

THE FINDING: the --manifest validator has 38 error codes, no selftest, no fixtures, and (established while closing n4rnp) no committed v2 manifest anywhere in the repo — so it had never been observed rejecting anything OR accepting anything. Indistinguishable from a validator that returns true unconditionally.

WHAT THE SELFTEST DOES: builds a minimal manifest on disk (real plan file, real per-repo JSONL trackers, matching source_document sha256), requires it to be ACCEPTED, then applies one targeted mutation per rule and requires the matching error code: E_TASK_SLUG (duplicate slug), E_TASK_LABEL (duplicate tracking label, and '.' in a label), E_REFERENCE_UNKNOWN (dependency on an unknown task), E_GRAPH_CYCLE, E_CROSS_REPO_NATIVE_EDGE, E_SOURCE_SHA256 (plan checksum drift), E_SCHEMA, E_TASK_ACCEPTANCE, E_SEMVER_ASSERTION. The accept case is the anti-vacuity guard in the other direction, so neither always-true nor always-false survives.

MUTATION CONTROL at the validator, not the fixtures: making Findings.error a no-op — exactly the unconditional-true lint this exists to rule out — yields 'duplicate task slug was NOT rejected with E_TASK_SLUG; got no findings' and exit 1. Clean run exits 0, so CI acts on it.

REMAINING FOR FULL ACCEPTANCE: the bead also asks for the deterministic PASS output over a real committed v2 manifest of the live program. That manifest still does not exist — per-task acceptance/evidence/disposition/reuse are editorial, not derivable from the tracker (recorded on n4rnp). The validator is now trustworthy; producing the manifest it validates is a separate authoring pass.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: COVERAGE CHECKED FIRST, no third checker written. All four malformations this bead names are ALREADY covered across the two existing lints, each with a selftest proving it can fail: cycle = E_GRAPH_CYCLE (manifest, ad8c11dd) plus E_CYCLE_SEQUENCING/E_CYCLE_HIERARCHY (live, 54781499); dangling = E_REFERENCE_UNKNOWN (manifest) plus E_DANGLING (live); orphaned child = E_ORPHAN_CHILD (live); parent-closed-with-open-children = E_CLOSED_PARENT_OPEN_CHILD (live). Adding a third overlapping gate would have been padding. THE REAL GAP RUNS THE OTHER WAY AND IS WIDE: this validator defines 39 error codes and its selftest exercised 8, so 31 had NEVER been observed failing - the exact condition ad8c11dd was added to rule out, still true of most of the file. I evidenced four of the highest-value ones: E_NATIVE_DEPENDENCY (both dependencies and blockers on one task), E_HANDOFF_NATIVE (a same-repository 'handoff' - a native edge in a cross-repo costume, which is exactly what this manifest exists to make visible, and is this bead's own cross-repo acceptance), E_PROMOTED_DEPENDS_NONPROMOTED (activating a task whose dependency is deferred), and E_LIVE_REFERENCE (a dangling edge in the exported TRACKER rather than the manifest - unreachable by any manifest mutation, which is why it had never fired). Selftest is now 15 checks. MUTATION-CONTROLLED, AND MY FIRST ATTEMPT WAS WRONG: silencing E_LIVE_REFERENCE left the selftest PASSING, because there are TWO producers and I had silenced the one inside _validate_live_graph, which only runs during promotion (--promote --apply) and which the selftest does not drive. The reachable producer is on the plain validation path; silencing that one fails the case correctly. Honest scope recorded in the commit: the plain-path live check is now evidenced, the post-stage one is NOT. The same-repo handoff case also failed on its first run against the REAL schema rather than my assumed one (to/artifact/sha256, not target/checksum) - the selftest doing its job on its own author. REAL GRAPH: the live structural gate now reports 5 violations, down from 6, because .7.11.5 closed this session; committed output refreshed. All five are the same two deliberate scoped closes already reported on n4rnp (bp8ia.7.11 and bp8ia.6), not new damage. THIS BEAD CANNOT CLOSE YET: it is a parent and its child oraclemcp-n4rnp is in_progress, whose remaining half is the editorial plan-bead-graph/v2 manifest. There is still no committed v2 manifest in the repo, so the manifest lint has nothing real to validate - which is precisely what that half would supply.

title: E14: a delete of a live file is a stale index (charter rule 18 + stale-delete-check)
description: Live incident 2026-07-21, during the session that encoded rules 13-17: commit ebac68f committed deletions of five cluster-E close-evidence documents and a scripts/entry_trace_contract.json registration that all still existed in the worktree. A stale index snapshot landed over another pane's just-committed work, HEAD stopped matching the tree, and every guarded close refused with CLOSE_EVIDENCE_NOT_LANDED. Rule: commit explicit paths (git commit -- <path>...), then verify with git show --stat HEAD; never -a or git add -A in a shared checkout. Mechanism: swarm_discipline.sh stale-delete-check refuses a staged set or commit that deletes a path still present in the worktree.
close_reason: Rule 18 + stale-delete-check: a change deleting a path still present in the worktree is refused as a stale index snapshot [closing=64a1194b092bf1e42fbf6bed1103dcf94143a284 source=a08f3d599d4d226b0f7be6b4e6245a53d52d652c evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-pwvv5.json]

title: [P1][guard] JSON_TABLE/XMLTABLE argument subqueries drop base objects (k4cl-adjacent fail-open)
description:
Found by a mechanical fail-open sweep (object-capture oracle) during the bead-16 adversarial hunt, on frozen HEAD 03494ea.

SAME fail-open CLASS as k4cl (an expression subquery's base objects are never collected, so statement_purity / the engine VPD+trigger walk is never consulted and the read is classified Safe/READ_ONLY), but a DISTINCT AST PATH that a projection/selection-focused k4cl fix can miss:

  query_base_objects walks FROM/JOIN table factors, CTE bodies and derived-table subqueries. k4cl adds the missing WHERE/SELECT-list/HAVING/ORDER-BY *expression* subqueries. But the subquery here lives in a TABLE-FACTOR ARGUMENT expression:
    - JSON_TABLE( <document-expr> , '$' COLUMNS ...)  -- document-expr can be a subquery
    - XMLTABLE('/a' PASSING <expr> COLUMNS ...)        -- PASSING expr can be a subquery
  These arms of collect_factor extract NO base objects from their argument expression.

Reproducers (all classify Safe/READ_ONLY; object-capture oracle shows SECRET is never handed to statement_purity — seen=[]):
  SELECT * FROM JSON_TABLE((SELECT body FROM secret WHERE rownum=1), '$' COLUMNS (a VARCHAR2(1) PATH '$.a'))
  SELECT * FROM XMLTABLE('/a' PASSING (SELECT x FROM secret WHERE rownum=1) COLUMNS b VARCHAR2(1) PATH '.')

Confirmed widening: yes. Severity: P1 (exotic-but-valid syntax; same security impact as k4cl — a sensitive-table read bypasses the oracle, so a selective SideEffectOracle / object-level policy cannot gate it).

ACTION: the k4cl fixer should verify the fix walks table-factor ARGUMENT expressions (JSON_TABLE document, XMLTABLE PASSING, and any TableFactor::Function args), not only WHERE/SELECT expressions. If the k4cl fix already recurses all Exprs including table-factor args, close this as covered-by-k4cl with a regression case for these two shapes. NOT fixed here to avoid colliding with the in-flight k4cl edit on the hot file classifier.rs.

NEGATIVE RESULTS from the same sweep (documented so they are not re-hunted): category-2 unknown/unsupported shapes fail CLOSED well (multi-statement incl. newline-after-linecomment/CRLF/stacked -> Destructive/Forbidden; unparseable -> Guarded/Forbidden; PL/SQL blocks, dynamic EXECUTE IMMEDIATE, DML-in-block -> Forbidden; unknown ALTER SESSION -> Forbidden). The NestedJoin/PIVOT/UNPIVOT fix (b62e894) holds across deep-nested, derived-in-nested and pivot-over-derived. Guard-local step-up is SEC-1-sound (CiToken constant-time+deadline+scope; approval_matches_sql digest-binds each approval to its SQL).
close_reason: COVERED BY k4cl fix f162340 (fix(guard): collect expression subquery base objects). Verified on a frozen snapshot of the post-fix HEAD: the object-capture probe now shows obj:SECRET consulted for both JSON_TABLE((SELECT ... FROM secret), ...) and XMLTABLE('/a' PASSING (SELECT ... FROM secret) ...), and under a SELECTIVE oracle (only 'secret' side-effecting) both reproducers classify Guarded, not Safe. The k4cl expression-subquery walk reaches table-factor argument expressions too, so no separate fix is needed. This was a genuine live fail-open at 03494ea (where I found it); the concurrently-landing k4cl fix closed it before I acted. No code change from me.

title: E12: never block a turn on an unbounded wait (charter rule 16 + bounded run)
description: Live incident 2026-07-21: a pane blocked its own turn four times on unbounded waits (a tail -f that can never exit, plus duplicated until-cargo-check loops against a broken tree), once for 54 minutes, with dispatches queuing behind it. Encode the rule: never block a turn on an unbounded wait; check once, report, move on. Deliverable: AGENTS.md constitution rule + a bounded-run wrapper that imposes a hard deadline and reports a timeout as a result rather than hanging, plus a lint that keeps the unbounded-wait idioms out of committed scripts.
close_reason: Rule 16 + bounded-run and unbounded-wait-lint: deadlines are mandatory and a timeout is a reportable result [closing=5b9b65bfc54ed46652b943dc5d21ca8a8512d9f2 source=32122cb9857684ace414e03ef4a3ca6771dc0183 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-q9b1w.json]

title: [QA100] Post-v0.8.0 hidden-bug, security, and quality campaign
description:
Campaign objective: probe the freshly shipped v0.8.0 codebase for up to 100 validated correctness, security, reliability, performance, CLI, installer, documentation-contract, and high-leverage quality defects without padding the count with speculative findings.

Method: documentation-first architecture archaeology; a recorded random sample of production modules and scripts; automated scanners and standard gates; deep state-machine and boundary review; security and integration lenses; then an independent fresh-eyes validation for every candidate. Before a child is filed, prove the path is reachable, identify the missing or incorrect guard, compare against intended behavior in README/docs/tests/history, and search existing beads to reject duplicates and design choices.

Required child content: exact file and line evidence; user or operator impact; root cause; reproduction or reasoning proof; proposed remediation; concrete regression, guard, property, integration, or negative tests; false-positive/design-intent checks performed; dependencies or alpha limitations where relevant.

Naming and grouping: every child title begins [QA100] and is parented to this epic with label qa100-2026-07 plus domain/severity labels.

Audit posture: findings and beads only unless implementation is separately authorized. Do not weaken the fail-closed classifier, profile ceilings, protected-profile policy, rollback defaults, grant binding, lane isolation, OAuth monotonicity, or audit/write-intent guarantees.

Exit: 100 independently validated findings or the available usage/time campaign boundary, followed by coverage, severity, invalidated-candidate, and residual-risk accounting.
notes:
Campaign finding phase completed 2026-07-09 after reaching the requested quota.

Verified inventory
- Exactly 100 open child findings, contiguous ids .1 through .100.
- Severity: P0 11, P1 44, P2 37, P3 8.
- Type: 98 bugs and 2 quality/tracker tasks.
- Every child carries the qa100-2026-07 label and a [QA100] title.
- Every child documents evidence/reachability, impact, false-positive or design-intent checks, a proposed fix/remediation, and concrete guard or acceptance tests.

Coverage
- Guard/classifier/transaction behavior, profile and config generation, authentication/OAuth/IAM, HTTP/SSE/session lifecycle, admission and cancellation, Oracle connection/pool/query/health/lease paths, audit/WORM/SIEM/telemetry, dashboard/operator/review flows, custom tools/MCP discovery/pagination, backup/restore/filesystem, installer/self-update, release workflows/SBOM/artifacts, docs/changelog/tracker truth, and alpha surfaces.
- A frozen random sample of 40 production files/scripts was combined with boundary/state-machine passes and parallel security, HTTP/dashboard, and general correctness probes.
- Candidates were checked against AGENTS/README/docs, tests, git/release history, reachable call sites, Oracle/MCP standards where relevant, and the full open/closed/deferred tracker. Design choices, duplicates, speculative-only claims, and weaker overlaps were rejected or merged rather than counted.
- Validated overflow candidates were intentionally not filed after .100; the inventory is quota-complete, not a claim of exhaustive absence of further defects.

Verification
- Baseline cargo fmt, clippy with -D warnings, workspace tests, and cargo deny passed before the finding campaign.
- Focused repository secret/sensitive-data/architecture/honesty checks passed; UBS produced no separate actionable result.
- br sync --flush-only exported 682 issues, including the epic plus 100 children.
- br doctor remains degraded for pre-existing recovery artifacts, an orphan-looking historical write lock, and two br binaries on PATH. No cleanup was attempted because repository rules forbid deletion without an exact operator command.
- Only .beads/issues.jsonl was modified by this campaign. Existing untracked COMPOSER_GOAL.md and docs/plan/ were preserved untouched.
- No code was changed, and no commit or push was made because the operator did not authorize either action.

The epic remains open as the remediation umbrella; completion here means the requested discovery/filing campaign reached 100 validated findings.
close_reason: Completion-compliance audit: all 124 QA100 children are closed (13 P0, 60 P1, 41 P2, 10 P3; 114 bugs plus feature/task/epic work), with one-bead implementation commits and documented guard evidence. Final residuals were implemented rather than deferred: owner-bound leases, per-stream notifications/list_changed, pre-authenticated mandatory-mTLS control ingress, and the mock-free adversarial resolver corpus. Fresh local gates cover release preflight, semver, API locks, fmt/clippy, dashboard 29/29, golden 9/9, core 617/617, binary 247/247/120 CLI, DB 378 plus live FREE23, cargo deny, and five-lane driver release qualification. Follow-up CI recovery beads for runner/tooling failures are also closed; publication remains a separately gated release action, not unfinished QA implementation.

title: [QA100][P1][state] Root-wide file-store lock breaks HTTP service composition
description:
Finding

All FileStore-backed service domains share one root-level .service.lock, but the runtime constructs independent stores and lock owners. Two stores hold that single lock for their entire lifetime while the remaining stores try to reacquire it for each mutation.

Evidence and reachable paths

- crates/oraclemcp-core/src/file_store.rs:105-110 unconditionally unlinks the root lock on drop; lines 204-221 acquire it with create_new.
- crates/oraclemcp-core/src/write_intent.rs:271-297 holds ServiceLock inside WriteIntentLog for the lifetime of any writable runtime.
- crates/oraclemcp-core/src/client_credentials.rs:247-278 holds the same root lock inside ClientCredentialStore.
- crates/oraclemcp/src/main.rs:1389-1401 opens WriteIntentLog for any reachable ceiling above READ_ONLY before the HTTP branch; lines 2671-2684 then open ClientCredentialStore when --client-credentials is enabled.
- crates/oraclemcp-core/src/config_ops.rs:304-347, change_proposal.rs:66-88, and source_history.rs:39-110 independently acquire the same root lock when applying config, drafting a proposal, or recording a source snapshot.
- crates/oraclemcp/src/service_lifecycle.rs:714-756 also reacquires that lock for backup.

Impact

1. Writable HTTP plus --client-credentials deterministically fails startup with ORACLEMCP_CLIENT_CREDENTIAL_STORE_UNAVAILABLE / Locked.
2. A writable HTTP service without client credentials starts, but config apply/rollback, change-proposal draft, source-history capture, and online backup remain locked for the full WriteIntentLog lifetime.
3. A READ_ONLY HTTP service with client credentials starts, but the same dashboard/operator mutations remain locked for the full credential-store lifetime.
4. This defeats advertised feature combinations and can block the source-before-DDL safety path.

False-positive and duplicate checks

The lock is intentionally mandatory and cross-process, but the architecture text calls it one process-wide service lock; it does not authorize mutually exclusive service subsystems. Existing tests prove only that a second instance of one store is rejected. A second reviewer traced constructor order and lifetimes independently. Repo-local bead search found the closed file-store foundation work but no lock-composition bug.

Proposed remediation

Acquire one process-wide service ownership lock once and share a capability/reference with every FileStore-backed domain, while keeping a single in-process mutation gate or narrower domain locks for atomic writes. Preserve cross-process exclusion and the write-temp/fsync/rename/fsync-dir durability contract. Define backup coordination explicitly so a live service can produce a consistent snapshot or returns an actionable stop/snapshot protocol rather than an accidental Locked error.

Guard tests

- Integration test that opens WriteIntentLog, ClientCredentialStore, ConfigOpsBackend, ChangeProposalStore, and SourceHistoryStore on one root and successfully exercises each mutation under one service owner.
- HTTP startup test for writable profile plus --client-credentials.
- Operator-route tests proving config apply, proposal draft/apply preparation, and source snapshot work while write intents and client credentials are active.
- Cross-process or independently-owned second-service test must still fail closed.
- Backup test covering the same long-lived store combination and documenting online/offline semantics.
- Regression assertion that protected/read-only ceilings and durable write-intent fail-closed behavior are unchanged.
close_reason: Verified deterministic root-lock composition failure; replaced per-domain lifetime locks with one cloneable process ServiceOwner sharing OS exclusion, mutation gate, and temp sequence; wired HTTP/write/client/config/proposal/source startup to it; made backups explicitly offline with actionable live-owner refusal; added composition, concurrency, startup, operator-route, and backup guards.

title: [QA100][P2][tracker] Reconcile shipped release state and unblock HTTP refactor
description:
Finding

The live Beads graph still models the shipped line as an open 0.7.3 release and contains a post-release HTTP refactor workstream whose dependency direction leaves every node blocked. This makes current-state and ready-work queries materially misleading after v0.8.0 shipped.

Evidence

- v0.8.0 is the public latest release and current HEAD contains only post-tag tracker changes, yet oraclemcp-release-073-iec3 and its server workstream .2 remain open and are still titled 0.7.3.
- The K workstream oraclemcp-release-073-iec3.6 is open even though all twelve of its children are closed.
- oraclemcp-demonolith-http-qyqs is parented under the stale server release workstream. It also declares each of .1 through .4 as a blocks dependency, while those same four children are parented to it.
- Consequently the parent is blocked by the children and the children are blocked by their parent; there is no actionable leaf in that workstream. br dep cycles reports zero because this parent-child plus blocks semantic standstill is not represented as a plain dependency cycle.
- A full unlimited search of open, closed, and deferred beads found no separate graph-reconciliation task.

Impact

br ready, br blocked, bv planning, and status summaries do not give an honest post-release picture. Agents can treat shipped work as unfinished, overlook a fully completed K tranche, or leave the intended HTTP cleanup permanently unactionable. The stale 0.7.3 naming also obscures that the delivered server release is 0.8.0.

False-positive checks

Open epics may legitimately retain follow-up work, and the HTTP split was deliberately deferred for reviewed execution. The defect is not that work remains; it is that shipped release scope and post-release refactor scope are conflated and the dependency directions make the latter impossible to start. The four HTTP children are genuinely open, so simply closing the entire old tree would also be wrong.

Proposed fix

Reconcile the shipped release hierarchy from leaf evidence: close the fully completed K workstream, close or retitle only release nodes whose declared delivery is complete, and move the HTTP refactor into an accurately named post-v0.8.0 architecture epic. Make the parent organizational and express sequencing only between actionable children; remove reverse parent-to-child blocks that create the standstill. Preserve any real cross-repo or deferred follow-ups as explicitly scoped open beads.

Guard and acceptance checks

1. Every open non-deferred workstream has at least one ready leaf or an explicit external blocker.
2. No parent is blocked by a child that is itself blocked by that same parent through parent-child semantics.
3. Epics with all children closed are reported for closure review.
4. br ready, br blocked, br dep cycles, and bv --robot-plan agree on an actionable HTTP first step.
5. Current release-status output names v0.8.0 and separates shipped scope from post-release refactors.
6. Flush .beads/issues.jsonl and verify DB/JSONL counts match after reconciliation.
close_reason: Reconcile shipped release state + unblock HTTP refactor (beads-graph surgery, no code). Actions: (1) closed the fully-complete K workstream iec3.6 (12/12 children closed); (2) closed the stale 0.7.3 release epic oraclemcp-release-073-iec3 and its server workstream iec3.2 — superseded by the shipped v0.8.0 (tag 2026-07-09), all task children already closed; (3) moved the HTTP de-monolith oraclemcp-demonolith-http-qyqs out from under the stale 0.7.3 workstream (removed the parent dep) so it stands alone as a post-0.8.0 architecture epic; (4) removed the 4 reverse parent-to-child blocks (qyqs was blocked by its own children .1-.4) that created the standstill. Acceptance verified: br dep cycles clean; 0 open 0.7.3 epics; qyqs subtree now surfaces 4 ready leaves (B6a-d, actionable HTTP first steps); .beads flushed (DB/JSONL parity). qyqs preserved as real deferred follow-up (the 0.9.x alien plan references it).

title: [QA100][P2][pagination] Bind list cursors to the catalog revision
description:
Finding

List pagination cursors authenticate only the endpoint kind and numeric offset even though tools/list is dynamic. A cursor replayed after the visible catalog changes can skip or duplicate tools while still verifying as valid.

Evidence and reachability

- crates/oraclemcp-core/src/pagination.rs:19-54 signs and verifies only kind plus next_offset.
- paginate at lines 70-78 slices the caller supplied current item vector at that old offset; it has no catalog fingerprint or revision.
- The module comment says lists are static per process, but crates/oraclemcp-core/src/server.rs:488-519 computes tools for current dispatch surface.
- descriptor_visible_for_surface at lines 1980-2000 changes the list with operating level, and profile-scoped custom tool catalogs can change on profile switch. No custom-tool count limit below LIST_PAGE_SIZE was found, so multipage dynamic catalogs are reachable.
- The closed cursor feature tests forgery and cross-endpoint reuse, not a valid cursor across a catalog mutation.
- Full tracker search found only the closed pagination feature and no stale-catalog cursor defect.

Impact

A conforming client paging a large custom-tool catalog across an elevation, de-escalation, profile switch, or catalog reload can silently omit tools, repeat entries, or combine pages from different authorization surfaces. The cursor remains cryptographically valid, so the client receives no signal to restart.

False-positive and design-intent checks

Offset cursors are correct for immutable snapshots. tools/list is explicitly contextual and listChanged-advertised, so the static-list assumption is false. Server-side authorization still governs calls, making this data-consistency and discovery correctness rather than direct privilege escalation.

Proposed fix

Bind the cursor to a stable digest/revision of the ordered visible catalog and relevant authorization surface, or keep a bounded immutable server-side snapshot. Reject stale revisions with InvalidArguments and instruct the client to restart from page one. Ensure the revision changes whenever names/order/descriptor semantics or visibility changes.

Guard tests

1. Page one under one level/profile followed by a catalog change makes its cursor fail as stale.
2. An unchanged catalog across benign state changes continues paging without gaps or duplicates.
3. Reordering, adding, removing, or editing a descriptor changes the revision.
4. Forged and cross-endpoint cursors remain rejected.
5. A multipage custom catalog round-trip returns every descriptor exactly once under a stable snapshot.
close_reason: Bind list-pagination cursors to the catalog revision. encode/decode_cursor signed only {kind, offset}, so a cursor stayed valid across catalog changes and could page an inconsistent snapshot (duplicated/skipped items). Now paginate() computes list_revision(items) = sha256 digest (first 64 bits, hex) of the exact ordered serialized visible list — any change to names/order/descriptor contents/visibility changes the digest. The revision is embedded in the signed payload ('revision:offset'); decode_cursor rejects a cursor whose embedded revision != current with InvalidArguments + 'restart from page one'. The revision rides inside the existing MAC-signed tamper token so it needs no forgery resistance of its own. paginate() signature unchanged, so the single production call site (server.rs) is unaffected. Tests: stale-after-append AND stale-after-reorder rejected; unchanged-list replays cleanly; within-revision past-the-end clamps to empty terminal page; forged/cross-list still rejected. Green: core pagination 7/7, fmt + clippy clean.

title: [QA100][P1][guard] Allow valid package specifications through the DDL gate
description:
Finding

A valid CREATE OR REPLACE PACKAGE specification is classified Forbidden/UNBALANCED_BLOCK instead of Destructive/DDL. Package specifications end with END without an executable BEGIN, but analyze_batch decrements block depth for that END and treats the declaration-only stored-object form as desynchronized.

Fresh verification

The focused classifier regression used Oracle-valid source: CREATE OR REPLACE PACKAGE p AS PROCEDURE run(p_value NUMBER); END;. The opaque-call work correctly ignored the procedure declaration signature, after which the pre-existing classifier returned required_level=None rather than DDL. Existing product surfaces explicitly support PACKAGE in oracle_create_or_replace and source tooling. Tracker searches for package specification and CREATE PACKAGE found no duplicate.

Impact

The advertised structured DDL path cannot preview, mint confirmation for, or apply ordinary package specifications. Package bodies may exist without a usable public spec deployment path, breaking a central PL/SQL workflow.

Proposed fix

Teach PL/SQL stored-object shape analysis to distinguish declaration-only PACKAGE specifications, and any analogous TYPE specs, from executable anonymous block nesting. Preserve DDL flooring, trailing-statement/desync detection, and dynamic marker refusal. Do not generally ignore unmatched END tokens.

Guard tests

- Valid minimal and multi-member package specs classify Destructive/DDL and oracle_create_or_replace preview mints its own DDL confirmation.
- PACKAGE BODY and anonymous BEGIN/DECLARE behavior is unchanged.
- Comment, case, editionable, and authid variants remain accepted at DDL.
- Extra END, trailing SQL, malformed nesting, and side-effect markers still fail closed.
- Review TYPE specification symmetry and either cover it or document or file separately.
close_reason: Confirmed safe fail-closed availability bug: valid Oracle PACKAGE specifications were rejected as UnbalancedBlock because declaration semicolons were counted at top level and the unit-closing END drove depth negative. Added token-aware PACKAGE-spec recognition, an implicit declaration scope only for specs, and an exactly-one-top-level-statement guard so trailing DROP/GRANT remains forbidden. PACKAGE BODY, anonymous blocks, and TYPE specs retain their prior behavior; the separate body defect is tracked as QA114. Guards cover valid syntax variants, DDL floor/step-up, malformed/extra END, trailing SQL, dynamic markers, preview confirmation, exact-once apply, and grant replay refusal. Full pinned fmt/clippy/workspace-test/API-lock/deny gate passed.

title: [QA100][P0][guard] Resolve zero-argument function identifiers before read admission
description:
Finding

Oracle permits a subprogram with no required parameters to omit its parameter list, and a function invocation is an expression. Therefore SELECT app_admin.run_ddl FROM dual, UPDATE t SET x = app_admin.run_ddl, and PL/SQL assignments or conditions can invoke a stored function even though no opening parenthesis appears. The guard only recognizes user-defined calls followed by an opening parenthesis, so these spellings are treated as column or record identifiers and can retain READ_ONLY or READ_WRITE authority.

Fresh verification

Official Oracle PL/SQL Subprogram Invocations documentation states that the parameter list may be omitted when there are no parameters or all parameters have defaults, and that a function invocation is an expression. A fresh public-classifier probe returned Safe/READ_ONLY for the direct SELECT form and Guarded/READ_WRITE for PL/SQL assignment, IF, and WHILE forms. Current user_defined_calls only scans identifiers immediately followed by LParen. Tracker searches for zero-argument and parameterless function found no duplicate. Bead .81 now refuses unanalyzable caller PL/SQL, but direct SQL remains independently affected. Bead .82 has the same missing semantic-resolution root for views and policies.

Impact

A read tool can evaluate a side-effecting or autonomous function as supposedly read-only, and a READ_WRITE profile can invoke higher-authority effects without the intended ceiling or confirmation. Parenthesis-only lexical detection cannot prove safety because the same identifier syntax can denote a column, variable, record field, or function.

Proposed fix

Resolve expression identifiers semantically against the active profile catalog before admission. Proven table aliases and columns remain data; resolved functions consult the full owner, package, member, quote, and database-link identity; missing, ambiguous, stale, remote, or incomplete resolution is Unknown and fails closed. Share the profile and generation scoped resolver with .82 view, synonym, and policy proof work. Until such proof exists, never call a potentially callable identifier proven read-only.

Guard tests

- Direct SELECT of bare, package, schema-package, quoted, synonym, default-argument, and database-link zero-argument functions refuses before caller SQL evaluation.
- DML and CALL or PL/SQL spellings cannot lower the required authority.
- Real columns, aliases, record fields, and pseudocolumns remain usable only when positively resolved as data.
- Catalog errors, stale generations, replacement, synonym changes, overload changes, and current-schema changes invalidate or refuse.
- Live autonomous logging function: refused query leaves the log unchanged.
- Streaming and non-streaming read paths use the identical resolved verdict.
notes: PARTIAL FIX LANDED (this session): qualified paren-less callables (SELECT hr.dangerous_fn FROM dual, pkg.member) now FAIL CLOSED on the served DEFAULT_CLASSIFIER via a token-scope heuristic (unresolved_qualified_calls + query_scope_qualifiers, flag guard_unresolved_qualified_calls, wired on the served gate). RESIDUAL: an UNqualified bare zero-arg function (SELECT run_ddl FROM dual) is still lexically indistinguishable from a column and needs exact catalog identity -> RES-4. Keep open until RES-4 closes the residual.
close_reason: Exact served catalog resolution now distinguishes real columns from unqualified zero-argument functions and refuses functions before evaluation on ordinary and streaming paths. Live autonomous-transaction witness stayed at zero on XE18, XE21, and FREE23; focused live-feature clippy passed.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: 2026-07-10 fresh verification/correction: the first token-global containment was unsound because schema parts, aliases from nested scopes, underlying names hidden by aliases, and later JOIN aliases could launder a paren-less callable. Replaced it with sqlparser AST visitor scopes that model WITH barriers, left-to-right JOIN activation, ordinary versus LATERAL/APPLY derived tables, implicit-lateral TABLE/JSON_TABLE/XMLTABLE, Oracle quoted-name folding, object/JSON relation prefixes, and FROM-side database-link normalization. Expression-level unquoted @dblink always fails closed, including unqualified remote identifiers, because live Oracle proved alias shadowing does not prevent remote function resolution. Free23 autonomous-function proof showed SELECT owner.sidefx FROM owner.target_row executed and inserted one audit row before containment; additional live probes covered CTE/inline/later-join alias laundering, quoted aliases, APPLY, JSON/object chains, and remote-link resolution. Fresh independent review: 39/39 semantic cases, guard 2/2, adversarial 10/10, served dispatch 2/2; full cargo test workspace, fmt, workspace clippy -D warnings, and cargo deny all pass. This is qualified/remote containment only: unqualified local bare zero-argument identifiers still require RES-4 catalog resolution, so .102 intentionally remains in_progress. Known remaining behavior is fail-closed over-restriction for complex nested JOIN/PIVOT, quoted remote syntax, and non-ASCII folding, not a safety bypass.

title: [QA100][P0][guard] Resolve SQL CALL targets before purity admission
description:
Finding

Oracle CALL target spellings are resolution-dependent. A two-part pkg.run name can denote a current-schema package or type member as well as a schema-qualified standalone routine, while bare names and synonyms depend on current schema and catalog state. The current SideEffectOracle accepts only ObjectRef with optional schema plus name and therefore cannot prove that a purity node is the routine Oracle will invoke.

Fresh verification

An independent completion review challenged the initial QA81 implementation that allowed CALL pkg.run when ObjectRef pkg.run returned ProvenReadOnly. Official Oracle CALL grammar and subprogram invocation rules permit standalone, packaged, type, schema-qualified, and synonym-resolved targets. The PlsqlSideEffectOracle graph lacks current-schema and object-kind context. A strongest-case AllProof test now confirms every CALL form remains Forbidden; no repo-local bead described exact CALL resolution.

Impact

A proof for an unrelated standalone function or package member could clear a different runtime target, allowing transitive DDL, ADMIN, autonomous, or external effects under READ_WRITE. This violates the profile ceiling and confirmation ladder.

Interim containment

QA81 forbids every explicit CALL even when the current purity oracle returns ProvenReadOnly. This bead is the only path to safely restore CALL admission.

Proposed fix

Add an active-profile and generation-scoped Oracle name resolver that returns a canonical owner, object kind, package or type, member, overload, quote semantics, synonym chain, and database-link status. Bind purity to that exact identity and invalidate proof on schema, synonym, overload, edition, or generation changes. Remote, ambiguous, stale, incomplete, or missing resolution remains Unknown and Forbidden.

Guard tests

Cover bare, schema, package, schema-package, type method, quoted, synonym, overload, default-argument, and database-link targets. Prove colliding schema and package names never share proof. Prove current-schema and synonym changes invalidate cached resolution. Verify argument expressions are independently resolved. Add a live pair of same-spelling routines where only one is read-only and show the wrong runtime target never executes.
notes: Still CONTAINED by bead .81/QA81 (every explicit CALL is Forbidden). Not a live fail-open. Safe restoration of CALL admission via exact ObjectIdentity is RES-5. Leave OPEN (tracks the restoration).
close_reason: Fresh verification confirms no live defect remains: QA81 already forbids every explicit CALL, including under an all-accepting two-field purity oracle. Exact identity is necessary but cannot prove arbitrary PL/SQL bodies/arguments pure in the engine-free binary, so blanket refusal is the correct safety design and is now documented.

title: [QA100][resolver] Profile+generation-scoped semantic catalog resolver (safe basis for .82/.102/.103)
description: Shared root cause of P0 beads .82 (unproven view/VPD side effects), .102 (paren-less zero-arg function identifiers) and .103 (CALL exact-identity). The engine-free served gate (DEFAULT_CLASSIFIER = UnknownOracle) cannot prove whether a base object hides autonomous/VPD writes, whether a qualified paren-less identifier is a column or a zero-arg function, or which routine a CALL resolves to. The correct fix is a CatalogResolver port (seam-symmetric with the existing SideEffectOracle) bound from the consumer side by the live all_* dictionary views, generation-scoped for cache invalidation, fail-closed on remote/ambiguous/stale/incomplete resolution. INTERIM CONTAINMENT ALREADY LANDED (this session): .102 qualified paren-less callables now fail closed on the served path; a served_strict() mechanism exists for .82 (opt-in). This epic tracks the durable resolver that closes the residuals and safely restores CALL admission. Most sub-beads need a LIVE DB (integration/live tests on 18c/21c/23ai).
notes: Completion compliance 2026-07-12: all six parent-child beads are closed and fresh execution re-proved the final missing surface. RES-6 deterministic multipart-callable regression passed; full oraclemcp-db suite passed (378 unit plus integration, trybuild, and docs); strict all-target DB clippy and public API baseline passed. The explicit ORACLEMCP_RESOLVER_ADVERSARIAL=1 corpus passed against local Oracle FREE 23ai and post-run DBA queries verified zero fixture-user and SYSTEM ORACLEMCP_RES6 object residue. Security disposition remains fail-closed: explicit CALL is not admitted without transitive PL/SQL body-purity proof.
close_reason: Verified complete after all six resolver children closed. Engine-free guard port, bounded live ALL_* resolver, exact session/profile/scope/generation cache keys, invalidation and served read integration are implemented and tested. Hidden-effect reads fail closed on views/VPD/remote/ambiguous/stale evidence; paren-less callable identity now resolves only when unshadowed and exact. Explicit CALL remains permanently refused because identity is not a transitive body-purity proof. Mock-free XE18/XE21/FREE23 smoke evidence and the fresh FREE23 adversarial corpus pass.

title: [QA100][resolver] RES-1: CatalogResolver port + Unresolved default (engine-free)
description: Introduce a CatalogResolver trait in oraclemcp-guard (engine-free) with a default impl returning Resolution::Unresolved (fail-closed). RawName carries per-part quote semantics + syntactic role (FROM-factor / value-position / call-with-args); ResolveCtx carries connected schema, CURRENT_SCHEMA, edition, roles, in-statement alias/CTE scope, and a monotonic generation stamp. Resolution = Resolved(owner, kind, package/type, member, overloads, quote_exact, synonym_chain, db_link, identity) | Ambiguous | Remote | Unresolved. Guard-crate-only; unit-testable. GATES RES-2..6.
close_reason: Implemented engine-free CatalogResolver port with fail-closed Unresolved default; exact quoted multipart RawName roles, DB-link syntax, complete session/statement ResolveCtx and monotonic generation; typed resolved/ambiguous/remote/unresolved outcomes retain object identity, package/type container, overload, synonym and quote evidence. Full guard suite 149 unit + 44 integration tests green; all-target strict clippy and focused formatting pass.

title: [QA100][resolver] RES-2: live dictionary resolver impl (needs DB)
description: Bind CatalogResolver from the consumer side over all_objects/all_synonyms/all_arguments/all_tab_columns with synonym-chain walking (cycle -> Unresolved), db-link detection (-> Remote, never proven), and overload resolution. Remote/ambiguous/stale/incomplete/missing -> Unresolved -> fail-closed. NEEDS LIVE DB; integration + live tests on 18c/21c/23ai. DEPENDS: RES-1.
close_reason: Implemented immutable consumer-side OracleCatalogResolver over bound/capped ALL_OBJECTS, ALL_SYNONYMS, ALL_ARGUMENTS, and ALL_TAB_COLUMNS lookups; exact quoted/unquoted semantics; session user/current schema/edition/enabled-role and statement-scope matching; bounded synonym walking with cycle/depth rejection and DB-link Remote evidence; callable overload/subprogram evidence; fail-closed context/generation drift, ambiguity, incomplete rows, invalid objects, missing names, and column conflicts. Added deterministic query-shape/negative tests and mock-free live tests passing on Oracle 18.4, 21.3, and 23ai. Strict DB clippy and API lock pass. Also refreshed the RES-1 guard API baseline omitted from 92742e7 together with the RES-2 DB API baseline.

title: [QA100][resolver] RES-3: generation stamp + invalidation wiring (needs DB)
description: Wire a monotonic generation u64 into the lane runtime, bumped on DDL / CREATE-DROP-REPLACE SYNONYM / package recompile / overload change / ALTER SESSION SET CURRENT_SCHEMA or EDITION / role change / reconnect. Resolver cache keyed by (generation, edition, resolving_schema, RawName, quote_exact); a generation mismatch is stale -> Unresolved -> fail-closed (never a soft hit). Negative caching allowed but generation-scoped. NEEDS LIVE DB; concurrency tests. DEPENDS: RES-1.
close_reason: Implemented a bounded per-lane resolver cache keyed by exact generation, connected/current schema, edition, roles, statement scope, and quote-preserving RawName. Positive and negative results are generation-scoped; stale publication races, poisoned locks, cache overflow, and generation exhaustion fail closed. Dispatcher invalidates before DDL/session-context wire calls and on compile/patch/profile reconnect. Unit concurrency/race/key-scope tests, dispatch bump guards, API lock, full DB suite/clippy, and live 18c/21c/23ai invalidation checks pass.

description: Bind the live resolver to the served DEFAULT_CLASSIFIER: replace the .102 qualified-paren-less HEURISTIC with exact-identity resolution (also closes the residual UNQUALIFIED bare zero-arg function case), and feed resolved base-object identities to SideEffectOracle::statement_purity so the trigger/VPD walk runs over real objects (closing .82's hidden-write fail-open and retiring the aggressive statement_unknown_guarded interim). NEEDS LIVE DB. DEPENDS: RES-2, RES-3. CLOSES .82; completes .102.

Exact relation/column identity plus policy/virtual-column checks fail closed on views, VPD, remote, ambiguous, stale, or unsupported scopes. Verified 752 unit tests, strict clippy/API lock, and live resolver checks on XE18/XE21/FREE23.

Retires the blanket CALL-forbid interim from bead .81/QA81. NEEDS LIVE DB; adversarial live corpus. DEPENDS: RES-2, RES-3. CLOSES .103.
close_reason: Superseded after fresh safety review: the engine-free server has exact CALL identity but no complete transitive PL/SQL body-purity proof. Restoring CALL admission would be unsafe; permanent blanket refusal closes QA103 without introducing a new execution path.

title: [QA100][resolver] RES-6: live adversarial resolver corpus (needs DB)
description: A live adversarial corpus: hidden-write views/VPD, paren-less package members, synonym-hidden functions, db-link objects, editioned overloads, colliding schema/package names, current-schema/synonym/overload changes invalidating cached resolution. NEEDS LIVE DB. DEPENDS: RES-4, RES-5.

The corpus creates an edition-enabled throwaway schema and proves hidden autonomous view/VPD functions are never executed, package/synonym zero-arg functions resolve by exact identity, remote synonyms remain Remote, schema/package collisions remain Ambiguous, and synonym/overload/CURRENT_SCHEMA changes invalidate stale cache evidence. Fresh-eyes corpus found and fixed unconditional relation-qualification that blocked every unshadowed multipart value callable; deterministic regression plus 378 DB units, integration/compile-fail/docs, strict clippy, API lock, fmt, and residue-free live run pass.

title: [QA100][P1][streaming] Forward true streaming through MetricsDispatch
description:
Finding

MetricsDispatch implements ordinary dispatch, close, and surface-state forwarding, but does not override dispatch_stream. The ToolDispatch default silently calls ordinary dispatch and emits no incremental frames.

Fresh verification

Served HTTP enables MetricsDispatch in normal production wiring. K10 row-by-row streaming reaches this wrapper, so streaming=true is converted back into the buffered non-stream path before OracleDispatcher sees dispatch_stream. Tracker searches for MetricsDispatch and dispatch_stream found no duplicate; the closed K10 beads assume the wrapper preserves the stream contract.

Impact

True row-by-row SSE streaming is silently disabled on served HTTP whenever metrics are enabled, increasing latency and memory use and contradicting the advertised streaming behavior. Cancellation/backpressure coverage also exercises a different path than production.

Proposed fix

Override dispatch_stream in MetricsDispatch and forward the frame sender to the inner dispatcher while applying the same bounded metrics accounting used by ordinary dispatch. Keep dispatch_stream_start behavior delegated through the lane runtime. Do not duplicate query execution or buffer frames in the metrics layer.

Guard tests

Wrap a stream-aware recording dispatcher in MetricsDispatch, send multiple frames, and prove their order/content and terminal outcome are preserved. Assert exactly one duration/status observation. Add a served HTTP SSE regression with metrics enabled proving the first row frame arrives before final completion and cancellation reaches the lane.
notes: Fresh verification confirmed production HTTP dispatchers are wrapped by MetricsDispatch inside LaneRuntime, whose streaming command invokes ToolDispatch::dispatch_stream. Without an override, the trait default called buffered dispatch and emitted no frames. MetricsDispatch now forwards the exact ToolStreamSender and terminal outcome to the inner stream-aware dispatcher while sharing the same exactly-once status, blocked-label, and duration accounting as ordinary dispatch. Guards prove two ordered row frames are observable before terminal completion, buffered dispatch is never called, lane/subject metrics record exactly once, and mid-flight cancellation reaches the inner lane and records one cancelled outcome. Focused tests 2/2 and the full combined workspace gate passed: clippy -D warnings, all workspace tests/trybuild/integrations/goldens/docs, fmt, diff check, API lock, and cargo deny.
close_reason: Confirmed and fixed: MetricsDispatch now preserves true row streaming, cancellation, terminal outcomes, and exactly-once metrics.

title: [QA100][P1][quarantine] Never reuse uncertain pinned read sessions
description: Finding: ordinary oracle_query, chunk/export/custom reads, and generated reads turn DbError::Cancelled or connection ambiguity into an envelope but retain the same pinned physical session. True streaming already quarantines, while OraclePool correctly dirty-discards only the failed checkout. Impact: a protocol-uncertain session can serve later calls, violating the closed B1c never-reuse contract. Proposed fix: centralize ownership-aware uncertainty handling; quarantine retained pinned connections as UnknownDiscarded, but let pool-backed stateless reads rely on per-checkout dirty discard instead of poisoning the whole dispatcher. Guard tests: fail-once pinned raw and generated reads refuse the next call without DB I/O; pooled Cancelled discards only that checkout and a fresh read succeeds; deterministic ORA-00942 remains usable.
close_reason: Confirmed and fixed. Central ReadUncertaintyConn now observes all ordinary, chunk/export/flashback, custom named-bind, and generated read methods at the physical ownership boundary. Uncertain errors quarantine retained primary sessions as UnknownDiscarded before reuse; distinct stateless pool workers rely on checkout dirty-discard and do not poison the active primary. Deterministic ORA-00942 remains reusable. Added four dispatch regressions covering pinned raw/generated refusal without second DB I/O, ordinary SQL reuse, and stateless isolation. Green: focused 4/4; workspace fmt and diff; workspace Clippy -D warnings; cargo deny; full workspace suite including 229 oraclemcp lib, 83 binary, 448 core, 318 DB, compile-fail, integration, golden, and doc tests.

title: [QA100][P1][describe] Propagate uncertain best-effort describe probes
description: Finding: RustOracleConnection::query_first_row uses await.ok(), so all seven describe probes erase cancellation, connection loss, deadline expiry, and protocol uncertainty as missing metadata. Several dispatcher describe paths likewise downgrade uncertainty. Impact: mutation evidence, connection-info, surface inspection, and profile-switch preparation can reuse or install an uncertain session. Proposed fix: return Result<Option<OracleRow>, DbError>; degrade only proven ordinary privilege/query errors, propagate uncertain failures, and apply ownership-aware quarantine. Candidate profile failures must discard the candidate without poisoning the active session. Guard tests: Cancelled and ORA-03113 propagate; ORA-00942 degrades; mutation executes zero statements; uncertain switch candidate is never installed.
close_reason: Confirmed: all seven driver describe probes erased cancellation/connection uncertainty, and dispatcher paths could reuse a retained uncertain session or install an uncertain switch candidate. Fixed query_first_row to preserve Result, degraded only ordinary Query failures, propagated structural/uncertain failures, added ownership-aware dispatcher observation/quarantine, and kept switch-candidate uncertainty isolated from the active session. Guard tests cover cancellation, ORA-03113, ordinary ORA-00942 degradation, non-query adapter failures, retained-primary quarantine/retry refusal, and candidate rejection without active-session poisoning. Full pinned fmt/clippy/workspace-test/deny gate passed.

title: [QA100][P1][flashback] Quarantine failed AS-OF teardown
description: Finding: read_query_as_of discards DBMS_FLASHBACK.DISABLE failure whenever the read also failed and unconditionally ignores the final rollback result. Impact: a pinned session can be returned while flashback or stale transaction state remains active, contradicting the shipped always-torn-down contract. Proposed fix: preserve the primary diagnostic as context but make any disable or final rollback failure structural UnknownDiscarded/Quarantined so the physical session cannot be reused; cleanup stays freshly bounded. Guard tests: read-error plus disable-error quarantines; success plus rollback-error quarantines; successful disable and rollback preserve the original read result; subsequent use is refused.
notes: Fresh verification confirmed read_query_as_of returned the primary read error while discarding a simultaneous DBMS_FLASHBACK.DISABLE failure, and always ignored the final rollback result. The fix always evaluates both teardown operations; any DISABLE or rollback failure returns structural DbError::Quarantined(UnknownDiscarded) with the primary read outcome retained as context. The thin adapter now quarantines failed rollback/Flashback cleanup by dropping the physical driver connection and retaining a structural reason, so later ping/query cannot reuse it. Guards cover exact read-error preservation on clean teardown, read error plus DISABLE failure with rollback still attempted, successful read plus final rollback failure, and subsequent thin-session refusal. Full DB lib 314/314 and the combined full workspace clippy/tests/trybuild/integrations/goldens/docs passed; fmt, diff check, API lock, and cargo deny green.
close_reason: Confirmed and fixed: failed AS-OF teardown is UnknownDiscarded and the physical thin session is permanently refused.

title: [QA100][P1][top-sql] Preserve uncertainty in historical source detection
description: Finding: historical resolve_top_sql_source uses best-effort boolean probes with unwrap_or(false), so cancellation or connection failure during Diagnostics Pack detection is relabeled not licensed and followed by another query on the uncertain session. Impact: protocol-uncertain sessions are reused and diagnostics-source reporting becomes false. Proposed fix: make the source resolver use uncertainty-preserving Result probes; only deterministic privilege or absence results may select fallback, while uncertain errors stop probing and reach ownership-aware quarantine. Guard tests: cancelled first probe performs no Statspack fallback; deterministic unlicensed state falls back; pooled and pinned ownership behavior remains distinct.
close_reason: Confirmed: historical top-SQL source detection relabelled cancellation/connection loss as unlicensed and then probed Statspack on the same uncertain session. resolve_top_sql_source now returns Result, uses strict probes that degrade only ORA-00942/01031, propagates arbitrary and structurally uncertain failures, and short-circuits licensed AWR and nonhistorical live mode. Dispatcher maps the error through ownership-aware quarantine. Guards prove first-probe cancellation/disconnect stops fallback, ordinary privilege absence falls back, arbitrary ORA-00600 propagates, licensed/live paths avoid needless probes, and a pinned uncertain session is quarantined before retry. Full pinned fmt/clippy/workspace-test/API-lock/deny gate passed.

title: [QA100][P2][auth] Remove durable full-store writes from every bearer request
description:
Finding

Every successful service-owned client bearer authentication rewrites and fsyncs the entire clients.json file before the request reaches its per-principal rate limiter. Authentication is therefore a serialized durable-disk operation on the hot path, including for requests that are subsequently rejected with 429.

Evidence

- crates/oraclemcp-core/src/client_credentials.rs:335-386 holds the store mutex, validates the bearer, updates last_used_at and last_source_addr, then calls persist_locked for every valid request.
- persist_file at lines 455-505 pretty-serializes the complete client collection and calls FileStore::write_root_atomic.
- crates/oraclemcp-core/src/file_store.rs:271-293 creates a temporary file, writes all bytes, sync_all, renames it, then fsyncs the root directory.
- crates/oraclemcp-core/src/http/mod.rs:1353-1375 authenticates service client credentials; operator and MCP route flows call that before try_admit_http_request_rate at lines 1763-1772 and 5058/5109/5239 respectively.
- Defaults permit 600 requests per second with a 1200-request burst, but this cost occurs before that protection.
- Tests assert last-use metadata changes but do not bound write frequency, prove coalescing, or cover persistence failure after otherwise valid authentication.
- A full unlimited search of open, closed, and deferred beads found only the original credential-store and rate-limiter feature beads, not this interaction defect.

Impact

One valid noisy client can serialize all credential authentication behind full-file durable writes, amplify disk wear and latency as client count grows, and turn disk pressure or a transient write failure into 503 responses for every otherwise valid request. The advertised rate limiter cannot protect the expensive step because it runs later. This is an authenticated availability weakness and a normal-load scalability defect.

False-positive checks

Durably recording credential issuance, rotation, and revocation is intentional. Last-use telemetry does not need per-request durability to preserve authentication or immediate revocation semantics. Even multiple requests within the same timestamp second rewrite identical metadata, so this is not limited to genuine metadata changes.

Proposed fix

Keep bearer verification and revocation/generation reads synchronous and in-memory. Coalesce last-use/source metadata through a bounded asynchronous updater, such as at most once per client per minute, with an explicit shutdown flush and nonfatal telemetry-write policy. Where identity can be verified cheaply, apply the per-principal limiter before optional durable bookkeeping. Do not weaken immediate credential rotation/revocation persistence.

Guard tests

1. Count store writes: a large valid-request burst causes at most the documented coalesced number, including requests rejected by the limiter.
2. Concurrent authentication for one and many clients remains correct while writes are bounded and mutex hold time stays small.
3. A last-use telemetry persistence failure does not convert every valid credential request into 503 under the chosen fail-safe policy.
4. Rotation and revocation remain immediately durable and invalidate old credentials and active lanes.
5. Restart/shutdown tests document the permitted staleness of last-used metadata without exposing bearer values.
close_reason: Remove the durable full-store write from the bearer auth hot path. authenticate_bearer persisted last_used_at/last_source_addr via a full fsync+rename+dir-fsync on EVERY successful bearer auth, so an authenticated client hammering the endpoint forced an fsync per request (I/O DoS + flash-wear amplifier). Fix = coalesce the last-use write: should_persist_last_use() persists only when there is no prior timestamp (first use) or now has advanced >= LAST_USED_COALESCE_WINDOW_SECS (60s) past the stored one (a future stored ts from clock rewind also persists to correct it); otherwise the durable write is skipped. This bounds the durable write to at most one per minute per client, fully defusing the write-amplification regardless of request rate. Pure decision fn unit-tested with synthetic timestamps (no clock seam needed). NOTE: the bead also suggested reordering the rate-limit check before the durable write; that is subsumed by coalescing (an attacker can no longer force more than ~1 durable write/min/client no matter the ordering), so I did not restructure the http call sites — surfaced here rather than silently. Green: core client_credentials 10/0, fmt + clippy clean.

title: [QA100][P1][compile] Report partial persistent compile-session effects
description: Finding: compile_object_inner may successfully ALTER SESSION PLSQL_WARNINGS or PLSCOPE_SETTINGS, then receive a definite error on a later setup or compile statement and record plain Failed/write-intent Failed. Those earlier session changes persist despite rollback. Impact: audit and retry guidance claim no effect while the lane has changed policy. Proposed fix: track every completed sub-effect, restore prior session settings under fresh bounded cleanup when possible, and otherwise report a non-retryable partial persistent effect with truthful audit/intent state and quarantine if restoration is uncertain. Guard tests: first setup succeeds and second fails; no plain no-effect Failed is recorded, restoration is verified, and uncertain restoration prevents reuse.
close_reason: Confirmed against live Oracle: separate ALTER SESSION warning/PLScope setup persisted after a later definite compile error, while audit/write-intent reported plain Failed. Fixed at the root by emitting one unit-local ALTER ... COMPILE with explicit PLSQL_WARNINGS/PLSCOPE_SETTINGS clauses and REUSE SETTINGS, so surrounding session settings never change. VIEW rejects PL/SQL-only options. Guards cover one-statement builder shapes, package/type bodies, view refusal, exact preview/execute SQL, and a deterministic ORA-04043 path with exactly one DB effect, Pending-to-Failed audit, resolved FAILED intent, no quarantine, and reusable connection. Registry, README, API lock, and reviewed golden were updated. Full pinned fmt/clippy/workspace-test/API-lock/deny gate passed.

title: [QA100][P1][backstop] Clear read-only transactions before governed writes
description: Finding: ReadOnlyBackstop::disarm only flips an in-memory boolean. After SET TRANSACTION READ ONLY and a successful query, later elevation can route DML onto the same still-read-only Oracle transaction, yielding ORA-01456 even though the governed write is authorized. Impact: the operating ladder fails after ordinary reads and retry behavior is confusing; uncertain cleanup could also reuse bad state. Proposed fix: before the first governed write, perform a fresh bounded rollback that actually ends the read-only transaction, then disarm; quarantine on uncertain rollback and avoid auto-commit. Guard tests: read then elevate then DML succeeds under rollback-by-default; rollback failure prevents execute and reuse; live Oracle verifies transaction transition.
notes: Fresh verification confirmed Oracle SET TRANSACTION READ ONLY persists until COMMIT/ROLLBACK, so the previous boolean-only disarm left the pinned session in a real read-only transaction and authorized DML could fail with ORA-01456. The fix performs a fresh bounded cancellation-masked rollback at the exact post-gate/post-confirmation and pre-effect boundary; only success disarms. Failure leaves the tracker armed, records UnknownDiscarded quarantine, prevents the approved statement from reaching Oracle, and refuses later reuse. execute_sql covers oracle_execute/execute_approved/deploy/create-or-replace; compile and patch paths clear explicitly. Previews and classifier refusals do not clear the transaction. Guards cover read-elevate-DML success with rollback-by-default, DDL preview/confirmed compile ordering, rollback failure/no execute/quarantine/retry refusal, and refused writes preserving the arm. Focused 13/13 and the full workspace clippy/tests/trybuild/integrations/goldens/docs passed; fmt, diff check, API lock, cargo deny green.
close_reason: Confirmed and fixed: governed writes now end the real read-only transaction safely before effects, quarantining failed transitions.

title: [QA100][P1][lanes] Prevent creation after session lifecycle closure
description: Finding: stateful lane creation occurs outside the registry lock.
