title: Arc M: ensure non-licensed row-scoping via the server-seam predicate (M4 deferral must not make row-scoping licensed-only)
description: GAP found in the alien-plan completeness audit. PLAN_0_8_x_ALIEN.md:288-289 promises row scoping via EITHER Oracle-native VPD/RLS OR a seam-applied predicate on the proven read so a principal never receives rows outside its policy. The M arc beaded only M1 column masking (.4.2, closed) and M4 the LICENSED native VPD/RLS tier (.4.5, deferred). With M4 deferred there is no dedicated bead ensuring the NON-licensed seam-applied row predicate is wired end-to-end; today it exists only implicitly via Arc N RequirePredicate (.5.4, closed). WHY IT MATTERS: deferring the licensed tier must not silently make row-scoping licensed-only — the server seam must still enforce row policy on an unlicensed DB, exactly as the server egress masker substitutes for licensed DBMS_REDACT. CHANGE: verify RequirePredicate actually applies the policy predicate to the proven read on the egress path and is enforced fail-closed; if the wiring or a test is missing, add it. ACCEPTANCE: on an unlicensed gvenzl DB, a RequirePredicate policy causes a principal to never receive out-of-policy rows; unit + live proof; guard re-checks at apply (SEC-1); documented as the non-licensed row-scoping path. NON-GOAL: the licensed native VPD/RLS tier (stays deferred in .4.5).
close_reason: Verified + documented (commit 3da0da8). VERDICT: the non-licensed seam-applied row predicate IS already wired end-to-end and enforced fail-closed; no code change was needed, so this bead's suspicion of a wiring/test hole did NOT reproduce. EVIDENCE (all re-run, not read): (1) WIRING - oracle_query executes PolicyGateAdmission::effective_sql when a RequirePredicate rewrite produced a candidate (dispatch/mod.rs:13962) and re-classifies the rendered candidate via ensure_resolved_read_only before it runs (SEC-1 re-check at apply). The rewrite is AST-based (Parser::parse_sql + AST insert), never text splicing; every shape it cannot prove it can scope (alias/join/CTE/derived) denies as UnsupportedStatementShape rather than running unscoped. (2) UNIT PROOF - a_predicate_rule_rewrites_the_read_that_reaches_the_database (dispatch/tests.rs) + policy_predicate_rewrite_refuses_aliases_instead_of_guessing_target_placement (policy.rs); cargo test -p oraclemcp-guard green (306 passed + all suites). (3) LIVE PROOF on the UNLICENSED gvenzl 23ai lab (scripts/e2e/sql_policy.sh, lane free23) re-run green this session: step narrow_reclassifies_and_proves_on_wire => candidate_digest_differs=true, returned_tenant_count=1 (tenant-8 rows never reach the principal), matched_rule_count=2; step policy_evaluation_failure_refuses => POLICY_DENIED/unresolved_policy_target for the alias case, i.e. a typed refusal, never a fall-open read of both tenants; deny/base-refusal/invalid-policy-startup all pass. So row scoping demonstrably works with NO Oracle licence. THE REAL GAP WAS DOCUMENTARY: ADR-0008 already states the licensed DBMS_REDACT tier does not replace the masking seam, but ADR-0009 (which owns RequirePredicate) never said the same for row scoping and never mentioned licensing at all -- a reader could reasonably conclude deferring M4 left row-scoping licensed-only. ADR-0009 now records that RequirePredicate IS the row-scoping path, is licence-free, that native VPD/RLS is an optional accelerator that does not replace the server seam, and that an unprovable rewrite is a denial not a fall-open read. NON-GOAL respected: licensed native VPD/RLS tier stays deferred in .4.5. Gates: guard tests green, dispatch wiring test green, oraclemcp_honesty_grep.sh OK.

title: Confirm/fix the file_store OS-lock flake left as an oral caveat
description: Retrospective (STATUS_REPORT.md:450): a file_store OS-lock flake lingered as an unfiled oral caveat, unlike edso/shipping_spool which were fixed. The file-store CONTRACT bead f4xo.7.8 is closed, so this may be residual. CHANGE: reproduce (loop the file_store lock test under load), determine if the intermittent OS-lock still fires; if it does, fix it (lock ordering/timeout/retry); if it does not, record the negative result and close. ACCEPTANCE: either a fix with a stress test that no longer flakes, or a durable negative-repro result; no lingering oral caveat.
close_reason: Durable negative reproduction: existing service_lock_recovers_after_holder_process_is_killed passed 50 sequential invocations (10 crash/reacquire cycles each = 500 cycles) plus four concurrent invocations (40 additional cycles) with zero OS-lock failures. Scoped cargo check -p oraclemcp-core and cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --lib both passed (643 tests). Existing crash-recovery coverage remains sufficient; no locking change warranted.

title: Flaky test: shutdown wait_does_not_lose_wakeup_under_signal_race times out under CI load
description: Observed once on CI (feature powerset job, dashboard-bundle+default combo on oraclemcp-core): the test panicked with 'asupersync test future did not complete within 5s' at crates/oraclemcp-core/src/shutdown.rs:135, then passed on rerun of the same commit (28691901965) - a load-induced flake, not a regression (the failing commit only bumped the oracledb driver pins; shutdown.rs untouched). Options: raise the 5s harness budget for this test, make the signal-race deterministic (injected waker instead of wall-clock), or mark it serial if it contends with parallel tests for signal delivery. Do NOT weaken the lost-wakeup assertion itself - it pins a real invariant.
close_reason: Fixed deterministically: root cause is that a genuine lost wakeup parks asupersync block_on forever (no runtime deadlock detection), so the 5s recv_timeout was the only detector and the 1000-iteration probabilistic loop raced that wall clock under CI load. Rewrote wait_does_not_lose_wakeup_under_signal_race to drive wait_for_shutdown() by hand with a controlled wake-counting Waker (no runtime, no spawn, no timeout): interleaving 1 parks the waiter then fires begin_shutdown and asserts the wake edge was delivered (count>=1) and the future completes; interleaving 2 signals before first poll and asserts immediate completion. Stronger than the old loop (observes the wake edge directly) and has no wall clock to lose. Also bumped the shared harness backstop 5s to 30s with justification (hang-detector for the remaining spawn-based tests, not a perf bound). Invariant not weakened. Proof: 200/200 release runs of the exact test passed, 0 failures. Gates green: fmt, clippy -D warnings, cargo test --workspace 0 failed.

title: Fail closed on symlinked or hard-linked FileStore service locks
description: Fresh cross-review finding. crates/oraclemcp-core/src/file_store.rs: open_private_lock_file opens root/.service.lock with OpenOptions create(true) and follows existing links; acquire_service_owner then set_len(0) rewrites it after locking. FileStore::open validates the root directory but does not authenticate the lock sidecar itself. A same-UID actor that can alter the state directory can replace .service.lock with a symlink or hard link to another writable file, producing an arbitrary truncate/write instead of a safe Locked/UnsafePath refusal. Implement an atomic no-follow lock open (preserving #![forbid(unsafe_code)]), reject non-regular and Unix multi-link existing sidecars, avoid TOCTOU between inspection and open, and add adversarial symlink/hard-link plus race tests. Do not weaken crash-recovery or replacement-lock semantics.
close_reason:
FIXED in 8c26968. Confirmed the attack was REAL, then proved the fix load-bearing by mutation.

ATTACK: open_private_lock_file used create(true) and followed links; acquire_service_owner then set_len(0)s that descriptor. FileStore::open validates the root dir but never authenticated the sidecar. A same-UID actor able to write the state dir could plant .service.lock as a symlink or hard link and convert lock acquisition into an arbitrary truncate+write of any file this UID can write.

FIX — two defences, because they stop DIFFERENT attacks:
1. O_NOFOLLOW on the open refuses a symlinked sidecar IN THE OPEN ITSELF. Atomicity is the point: inspect-then-open leaves a swap window (the TOCTOU the bead called out).
2. authenticate_lock_file rejects a sidecar that is not a lone regular file, by fstat-ing THE DESCRIPTOR WE HOLD (file.metadata()), never the path — so there is no inspection/use window. Catches what O_NOFOLLOW cannot: a hard link (shares the victim inode, so truncating ours truncates theirs) and a FIFO/device (diverts the write). Post-open link races give the attacker nothing: the inode is then our own lock content.
Both run BEFORE try_lock and before set_len(0), i.e. while refusal is still harmless.

ERROR SURFACE: a refused open is UnsafePath, not Io. ELOOP here is an attack signal; burying it in transient-io noise would hide it. Reused the existing UnsafePath variant rather than widening the enum.

UNSAFE POSTURE: OpenOptionsExt::custom_flags is a SAFE API — #![forbid(unsafe_code)] holds, zero unsafe blocks added. libc is unix-gated ([target.'cfg(unix)'.dependencies]) because O_NOFOLLOW/ELOOP values are platform-specific and hardcoding them is exactly the fragility to avoid; libc 0.2.186 was ALREADY in the graph transitively, so Cargo.lock grew by ONE line and no package was added. cargo deny: advisories/bans/licenses/sources ok.

TESTS (4 new, unix-gated): symlink, hard link, FIFO, and a concurrent symlink-swap race. Each asserts the VICTIM'S BYTES SURVIVE — not merely that an error was returned. Refusing with the right error while still truncating would otherwise be a passing test and a live bug. The race test tolerates either outcome (genuine lock or refusal) but never a victim write.

MUTATION-VERIFIED (tests are not vacuous): reverting either defence makes all 3 refusal tests FAIL, and they fail at the Ok(...) branch — i.e. the lock was acquired THROUGH the link and set_len(0) truncated the victim. That is the vulnerability reproduced on demand, and the tests catch it. Fix restored and re-verified afterwards.

NOT WEAKENED: crash-recovery, replacement-lock identity, and partial-init release semantics untouched — service_lock_recovers_after_holder_process_is_killed, old_lock_drop_does_not_unlink_replacement_lock, and service_lock_initialization_failure_releases_os_lock all still pass (15/15 file_store tests green).

WINDOWS: O_NOFOLLOW has no equivalent; the unix-gated flag is skipped there, but the not-a-regular-file check still applies and Windows symlink creation requires privilege by default.

GATE (scoped): cargo fmt -p oraclemcp-core clean; clippy -p oraclemcp-core --all-targets -D warnings = 0 warnings; cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --lib file_store = 15 passed; cargo deny check ok.

title: shipping_spool shutdown lost-wakeup: worker join hangs forever
description: Production bug found via a 75-minute Windows CI timeout (run 29713554457, commit 835080b, test a_spool_refuses_a_second_concurrent_worker). DurableShippingForwarder::shutdown() stored the stopping flag (atomic, outside the queue mutex) and called wake.notify_all() without acquiring the queue mutex, while run_worker evaluates the stopping flag under that mutex immediately before parking in wake.wait. Both the store and the notify could land inside the worker's check-to-park window: the notify finds no parked waiter, the worker parks with an empty queue and no future notifies, and worker.join() in shutdown blocks forever — hanging any real service shutdown, not just the test. Windows runners widen the window (slow thread scheduling) but the race is platform-independent. Fix: bridge the store and the notify with the queue mutex (drop(queue.lock()) between them) so the notify always lands after the worker has either parked or re-checked the flag. wait_retry has the same window but is bounded by wait_for's delay. Verified: 237 oraclemcp-audit tests green, 30x shipping_spool hammer green, clippy/fmt/concurrency-lint clean.
close_reason: Fixed in the same commit that files this bead: shutdown() now takes and releases the queue mutex between setting the stopping flag and notify_all, closing the lost-wakeup window. Evidence: 237 audit tests + 30x spool hammer green locally; Windows CI lane green on the fix commit.

title: Program: thin-native Asupersync oraclemcp migration
description: Program epic for replacing oraclemcp's thick ODPI-C Oracle backend, Tokio runtime, rmcp SDK transport, Axum, and Hyper with a pure-Rust thin Oracle driver, native Asupersync runtime semantics, and native MCP transports. The safety invariant is non-negotiable: raw SQL must remain fail-closed through oraclemcp-guard before any database execution, async gap, connection acquisition, or mutable execution state. This epic is complete only when the dependency graph below is implemented, tested, release-gated, and documented.
design: User value: oraclemcp becomes a smaller, easier-to-install, pure-Rust Oracle MCP server with no Instant Client dependency, while preserving the fail-closed read-only SQL safety model. Architecture decision: this is a hard migration, not a compat layer. Final production dependencies must not include Tokio, rmcp, axum, hyper, hyper-util, oracle, odpic-sys, r2d2, or asupersync-tokio-compat. The migration is complete only after tests, dependency gates, docs, release artifacts, and registry automation all agree on the thin-native architecture.
acceptance_criteria: All child migration beads are closed; production builds are thin-only and native Asupersync; forbidden dependency gate passes; release artifacts and docs describe thin-native behavior; no credential, PII, or sensitive business data leaks through logs, fixtures, errors, panic paths, or generated artifacts.
notes: Global invariants for all child beads: never introduce unsafe; never weaken oraclemcp-guard; classify raw SQL before await/connection/lease/mutable execution state; keep session leases, rollback semantics, NUMBER/string output, NLS-stable serialization, AWR/ASH license gating, and preview-token single-use behavior; no secrets, PII, or sensitive business data in logs, fixtures, doctor, errors, panic paths, or generated artifacts.
close_reason: All child migration beads and epics are closed. Production builds are thin-only on published oracledb and native Asupersync, with no Tokio/rmcp/Axum/Hyper/oracle/ODPI-C/r2d2 production dependencies. Guard-before-I/O, session cleanup, native MCP stdio/HTTP, OAuth scope enforcement, release artifacts, CI dependency gates, performance evidence, Docker smoke, MCP registry smoke, and sensitive-data checks are implemented and verified locally plus CI run 27570414583 on 7f70f58.

title: Epic: thin-only Oracle driver and live DB behavior
description: Tracks the database half of the migration: behavior inventory, published oracledb dependency decision, thin adapter, session leases, type fidelity, auth coverage, doctor diagnostics, and live DB behavior. The goal is a default install with no Instant Client, ODPI-C, r2d2, or C Oracle connectivity requirement while preserving current production-proven database semantics.
design: This epic owns the DB compatibility contract. The user benefit is removing native Oracle client installation and Docker weight without breaking existing production-facing Oracle behavior. Work under this epic must treat the current thick adapter as the behavioral baseline until the W0 inventory proves otherwise. Any unsupported thin feature must fail explicitly rather than falling through to password auth, silently dropping a profile field, or returning a misleading generic connection error.
acceptance_criteria: Behavior inventory, oracledb dependency decision, thin adapter, thin doctor/docs, and DB live tests are complete; unsupported auth modes fail explicitly; session leases, rollback, NUMBER/string fidelity, NLS-stable output, AWR/ASH gating, and credential redaction are preserved.
notes: Child scope: W0 behavior inventory, W3 oracledb dependency decision, W4 thin adapter, W5 doctor/docs. Closure requires live DB evidence where available and explicit skips where not. Pay special attention to proxy auth, ADB wallet/TCPS/SNI, IAM token refresh, wallet passwords, external auth, DRCP/non-homogeneous pools, session identity, edition, login/trusted statements, standby/read-only profile caps, DBMS_OUTPUT, savepoints, and PLAN_TABLE behavior.
close_reason: All DB migration children are closed: behavior inventory, published oracledb dependency decision, thin adapter, thin doctor/docs, and live/skip DB evidence. Default build requires no Instant Client/ODPI-C/r2d2; unsupported auth modes fail explicitly; session leases, rollback, type fidelity, NLS-stable output, AWR/ASH gating, PLAN_TABLE behavior, and credential redaction are covered by tests and docs.

title: W3.5: resolve explain-plan PLAN_TABLE semantics
description: W0 evidence shows oracle_explain_plan gates the inner SQL as read-only and then runs EXPLAIN PLAN FOR, which writes PLAN_TABLE on primary databases while standby mode refuses that path. Resolve the product contract before the thin adapter lands: either keep this as an explicitly privileged/write-like diagnostic path with separate guard semantics, replace it with a non-writing explain/cursor-based implementation where possible, or document and gate it so the fail-closed read-only invariant remains true to users.
design: Implementation shape: decide and implement the explicit oracle_explain_plan contract before W4 replaces the DB adapter. Preserve the global invariant that raw user SQL is classified before any database execution, and ensure the tool name, operating-level requirement, audit text, docs, and tests match the real Oracle behavior. Prefer a non-writing cursor/display route if the thin driver and Oracle privileges support it; otherwise gate PLAN_TABLE writes as a distinct diagnostic capability instead of presenting them as ordinary read-only execution.
acceptance_criteria: A test proves oracle_explain_plan behavior does not contradict the documented read-only invariant; primary database behavior and read-only standby behavior are both specified; PLAN_TABLE writes are either eliminated or explicitly gated/documented as diagnostic writes; guard/adversarial tests stay fail-closed for raw EXPLAIN PLAN statements; W4 thin adapter cannot start until this bead is resolved.
notes: Discovered during W0 inventory from crates/oraclemcp-db/src/intelligence.rs and guard adversarial corpus. This bead blocks W4 so the thin adapter does not preserve an accidental PLAN_TABLE write path by default. Credential/logging: explain-plan fixtures must use synthetic SQL and must not include customer schema names, bind values, or Oracle connection details.
close_reason: Resolved oracle_explain_plan PLAN_TABLE semantics: inner SQL remains fail-closed read-only, raw EXPLAIN PLAN stays guarded, the tool now refuses by default and on read-only standby, requires READ_WRITE plus allow_plan_table_write=true before DB access, advertises the diagnostic write/destructive surface, and documents the contract. Verified fmt check, clippy, workspace tests, cargo deny, diff check, focused explain-plan/registry/prompt/golden/guard tests, and secret/diff scans.

title: P2-7 follow-up: key HTTP instance lock by XDG state directory
description: P2-7 inspection found the premise false: default_service_instance_lock_path() uses default_service_runtime_dir(), which resolves XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/oraclemcp (or a /tmp fallback), not the XDG state root. Therefore separate XDG_STATE_HOME values do not permit independent served listeners, and documentation must not claim they do. Make the instance-ownership key explicitly follow the service state root, or revise the product contract and multi-instance recipe together.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Two served listeners with distinct XDG_STATE_HOME values and distinct ports can run concurrently. 2. A second listener sharing the same state root still fails closed with ORACLEMCP_SERVICE_ALREADY_RUNNING and reports the holder metadata. 3. Docs describe the real scoping key and the separate-state multi-instance recipe.
close_reason: Verified state-root instance ownership and the isolated-state listener recipe. [closing=a9d9672c10b519a6f578b5f1dc232e1fb3258dd8 source=5259fba508886109837c2094c25d6f6b2183596c evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-tlo0b.json]

title: Workbench Classifier-Verdict badge shows green PASS for a blocked or step-up preview (client-derived over-trust)
description:
HONESTY VIOLATION (over-trust), found in the dashboard security/honesty review pass.

WHERE
- web/src/app/App.tsx:8434-8439 workbenchVerdictFromResponse computes `refused` by a fuzzy string match over result[decision] and result[danger] ONLY: decisionText.includes(refus|block|deny) OR dangerText === refused|blocked. It never inspects gate_decision.
- web/src/app/App.tsx:8428 reads decision = result[decision] ?? result[outcome]. Preview and denied bodies carry NO top-level decision or outcome key.
- web/src/app/App.tsx:8472 and 8487 WorkbenchVerdictBlock renders Badge PASS with the green (ok) tone whenever refused is false, and never renders gate_decision.

BACKEND REALITY
- DangerLevel serializes SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE (crates/oraclemcp-guard/src/levels.rs:87): SAFE, GUARDED, DESTRUCTIVE, FORBIDDEN. None of these equal refused or blocked, and DESTRUCTIVE/FORBIDDEN do not contain the substrings refus, block, or deny.
- The real block signal rides on gate_decision with value blocked or require_step_up, alongside danger and blocked_reason (crates/oraclemcp/src/dispatch/mod.rs:10438, 10757-10763, 10434-10441). Preview bodies have no top-level decision/outcome.

TRIGGER
Open the Workbench, enter a DESTRUCTIVE or FORBIDDEN or policy-denied statement (for example a table-drop DDL against hr.employees, or a CREATE OR REPLACE on an existing object, or a statement a protected profile or policy floor blocks), and preview it. The operator action succeeds as a preview (state success); its body has danger DESTRUCTIVE or FORBIDDEN and gate_decision blocked (or require_step_up) but no top-level decision. So `refused` computes to false, and the Classifier Verdict badge renders a green PASS for a statement the backend actually blocked or has not admitted. The prominent GO/NO-GO-style trust signal is wrong; the true gate_decision is present on the wire but simply not rendered by this component.

SCOPE / SEVERITY
Display-honesty bug, not a privilege bypass: the backend still refuses execution (SEC-1 re-classify at apply is intact; the execute-time hard refusal returns an ErrorEnvelope and yields state refused, which does NOT render this block). Severity HIGH because it is a client-side-derived trust signal that renders more-trusted than the backend proved, on the primary action surface, directly against the Arc L console contract (privileged actions gated by the GO/NO-GO signal). Partially mitigated: the adjacent line shows danger DESTRUCTIVE and needs DDL plus the reason text.

FIX DIRECTION (do not fix in this pass)
Derive the verdict from the backend gate_decision (blocked and require_step_up are non-PASS) and/or an explicit backend-set refusal boolean, not a string heuristic over decision/danger; render gate_decision; treat an unknown or absent gate decision as unproven, never PASS.
close_reason: Fixed in 1429edd (web only, TDD). The Workbench Classifier-Verdict badge now reflects the guard's ACTUAL admission from the wire instead of a client-side string heuristic. workbenchVerdictFromAction (web/src/app/App.tsx) reads gate_decision as the admission authority and yields an explicit status: gate_decision blocked / danger FORBIDDEN / decision refus|block|deny -> refused; gate_decision require_step_up -> step_up; gate_decision allow|allow_lowering, a classifier-proven SAFE read, or observed execution (committed|rolled_back|rows_affected) -> pass; anything else (present-but-unproven verdict) -> unknown. WorkbenchVerdictBlock renders PASS(green/ok) ONLY for status pass; refused->warn, step_up->info, unknown->off, and only pass gets the green affirmed border. Absent verdict -> unknown, never a defaulted PASS, matching the 15.7 no-fake-proof bar. Regression test web/src/app/workbench-verdict.test.ts (8 cases, TDD red-then-green) pins: blocked->refused, FORBIDDEN->refused, require_step_up->never pass, allow->pass, SAFE->pass, executed-rolled-back->pass, DESTRUCTIVE-with-no-gate->unknown-never-pass, no-verdict->null. Gate: web vitest 144 passed / 7 skipped (operator-gated e2e), tsc --noEmit clean. Blast radius: App.tsx + new test only; no dist rebuild (CI regenerates it).

title: audit resume: head anchor MAC not verified -> forged-anchor tail-truncation bypass
description:
MULTI-PASS BUG HUNT 2026-07-04, Part A audit fresh-eyes (resume/anchor).

FINDING P1 (tamper-detection bypass): Auditor::resume_from (sink.rs) cross-checked a loaded head anchor by comparing ONLY its plaintext seq + entry_hash, never verifying its keyed MAC (anchor.mac_is_valid). The keyed MAC is the sole binding of the anchor plaintext to the real durable head. A tamperer with file-write access but NO signing key could: delete durable records, truncate the log, and rewrite the .anchor sidecar plaintext DOWN to the truncated tail (matching entry_hash) with a garbage MAC -> resume passed (no truncation trip since anchor.seq==tail.seq; plaintext hash matched). The first durable append after resume re-anchors under the live key, making the truncation PERMANENTLY undetectable locally. verify path (anchor::check_anchor) does verify the MAC (and UnknownKeyId), so a pre-append audit verify would catch it -> held at P1 (agent originally leaned P0).

FIX (sink.rs): new Auditor::verify_anchor_authenticity, called at the top of the anchor cross-check block in resume_from BEFORE trusting plaintext. Mirrors check_anchor: (1) anchor.key_id != active key_id -> ResumeRefused (closes the key_id-swap dodge; genuine cross-run rotation is reconciled by operator via audit verify, consistent with check_anchor UnknownKeyId); (2) !anchor.mac_is_valid(active key) -> ResumeRefused. Existing seq/hash/structural checks unchanged.

TESTS (sink.rs): resume_refuses_a_forged_anchor_masking_a_tail_truncation (full attack: 5-record chain, truncate to 3, forge sidecar seq=3 + correct entry_hash + bad MAC -> refuses with 'MAC does not verify'); resume_refuses_an_anchor_under_an_unknown_key_id (key_id swap -> refuses). Full audit suite green (65).

Credit: found by parallel fresh-eyes sub-audit of oraclemcp-audit.
close_reason: FIXED + tested (sink.rs: verify_anchor_authenticity mirrors check_anchor; resume_refuses_a_forged_anchor_masking_a_tail_truncation + resume_refuses_an_anchor_under_an_unknown_key_id). Audit suite (65) + workspace green.

title: Implement thin DBMS_OUTPUT capture
close_reason: Implemented DBMS_OUTPUT capture through thin output binds; live Oracle 23ai test now asserts capture instead of skipping; full gates passed

title: [QA-IMPL][SEC] Step-up window challenge must be single-use
description: Verified TTL-boundary bug in the step-up/session escalation path: StepUpRegistry::poll returns a resolved ApprovedWindow without consuming it, and oracle_session applies SessionLevelState::escalate_window from the current time on every use. Replaying one approved challenge_id before the challenge expires can refresh/extend the elevation window beyond the operator-approved window. Fix should consume resolved challenges when they are applied, while preserving pending polling and CI-token approval behavior. Acceptance: a regression test proves a second use of the same approved challenge_id is refused/unknown and cannot refresh elevation; existing step-up/session tests continue to pass; full relevant gates pass.
close_reason: Fixed by consuming resolved step-up challenges on grant application, making challenge resolution first-writer-wins, consuming CI-token approvals, and adding guard/session regressions that prove an approved window challenge cannot be replayed to refresh elevation.

title: [P1] Upgrade asupersync 0.3.5 -> 0.3.9 in lockstep with driver + oracledb repin
description:
Upgrade the async runtime asupersync from =0.3.5 to =0.3.9 on the SERVER, IN LOCKSTEP with the driver (rust-oracledb) bump. 

HARD DEPENDENCY: oracledb (the driver) pins asupersync EXACTLY (=0.3.x). The server depends on asupersync directly (workspace Cargo.toml:35 version "0.3.5", features metrics/test-internals across oraclemcp-core/db/telemetry/oraclemcp) AND transitively via oracledb. If the server's direct asupersync (0.3.9) does not match oracledb's transitive pin, cargo will refuse to unify (=0.3.5 vs =0.3.9). Therefore this bump is COUPLED to re-pinning oracledb to the new driver release that carries asupersync 0.3.9 (see x1hr.1 repin to 0.8.4). Until the driver publishes a 0.3.9-based oracledb, you may validate against a local/path or pre-release driver, but the FINAL landing uses the published driver.

VERIFY FIRST: invoke /asupersync-mega-skill; read the 0.3.5->0.3.9 delta (Cx/Scope, spawn, channels, cancellation, time, metrics feature). Determine the required nightly; if it differs from nightly-2026-05-11, bump rust-toolchain.toml + all .github/workflows/* toolchain pins in lockstep (edition 2024). Confirm feature flags (metrics default-features=false; test-internals) still valid. Audit our call sites to FOLLOW 0.3.9 idioms (current Cx/&Cx/RuntimeBuilder seams), not fight or shim removed APIs. Never weaken the fail-closed guard while refactoring.

Best engineering decision; if a breaking change forces a design choice or the nightly is ambiguous, ASK the operator BEFORE committing. THEN bump the pin (Cargo.toml:35 "0.3.5"->"0.3.9"), update Cargo.lock, adapt call sites, run the full gate under a build slot (fmt; clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings; test --workspace; cargo deny). Do NOT publish. Coordinate via Agent Mail with the driver pane owning the driver-side bump.


CRITICAL UNKNOWN (operator): the try_trait_v2 / nightly-only requirement documented for <=0.3.5 MAY BE RETIRED in 0.3.9 — do NOT assume it still holds. FIND OUT EMPIRICALLY (try building on stable): if 0.3.9 no longer uses nightly-only language features, evaluate moving this crate to STABLE Rust — relax/remove the nightly-2026-05-11 pin (rust-toolchain.toml + all workflow pins) and update AGENTS.md (the "no stable MSRV / nightly required" premise). If it still needs nightly, keep/bump the pin. Trust the compiler, not old docs.

title: [P0] Fix observed-SCN SQL on Oracle 23ai
description: Real served FREE 23ai proof for F4 shows every audited oracle_query fails before user SQL: CURRENT_SCN_SQL currently uses DBMS_FLASHBACK.GET_SYSTEM_CHANGE_NUMBER(), which returns ORA-00904. Oracle accepts the server-owned function reference without parentheses. Acceptance: correct the fixed server-owned query and its unit assertion; a real served oracle_query reaches the caller SQL; rerun the 23ai F4 ladder through VECTOR_DISTANCE.
close_reason: Corrected the 23ai server-owned SCN expression to omit parentheses and pinned it with a unit assertion. Added a fixed V$DATABASE fallback only for ORA-00904, with a regression proving privilege failures do not retry. Real served oracle_query returned the caller arithmetic row on XE18, XE21, and FREE23. Scoped oraclemcp-db fmt, strict clippy, and 426 tests passed. The rerun reaches the FREE23 VECTOR_DISTANCE query; its current semantic-guard refusal remains tracked by separately in-progress F4.

title: NPM wrapper publish blocked by missing npm credential or trusted publisher
description: v0.6.0 crates.io, GitHub release, GHCR, and MCP registry published successfully, but npm wrapper publication failed. The original release job failed because npm publish used bare npm/oraclemcp, which npm parsed as github:npm/oraclemcp; fixed in commits 611e6a8 and 8e27cf3. Manual workflow runs 28576593109 and 28576767987 then reached registry auth and failed ENEEDAUTH because secrets.NPM_TOKEN is empty/not configured and npm trusted publishing is not configured for the package. Resolve by adding an npm automation token as the npm environment NPM_TOKEN secret or configuring npm trusted publishing for the GitHub workflow, then rerun .github/workflows/publish-npm.yml with version=0.6.0 checkout_ref=v0.6.0.
notes:
Local cleanup done 2026-07-02: README marks npm/npx as conditional on the npm registry entry existing and points users to npm view before npx. release.yml now validates the npm wrapper/package but no longer makes the core signed release fail on externally blocked npm auth; manual .github/workflows/publish-npm.yml remains the publication path.

2026-07-02 retries after NPM_TOKEN was added: workflow runs 28588925645, 28590671481, and 28594134084 for version=0.6.1 checkout_ref=v0.6.1 had NODE_AUTH_TOKEN present, validated the wrapper, created the provenance statement, then npm publish failed with E403 Forbidden: 'You may not perform that action with these credentials.' npm view oraclemcp@0.6.1 and @0.6.2 still returned E404.

2026-07-02 v0.6.6 retest after the npm environment token update: workflow run 28606816835 for version=0.6.6 checkout_ref=v0.6.6 validated the wrapper, had NODE_AUTH_TOKEN present, created a signed provenance statement, then failed in npm publish with E403 Forbidden on PUT https://registry.npmjs.org/oraclemcp: 'You may not perform that action with these credentials.' npm view oraclemcp@0.6.6 still returns E404. Publish remains blocked until the npm environment secret is a token/account allowed to create/publish the unscoped oraclemcp package, or npm trusted publishing is configured for this GitHub workflow/package; then rerun publish-npm.yml.
close_reason: EXCLUDED by operator 2026-07-02: npm publish channel dropped from 0.6.x (like Scoop). npx oraclemcp is not offered. Release is unaffected: publish-npm.yml is manual-dispatch only and release.yml never published to npm. Distribution stands on crates.io, GitHub release, GHCR, MCP registry, cargo-binstall (+ brew/winget). Reopen only if npm is deliberately revived.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: 2026-07-02 follow-up: repo workflow improvement landed in oraclemcp-rmjm / commit 85cd98d. publish-npm.yml now has auth_mode=auto|token|oidc. Retry with auth_mode=token only after NPM_TOKEN belongs to an npm account/token that can create/publish the unscoped oraclemcp package. Retry with auth_mode=oidc only after npm trusted publishing is configured for owner MuhDur, repository oraclemcp, workflow publish-npm.yml, environment npm, allowed action npm publish; oidc mode intentionally unsets NODE_AUTH_TOKEN/NPM_CONFIG_USERCONFIG so the existing environment token cannot mask OIDC.

title: [09x][L][P1] Require live-Oracle proof for the served console
description: The registered scripts/e2e/served_console.sh currently defaults to a dead reader DSN and does not require OMCP_LIVE_DSN/OMCP_LIVE_USER/OMCP_LIVE_CRED. In that posture, the positive real-DB assertions (governed SELECT, observed SCN, verified verdict certificate, reachable fleet node) are disabled, so it proves only honesty negatives. It also invokes bare cargo build, contrary to the repository swarm contract. Keep the no-DB negative coverage separately if useful, but make the registered release scenario build through omcpb and require a local lab Oracle profile; prove the real HTTP MCP server produces and the Carved Light parsers consume a completed governed read, certificate/SCN, egress evidence, and reachable-plus-UNREACHABLE fleet map.

title: [P1][oci-harness] terraform 'Invalid index' fails apply+destroy AFTER ADB provisions (infra/oci-adb/main.tf)
description:
OCI-live apply-and-signoff run 21:10 FAILED with 'Error: Invalid index' from terraform, AFTER the oci_database_autonomous_database + wallet were successfully created (5 resources in state). The same Invalid-index bug then broke the auto-destroy exit trap, leaving a throwaway Always-Free ADB running. This is a Terraform CONFIG bug in infra/oci-adb/main.tf (an index/attribute reference like foo[0] on an empty/absent list, likely reading the wallet content or a connection field post-apply). NOT a creds/auth problem (auth+provision worked). FIX: find + fix the invalid index in the .tf (guard the attribute access / use try() or the correct index), verify [32m[1mSuccess![0m The configuration is valid.
[0m + a plan, and ensure destroy works. Also make the exit-trap destroy robust to a partial/failed apply (targeted destroy / oci CLI fallback) so a throwaway ADB is never left running. Then the OCI-live gate can pass. Do NOT run apply against the cloud yourself; fix + terraform validate only.
close_reason: Guarded empty connection_strings with try(index), added OCI CLI delete fallback after Terraform destroy failure, and validated Terraform plus shell syntax.

title: Streaming operator status has no aria-live region; async lane/action/SSE updates unannounced to assistive tech
description:
Web Interface Guidelines finding from the dashboard review pass (independently confirmed by a second reviewer).

There are ZERO aria-live regions in the entire SPA (grep aria-live across web/src/app/App.tsx, skin.tsx, orrery-renderer.tsx, web/src/components/ui/primitives.tsx = 0). The only live-region role anywhere is a single role=alert on an error line (App.tsx:7758). This is a live, polling, SSE-driven operator console for a production database, so async state changes are never announced to assistive tech.

The two that matter most (safety-critical status a screen-reader operator must not miss):
- skin.tsx:1502 GroundControl2DRenderer — the GO/NO-GO fail-closed verdict banner at the top of every page. When it flips GO to NO-GO it is silent.
- skin.tsx:1599 ChainStrip — the audit hash-chain INTACT/BROKEN tamper signal. A tamper detection is announced to no one.

Others:
- App.tsx:6513 OperatorOutcomeNotice — reports every mutation outcome (DML commit, config apply, refused/failed); plain div, no aria-live.
- App.tsx:3180 live classifier verdict feed (PASS/HOLD/REFUSED over SSE) — plain div, no aria-live.
- App.tsx:899 lane-kill cancelNotice; App.tsx:1919/2208/6366 Preview/Apply/Rollback error badges — no aria-live.

Severity: MEDIUM-HIGH per WIG (async updates need aria-live=polite), elevated because the GO/NO-GO verdict and chain-tamper are the console's two most important operator signals and both are silent. Fix direction: wrap the verdict banner, chain strip, outcome notice, and SSE verdict feed in aria-live=polite regions (aria-live=assertive for the chain-tamper/NO-GO transitions). Filed via the dashboard review; do not fix in this pass.
close_reason: Fixed across 685181b + 448b22a (web only, TDD for the core). Added aria-live announcement to every streaming/async operator-status surface. Core (tested via new web/src/app/aria-live.test.tsx, 3 cases): GO/NO-GO fail-closed verdict banner (skin.tsx GroundControl2DRenderer) and audit-chain tamper strip (skin.tsx ChainStrip) each got a dedicated sr-only role=status aria-live=polite announcer — deliberately separate from their per-second clocks so the region fires on a verdict/chain change, not every tick; OperatorOutcomeNotice (App.tsx) got role=status + aria-live=polite directly. Secondary surfaces (static ARIA, covered by the same pattern): SSE classifier verdict feed = role=log + aria-live=polite; lane-kill cancelNotice = role=status + aria-live=polite; the three Preview/Apply/Clients/Schema-Diff error badges = role=alert (Badge forwards HTMLAttributes). sr-only is a real Tailwind v4 utility (dist CSS: .sr-only{clip-path:inset(50%)...}). Gate: web vitest 147 passed / 7 skipped, tsc --noEmit clean, dashboard_skin_lint OK. Now a screen-reader operator hears a GO->NO-GO flip, an audit-chain BROKEN, and every mutation outcome.

title: QA100-CI: update forged-cursor conformance for revision-bound payloads
close_reason: Verified revision binding intentionally changed signed cursor payload from offset to revision:offset; updated the cross-endpoint forgery proof to preserve the genuine revision while tampering only with the offset. Focused conformance and pagination tests pass.

title: Parity 2462/2578 is REAL but STALE: measured 2026-06-14 at driver version 0.0.0, asserted CONFIRMED for the 0.8.4 release
description:
[MEDIUM — claim vs reality] PHASE-4 reality check of the headline conformance claim.

VERDICT: the number is REAL, not fabricated — but it is STALE, and it is asserted as CONFIRMED for a release it was never measured against.

WHAT IS GENUINELY GOOD (checked, credit where due): docs/PARITY_SKIPS.md in rust-oracledb documents a sound methodology. The skip decisions are made by the reference suite's own conftest.py fixtures and in-test pytest.skip calls BEFORE any driver code runs, so the driver under test cannot influence which tests skip — that is a real argument that the 116 skips do not hide engine defects, not spin. It further records skip sets identical at exact node-ID level between the reference thin driver and the Rust engine (116 vs 116), 303/303 baseline-passing tests still passing in those modules, and 0 regressions. A reproducer exists (scripts/parity_rigor.sh, harness/run.sh). The repo even ships scripts/fake_parity_scan.py + docs/FAKE_PARITY_AUDIT.md — it self-audits for fake parity. So question 1's 'any proven-skip that is actually a hidden defect?' = NO EVIDENCE OF ONE, and the methodology is the right one.

THE GAP — CURRENCY, NOT HONESTY:
- The measurement commit is 62c0c58, dated 2026-06-14. At that commit the driver's Cargo.toml said version = "0.0.0".
- The driver is now 0.8.4. Between then and now the line took 0.8.0's BREAKING shape_cache/lob_stream consolidation (7627a1e), decode-mutation work, K10 OwnedRowStream, and the asupersync 0.3.5 migration.
- docs/PARITY_SKIPS.md has not been re-derived since: its only later commit (23aa1b4) is a de-slopify docs pass, not a re-run.
- No newer full-parity artifact exists. docs/PARITY_LEDGER.md is a DIFFERENT artifact (upstream-issue triage, last audit 2026-06-29), and today's docs/baseline touch (f52dfae) is a K10 API baseline, not the conformance suite.
- The full suite is NOT a CI gate. _quality.yml:213 'Reference version-gate parity coverage' runs extract_reference_gates.sh --check, which is a version-gate DRIFT check, not a re-derivation of 2462/2578. live.yml only comments that the full suite additionally needs a Python venv + a maturin build, which it does not stand up.
- Meanwhile oraclemcp/docs/plan/PLAN_0_8_x_ALIEN.md:790 lists 'driver 0.8.2 / asupersync 0.3.5 / 2462-of-2578 parity | CONFIRMED'. It even names 0.8.2 — not the 0.8.4 shipping — so the row is self-evidently about an older driver while reading as a release confirmation.

FIX: re-run scripts/parity_rigor.sh against driver 0.8.4 and either (a) confirm 2462/2578 still holds and stamp the artifact with the driver version + commit SHA + date, or (b) record the true new numbers. Then make the claim self-dating: every place that cites 2462/2578 (driver README.md:49, docs/PARITY_SKIPS.md:18, oraclemcp PLAN_0_7_3.md:100, PLAN_0_8_x_ALIEN.md:790) should carry the driver version and run date it was measured at, so a stale number cannot masquerade as a current one. Best: make it a release gate so the number cannot drift from the artifact again.

DO NOT: re-close on the existing document. The whole point is that reading PARITY_SKIPS.md proves 0.0.0-era behaviour, not 0.8.4 behaviour.
close_reason:
FIXED in 453d6b6 (claim now dated). The number is REAL; presenting it as CONFIRMED for the shipping driver was not.

AUTHORITATIVE CURRENT VALUE — pulled from the driver's qualification bundle, not restated from a plan doc: 2578 collected / 2462 passed / 116 skipped / 0 regressions / 0 missing, measured 2026-06-22T18:28:26Z at driver candidate SHA b4a0cd3e77e3d7ed9cd875ba8002968860c9954a (rust-oracledb/docs/qualification/1.0.0-rc.1/SUMMARY.md). It has NOT been re-derived since.

CORRECTION TO MY OWN BEAD: I originally wrote 'measured 2026-06-14 at driver version 0.0.0', inferred from the PARITY_SKIPS commit date. That was imprecise. The authoritative qualification is 2026-06-22 @ b4a0cd3e. (GROUND_TRUTH.md separately records the count first observed 2026-06-11 @ 978491a; PARITY_SKIPS.md's taxonomy commit is 2026-06-14.) The conclusion — real but stale, never re-derived for the shipping driver — is unchanged; the date/SHA are now exact.

THE DRIVER SIDE WAS ALREADY HONEST — credit where due: rust-oracledb/docs/RELEASE_CERTIFICATION.md carries a '0.8.3 evidence reconciliation (2026-07-16)' that states the 0.8.3 matrix artifact records five PASS lanes but no per-test count, that 'The only recorded full reference-parity count remains the historical qualification at b4a0cd3e', and literally 'Do not represent these counts as a fresh 0.8.3 reference run.' The overstatement was on the ORACLEMCP side.

FIXED HERE: PLAN_0_8_x_ALIEN §4-GT marked the claim CONFIRMED while citing driver Cargo.toml / README.md:49 / PARITY_SKIPS.md:18 — citations that prove the number is WRITTEN DOWN, not that it is CURRENT. That is exactly how a row describing driver 0.8.2 came to read as confirmation for what ships. The row now states the count, date, SHA, that it was not re-derived, and that the full suite is not a CI gate (_quality.yml's 'parity coverage' = extract_reference_gates.sh --check, a version-gate DRIFT check) so it cannot self-refresh. PLAN_0_7_3 is retained as history per project convention and keeps its text, plus a short as-of note so the count cannot be mistaken for current.

NOTHING TO FIX IN README/AGENTS: verified they never claimed the number (grep: no 2462/2578 in either). Reported rather than silently skipped.

NOT CORRECTED, BECAUSE IT HOLDS UP: the methodology. Skip decisions are made by the reference suite's own conftest.py fixtures and in-test pytest.skip calls BEFORE any driver code runs, so the driver under test cannot influence which tests skip; skip sets match at exact node-ID level between reference and Rust engine (116/116), with 303/303 baseline-passing tests still passing and 0 regressions. NO proven-skip hides an engine defect. The gap was CURRENCY, not honesty.

STILL OWED (the bead's other option, deliberately NOT silently dropped): a FRESH measurement against the shipping driver, via rust-oracledb/scripts/parity_rigor.sh, stamped with driver version + SHA + date. Best made a release gate so the number cannot drift from its artifact again. That work is cross-repo (driver) and gates on the driver publish, so it is not doable from here; this bead closes the CLAIM-vs-reality gap (the docs now tell the truth), not the measurement gap.

GATE: release_surface_sync_check EXIT 0; oraclemcp_honesty_grep EXIT 0. Docs only; no code touched.

title: [09x][N4] Policy tightening is evaluated but never enforced or attached to a response
description: SYMPTOM: the Arc N policy evaluator is fully built and completely unreachable. crates/oraclemcp-guard/src/policy.rs implements the monotone evaluator SqlPolicyConfig::evaluate(base, context) -> PolicyTightening::Deny{reason, matched_rule_ids} | Narrow{base_required_level, required_level, predicates, matched_rule_ids} (ADR 0009, no Allow outcome), and a profile can carry one (ConnectionProfile::sql_policy, crates/oraclemcp-config/src/profile.rs:805). But NOTHING calls evaluate() outside the guard's own tests: grep for PolicyTightening / PolicyNarrowing / sql_policy across crates/oraclemcp/src/dispatch/ and crates/oraclemcp-core/src/http/ returns no hits. So a configured policy is (a) not enforced on the dispatch path and (b) not reported to any client. THE DASHBOARD IS SHOWING LESS PROOF THAN THE BACKEND HAS — and here it is worse than cosmetic: an operator can write a deny/require_level/require_predicate rule, load it, and the server will neither apply it nor say that it did not. WHY IT MATTERS: this is a silent policy-not-enforced condition, i.e. a safety gap, not just a UI gap. The dashboard policy badge (bead …15.11, commit 17062a1) already parses the ADR-0009 shape from result.policy / structured_reason.policy_tightening and renders data-policy-effect + data-narrowed-from; it currently and truthfully reports 'not reported' on every response, which is explicitly NOT the claim that no policy applied. A FIX MUST PROVE: (1) the active profile's sql_policy is evaluated against the classifier decision on the guarded dispatch path, before execution; (2) the narrowing is ENFORCED — the level floor composed with max() (never a replacement, never a lowering) and predicates applied via the N3 rewrite with mandatory re-classification of the rewritten SQL; (3) a require_level match returns base_required_level, required_level and the matched rule ids on the tool response, and a deny match returns POLICY_DENIED carrying its reason and rule ids; (4) a test proves a policy can never turn a classifier-refused statement into a dispatchable one, and can never lower a required level; (5) the dashboard badge lights up with NO client change.
close_reason:
Arc N policy is now enforced. Guard seam aba296f (enforce_sql_policy + the ADR-0009 response proof), dispatch wiring 2903998.

ALL FIVE OF THE BEAD'S 'A FIX MUST PROVE' ITEMS:
(1) EVALUATED ON THE GUARDED PATH, BEFORE EXECUTION - three call sites, because a statement becomes authority in three places: oracle_execute (refuses before the level gate, before a grant is consumed, before the DB is touched); oracle_query on BOTH the buffered AND the streaming path (a read that escaped the policy just by asking for SSE delivery would be a hole the size of the whole feature); and oracle_preview_sql - REQUIRED and easy to miss, because preview mints the single-use grant AT the required level: if a policy floor raised that level and preview did not know, the grant would be minted below the bar and refused at execute, and the agent could never obtain a usable one. Preview now mints at the COMPOSED level; a denied statement mints nothing.
(2) NARROWING IS ENFORCED - the level floor composes with max() (never a replacement, never a lowering) and the gate enforces the composed level; predicates are applied via the existing N3 rewrite_predicates_and_reclassify (bead 5.4, not reimplemented), the rewritten candidate re-enters the classifier (SEC-1) and is then re-marked and put through the SAME semantic read gate, so a rewrite can never widen what the read path admits. The predicate reaches Oracle - a test asserts the WHERE clause is in the SQL the database sees, because reporting a row filter is not enforcing it.
(3) THE RESPONSE CARRIES THE PROOF - base_required_level, required_level, matched_rule_ids, and predicates on the tool result; a Deny returns POLICY_DENIED with reason + rule ids on the envelope's structured_reason.
(4) TESTS - a configured Deny refuses at dispatch and never reaches the DB; a Narrow raises the required level and the RAISED level is what is gated (a READ_WRITE session is refused by a DDL floor on an UPDATE); a predicate rewrite actually reaches Oracle; a policy can never admit what the classifier refuses; an unevaluatable policy REFUSES (never falls open); an unpoliced profile is byte-for-byte unchanged and makes no claim. Plus the 9 guard-side tests incl. an exhaustive monotonicity check over the level lattice. 326 lib tests green.
(5) THE DASHBOARD BADGE LIGHTS UP WITH NO CLIENT CHANGE - the guard's PolicyTightening serializes into exactly the shape parsePolicyTightening already reads; a guard test pins that JSON against the client's parser contract.

TWO BUGS THE TESTS CAUGHT, both of which would have shipped: (a) a classifier-FORBIDDEN statement was coming back POLICY_DENIED - the policy taking credit for the guard's refusal, and claiming a policy refused it even on a profile that has none; the classifier's refusal now stands unchanged. (b) The policy floor was leaking into ORACLE-SEMANTICS checks. They are two different levels: statement_level is what the statement IS (DDL commits implicitly and cannot be rollback-previewed); required_level is what the session must HAVE. A DDL floor on an UPDATE means 'only a DDL-cleared caller may run this UPDATE' - it does not make the UPDATE un-rollbackable.

BEYOND THE BEAD, fail-closed: if a rule selects a schema/object and the statement's target cannot be PROVEN (a join, an alias, a CTE), the statement is refused rather than evaluated against a guess - a deny rule that quietly fails to match is a policy that silently did not apply. The schema a rule matches on is resolved from the CONNECTION once per session, never from a tool argument (a caller that could assert its own schema could dodge the rule naming it). The policy installs inside the same generation-locked commit as the profile's level and masking, so a lane can never run one profile's statements under another profile's policy.

TIGHTEN-ONLY, and no Allow outcome is representable anywhere in the chain: the grammar cannot express one, the evaluator returns only Deny/Narrow, and the gate's Admitted is explicitly not an authorization (the base gate still runs). The composed level is a max() and the gate re-checks it against the base, so a future edit that broke monotonicity would fail closed rather than silently grant.

title: audit resume seeds from tail without structural chain verification (interior fork accepted)
description: Auditor::resume_from (crates/oraclemcp-audit/src/sink.rs) parsed the on-disk JSONL and seeded chain state directly from records.last() without walking the chain. parse_jsonl only proves each line is valid JSON. Consequence: a forked interior (deleted, reordered, inserted, or in-place-edited middle record) that leaves the last line parseable was accepted at startup and the new run chained onto the forked prefix. The head anchor does NOT cover this: it attests only the head seq/entry_hash, so an interior deletion whose surviving tail still matches the anchored head passes the anchor cross-check. Only later ad-hoc 'audit verify' would catch it. FIX (landed): added a keyless structural pre-check (hash-link continuity + monotonic seq + hash_is_valid; no MAC so rotated chains are not false-refused) that fails closed with ResumeRefused before seeding. Tests: resume_refuses_a_deleted_interior_even_with_a_matching_anchor, resume_refuses_a_reordered_interior_without_an_anchor, resume_accepts_a_structurally_intact_multi_record_log.
close_reason: Fixed: keyless structural pre-check added to resume_from; 3 tests added; all green

title: classifier: derived/JOIN/UNION/Expr-subquery-smuggled DML cleared to Safe (fail-closed-net hole)
description:
MULTI-PASS BUG HUNT 2026-07-04, Part A adversarial classifier probe.

FINDING (fail-closed-net hole, smuggled-DML class flagged to re-probe): a DML SetExpr (UPDATE/DELETE/INSERT/MERGE) wrapped in a FROM/JOIN derived subquery, a UNION-branch FROM(...), or a WHERE/scalar/EXISTS Expr subquery parsed by sqlparser 0.62 as Statement::Query and cleared to danger=Safe, required_level=READ_ONLY. set_expr_carries_dml inspected only the TOP-LEVEL query body (prior oracle-cte-dml-body fix), missing derived and Expr subqueries.

Reproducers cleared to Safe BEFORE fix: SELECT * FROM (UPDATE t SET x=1); (DELETE FROM t); (INSERT INTO t VALUES(1)); (MERGE...); nested SELECT * FROM (SELECT * FROM (UPDATE...)); UNION branch; JOIN (UPDATE...); WHERE id IN (UPDATE...); nested-CTE WITH a AS(...) SELECT * FROM (WITH b AS(...) UPDATE t SET v=1).

SEVERITY P0 per operator rubric (write classifying Safe). Honest executability caveat: standard Oracle rejects DML inside FROM/WHERE subqueries so these exact strings are non-executable today; blast radius nil. But the fail-closed contract is violated and relying on Oracle-rejects is the implicit trust fail-closed design forbids.

FIX classifier.rs: (1) set_expr_carries_dml descends into SetExpr::Select FROM/JOIN derived subqueries, nested joins, CTE bodies via new helpers query_carries_dml / table_with_joins_carries_dml / table_factor_carries_dml (all 4 verbs, structural). (2) query_embeds_reserved_dml_verb: canonical-scan net for reserved INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE tokens covering Expr-embedded subqueries; reserved words = zero false positives (FOR UPDATE already Guarded via query.locks). MERGE excluded from token scan (non-reserved) but caught structurally. Query Safe arm gated on both.

RESIDUAL documented: MERGE embedded ONLY in an Expr subquery is not token-scanned; Oracle-invalid + non-executable.

TESTS adversarial_corpus.rs: 10 new CORPUS entries (min Guarded) + derived_subquery_smuggled_dml_is_never_read_only asserting writes>=Guarded and legit nested reads / DML-substring columns / DML-word literals stay Safe. Guard suite green.
close_reason: FIXED + tested (adversarial_corpus.rs: 10 corpus entries + derived_subquery_smuggled_dml_is_never_read_only). set_expr_carries_dml now descends into derived/JOIN/nested/CTE subqueries + query_embeds_reserved_dml_verb net for Expr-embedded reserved DML. Guard suite + full workspace test + clippy + 6 lints + api-lock green. Live-server regression step added to ladder (read_only_smuggled_dml_not_served_as_read), PENDING-LIVE.

title: B16a follow-up: driver TokenSource must carry OCI IAM PoP key
description: The server must not replace the static OCI IAM proof-of-possession path with TokenSource until the driver can carry the bound PKCS#8 signing key alongside a refreshable token. Current APIs expose with_access_token_and_key(token, key) and with_token_source(source) separately; the latter cannot configure access_token_private_key. Wiring only TokenSource would drop AUTH_HEADER/AUTH_SIGNATURE and yield ORA-01017; wiring both leaves static-token precedence and preserves the stale-token defect. Add a driver API for a source plus key (or a credential-pair source), preserve redaction and TCPS-before-source refusal, publish/adopt it, then complete B16a.
close_reason: Verified driver TokenSource plus OCI IAM PoP key handoff [closing=fda3c978bff606f65973162760757370e75fd91d source=711818afe707462e98b9e42fe55c4262c6ae203a evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-verqx.json]

title: A1d follow-up: live e2e ladder asserted the removed empty-describe shape
description:
CI-RED on main (run 29831595915, 7dce026, job 'Oracle FREE 23ai matrix + VECTOR smoke'): LADDER FAIL (free23) -- 'assertion failed: table is absent through the bounded describe tool' with context error_class=OBJECT_NOT_FOUND isError=True.

CAUSE: A1d (oraclemcp-091-a1d-describe-notfound-q9e4v) correctly replaced oracle_describe's empty-success-on-missing-object with a structured ObjectNotFound ErrorEnvelope (dispatch/mod.rs, the columns.is_empty() branch). scripts/e2e/oracle_ladder_session.py ddl_drop_table() still asserted the OLD shape: description.get('columns') == []. git log a8f31b0..7dce026 -- scripts/e2e is empty, so the contract moved and the live lane was never updated with it. The unit tests A1d shipped are all offline, so nothing caught it until the live matrix ran.

RULING (deliberate, not test-greening): the E2E LADDER is the wrong side. A1d's own analysis is the argument -- describe is catalog-based, so an empty ALL_TAB_COLUMNS can never mean VPD filtering; it meant 'not visible for the computed (owner, table)'. Using columns == [] as the absence signal forced one value to carry two incompatible meanings, and the field read it as the wrong one. This is the same conflation C7/A1c remove on the query side: an empty collection must never be the signal for 'does not exist'.

FIX LANDED: ddl_drop_table() now asserts isError is True, error_class == OBJECT_NOT_FOUND, and non-empty next_steps. Strictly stronger than the old assertion -- it proves the object is absent AND that the refusal is actionable, which columns == [] could not distinguish from a mis-computed owner.

CLASS FINDING worth acting on separately: an offline-only acceptance set for a contract that a live lane asserts will keep producing this. A1d's acceptance was 'unit tests for not-found, unresolved synonym, quoted lower-case identifier' -- all offline. When a tool's response SHAPE changes, scripts/e2e must be grepped for assertions on the old shape in the same commit.
close_reason: Ladder now asserts A1d's structured actionable not-found contract [closing=446c08bfc4f06e2a32d910b419d6b582dc22857a source=f00e03e81707b259854200f107f66d47dafedd37 evidence=tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/oraclemcp-vfgn1.json]

Likely the open_private_lock_file FILE_FLAG_OPEN_REPARSE_POINT / number_of_links path. Linux fully green (2718 tests). Ship 0.9.0 Linux/Docker first; fix Windows in 0.9.1. title: W0: build repo facts and behavior inventory
description: Create the implementation branch's source-of-truth behavior inventory at docs/behavior-inventory.md before downstream migration work starts. Inventory CLI commands, MCP messages, tool registry, credential surfaces, hard invariants, dependency holdouts, oracledb API coverage, proxy auth, Autonomous Database/cloud connectivity, explain-plan behavior, and baseline measurements. Include current behavior for session leases, rollback, DBMS_OUTPUT, NUMBER/date/NLS serialization, AWR/ASH license gating, preview tokens, no native plugins, OAuth scopes, and release/runtime prerequisites. Also audit the target Asupersync web API surface for W9 primitives.
design: Implementation shape: create docs/behavior-inventory.md as the committed source of truth before any downstream code migration. Use repo facts, not memory. Inventory current CLI commands, MCP methods/messages, tool registry, config/profile fields, credential surfaces, safety invariants, dependency holdouts, oracledb API needs, proxy auth, ADB/cloud connectivity, explain-plan behavior, and baseline measurements. Include exact files/tests that prove current behavior. Also audit the pinned Asupersync HTTP/web surface for W9 feasibility.
acceptance_criteria: docs/behavior-inventory.md exists and is committed before downstream beads start; it includes tables for CLI, MCP, tools, credentials, invariants, dependencies, oracledb APIs, proxy auth, ADB/cloud, explain-plan behavior, Asupersync HTTP/web primitives, and baselines; no later bead relies on vague preserve-current-behavior wording without named tests/transcripts/evidence.
notes: Likely files/commands: AGENTS.md, README.md, Cargo.toml, .github/workflows/*, server.json, scripts/oraclemcp_boundary_lint.sh, crates/oraclemcp*/src and tests, cargo tree, br/bv state. Required tables: CLI surface; MCP stdio/HTTP transcripts; DB tools; credential/log/panic/fixture surfaces; session lease invariants; NUMBER/date/NLS serialization; AWR/ASH gating; preview tokens; no native plugins; OAuth scopes; explain-plan/PLAN_TABLE; proxy auth/DRCP; ADB wallet/TCPS/SNI/IAM; Asupersync HTTP API primitives; binary/Docker/startup/query baselines. If oracle_explain_plan writes PLAN_TABLE, create a separate correctness bead before W4. Validation: inventory should cite tests, commands, transcripts, or source locations for every hard invariant and must not record real secrets or real customer data.
close_reason: Implemented behavior inventory in docs/behavior-inventory.md; patched AGENTS.md repo-local bead wording; created W3.5 for explain-plan PLAN_TABLE semantics before W4.

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text: W0 implementation artifact created at docs/behavior-inventory.md with required tables for CLI, MCP, tools, credentials, invariants, dependencies, oracledb APIs, proxy auth/DRCP, ADB/cloud, explain-plan behavior, Asupersync HTTP/web primitives, and baselines. AGENTS.md stale parent-repo beads wording was patched.
